verses.translation: 'If fate has written a mad husband for me, then why blame anyone? Can you erase the writing of destiny, O mother? Do not invite disgrace in vain.'

verses.original:
जनि लेहु मातु कलंकु करुना परिहरहु अवसर नहीं।
दुखु सुखु जो लिखा लिलार हमरें जाब जहँ पाउब तहीं।।
सुनि उमा बचन बिनीत कोमल सकल अबला सोचहीं।।
बहु भाँति बिधिहि लगाइ दूषन नयन बारि बिमोचहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Do not invite disgrace, O mother; abandon this sorrow — this is not the time. Whatever joy or sorrow is written on our forehead, we will go where it takes us and receive what it gives.' Hearing Uma's humble and gentle words, all the women grieved. Blaming the Creator in many ways, they shed tears from their eyes.

verses.original:
तेहि अवसर नारद सहित अरु रिषि सप्त समेत।
समाचार सुनि तुहिनगिरि गवने तुरत निकेत।।97।।
verses.translation: At that time, Narada, accompanied by the seven sages, heard the news and came quickly to the mountain king's dwelling.

verses.original:
तब नारद सबहि समुझावा। पूरुब कथाप्रसंगु सुनावा।।
मयना सत्य सुनहु मम बानी। जगदंबा तव सुता भवानी।।
verses.translation: Then Narada consoled everyone and narrated the full story of the past. 'Maina, hear my true words — your daughter Bhavani is the Mother of the Universe.'

verses.original:
अजा अनादि सक्ति अबिनासिनि। सदा संभु अरधंग निवासिनि।।
जग संभव पालन लय कारिनि। निज इच्छा लीला बपु धारिनि।।
verses.translation: 'She is the unborn, eternal, indestructible power, forever dwelling as Shambhu's other half. She is the cause of the creation, sustenance, and dissolution of the world, who assumes a body through her own playful will.'

verses.original:
जनमीं प्रथम दच्छ गृह जाई। नामु सती सुंदर तनु पाई।।
तहँहुँ सती संकरहि बिबाहीं। कथा प्रसिद्ध सकल जग माहीं।।
verses.translation: 'First, she was born in the house of Daksha and received a beautiful body named Sati. There too, Sati married Shankara — this story is well-known throughout the world.'

verses.original:
एक बार आवत सिव संगा। देखेउ रघुकुल कमल पतंगा।।
भयउ मोहु सिव कहा न कीन्हा। भ्रम बस बेषु सीय कर लीन्हा।।
verses.translation: 'Once, while traveling with Shiva, she beheld the sun of the Raghu dynasty (Rama). She was overcome by delusion and, disregarding Shiva's words, assumed the form of Sita.'

verses.original:
सिय बेषु सती जो कीन्ह तेहि अपराध संकर परिहरीं।
हर बिरहँ जाइ बहोरि पितु कें जग्य जोगानल जरीं।।
अब जनमि तुम्हरे भवन निज पति लागि दारुन तपु किया।
अस जानि संसय तजहु गिरिजा सर्बदा संकर प्रिया।।
verses.translation: 'Because Sati assumed Sita's form, Shankara abandoned her for that transgression. Separated from Hara, she returned to her father's sacrifice and burned herself in the sacrificial fire. Now, having been reborn in your home, she performed severe penance for her lord. Knowing this, give up your doubts — Girija is forever dear to Shankara.'

verses.original:
सुनि नारद के बचन तब सब कर मिटा बिषाद।
छन महुँ ब्यापेउ सकल पुर घर घर यह संबाद।।98।।
verses.translation: Hearing Narada's words, everyone's sorrow vanished. In an instant, this news spread throughout the city, from house to house.

verses.original:
तब मयना हिमवंतु अनंदे। पुनि पुनि पारबती पद बंदे।।
नारि पुरुष सिसु जुबा सयाने। नगर लोग सब अति हरषाने।।
verses.translation: Then Maina and Himavan were filled with joy and bowed again and again at Parvati's feet. Women and men, children, youths, and elders — all the people of the city were overjoyed.

verses.original:
लगे होन पुर मंगलगाना। सजे सबहि हाटक घट नाना।।
भाँति अनेक भई जेवराना। सूपसास्त्र जस कछु ब्यवहारा।।
verses.translation: Auspicious songs began to be sung throughout the city; everyone decorated golden pots of various kinds. Feasts of many varieties were prepared, following every tradition known to the culinary arts.

verses.original:
सो जेवनार कि जाइ बखानी। बसहिं भवन जेहिं मातु भवानी।।
सादर बोले सकल बराती। बिष्नु बिरंचि देव सब जाती।।
verses.translation: Can that feast even be described — in the house where Mother Bhavani herself dwelt? All the members of the wedding procession were respectfully invited — Vishnu, Brahma, gods of every kind.

verses.original:
बिबिधि पाँति बैठी जेवनारा। लागे परुसन निपुन सुआरा।।
नारिबृंद सुर जेवँत जानी। लगीं देन गारीं मृदु बानी।।
verses.translation: The guests sat in various rows for the feast, and skilled cooks began serving them. When the women learned that the gods were dining, they began singing gentle, teasing songs.

verses.original:
गारीं मधुर स्वर देहिं सुंदरि बिंग्य बचन सुनावहीं।
भोजनु करहिं सुर अति बिलंबु बिनोदु सुनि सचु पावहीं।।
जेवँत जो बढ्यो अनंदु सो मुख कोटिहूँ न परै कह्यो।
अचवाँइ दीन्हे पान गवने बास जहँ जाको रह्यो।।
verses.translation: The beautiful women sang sweet teasing songs and made witty remarks. The gods ate their meal very slowly, delighting in the jests they heard. The joy that swelled during the feast cannot be described even by millions of mouths. After rinsing their mouths and receiving betel leaves, each guest went to their assigned lodging.

verses.original:
बहुरि मुनिन्ह हिमवंत कहुँ लगन सुनाई आइ।
समय बिलोकि बिबाह कर पठए देव बोलाइ।।99।।
verses.translation: Then the sages came to Himavan and announced the auspicious time. Seeing the right moment for the wedding, they summoned the gods.

verses.original:
बोलि सकल सुर सादर लीन्हे। सबहि जथोचित आसन दीन्हे।।
बेदी बेद बिधान सँवारी। सुभग सुमंगल गावहिं नारी।।
verses.translation: All the gods were respectfully invited and given appropriate seats. The wedding altar was prepared according to Vedic rites, and women sang beautiful auspicious songs.

verses.original:
सिंघासनु अति दिब्य सुहावा। जाइ न बरनि बिरंचि बनावा।।
बैठे सिव बिप्रन्ह सिरु नाई। हृदयँ सुमिरि निज प्रभु रघुराई।।
verses.translation: A most divine and beautiful throne — beyond description, fashioned by Brahma himself — was placed there. Shiva sat down after bowing to the Brahmins, remembering his Lord Raghurai in his heart.

verses.original:
बहुरि मुनीसन्ह उमा बोलाई। करि सिंगारु सखीं लै आई।।
देखत रूपु सकल सुर मोहे। बरनै छबि अस जग कबि को है।।
verses.translation: Then the great sages called for Uma. Adorned in bridal finery, her companions brought her forth. Seeing her beauty, all the gods were enchanted; what poet in the world could describe her loveliness?

verses.original:
जगदंबिका जानि भव भामा। सुरन्ह मनहिं मन कीन्ह प्रनामा।।
सुंदरता मरजाद भवानी। जाइ न कोटिहुँ बदन बखानी।।
verses.translation: Knowing her as the Mother of the Universe and Shiva's consort, the gods silently bowed to her in their hearts. Bhavani's beauty is the very boundary of loveliness — it cannot be described even by millions of mouths.

verses.original:
कोटिहुँ बदन नहिं बनै बरनत जग जननि सोभा महा।
सकुचहिं कहत श्रुति सेष सारद मंदमति तुलसी कहा।।
छबिखानि मातु भवानि गवनी मध्य मंडप सिव जहाँ।।
अवलोकि सकहिं न सकुच पति पद कमल मनु मधुकरु तहाँ।।
verses.translation: Even millions of mouths cannot describe the great splendor of the Mother of the Universe. The Vedas, Shesha, and Sarada hesitate to speak of it — what then can the dull-witted Tulsi say? Bhavani, the treasure of beauty, entered the central pavilion where Shiva was seated. Overcome with shyness, she could not look at her lord; her mind was like a bee hovering around his lotus feet.

verses.original:
मुनि अनुसासन गनपतिहि पूजेउ संभु भवानि।
कोउ सुनि संसय करै जनि सुर अनादि जियँ जानि।।100।।
verses.translation: Following the sages' instructions, Shambhu and Bhavani worshipped Ganapati. Let no one hear this and raise doubt, knowing that the gods are eternal and beginningless.

verses.original:
जसि बिबाह कै बिधि श्रुति गाई। महामुनिन्ह सो सब करवाई।।
गहि गिरीस कुस कन्या पानी। भवहि समरपीं जानि भवानी।।
verses.translation: The great sages performed all the wedding rites as prescribed by the Vedas. The mountain king placed kusha grass in his daughter's hand and, recognizing her as Bhavani, offered her to Shiva.

verses.original:
पानिग्रहन जब कीन्ह महेसा। हिंयँ हरषे तब सकल सुरेसा।।
बेद मंत्र मुनिबर उच्चरहीं। जय जय जय संकर सुर करहीं।।
verses.translation: When Maheshwara performed the ceremony of taking her hand, all the great gods rejoiced in their hearts. The great sages chanted Vedic mantras, and the gods cried 'Victory, victory, victory to Shankara!'

verses.original:
बाजहिं बाजन बिबिध बिधाना। सुमनबृष्टि नभ भै बिधि नाना।।
हर गिरिजा कर भयउ बिबाहू। सकल भुवन भरि रहा उछाहू।।
verses.translation: Instruments of many kinds played, and flowers of various sorts rained from the sky. The marriage of Hara and Girija was accomplished; the entire universe was filled with rejoicing.

verses.original:
दासीं दास तुरग रथ नागा। धेनु बसन मनि बस्तु बिभागा।।
अन्न कनकभाजन भरि जाना। दाइज दीन्ह न जाइ बखाना।।
verses.translation: Maidservants, menservants, horses, chariots, elephants, cows, garments, jewels, and various goods — vessels filled with grain and gold — the dowry that was given defies description.

verses.original:
दाइज दियो बहु भाँति पुनि कर जोरि हिमभूधर कह्यो।
का देउँ पूरनकाम संकर चरन पंकज गहि रह्यो।।
सिवँ कृपासागर ससुर कर संतोषु सब भाँतिहिं कियो।
पुनि गहे पद पाथोज मयनाँ प्रेम परिपूरन हियो।।
verses.translation: Much dowry was given in many ways; then Himavan joined his palms and said, 'What can I give to the all-fulfilled Shankara? I simply cling to his lotus feet.' Shiva, the ocean of mercy, satisfied his father-in-law in every way. Then Maina, her heart filled with love, clasped Shiva's lotus feet.

verses.original:
नाथ उमा मन प्रान सम गृहकिंकरी करेहु।
छमेहु सकल अपराध अब होइ प्रसन्न बरु देहु।।101।।
verses.translation: 'O Lord, Uma is dear as my life and breath. Please treat her as your humble maidservant. Forgive all her faults, and please grant this boon with a glad heart.'

verses.original:
बहु बिधि संभु सास समुझाई। गवनी भवन चरन सिरु नाई।।
जननीं उमा बोलि तब लीन्ही। लै उछंग सुंदर सिख दीन्ही।।
verses.translation: Shambhu consoled his mother-in-law in many ways. She bowed her head at his feet and returned home. Then the mother called Uma to her and, taking her in her lap, gave her beautiful counsel.

verses.original:
करेहु सदा संकर पद पूजा। नारिधरमु पति देउ न दूजा।।
बचन कहत भरे लोचन बारी। बहुरि लाइ उर लीन्हि कुमारी।।
verses.translation: 'Always worship Shankara's feet; a wife's duty is to her husband alone, none other.' As she spoke, her eyes filled with tears, and she again embraced her daughter.

verses.original:
कत बिधि सृजीं नारि जग माहीं। पराधीन सपनेहुँ सुखु नाहीं।।
भै अति प्रेम बिकल महतारी। धीरजु कीन्ह कुसमय बिचारी।।
verses.translation: 'Why did the Creator make women in this world? Those who are dependent never find happiness, even in dreams.' The mother became extremely distraught with love, but she maintained her composure considering the occasion.

verses.original:
पुनि पुनि मिलति परति गहि चरना। परम प्रेम कछु जाइ न बरना।।
सब नारिन्ह मिलि भेटि भवानी। जाइ जननि उर पुनि लपटानी।।
verses.translation: Again and again Uma embraced her mother and fell at her feet — the supreme love was beyond description. Bhavani met all the women and then clung once more to her mother's bosom.

verses.original:
जननिहि बहुरि मिलि चली उचित असीस सब काहूँ दईं।
फिरि फिरि बिलोकति मातु तन तब सखीं लै सिव पहिं गई।।
जाचक सकल संतोषि संकरु उमा सहित भवन चले।
सब अमर हरषे सुमन बरषि निसान नभ बाजे भले।।
verses.translation: After meeting her mother once more, she departed. Everyone gave her fitting blessings. She turned again and again to look at her mother, and then her companions took her to Shiva. Having satisfied all the supplicants, Shankara set out for home with Uma. All the gods rejoiced, showered flowers, and divine drums resounded beautifully in the sky.

verses.original:
चले संग हिमवंतु तब पहुँचावन अति हेतु।
बिबिध भाँति परितोषु करि बिदा कीन्ह बृषकेतु।।102।।
verses.translation: Then Himavan went along to see them off, with great affection. The bull-bannered Lord (Shiva) comforted him in various ways and bade him farewell.

verses.original:
तुरत भवन आए गिरिराई। सकल सैल सर लिए बोलाई।।
आदर दान बिनय बहुमाना। सब कर बिदा कीन्ह हिमवाना।।
verses.translation: The mountain king quickly returned home and summoned all the mountains and lakes. With respect, gifts, and humble courtesy, Himavan bade farewell to them all.

verses.original:
जबहिं संभु कैलासहिं आए। सुर सब निज निज लोक सिधाए।।
जगत मातु पितु संभु भवानी। तेही सिंगारु न कहउँ बखानी।।
verses.translation: When Shambhu arrived at Kailasa, all the gods departed to their respective abodes. Shambhu and Bhavani are the father and mother of the universe — I shall not describe their conjugal bliss.

verses.original:
करहिं बिबिध बिधि भोग बिलासा। गनन्ह समेत बसहिं कैलासा।।
हर गिरिजा बिहार नित नयऊ। एहि बिधि बिपुल काल चलि गयऊ।।
verses.translation: They enjoyed pleasures of various kinds and dwelt at Kailasa with the attendants. Hara and Girija found ever-new delight each day; in this manner, a vast span of time passed.

verses.original:
तब जनमेउ षटबदन कुमारा। तारकु असुर समर जेहिं मारा।।
आगम निगम प्रसिद्ध पुराना। षन्मुख जन्मु सकल जग जाना।।
verses.translation: Then their six-faced son Kumara (Kartikeya) was born, who slew the demon Taraka in battle. His birth is renowned in the Agamas, Nigamas, and Puranas — the whole world knows of Shanmukha's birth.

verses.original:
जगु जान षन्मुख जन्मु कर्मु प्रतापु पुरुषारथु महा।
तेहि हेतु मैं बृषकेतु सुत कर चरित संछेपहिं कहा।।
यह उमा संगु बिबाहु जे नर नारि कहहिं जे गावहीं।
कल्यान काज बिबाह मंगल सर्बदा सुखु पावहीं।।
verses.translation: The whole world knows of the birth, deeds, valor, and great heroism of Shanmukha. For that reason, I have briefly told the story of the son of the bull-bannered one. Those men and women who narrate or sing of this union and marriage of Uma shall always enjoy happiness, auspiciousness, and the blessings of a joyful wedding.

verses.original:
चरित सिंधु गिरिजा रमन बेद न पावहिं पारु।
बरनै तुलसीदासु किमि अति मतिमंद गवाँरु।।103।।
verses.translation: The exploits of Girija's lord are an ocean; even the Vedas cannot find its further shore. How then can the dull and ignorant Tulsidas describe them?

verses.original:
संभु चरित सुनि सरस सुहावा। भरद्वाज मुनि अति सुख पावा।।
बहु लालसा कथा पर बाढ़ी। नयनन्हि नीरु रोमावलि ठाढ़ी।।
verses.translation: Having heard the beautiful and delightful story of Shambhu, the sage Bharadvaja was filled with immense joy. His eager longing for the story grew manifold; tears filled his eyes and his hair stood on end.

verses.original:
प्रेम बिबस मुख आव न बानी। दसा देखि हरषे मुनि ग्यानी।।
अहो धन्य तव जन्मु मुनीसा। तुम्हहि प्रान सम प्रिय गौरीसा।।
verses.translation: Overcome with love, no words came from his mouth. Seeing his condition, the wise sage (Yajnavalkya) was pleased. 'Blessed, blessed is your birth, O chief of sages! The Lord of Gauri is dear to you as life itself.'

verses.original:
सिव पद कमल जिन्हहि रति नाहीं। रामहि ते सपनेहुँ न सोहाहीं।।
बिनु छल बिस्वनाथ पद नेहू। राम भगत कर लच्छन एहू।।
verses.translation: 'Those who have no love for Shiva's lotus feet can never be dear to Rama, even in dreams. Sincere devotion to the feet of Vishvanatha (Shiva) — that is the very mark of a devotee of Rama.'

verses.original:
सिव सम को रघुपति ब्रतधारी। बिनु अघ तजी सती असि नारी।।
पनु करि रघुपति भगति देखाई। को सिव सम रामहि प्रिय भाई।।
verses.translation: 'Who is as devoted to Raghupati as Shiva? Without any fault on her part, he renounced Sati, such a noble woman. Through his vow, he demonstrated his devotion to Raghupati. Who indeed is as dear to Rama as Shiva?'

verses.original:
प्रथमहिं मै कहि सिव चरित बूझा मरमु तुम्हार।
सुचि सेवक तुम्ह राम के रहित समस्त बिकार।।104।।
verses.translation: 'First I told you of Shiva's character to test your inner nature. You are a pure servant of Rama, free from all impurities.'

verses.original:
मैं जाना तुम्हार गुन सीला। कहउँ सुनहु अब रघुपति लीला।।
सुनु मुनि आजु समागम तोरें। कहि न जाइ जस सुखु मन मोरें।।
verses.translation: 'I have come to know your virtues and character. Now I shall tell you — listen to the deeds of Raghupati. O sage, from our meeting today, the joy in my heart is beyond expression.'

verses.original:
राम चरित अति अमित मुनिसा। कहि न सकहिं सत कोटि अहीसा।।
तदपि जथाश्रुत कहउँ बखानी। सुमिरि गिरापति प्रभु धनुपानी।।
verses.translation: 'The deeds of Rama are immeasurable and infinite, O sage; even a hundred million Sheshas could not narrate them. Yet I shall describe them as I have heard them, remembering the Lord of speech and the Lord who holds the bow.'

verses.original:
सारद दारुनारि सम स्वामी। रामु सूत्रधर अंतरजामी।।
जेहि पर कृपा करहिं जनु जानी। कबि उर अजिर नचावहिं बानी।।
verses.translation: 'Sarada (Saraswati) is like a puppet, and Rama is the puppeteer, the inner controller. Upon whomever he bestows his grace, recognizing them as his own, he makes speech dance in the courtyard of the poet's heart.'

verses.original:
प्रनवउँ सोइ कृपाल रघुनाथा। बरनउँ बिसद तासु गुन गाथा।।
परम रम्य गिरिबरु कैलासू। सदा जहाँ सिव उमा निवासू।।
verses.translation: 'I bow to that compassionate Raghunatha and shall describe his glorious tale. The supremely beautiful mountain Kailasa, where Shiva and Uma always dwell —'

verses.original:
सिद्ध तपोधन जोगिजन सूर किंनर मुनिबृंद।
बसहिं तहाँ सुकृती सकल सेवहिं सिब सुखकंद।।105।।
verses.translation: Siddhas, ascetics, yogis, gods, kinnaras, and hosts of sages — all the virtuous ones dwell there and serve Shiva, the root of all bliss.

verses.original:
हरि हर बिमुख धर्म रति नाहीं। ते नर तहँ सपनेहुँ नहिं जाहीं।।
तेहि गिरि पर बट बिटप बिसाला। नित नूतन सुंदर सब काला।।
verses.translation: Those who are averse to Hari and Hara and have no love for righteousness — such persons never go there, even in dreams. On that mountain stands a great banyan tree, ever new and beautiful in all seasons.

verses.original:
त्रिबिध समीर सुसीतलि छाया। सिव बिश्राम बिटप श्रुति गाया।।
एक बार तेहि तर प्रभु गयऊ। तरु बिलोकि उर अति सुखु भयऊ।।
verses.translation: It has a threefold cooling breeze and shade; the Vedas sing of it as Shiva's resting tree. Once the Lord went beneath that tree and was greatly delighted to behold it.

verses.original:
निज कर डासि नागरिपु छाला। बैठै सहजहिं संभु कृपाला।।
कुंद इंदु दर गौर सरीरा। भुज प्रलंब परिधन मुनिचीरा।।
verses.translation: Spreading a serpent skin with his own hand, the compassionate Shambhu sat down at ease. His body was fair as jasmine, the moon, or a conch shell; his arms were long, and his garment was an ascetic's robe.

verses.original:
तरुन अरुन अंबुज सम चरना। नख दुति भगत हृदय तम हरना।।
भुजग भूति भूषन त्रिपुरारी। आननु सरद चंद छबि हारी।।
verses.translation: His feet were like fresh red lotuses, the radiance of his toenails dispelling darkness from his devotees' hearts. Adorned with serpents and ashes, the destroyer of Tripura had a face that put the autumn moon to shame.

verses.original:
जटा मुकुट सुरसरित सिर लोचन नलिन बिसाल।
नीलकंठ लावन्यनिधि सोह बालबिधु भाल।।106।।
verses.translation: With matted hair as his crown, the Ganga on his head, his lotus-like eyes large and beautiful — the blue-throated treasure of grace shone with the crescent moon on his forehead.

verses.original:
बैठे सोह कामरिपु कैसें। धरें सरीरु सांतरसु जैसें।।
पारबती भल अवसरु जानी। गई संभु पहिं मातु भवानी।।
verses.translation: How splendid did the enemy of Kamadeva look as he sat there — as if the sentiment of peace had assumed a body. Parvati, knowing it to be a good opportunity, went to Shambhu — she, the blessed Bhavani.

verses.original:
जानि प्रिया आदरु अति कीन्हा। बाम भाग आसनु हर दीन्हा।।
बैठीं सिव समीप हरषाई। पूरुब जन्म कथा चित आई।।
verses.translation: Recognizing his beloved, Shiva showed her great respect and gave her a seat to his left. She sat near Shiva with joy, and memories of her previous birth came to her mind.

verses.original:
पति हियँ हेतु अधिक अनुमानी। बिहसि उमा बोलीं प्रिय बानी।।
कथा जो सकल लोक हितकारी। सोइ पूछन चह सैलकुमारी।।
verses.translation: Perceiving great affection in her lord's heart, Uma smiled and spoke in sweet words. The question that the daughter of the mountain king wished to ask was one that would benefit the entire world.

verses.original:
बिस्वनाथ मम नाथ पुरारी। त्रिभुवन महिमा बिदित तुम्हारी।।
चर अरु अचर नाग नर देवा। सकल करहिं पद पंकज सेवा।।
verses.translation: 'O Vishvanatha, my Lord, Destroyer of Tripura — your glory throughout the three worlds is well-known. All beings, animate and inanimate — serpents, men, and gods — all serve your lotus feet.'

verses.original:
प्रभु समरथ सर्बग्य सिव सकल कला गुन धाम।।
जोग ग्यान बैराग्य निधि प्रनत कलपतरु नाम।।107।।
verses.translation: 'O Lord, you are almighty, omniscient, the abode of all arts and virtues — a storehouse of yoga, wisdom, and renunciation; your very name is a wish-fulfilling tree for the suppliant.'

verses.original:
जौं मो पर प्रसन्न सुखरासी। जानिअ सत्य मोहि निज दासी।।
तौं प्रभु हरहु मोर अग्याना। कहि रघुनाथ कथा बिधि नाना।।
verses.translation: 'If you are pleased with me, O treasure of bliss, and truly consider me your handmaid, then, O Lord, dispel my ignorance by narrating the story of Raghunatha in various ways.'

verses.original:
जासु भवनु सुरतरु तर होई। सहि कि दरिद्र जनित दुखु सोई।।
ससिभूषन अस हृदयँ बिचारी। हरहु नाथ मम मति भ्रम भारी।।
verses.translation: 'Can one whose dwelling is beneath the wish-fulfilling tree ever suffer the misery of poverty? O moon-crested Lord, thinking thus in my heart, please remove my heavy delusion, O Lord.'

verses.original:
प्रभु जे मुनि परमारथबादी। कहहिं राम कहुँ ब्रह्म अनादी।।
सेस सारदा बेद पुराना। सकल करहिं रघुपति गुन गाना।।
verses.translation: 'O Lord, the sages who speak of the highest truth call Rama the eternal, beginningless Brahman. Shesha, Sarada, the Vedas, and the Puranas — all sing the glories of Raghupati.'

verses.original:
तुम्ह पुनि राम राम दिन राती। सादर जपहु अनँग आराती।।
रामु सो अवध नृपति सुत सोई। की अज अगुन अलखगति कोई।।
verses.translation: 'You too, O enemy of Kamadeva, reverently chant Rama's name day and night. Is Rama the same as the son of the king of Ayodhya, or is he someone else — the unborn, attributeless, and unknowable one?'

verses.original:
जौं नृप तनय त ब्रह्म किमि नारि बिरहँ मति भोरि।
देख चरित महिमा सुनत भ्रमति बुद्धि अति मोरि।।108।।
verses.translation: 'If he is a prince, then how can he be Brahman? And how could his mind be bewildered by separation from a woman? Seeing his deeds and hearing of his greatness, my understanding is utterly confused.'

verses.original:
जौं अनीह ब्यापक बिभु कोऊ। कबहु बुझाइ नाथ मोहि सोऊ।।
अग्य जानि रिस उर जनि धरहू। जेहि बिधि मोह मिटै सोइ करहू।।
verses.translation: 'If he is the desireless, all-pervading, supreme Lord, please explain that to me sometime, O Master. Do not hold anger in your heart knowing me to be ignorant; please do whatever will remove my delusion.'

verses.original:
मै बन दीखि राम प्रभुताई। अति भय बिकल न तुम्हहि सुनाई।।
तदपि मलिन मन बोधु न आवा। सो फलु भली भाँति हम पावा।।
verses.translation: 'I witnessed Rama's majesty in the forest and was greatly frightened, but I did not tell you about it. Yet my tainted mind did not gain understanding — and I fully reaped the consequences of that.'

verses.original:
अजहूँ कछु संसउ मन मोरे। करहु कृपा बिनवउँ कर जोरें।।
प्रभु तब मोहि बहु भाँति प्रबोधा। नाथ सो समुझि करहु जनि क्रोधा।।
verses.translation: 'Even now some doubt lingers in my mind; I beseech you with folded hands, please show me your grace. O Lord, you then enlightened me in many ways; please remember that and do not be angry.'

verses.original:
तब कर अस बिमोह अब नाहीं। रामकथा पर रुचि मन माहीं।।
कहहु पुनीत राम गुन गाथा। भुजगराज भूषन सुरनाथा।।
verses.translation: 'The delusion I had then is no more; now I have a keen interest in the story of Rama. Please narrate the holy story of Rama's glories, O Lord adorned with serpents, O Lord of the gods.'

verses.original:
बंदउ पद धरि धरनि सिरु बिनय करउँ कर जोरि।
बरनहु रघुबर बिसद जसु श्रुति सिद्धांत निचोरि।।109।।
verses.translation: 'I bow down, placing my head on the ground, and make this entreaty with folded hands: please describe the pure fame of Raghuvara, distilling the essence of the Vedas.'

verses.original:
जदपि जोषिता नहिं अधिकारी। दासी मन क्रम बचन तुम्हारी।।
गूढ़उ तत्व न साधु दुरावहिं। आरत अधिकारी जहँ पावहिं।।
verses.translation: 'Though a woman has no right to such knowledge, I am your servant in thought, word, and deed. Saints do not hide even the most profound truths when they find a worthy and earnest seeker.'

verses.original:
अति आरति पूछउँ सुरराया। रघुपति कथा कहहु करि दाया।।
प्रथम सो कारन कहहु बिचारी। निर्गुन ब्रह्म सगुन बपु धारी।।
verses.translation: 'With deep yearning I ask you, O King of the Gods — please narrate the story of Raghupati with compassion. First, explain this after due reflection: why did the formless Brahman assume a bodily form?'

verses.original:
पुनि प्रभु कहहु राम अवतारा। बालचरित पुनि कहहु उदारा।।
कहहु जथा जानकी बिबाहीं। राज तजा सो दूषन काहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Then, O Lord, tell of Rama's incarnation, and then of his generous childhood exploits. Tell how Janaki was wedded, and for what fault the kingdom was renounced.'

verses.original:
बन बसि कीन्हे चरित अपारा। कहहु नाथ जिमि रावन मारा।।
राज बैठि कीन्हीं बहु लीला। सकल कहहु संकर सुखलीला।।
verses.translation: 'Tell of the countless deeds he performed while dwelling in the forest, and how he slew Ravana, O Lord. Tell of the many divine sports he performed while seated on the throne — narrate all, O Shankara, the blissful deeds.'

verses.original:
बहुरि कहहु करुनायतन कीन्ह जो अचरज राम।
प्रजा सहित रघुबंसमनि किमि गवने निज धाम।।110।।
verses.translation: 'And then tell, O abode of compassion, what wonder Rama performed — how the jewel of the Raghu dynasty, along with his subjects, departed for his own divine abode.'

verses.original:
पुनि प्रभु कहहु सो तत्व बखानी। जेहिं बिग्यान मगन मुनि ग्यानी।।
भगति ग्यान बिग्यान बिरागा। पुनि सब बरनहु सहित बिभागा।।
verses.translation: 'Then, O Lord, explain that truth by which the wise sages remain absorbed in supreme knowledge. Devotion, knowledge, realization, and renunciation — describe all these along with their divisions.'

verses.original:
औरउ राम रहस्य अनेका। कहहु नाथ अति बिमल बिबेका।।
जो प्रभु मैं पूछा नहि होई। सोउ दयाल राखहु जनि गोई।।
verses.translation: 'And the many other mysteries of Rama — please tell them, O Lord, with your supremely pure discernment. And whatever I have not asked, O Lord, do not withhold that either, O compassionate one.'

verses.original:
तुम्ह त्रिभुवन गुर बेद बखाना। आन जीव पाँवर का जाना।।
प्रस्न उमा कै सहज सुहाई। छल बिहीन सुनि सिव मन भाई।।
verses.translation: 'You are the guru of the three worlds, as the Vedas declare; what can other poor creatures know?' Uma's question was naturally beautiful, free from guile, and it pleased Shiva's heart.

verses.original:
हर हियँ रामचरित सब आए। प्रेम पुलक लोचन जल छाए।।
श्रीरघुनाथ रूप उर आवा। परमानंद अमित सुख पावा।।
verses.translation: All the stories of Rama's deeds arose in Hara's heart; his body thrilled with love and tears filled his eyes. The form of Shri Raghunatha appeared in his heart, and he experienced supreme, infinite bliss.

verses.original:
मगन ध्यानरस दंड जुग पुनि मन बाहेर कीन्ह।
रघुपति चरित महेस तब हरषित बरनै लीन्ह।।111।।
verses.translation: Absorbed in the nectar of meditation for two watches, he then turned his mind outward. Maheshwara then joyfully began to narrate the deeds of Raghupati.

verses.original:
झूठेउ सत्य जाहि बिनु जानें। जिमि भुजंग बिनु रजु पहिचानें।।
जेहि जानें जग जाइ हेराई। जागें जथा सपन भ्रम जाई।।
verses.translation: Even the false appears true without knowing Him, just as a rope appears to be a snake without recognizing it. By knowing whom the world vanishes, just as the illusion of a dream disappears upon waking —

verses.original:
बंदउँ बालरूप सोई रामू। सब सिधि सुलभ जपत जिसु नामू।।
मंगल भवन अमंगल हारी। द्रवउ सो दसरथ अजिर बिहारी।।
verses.translation: I worship that same Rama in his child form, by chanting whose name all spiritual powers become easily attainable. The abode of all blessings and destroyer of all misfortune — may that Rama who plays in Dasharatha's courtyard have mercy on me.

verses.original:
करि प्रनाम रामहि त्रिपुरारी। हरषि सुधा सम गिरा उचारी।।
धन्य धन्य गिरिराजकुमारी। तुम्ह समान नहिं कोउ उपकारी।।
verses.translation: Bowing to Rama, the destroyer of Tripura spoke with joy, his voice sweet as nectar: 'Blessed, blessed are you, O daughter of the mountain king! There is no benefactor equal to you.'

verses.original:
पूँछेहु रघुपति कथा प्रसंगा। सकल लोक जग पावनि गंगा।।
तुम्ह रघुबीर चरन अनुरागी। कीन्हहु प्रस्न जगत हित लागी।।
verses.translation: 'You have asked about the story of Raghupati — the purifying Ganga for all the worlds. You are devoted to the feet of Raghuvira and have asked this question for the welfare of the world.'

verses.original:
रामकृपा तें पारबति सपनेहुँ तव मन माहिं।
सोक मोह संदेह भ्रम मम बिचार कछु नाहिं।।112।।
verses.translation: 'By Rama's grace, O Parvati, even in dreams there is no sorrow, delusion, doubt, or confusion in your mind — nor any impure thought of mine.'

verses.original:
तदपि असंका कीन्हिहु सोई। कहत सुनत सब कर हित होई।।
जिन्ह हरि कथा सुनी नहिं काना। श्रवन रंध्र अहिभवन समाना।।
verses.translation: 'Yet you have raised this doubt — and that is good, for both the telling and hearing of it will benefit everyone. Those whose ears have never heard the story of Hari — their ear-holes are like snake-holes.'

verses.original:
नयनन्हि संत दरस नहिं देखा। लोचन मोरपंख कर लेखा।।
ते सिर कटु तुंबरि समतूला। जे न नमत हरि गुर पद मूला।।
verses.translation: 'Eyes that have never beheld a saint's form are like the spots on a peacock's feather. Those heads that do not bow at the feet of Hari and the guru are like bitter gourds.'

verses.original:
जिन्ह हरिभगति हृदयँ नहिं आनी। जीवत सव समान तेइ प्रानी।।
जो नहिं करइ राम गुन गाना। जीह सो दादुर जीह समाना।।
verses.translation: 'Those beings who have not brought devotion to Hari into their hearts are as good as dead while living. A tongue that does not sing the glories of Rama is like the tongue of a frog.'

verses.original:
कुलिस कठोर निठुर सोइ छाती। सुनि हरिचरित न जो हरषाती।।
गिरिजा सुनहु राम कै लीला। सुर हित दनुज बिमोहनसीला।।
verses.translation: 'That heart is hard as a thunderbolt and pitiless which does not rejoice upon hearing Hari's deeds. Listen, O Girija, to Rama's divine play — which delights the gods and bewilders the demons.'

verses.original:
रामकथा सुरधेनु सम सेवत सब सुख दानि।
सतसमाज सुरलोक सब को न सुनै अस जानि।।113।।
verses.translation: 'The story of Rama is like the divine wish-fulfilling cow; serving it grants all happiness. The assembly of saints is like heaven itself — who, knowing this, would not listen?'

verses.original:
रामकथा सुंदर कर तारी। संसय बिहग उडावनिहारी।।
रामकथा कलि बिटप कुठारी। सादर सुनु गिरिराजकुमारी।।
verses.translation: 'The story of Rama is like the beautiful snapping of fingers that drives away the birds of doubt. The story of Rama is like an axe to the tree of the Kali age. Listen reverently, O daughter of the mountain king.'

verses.original:
राम नाम गुन चरित सुहाए। जनम करम अगनित श्रुति गाए।।
जथा अनंत राम भगवाना। तथा कथा कीरति गुन नाना।।
verses.translation: 'The names, virtues, and lovely deeds of Rama, his births and actions — the Vedas sing of them without end. Just as Lord Rama is infinite, so too are his stories, fame, and virtues without limit.'

verses.original:
तदपि जथा श्रुत जसि मति मोरी। कहिहउँ देखि प्रीति अति तोरी।।
उमा प्रस्न तव सहज सुहाई। सुखद संतसंमत मोहि भाई।।
verses.translation: 'Yet as I have heard and as my intellect permits, I shall narrate them, seeing your great love. O Uma, your question is naturally beautiful, delightful, and approved by the saints — it has pleased me.'

verses.original:
एक बात नहि मोहि सोहानी। जदपि मोह बस कहेहु भवानी।।
तुम जो कहा राम कोउ आना। जेहि श्रुति गाव धरहिं मुनि ध्याना।।
verses.translation: 'But one thing you said did not please me — even though you spoke it under the influence of delusion, O Bhavani. You suggested that Rama is someone other than the one whom the Vedas praise and upon whom the sages meditate.'

verses.original:
कहहि सुनहि अस अधम नर ग्रसे जे मोह पिसाच।
पाषंडी हरि पद बिमुख जानहिं झूठ न साच।।114।।
verses.translation: 'Those degraded people who say and hear such things are possessed by the demon of delusion. Heretics who are averse to Hari's feet — they cannot distinguish truth from falsehood.'

verses.original:
अग्य अकोबिद अंध अभागी। काई बिषय मुकर मन लागी।।
लंपट कपटी कुटिल बिसेषी। सपनेहुँ संतसभा नहिं देखी।।
verses.translation: 'The ignorant, the unlearned, the blind, and the unfortunate — whose minds cling to the mirror of sense objects. Lustful, deceitful, and especially crooked — they have never even seen the assembly of saints, not even in a dream.'

verses.original:
कहहिं ते बेद असंमत बानी। जिन्ह कें सूझ लाभु नहिं हानी।।
मुकर मलिन अरु नयन बिहीना। राम रूप देखहिं किमि दीना।।
verses.translation: 'They speak words contrary to the Vedas, unable to see gain from loss. Their mirror is dirty and they are also blind — how can such wretched ones behold Rama's form?'

verses.original:
जिन्ह कें अगुन न सगुन बिबेका। जल्पहिं कल्पित बचन अनेका।।
हरिमाया बस जगत भ्रमाहीं। तिन्हहि कहत कछु अघटित नाहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Those who have no understanding of either the formless or the formed aspect of God babble many imagined words. Deluded by Hari's Maya, they wander the world — nothing is impossible for them to say.'

verses.original:
बातुल भूत बिबस मतवारे। ते नहिं बोलहिं बचन बिचारे।।
जिन्ह कृत महामोह मद पाना। तिन् कर कहा करिअ नहिं काना।।
verses.translation: 'The insane, the possessed, the intoxicated — they do not speak considered words. Those who have drunk the wine of great delusion — do not lend your ear to their words.'

verses.original:
अस निज हृदयँ बिचारि तजु संसय भजु राम पद।
सुनु गिरिराज कुमारि भ्रम तम रबि कर बचन मम।।115।।
verses.translation: 'Reflect thus in your heart and abandon doubt; worship Rama's feet. Listen, O daughter of the mountain king — my words are like sunlight to the darkness of delusion.'

verses.original:
सगुनहि अगुनहि नहिं कछु भेदा। गावहिं मुनि पुरान बुध बेदा।।
अगुन अरुप अलख अज जोई। भगत प्रेम बस सगुन सो होई।।
verses.translation: 'Between the formed and the formless, there is no difference at all — so say the wise, the Puranas, and the Vedas. The one who is without attributes, form, invisible, and unborn becomes, through the power of a devotee's love, the same with form and attributes.'

verses.original:
जो गुन रहित सगुन सोइ कैसें। जलु हिम उपल बिलग नहिं जैसें।।
जासु नाम भ्रम तिमिर पतंगा। तेहि किमि कहिअ बिमोह प्रसंगा।।
verses.translation: 'How can the attributeless become the one with attributes? Just as water and ice are not different. He whose very name is like a moth to the darkness of delusion — how can one speak of him in connection with bewilderment?'

verses.original:
राम सच्चिदानंद दिनेसा। नहिं तहँ मोह निसा लवलेसा।।
सहज प्रकासरुप भगवाना। नहिं तहँ पुनि बिग्यान बिहाना।।
verses.translation: 'Rama is like the sun of truth-consciousness-bliss; not a trace of the night of delusion exists there. The Lord is by nature self-luminous; there is no dawn of knowledge there (for he is ever luminous).'

verses.original:
हरष बिषाद ग्यान अग्याना। जीव धर्म अहमिति अभिमाना।।
राम ब्रह्म ब्यापक जग जाना। परमानन्द परेस पुराना।।
verses.translation: 'Joy and sorrow, knowledge and ignorance, the nature of the individual soul and the sense of ego — Rama is the all-pervading Brahman, known to the world as the Supreme Bliss, the ancient supreme Lord.'

verses.original:
पुरुष प्रसिद्ध प्रकास निधि प्रगट परावर नाथ।।
रघुकुलमनि मम स्वामि सोइ कहि सिवँ नायउ माथ।।116।।
verses.translation: 'He is the famous Supreme Person, the treasury of light, the manifest Lord of the high and the low. That jewel of the Raghu dynasty is my Master.' So saying, Shiva bowed his head.

verses.original:
निज भ्रम नहिं समुझहिं अग्यानी। प्रभु पर मोह धरहिं जड़ प्रानी।।
जथा गगन घन पटल निहारी। झाँपेउ मानु कहहिं कुबिचारी।।
verses.translation: 'The ignorant do not understand their own delusion, and foolish beings attribute bewilderment to the Lord. Just as, seeing a veil of clouds in the sky, ill-minded ones declare that the sun has been covered.'

verses.original:
चितव जो लोचन अंगुलि लाएँ। प्रगट जुगल ससि तेहि के भाएँ।।
उमा राम बिषइक अस मोहा। नभ तम धूम धूरि जिमि सोहा।।
verses.translation: 'One who looks with a finger pressed against the eye sees two moons where there is one. O Uma, such is the delusion regarding Rama — it is like darkness, smoke, or dust appearing in the sky.'

verses.original:
बिषय करन सुर जीव समेता। सकल एक तें एक सचेता।।
सब कर परम प्रकासक जोई। राम अनादि अवधपति सोई।।
verses.translation: 'Sense objects, the senses, the gods, and the individual soul — each one is more conscious than the one before. He who is the supreme illuminator of them all — that Rama, the beginningless Lord of Ayodhya, is the same.'

verses.original:
जगत प्रकास्य प्रकासक रामू। मायाधीस ग्यान गुन धामू।।
जासु सत्यता तें जड माया। भास सत्य इव मोह सहाया।।
verses.translation: 'The world is that which is illuminated, and Rama is the illuminator — the Lord of Maya, the abode of knowledge and virtue. It is due to his reality that inert Maya appears to be real, aided by delusion.'

verses.original:
रजत सीप महुँ मास जिमि जथा भानु कर बारि।
जदपि मृषा तिहुँ काल सोइ भ्रम न सकइ कोउ टारि।।117।।
verses.translation: 'Just as silver appears in a shell, or a mirage appears in sunlight — though false in all three times, no one can remove that illusion (without true knowledge).'

verses.original:
एहि बिधि जग हरि आश्रित रहई। जदपि असत्य देत दुख अहई।।
जौं सपनें सिर काटै कोई। बिनु जागें न दूरि दुख होई।।
verses.translation: 'In this way, the world exists dependent on Hari; though unreal, it causes suffering. If someone cuts off your head in a dream, the pain does not go away without waking up.'

verses.original:
जासु कृपाँ अस भ्रम मिटि जाई। गिरिजा सोइ कृपाल रघुराई।।
आदि अंत कोउ जासु न पावा। मति अनुमानि निगम अस गावा।।
verses.translation: 'The one by whose grace such delusion is destroyed — O Girija, that compassionate one is Raghurai. No one has found his beginning or end; the Vedas, reasoning by inference, have sung thus.'

verses.original:
बिनु पद चलइ सुनइ बिनु काना। कर बिनु करम करइ बिधि नाना।।
आनन रहित सकल रस भोगी। बिनु बानी बकता बड़ जोगी।।
verses.translation: 'He walks without feet, hears without ears, performs all manner of actions without hands. Without a face, he enjoys all tastes; without speech, he is the greatest orator and yogi.'

verses.original:
तनु बिनु परस नयन बिनु देखा। ग्रहइ घ्रान बिनु बास असेषा।।
असि सब भाँति अलौकिक करनी। महिमा जासु जाइ नहिं बरनी।।
verses.translation: 'Without a body, he touches; without eyes, he sees; without a nose, he perceives all fragrances. Such are his supernatural doings in every way — his glory cannot be described.'

verses.original:
जेहि इमि गावहि बेद बुध जाहि धरहिं मुनि ध्यान।।
सोइ दसरथ सुत भगत हित कोसलपति भगवान।।118।।
verses.translation: 'He whom the Vedas and the wise thus sing, upon whom the sages meditate — that same one is the son of Dasharatha, devoted to his devotees, the Lord of Koshala, the Supreme God.'

verses.original:
कासीं मरत जंतु अवलोकी। जासु नाम बल करउँ बिसोकी।।
सोइ प्रभु मोर चराचर स्वामी। रघुबर सब उर अंतरजामी।।
verses.translation: 'Seeing beings dying in Kashi, by the power of whose name I free them from sorrow — that Lord is my master of all animate and inanimate beings, Raghuvara, the indweller of all hearts.'

verses.original:
बिबसहुँ जासु नाम नर कहहीं। जनम अनेक रचित अघ दहहीं।।
सादर सुमिरन जे नर करहीं। भव बारिधि गोपद इव तरहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Even those who utter his name unwittingly burn away sins accumulated over countless births. Those who remember him reverently cross the ocean of worldly existence as easily as a cow's hoofprint.'

verses.original:
राम सो परमातमा भवानी। तहँ भ्रम अति अबिहित तव बानी।।
अस संसय आनत उर माहीं। ग्यान बिराग सकल गुन जाहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Rama is the Supreme Soul, O Bhavani; to raise doubt about this is most improper — your words were wrong. Bringing such doubt into one's heart destroys all knowledge, dispassion, and virtues.'

verses.original:
सुनि सिव के भ्रम भंजन बचना। मिटि गै सब कुतरक कै रचना।।
भइ रघुपति पद प्रीति प्रतीती। दारुन असंभावना बीती।।
verses.translation: Hearing Shiva's words that shattered delusion, all the constructions of false reasoning were demolished. Faith and love for Raghupati's feet arose, and the terrible impossibility was overcome.

verses.original:
पुनि पुनि प्रभु पद कमल गहि जोरि पंकरुह पानि।
बोली गिरिजा बचन बर मनहुँ प्रेम रस सानि।।119।।
verses.translation: Again and again clasping the Lord's lotus feet, with lotus-like palms joined, Girija spoke beautiful words that were steeped in the nectar of love.

verses.original:
ससि कर सम सुनि गिरा तुम्हारी। मिटा मोह सरदातप भारी।।
तुम्ह कृपाल सबु संसउ हरेऊ। राम स्वरुप जानि मोहि परेऊ।।
verses.translation: 'Hearing your words, cool as moonbeams, my heavy delusion — like the heat of autumn — has been dispelled. O compassionate Lord, you have removed all my doubt; I now understand Rama's true nature.'

verses.original:
नाथ कृपाँ अब गयउ बिषादा। सुखी भयउँ प्रभु चरन प्रसादा।।
अब मोहि आपनि किंकरि जानी। जदपि सहज जड नारि अयानी।।
verses.translation: 'By your grace, O Lord, my sorrow is gone; I am now happy through the blessing of your feet. Now, knowing me as your humble maidservant — though I am by nature foolish and ignorant, being a woman —'

verses.original:
प्रथम जो मैं पूछा सोइ कहहू। जौं मो पर प्रसन्न प्रभु अहहू।।
राम ब्रह्म चिनमय अबिनासी। सर्ब रहित सब उर पुर बासी।।
verses.translation: 'Please answer what I first asked, if you are pleased with me, O Lord. Rama is Brahman — pure consciousness, imperishable, free from all, yet dwelling in the hearts of all.'

verses.original:
नाथ धरेउ नरतनु केहि हेतू। मोहि समुझाइ कहहु बृषकेतू।।
उमा बचन सुनि परम बिनीता। रामकथा पर प्रीति पुनीता।।
verses.translation: 'O Lord, for what reason did he assume a human body? Please explain this to me, O bull-bannered one.' Hearing Uma's words, so deeply humble, and seeing her pure love for the story of Rama —

verses.original:
हिंयँ हरषे कामारि तब संकर सहज सुजान
बहु बिधि उमहि प्रसंसि पुनि बोले कृपानिधान।।120(क)।।
सुनु सुभ कथा भवानि रामचरितमानस बिमल।
कहा भुसुंडि बखानि सुना बिहग नायक गरुड।।120(ख)।।
सो संबाद उदार जेहि बिधि भा आगें कहब।
सुनहु राम अवतार चरित परम सुंदर अनघ।।120(ग)।।
हरि गुन नाम अपार कथा रूप अगनित अमित।
मैं निज मति अनुसार कहउँ उमा सादर सुनहु।।120(घ।।
verses.translation: The enemy of Kamadeva rejoiced in his heart. The naturally wise Shankara praised Uma in many ways and then the treasury of compassion spoke: 'Listen to the pure and beautiful story, O Bhavani — the spotless Ramcharitmanas. Kakabhushundi narrated it, and the king of birds, Garuda, listened to it. That noble dialogue — how it came about, I shall tell you next. Now hear of Rama's incarnation — his supremely beautiful and sinless character. The names and virtues of Hari are infinite, his stories countless and immeasurable. I shall tell them according to my own understanding; listen attentively, O Uma.'

verses.original:
सुनु गिरिजा हरिचरित सुहाए। बिपुल बिसद निगमागम गाए।।
हरि अवतार हेतु जेहि होई। इदमित्थं कहि जाइ न सोई।।
verses.translation: 'Listen, O Girija, to the delightful deeds of Hari — the Vedas and scriptures have sung of them in abundance and clarity. The reason for Hari's incarnation cannot be stated as simply this or that.'

verses.original:
राम अर्तक्य बुद्धि मन बानी। मत हमार अस सुनहि सयानी।।
तदपि संत मुनि बेद पुराना। जस कछु कहहिं स्वमति अनुमाना।।
verses.translation: 'Rama is beyond the grasp of reason, mind, and speech — this is my considered view, O wise one. Yet the saints, sages, Vedas, and Puranas speak of it as they infer from their own understanding.'

verses.original:
तस मैं सुमुखि सुनावउँ तोही। समुझि परइ जस कारन मोही।।
जब जब होइ धरम कै हानी। बाढहिं असुर अधम अभिमानी।।
verses.translation: 'So too, O fair-faced one, I shall tell you what reason appears to me. Whenever there is a decline in righteousness, and wicked, arrogant demons proliferate —'

verses.original:
करहिं अनीति जाइ नहिं बरनी। सीदहिं बिप्र धेनु सुर धरनी।।
तब तब प्रभु धरि बिबिध सरीरा। हरहि कृपानिधि सज्जन पीरा।।
verses.translation: 'When they commit unspeakable injustices and the Brahmins, cows, gods, and the earth suffer — then the compassionate Lord assumes various forms and relieves the suffering of the virtuous.'

verses.original:
असुर मारि थापहिं सुरन्ह राखहिं निज श्रुति सेतु।
जग बिस्तारहिं बिसद जस राम जन्म कर हेतु।।121।।
verses.translation: 'Slaying the demons, he establishes the gods, preserves the boundaries set by the Vedas, and spreads his pure glory throughout the world — this is the purpose of Rama's birth.'

verses.original:
सोइ जस गाइ भगत भव तरहीं। कृपासिंधु जन हित तनु धरहीं।।
राम जनम के हेतु अनेका। परम बिचित्र एक तें एका।।
verses.translation: 'Singing that glory, devotees cross the ocean of worldly existence; the ocean of mercy takes a body for the sake of his devotees. The reasons for Rama's birth are many — each one more wonderfully varied than the last.'

verses.original:
जनम एक दुइ कहउँ बखानी। सावधान सुनु सुमति भवानी।।
द्वारपाल हरि के प्रिय दोऊ। जय अरु बिजय जान सब कोऊ।।
verses.translation: 'I shall describe one or two of them in detail; listen attentively, O wise Bhavani. The two beloved gatekeepers of Hari — Jaya and Vijaya, known to all —'

verses.original:
बिप्र श्राप तें दूनउ भाई। तामस असुर देह तिन्ह पाई।।
कनककसिपु अरु हाटक लोचन। जगत बिदित सुरपति मद मोचन।।
verses.translation: 'Due to a Brahmin's curse, those two brothers received demonic bodies of the tamasic nature. One became Hiranyakashipu and the other Hiranyaksha — famous throughout the world as humiliators of Indra.'

verses.original:
बिजई समर बीर बिख्याता। धरि बराह बपु एक निपाता।।
होइ नरहरि दूसर पुनि मारा। जन प्रहलाद सुजस बिस्तारा।।
verses.translation: 'Victorious in battle, these mighty warriors were renowned. Taking the form of a boar, the Lord slew one. Assuming the form of Narasimha, he killed the other and spread the fame of his devotee Prahlada.'

verses.original:
भए निसाचर जाइ तेइ महाबीर बलवान।
कुंभकरन रावण सुभट सुर बिजई जग जान।।122 ।
verses.translation: 'Those same two were reborn as demons — mighty and powerful warriors. Kumbhakarna and Ravana, great champions, conquerors of the gods — the world knows them well.'

verses.original:
मुकुत न भए हते भगवाना। तीनि जनम द्विज बचन प्रवाना।।
एक बार तिन्ह के हित लागी। धरेउ सरीर भगत अनुरागी।।
verses.translation: 'They were not liberated even though slain by the Lord, for the Brahmin's curse was for three births. Once again, for their sake, the Lord who is devoted to his devotees assumed a body.'

verses.original:
कस्यप अदिति तहाँ पितु माता। दसरथ कौसल्या बिख्याता।।
एक कलप एहि बिधि अवतारा। चरित्र पवित्र किए संसारा।।
verses.translation: 'There, Kashyapa and Aditi were his father and mother, known as Dasharatha and Kausalya. In one cosmic cycle, the incarnation took place in this manner, and the Lord sanctified the world with his holy deeds.'

verses.original:
एक कलप सुर देखि दुखारे। समर जलंधर सन सब हारे।।
संभु कीन्ह संग्राम अपारा। दनुज महाबल मरइ न मारा।।
verses.translation: 'In another cosmic cycle, seeing the gods in distress, all defeated in battle by the demon Jalandhara — Shambhu waged a great war, but the mighty demon could not be killed.'

verses.original: परम सती असुराधिप नारी। तेहि बल ताहि न जितहिं पुरारी।।
verses.translation: 'The wife of the demon lord was supremely virtuous; by her power, even the destroyer of Tripura could not defeat him.'

verses.original:
छल करि टारेउ तासु ब्रत प्रभु सुर कारज कीन्ह।।
जब तेहि जानेउ मरम तब श्राप कोप करि दीन्ह।।123।।
verses.translation: 'The Lord destroyed her vow through deception and thus accomplished the gods' purpose. When the demoness discovered the truth, she cursed the Lord in anger.'

verses.original:
तासु श्राप हरि दीन्ह प्रमाना। कौतुकनिधि कृपाल भगवाना।।
तहाँ जलंधर रावन भयऊ। रन हति राम परम पद दयऊ।।
verses.translation: 'The Lord accepted her curse, for the playful and compassionate God respects such things. In that cycle, Jalandhara became Ravana, and Rama slew him in battle and granted him the supreme state.'

verses.original:
एक जनम कर कारन एहा। जेहि लागि राम धरी नरदेहा।।
प्रति अवतार कथा प्रभु केरी। सुनु मुनि बरनी कबिन्ह घनेरी।।
verses.translation: 'This is the reason for one incarnation — for which Rama assumed a human body. In each incarnation, the Lord's story has been extensively narrated by many poets, O sage.'

verses.original:
नारद श्राप दीन्ह एक बारा। कलप एक तेहि लगि अवतारा।।
गिरिजा चकित भई सुनि बानी। नारद बिष्नुभगत पुनि ग्यानि।।
verses.translation: 'Once, Narada's curse was the reason for the incarnation in a particular cosmic cycle.' Girija was astonished to hear this. 'Narada — a devotee of Vishnu and a man of wisdom!'

verses.original:
कारन कवन श्राप मुनि दीन्हा। का अपराध रमापति कीन्हा।।
यह प्रसंग मोहि कहहु पुरारी। मुनि मन मोह आचरज भारी।।
verses.translation: 'For what reason did the sage give a curse, and what offense did the Lord of Lakshmi commit? Tell me this episode, O destroyer of Tripura — it is a great wonder that a sage's mind should be deluded.'

verses.original:
बोले बिहसि महेस तब ग्यानी मूढ़ न कोइ।
जेहि जस रघुपति करहिं जब सो तस तेहि छन होइ।।124(क)।।
कहउँ राम गुन गाथ भरद्वाज सादर सुनहु।
भव भंजन रघुनाथ भजु तुलसी तजि मान मद।।124(ख)।।
verses.translation: Maheshwara laughed and said, 'No one is truly wise or foolish. Whomever Raghupati makes whatever at any given moment — that is what they become at that instant.' 'I shall now tell the tale of Rama's glories; listen attentively, O Bharadvaja. Worship Raghunatha, the destroyer of worldly existence, and abandon pride and arrogance, says Tulsi.'

verses.original:
हिमगिरि गुहा एक अति पावनि। बह समीप सुरसरी सुहावनि।।
आश्रम परम पुनीत सुहावा। देखि देवरिषि मन अति भावा।।
verses.translation: In the Himalayas there was a most sacred cave, near which flowed the beautiful river Ganga. The hermitage was supremely holy and charming; seeing it, the divine sage (Narada) was greatly pleased.

verses.original:
निरखि सैल सरि बिपिन बिभागा। भयउ रमापति पद अनुरागा।।
सुमिरत हरिहि श्राप गति बाधी। सहज बिमल मन लागि समाधी।।
verses.translation: Gazing at the mountain, river, and forest, love for the feet of Lakshmi's Lord arose in him. As he remembered Hari, the power of the curse was obstructed, and naturally pure meditation settled in his mind.

verses.original:
मुनि गति देखि सुरेस डेराना। कामहि बोलि कीन्ह सनमाना।।
सहित सहाय जाहु मम हेतू। चकेउ हरषि हियँ जलचरकेतू।।
verses.translation: Seeing the sage's state, Indra became alarmed. He summoned Kamadeva and honored him, saying, 'Go with your helpers for my sake.' The fish-bannered one was startled yet glad at heart.

verses.original:
सुनासीर मन महुँ असि त्रासा। चहत देवरिषि मम पुर बासा।।
जे कामी लोलुप जग माहीं। कुटिल काक इव सबहि डेराहीं।।
verses.translation: Indra had this fear in his mind: 'The divine sage wants to take my heavenly throne.' Those who are lustful and greedy in this world — like cunning crows, they are frightened of everyone.

verses.original:
सुख हाड़ लै भाग सठ स्वान निरखि मृगराज।
छीनि लेइ जनि जान जड़ तिमि सुरपतिहि न लाज।।125।।
verses.translation: 'The fool is like a dog who, seeing a lion, runs away with a dry bone, lest the lion snatch it away.' Similarly, Indra had no shame.

verses.original:
तेहि आश्रमहिं मदन जब गयऊ। निज मायाँ बसंत निरमयऊ।।
कुसुमित बिबिध बिटप बहुरंगा। कूजहिं कोकिल गुंजहि भृंगा।।
verses.translation: When Kamadeva reached that hermitage, he created spring through his own magic. Trees of many colors blossomed, cuckoos sang, and bees hummed.

verses.original:
चली सुहावनि त्रिबिध बयारी। काम कृसानु बढ़ावनिहारी।।
रंभादिक सुरनारि नबीना । सकल असमसर कला प्रबीना।।
verses.translation: A delightful threefold breeze began to blow — one that fans the fire of passion. Rambha and other young celestial nymphs, all skilled in the art of the bodiless god of love, appeared.

verses.original:
करहिं गान बहु तान तरंगा। बहुबिधि क्रीड़हि पानि पतंगा।।
देखि सहाय मदन हरषाना। कीन्हेसि पुनि प्रपंच बिधि नाना।।
verses.translation: They sang songs in many melodious tunes and played various games like butterflies in their hands. Seeing his allies, Kamadeva rejoiced and devised many more deceptive schemes.

verses.original:
काम कला कछु मुनिहि न ब्यापी। निज भयँ डरेउ मनोभव पापी।।
सीम कि चाँपि सकइ कोउ तासु। बड़ रखवार रमापति जासू।।
verses.translation: But none of Kamadeva's arts affected the sage at all. The sinful Kamadeva himself trembled with fear. Can anyone violate the boundaries of one whose mighty protector is the Lord of Lakshmi?

verses.original:
सहित सहाय सभीत अति मानि हारि मन मैन।
गहेसि जाइ मुनि चरन तब कहि सुठि आरत बैन।।126।।
verses.translation: Greatly frightened, Kamadeva and his companions accepted defeat in their hearts. He went and clasped the sage's feet, speaking words of extreme distress.

verses.original:
भयउ न नारद मन कछु रोषा। कहि प्रिय बचन काम परितोषा।।
नाइ चरन सिरु आयसु पाई। गयउ मदन तब सहित सहाई।।
verses.translation: No anger arose in Narada's mind; he consoled Kamadeva with kind words. Bowing his head at the sage's feet and receiving permission, Kamadeva departed with his allies.

verses.original:
मुनि सुसीलता आपनि करनी। सुरपति सभाँ जाइ सब बरनी।।
सुनि सब कें मन अचरजु आवा। मुनिहि प्रसंसि हरिहि सिरु नावा।।
verses.translation: The sage's good nature and his own experience — Kamadeva went to the court of Indra and described it all. Hearing everything, all were amazed; they praised the sage and bowed to Hari.

verses.original:
तब नारद गवने सिव पाहीं। जिता काम अहमिति मन माहीं।।
मार चरित संकरहिं सुनाए। अतिप्रिय जानि महेस सिखाए।।
verses.translation: Then Narada went to Shiva, with the pride in his heart that he had conquered Kamadeva. He narrated the episode of Kamadeva to Shankara, and Maheshwara, knowing him to be very dear, gave him counsel.

verses.original:
बार बार बिनवउँ मुनि तोहीं। जिमि यह कथा सुनायहु मोहीं।।
तिमि जनि हरिहि सुनावहु कबहूँ। चलेहुँ प्रसंग दुराएडु तबहूँ।।
verses.translation: 'Again and again I entreat you, O sage — just as you have told this story to me, never tell it to Hari. And if the topic comes up, conceal it even then.'

verses.original:
संभु दीन्ह उपदेस हित नहिं नारदहि सोहान।
भारद्वाज कौतुक सुनहु हरि इच्छा बलवान।।127।।
verses.translation: Shambhu gave this beneficial advice, but Narada did not find it agreeable. O Bharadvaja, hear this amusing tale — the will of Hari is all-powerful.

verses.original:
राम कीन्ह चाहहिं सोइ होई। करै अन्यथा अस नहिं कोई।।
संभु बचन मुनि मन नहिं भाए। तब बिरंचि के लोक सिधाए।।
verses.translation: 'Whatever Rama wills, that alone happens; no one can make it otherwise.' Shambhu's words did not please the sage, so he proceeded to the realm of Brahma.

verses.original:
एक बार करतल बर बीना। गावत हरि गुन गान प्रबीना।।
छीरसिंधु गवने मुनिनाथा। जहँ बस श्रीनिवास श्रुतिमाथा।।
verses.translation: Once, with his excellent veena in hand, singing the glories of Hari with great skill, the lord of sages traveled to the Ocean of Milk, where Shrinivasa, the crown of the Vedas, resides.

verses.original:
हरषि मिले उठि रमानिकेता। बैठे आसन रिषिहि समेता।।
बोले बिहसि चराचर राया। बहुते दिनन कीन्हि मुनि दाया।।
verses.translation: The Lord of Lakshmi rose joyfully to greet him and seated the sage beside him. The Lord of all creation laughed and said, 'After many days has the sage shown us this kindness.'

verses.original:
काम चरित नारद सब भाषे। जद्यपि प्रथम बरजि सिवँ राखे।।
अति प्रचंड रघुपति कै माया। जेहि न मोह अस को जग जाया।।
verses.translation: Narada narrated the entire episode of Kamadeva, even though Shiva had previously warned him against it. Most powerful is the Maya of Raghupati — who in this world, having been born, has not been deluded by it?

verses.original:
रूख बदन करि बचन मृदु बोले श्रीभगवान ।
तुम्हरे सुमिरन तें मिटहिं मोह मार मद मान।।128।।
verses.translation: Making his face stern but his words gentle, Shri Bhagavan said, 'By remembering you, delusion, passion, pride, and arrogance are all destroyed.'

verses.original:
सुनु मुनि मोह होइ मन ताकें। ग्यान बिराग हृदय नहिं जाके।।
ब्रह्मचरज ब्रत रत मतिधीरा। तुम्हहि कि करइ मनोभव पीरा।।
verses.translation: 'Listen, O sage — delusion befalls one whose heart lacks knowledge and dispassion. You are steadfast in your vow of celibacy, firm of mind — can Kamadeva's torment ever trouble you?'

verses.original:
नारद कहेउ सहित अभिमाना। कृपा तुम्हारि सकल भगवाना।।
करुनानिधि मन दीख बिचारी। उर अंकुरेउ गरब तरु भारी।।
verses.translation: Narada replied with pride, 'It is all your grace, O Lord.' The compassionate Lord looked into Narada's mind and saw that a great tree of pride had sprouted in his heart.

verses.original:
बेगि सो मै डारिहउँ उखारी। पन हमार सेवक हितकारी।।
मुनि कर हित मम कौतुक होई। अवसि उपाय करबि मै सोई।।
verses.translation: 'I shall quickly uproot it, for my vow is to protect the welfare of my servants. For the sage's own good, I shall arrange a playful sport; I shall certainly devise a plan for that.'

verses.original:
तब नारद हरि पद सिर नाई। चले हृदयँ अहमिति अधिकाई।।
श्रीपति निज माया तब प्रेरी। सुनहु कठिन करनी तेहि केरी।।
verses.translation: Then Narada bowed at Hari's feet and departed, his heart swelling with pride. The Lord of Lakshmi then set his own Maya in motion. Now hear of her formidable doings.

verses.original:
बिरचेउ मग महुँ नगर तेहिं सत जोजन बिस्तार।
श्रीनिवासपुर तें अधिक रचना बिबिध प्रकार।।129।।
verses.translation: She created a city on the sage's path, a hundred yojanas in extent — more magnificent than the city of Shrinivasa himself, with designs of every kind.

verses.original:
बसहिं नगर सुंदर नर नारी। जनु बहु मनसिज रति तनुधारी।।
तेहिं पुर बसइ सीलनिधि राजा। अगनित हय गय सेन समाजा।।
verses.translation: Handsome men and women dwelt in the city, as if many Kamadevas and Ratis had taken bodily form. In that city lived a king who was a treasure of virtue, with countless horses, elephants, and armies.

verses.original:
सत सुरेस सम बिभव बिलासा। रूप तेज बल नीति निवासा।।
बिस्वमोहनी तासु कुमारी। श्री बिमोह जिसु रूपु निहारी।।
verses.translation: His splendor and luxury equaled that of a hundred Indras; he was a treasury of beauty, majesty, strength, and wisdom. His daughter enchanted the whole world — even Lakshmi would be bewildered seeing her beauty.

verses.original:
सोइ हरिमाया सब गुन खानी। सोभा तासु कि जाइ बखानी।।
करइ स्वयंबर सो नृपबाला। आए तहँ अगनित महिपाला।।
verses.translation: She was Hari's own Maya, a mine of all virtues — who could describe her beauty? That princess was to hold a swayamvara (self-choice wedding), and countless kings had gathered there.

verses.original:
मुनि कौतुकी नगर तेहिं गयऊ। पुरबासिन्ह सब पूछत भयऊ।।
सुनि सब चरित भूपगृहँ आए। करि पूजा नृप मुनि बैठाए।।
verses.translation: The curious sage entered that city and asked the citizens about everything. Hearing the full account, he went to the king's palace, where the king worshipped him and seated the sage.

verses.original:
आनि देखाई नारदहि भूपति राजकुमारि।
कहहु नाथ गुन दोष सब एहि के हृदयँ बिचारि।।130।।
verses.translation: The king brought the princess to show Narada and said, 'O Lord, examine her carefully and tell me all her merits and faults, reflecting in your heart.'

verses.original:
देखि रूप मुनि बिरति बिसारी। बड़ी बार लगि रहे निहारी।।
लच्छन तासु बिलोकि भुलाने। हृदयँ हरष नहिं प्रगट बखाने।।
verses.translation: Seeing her beauty, the sage forgot his detachment and kept gazing at her for a long time. He studied her auspicious marks and was enraptured, but he rejoiced only in his heart without speaking openly.

verses.original:
जो एहि बरइ अमर सोइ होई। समरभूमि तेहि जीत न कोई।।
सेवहिं सकल चराचर ताही। बरइ सीलनिधि कन्या जाही।।
verses.translation: 'Whoever marries her shall become immortal; no one shall defeat him on the battlefield. All beings, animate and inanimate, shall serve him — whoever that treasury of virtue gives her daughter to.'

verses.original:
लच्छन सब बिचारि उर राखे। कछुक बनाइ भूप सन भाषे।।
सुता सुलच्छन कहि नृप पाहीं। नारद चले सोच मन माहीं।।
verses.translation: He considered all the auspicious marks and kept them to himself; he spoke only selectively to the king. Having told the king that his daughter was most fortunate, Narada departed with anxiety in his heart.

verses.original:
करौं जाइ सोइ जतन बिचारी। जेहि प्रकार मोहि बरै कुमारी।।
जप तप कछु न होइ तेहि काला। हे बिधि मिलइ कवन बिधि बाला।।
verses.translation: 'I shall go and devise a plan so that the princess chooses me. Neither meditation nor penance will avail at this time. O Creator, how can I obtain the maiden?'

verses.original:
एहि अवसर चाहिअ परम सोभा रूप बिसाल।
जो बिलोकि रीझै कुअँरि तब मेलै जयमाल।।131।।
verses.translation: 'At this moment, I need supreme beauty and a magnificent form. If the princess is pleased seeing me, she will place the victory garland around my neck.'

verses.original:
हरि सन मागौं सुंदरताई। होइहि जात गहरु अति भाई।।
मोरें हित हरि सम नहिं कोऊ। एहि अवसर सहाय सोइ होऊ।।
verses.translation: 'I shall ask Hari for beauty; there is great urgency in going, brother. No one cares for me more than Hari; may he be my helper at this time.'

verses.original:
बहुबिधि बिनय कीन्हि तेहि काला। प्रगटेउ प्रभु कौतुकी कृपाला।।
प्रभु बिलोकि मुनि नयन जुड़ाने। होइहि काजु हिएँ हरषाने।।
verses.translation: He made many kinds of entreaties at that time, and the playful, compassionate Lord appeared. Seeing the Lord, the sage's eyes were cooled; his heart rejoiced, thinking his purpose would be accomplished.

verses.original:
अति आरति कहि कथा सुनाई। करहु कृपा करि होहु सहाई।।
आपन रूप देहु प्रभु मोही। आन भाँति नहिं पावौं ओही।।
verses.translation: With great earnestness, he told his story and said, 'Show me your grace and help me. Give me your own form, O Lord; in no other way can I obtain her.'

verses.original:
जेहि बिधि नाथ होइ हित मोरा। करहु सो बेगि दास मैं तोरा।।
निज माया बल देखि बिसाला। हियँ हँसि बोले दीनदयाला।।
verses.translation: 'In whatever way my good may be achieved, O Lord, please do that quickly — I am your servant.' Seeing the great power of his own Maya, the compassionate Lord smiled within and spoke.

verses.original:
जेहि बिधि होइहि परम हित नारद सुनहु तुम्हार।
सोइ हम करब न आन कछु बचन न मृषा हमार।।132।।
verses.translation: 'Whatever is in your supreme interest, O Narada, hear — that alone shall I do. I shall do nothing else; my words are never false.'

verses.original:
कुपथ माग रुज ब्याकुल रोगी। बैद न देइ सुनहु मुनि जोगी।।
एहि बिधि हित तुम्हार मैं ठयऊ। कहि अस अंतरहित प्रभु भयऊ।।
verses.translation: 'A doctor does not give a patient what he craves if it would worsen his disease — listen, O sage and yogi. In this way, I have resolved what is best for you.' Saying this, the Lord disappeared.

verses.original:
माया बिबस भए मुनि मूढ़ा। समुझी नहिं हरि गिरा निगूढ़ा।।
गवने तुरत तहाँ रिषिराई। जहाँ स्वयंबर भूमि बनाई।।
verses.translation: Under the spell of Maya, the sage became foolish and did not understand Hari's cryptic words. The lord of sages immediately went to the place where the swayamvara arena had been prepared.

verses.original:
निज निज आसन बैठे राजा। बहु बनाव करि सहित समाजा।।
मुनि मन हरष रूप अति मोरें। मोहि तजि आनहि बारिहि न भोरें।।
verses.translation: Each king sat on his own throne, elaborately adorned with their retinues. The sage thought, 'My form is supremely handsome; she will not even glance at anyone else.'

verses.original:
मुनि हित कारन कृपानिधाना। दीन्ह कुरूप न जाइ बखाना।।
सो चरित्र लखि काहुँ न पावा। नारद जानि सबहिं सिर नावा।।
verses.translation: For the sage's own good, the compassionate Lord had given him an unspeakably ugly form. No one perceived this trick, but Narada, thinking everyone respected him, bowed to all.

verses.original:
रहे तहाँ दुइ रुद्र गन ते जानहिं सब भेउ।
बिप्रबेष देखत फिरहिं परम कौतुकी तेउ।।133।।
verses.translation: Two attendants of Shiva were present there, and they knew the whole secret. Disguised as Brahmins, these supremely playful ones walked about watching everything.

verses.original:
जेंहि समाज बैंठे मुनि जाई। हृदयँ रूप अहमिति अधिकाई।।
तहँ बैठ महेस गन दोऊ। बिप्रबेष गति लखइ न कोऊ।।
verses.translation: In the assembly where the sage sat, swelling with pride in his beauty, those two attendants of Maheshwara also sat, disguised as Brahmins, their true identity known to no one.

verses.original:
करहिं कूटि नारदहि सुनाई। नीकि दीन्हि हरि सुंदरताई।।
रीझहि राजकुअँरि छबि देखी। इन्हहि बरिहि हरि जानि बिसेषी।।
verses.translation: They mockingly said within Narada's hearing, 'Hari has certainly given him fine beauty! The princess will be charmed by his looks and will choose him, knowing him to be Hari's special favorite!'

verses.original:
मुनिहि मोह मन हाथ पराएँ। हँसहिं संभु गन अति सचु पाएँ।।
जदपि सुनहिं मुनि अटपटि बानी। समुझि न परइ बुद्धि भ्रम सानी।।
verses.translation: The sage's mind was deluded, controlled by another's hand; Shambhu's attendants laughed heartily at the fun. Though the sage heard their cryptic remarks, his understanding, mixed with delusion, could not grasp their meaning.

verses.original:
काहुँ न लखा सो चरित बिसेषा। सो सरूप नृपकन्याँ देखा।।
मर्कट बदन भयंकर देही। देखत हृदयँ क्रोध भा तेही।।
verses.translation: No one else perceived this special trick, but the princess saw his true form — a monkey's face on a hideous body. Seeing it, anger arose in her heart.

verses.original:
सखीं संग लै कुअँरि तब चलि जनु राजमराल।
देखत फिरइ महीप सब कर सरोज जयमाल।।134।।
verses.translation: The princess then walked with her companions like a royal swan, viewing all the kings with the victory garland in her lotus-like hands.

verses.original:
जेहि दिसि बैठे नारद फूली। सो दिसि देहि न बिलोकी भूली।।
पुनि पुनि मुनि उकसहिं अकुलाहीं। देखि दसा हर गन मुसकाहीं।।
verses.translation: In the direction where the inflated Narada sat, the princess did not even glance, not even by mistake. Again and again the sage rose and strained forward; seeing his state, Shiva's attendants smiled.

verses.original:
धरि नृपतनु तहँ गयउ कृपाला। कुअँरि हरषि मेलेउ जयमाला।।
दुलहिनि लै गे लच्छिनिवासा। नृपसमाज सब भयउ निरासा।।
verses.translation: The compassionate Lord himself came there in the form of a king, and the princess joyfully placed the victory garland around his neck. The Lord of Lakshmi took the bride away, and the entire assembly of kings was left in despair.

verses.original:
मुनि अति बिकल मोंहँ मति नाठी। मनि गिरि गई छूटि जनु गाँठी।।
तब हर गन बोले मुसुकाई। निज मुख मुकुर बिलोकहु जाई।।
verses.translation: The sage was utterly distraught; his senses had fled, as if a jewel had slipped from a loosened knot. Then Shiva's attendants spoke with a smile: 'Go and see your face in a mirror!'

verses.original:
अस कहि दोउ भागे भयँ भारी। बदन दीख मुनि बारि निहारी।।
बेषु बिलोकि क्रोध अति बाढ़ा। तिन्हहि सराप दीन्ह अति गाढ़ा।।
verses.translation: Saying this, both of them fled in great fear. The sage looked at his face reflected in water. Seeing his appearance, his anger swelled enormously, and he cursed them both with a terrible curse.

verses.original:
होहु निसाचर जाइ तुम्ह कपटी पापी दोउ।
हँसेहु हमहि सो लेहु फल बहुरि हँसेहु मुनि कोउ।।135।।
verses.translation: 'Go and become demons, you two deceitful sinners! You laughed at me — now reap the fruit of it. Let no other sage ever laugh again.'

verses.original:
पुनि जल दीख रूप निज पावा। तदपि हृदयँ संतोष न आवा।।
फरकत अधर कोप मन माहीं। सपदी चले कमलापति पाहीं।।
verses.translation: Then, looking in the water again, he saw his own true form restored, yet his heart found no peace. His lips quivered with anger as he strode swiftly toward the Lord of Lakshmi.

verses.original:
देहउँ श्राप कि मरिहउँ जाई। जगत मोर उपहास कराई।।
बीचहिं पंथ मिले दनुजारी। संग रमा सोइ राजकुमारी।।
verses.translation: 'I shall either curse him or go and die — he has made me a laughingstock before the world!' On the way, he met the slayer of demons, with Lakshmi and that same princess by his side.

verses.original:
बोले मधुर बचन सुरसाईं। मुनि कहँ चले बिकल की नाईं।।
सुनत बचन उपजा अति क्रोधा। माया बस न रहा मन बोधा।।
verses.translation: The Lord of the gods spoke in sweet words: 'O sage, where are you going in such agitation?' Hearing his words, extreme anger arose; under Maya's power, wisdom did not remain in his mind.

verses.original:
पर संपदा सकहु नहिं देखी। तुम्हरें इरिषा कपट बिसेषी।।
मथत सिंधु रुद्रहि बौरायहु। सुरन्ह प्रेरी बिष पान करायहु।।
verses.translation: 'You cannot bear to see another's prosperity; you are full of envy and deceit. When churning the ocean, you drove Rudra mad and made the gods give him poison to drink.'

verses.original:
असुर सुरा बिष संकरहि आपु रमा मनि चारु।
स्वारथ साधक कुटिल तुम्ह सदा कपट ब्यवहारु।।136।।
verses.translation: 'You gave the demons the wine, poison to Shankara, and kept Lakshmi and the precious jewel for yourself. You are a selfish schemer — your dealings are always full of deceit.'

verses.original:
परम स्वतंत्र न सिर पर कोई। भावइ मनहि करहु तुम्ह सोई।।
भलेहि मंद मंदेहि भल करहू। बिसमय हरष न हियँ कछु धरहू।।
verses.translation: 'You are supremely independent with no one above you; you do whatever pleases your mind. You turn good into bad and bad into good, with neither wonder nor joy in your heart.'

verses.original:
डहकि डहकि परिचेहु सब काहू। अति असंक मन सदा उछाहू।।
करम सुभासुभ तुम्हहि न बाधा। अब लगि तुम्हहि न काहूँ साधा।।
verses.translation: 'Deceiving and cheating everyone, your mind is always fearless and joyful. Good or bad deeds do not bind you; until now, no one has bested you.'

verses.original:
भले भवन अब बायन दीन्हा। पावहुगे फल आपन कीन्हा।।
बंचेहु मोहि जवनि धरि देहा। सोइ तनु धरहु श्राप मम एहा।।
verses.translation: 'Well, you have now given a fine wedding gift! You shall reap the fruit of what you have done. The form in which you deceived me — that very body you shall assume; this is my curse.'

verses.original:
कपि आकृति तुम्ह कीन्हि हमारी। करिहहिं कीस सहाय तुम्हारी।।
मम अपकार कीन्ही तुम्ह भारी। नारी बिरहँ तुम्ह होब दुखारी।।
verses.translation: 'You gave me the face of a monkey — monkeys shall be your helpers. You have done me great harm; you shall suffer the anguish of separation from your wife.'

verses.original:
श्राप सीस धरी हरषि हियँ प्रभु बहु बिनती कीन्हि।
निज माया कै प्रबलता करषि कृपानिधि लीन्हि।।137।।
verses.translation: The Lord joyfully accepted the curse upon his head and humbly entreated the sage in many ways. Then the compassionate Lord drew back the power of his own Maya.

verses.original:
जब हरि माया दूरि निवारी। नहिं तहँ रमा न राजकुमारी।।
तब मुनि अति सभीत हरि चरना। गहे पाहि प्रनतारति हरना।।
verses.translation: When Hari withdrew his Maya, neither Lakshmi nor the princess was there any longer. Then the sage, greatly frightened, clasped Hari's feet, crying, 'Protect me, O reliever of the suppliant's distress!'

verses.original:
मृषा होउ मम श्राप कृपाला। मम इच्छा कह दीनदयाला।।
मैं दुर्बचन कहे बहुतेरे। कह मुनि पाप मिटिहिं किमि मेरे।।
verses.translation: 'Let my curse become false, O compassionate Lord!' 'It is my will,' said the merciful one. 'I spoke many harsh words,' said the sage; 'how shall my sins be cleansed?'

verses.original:
जपहु जाइ संकर सत नामा। होइहि हृदयँ तुरंत बिश्रामा।।
कोउ नहिं सिव समान प्रिय मोरें। असि परतीति तजहु जनि भोरें।।
verses.translation: 'Go and chant the hundred names of Shankara; your heart will find peace at once. No one is as dear to me as Shiva; never abandon this faith, not even in confusion.'

verses.original:
जेहि पर कृपा न करहिं पुरारी। सो न पाव मुनि भगति हमारी।।
अस उर धरि महि बिचरहु जाई। अब न तुम्हहि माया निअराई।।
verses.translation: 'One upon whom the destroyer of Tripura does not bestow his grace can never obtain devotion to me, O sage. Hold this truth in your heart and go wander the earth; henceforth, Maya shall never come near you.'

verses.original:
बहुबिधि मुनिहि प्रबोधि प्रभु तब भए अंतरधान।।
सत्यलोक नारद चले करत राम गुन गान।।138।।
verses.translation: Having consoled the sage in many ways, the Lord then vanished from sight. Narada went to Satyaloka, singing the glories of Rama.

verses.original:
हर गन मुनिहि जात पथ देखी। बिगतमोह मन हरष बिसेषी।।
अति सभीत नारद पहिं आए। गहि पद आरत बचन सुनाए।।
verses.translation: Shiva's attendants saw the sage going along the path, free from delusion and with great joy in his heart. Greatly frightened, they approached Narada, clasped his feet, and spoke words of distress.

verses.original:
हर गन हम न बिप्र मुनिराया। बड़ अपराध कीन्ह फल पाया।।
श्राप अनुग्रह करहु कृपाला। बोले नारद दीनदयाला।।
verses.translation: 'We are Shiva's attendants, not Brahmins, O lord of sages. We committed a great sin and have received the consequence. Please grant us both your curse and your grace, O compassionate one.' Narada, merciful to the helpless, spoke:

verses.original:
निसिचर जाइ होहु तुम्ह दोऊ। बैभव बिपुल तेज बल होऊ।।
भुजबल बिस्व जितब तुम्ह जहिआ। धरिहहिं बिष्नु मनुज तनु तहिआ।
verses.translation: 'Go, both of you, and become demons. You shall have immense wealth, splendor, and strength. When you conquer the world by the might of your arms, then Vishnu shall assume a human body.'

verses.original:
समर मरन हरि हाथ तुम्हारा। होइहहु मुकुत न पुनि संसारा।।
चले जुगल मुनि पद सिर नाई। भए निसाचर कालहि पाई।।
verses.translation: 'You shall die at the hands of Hari in battle, and you shall attain liberation — never to return to this world.' Bowing their heads at the sage's feet, both departed. When the time came, they were born as demons.

verses.original:
एक कलप एहि हेतु प्रभु लीन्ह मनुज अवतार।
सुर रंजन सज्जन सुखद हरि भंजन भुबि भार।।139।।
verses.translation: In one cosmic cycle, for this very reason, the Lord took a human incarnation — to delight the gods, bring happiness to the virtuous, to destroy the wicked, and to relieve the earth's burden.

verses.original:
एहि बिधि जनम करम हरि केरे। सुंदर सुखद बिचित्र घनेरे।।
कलप कलप प्रति प्रभु अवतरहीं। चारु चरित नानाबिधि करहीं।।
verses.translation: In this way, Hari's births and deeds are numerous, beautiful, blissful, and wonderfully varied. In each cosmic cycle, the Lord incarnates and performs charming deeds in diverse ways.

verses.original:
तब तब कथा मुनीसन्ह गाई। परम पुनीत प्रबंध बनाई।।
बिबिध प्रसंग अनूप बखाने। करहिं न सुनि आचरजु सयाने।।
verses.translation: Each time, the great sages have composed and sung these supremely holy narratives. They have described incomparable episodes of various kinds; the wise are not surprised to hear them.

verses.original:
हरि अनंत हरिकथा अनंता। कहहिं सुनहिं बहुबिधि सब संता।।
रामचंद्र के चरित सुहाए। कलप कोटि लगि जाहिं न गाए।।
verses.translation: Hari is infinite and his stories are infinite; the saints tell and hear them in manifold ways. The delightful deeds of Ramachandra cannot be fully sung even in countless cosmic cycles.

verses.original:
यह प्रसंग मैं कहा भवानी। हरिमायाँ मोहहिं मुनि ग्यानी।।
प्रभु कौतुकी प्रनत हितकारी।।सेवत सुलभ सकल दुख हारी।।
verses.translation: 'This episode I have told you, O Bhavani — how Hari's Maya deludes even wise sages. The Lord is playful, a benefactor of the suppliant, easy to serve, and the remover of all suffering.'

verses.original:
सुर नर मुनि कोउ नाहिं जेहि न मोह माया प्रबल।।
अस बिचारि मन माहिं भजिअ महामाया पतिहि।।140।।
verses.translation: 'There is no god, man, or sage whom the mighty Maya has not deluded. Reflecting thus in your heart, worship the Lord of that great Maya.'

verses.original:
अपर हेतु सुनु सैलकुमारी। कहउँ बिचित्र कथा बिस्तारी।।
जेहि कारन अज अगुन अरूपा। ब्रह्म भयउ कोसलपुर भूपा।।
verses.translation: 'Now hear another reason, O daughter of the mountain, which I shall narrate as an elaborate and wonderful tale — the reason why the unborn, attributeless, formless Brahman became the king of Koshala.'

verses.original:
जो प्रभु बिपिन फिरत तुम्ह देखा। बंधु समेत धरें मुनिबेषा।।
जासु चरित अवलोकि भवानी। सती सरीर रहिहु बौरानी।।
verses.translation: 'The Lord whom you saw wandering in the forest with his brother, dressed as sages — upon seeing whose deeds, O Bhavani, you, as Sati, became so bewildered —'

verses.original:
अजहुँ न छाया मिटति तुम्हारी। तासु चरित सुनु भ्रम रुज हारी।।
लीला कीन्हि जो तेहिं अवतारा। सो सब कहिहउँ मति अनुसारा।।
verses.translation: 'Even now the shadow of that delusion has not left you. Listen to his story, which cures the disease of doubt. The divine deeds he performed in that incarnation — I shall narrate them all according to my understanding.'

verses.original:
भरद्वाज सुनि संकर बानी। सकुचि सप्रेम उमा मुसकानी।।
लगे बहुरि बरने बृषकेतू। सो अवतार भयउ जेहि हेतू।।
verses.translation: O Bharadvaja, hearing Shankara's words, Uma shyly smiled with love. The bull-bannered one resumed his narration, telling of the reason for that incarnation.

verses.original:
सो मैं तुम्ह सन कहउँ सबु सुनु मुनीस मन लाई।।
राम कथा कलि मल हरनि मंगल करनि सुहाइ।।141।।
verses.translation: 'All this I shall tell you; listen attentively, O lord of sages. The story of Rama destroys the impurities of the Kali age and is auspicious and beautiful.'

verses.original:
स्वायंभू मनु अरु सतरूपा। जिन्ह तें भै नरसृष्टि अनूपा।।
दंपति धरम आचरन नीका। अजहुँ गाव श्रुति जिन्ह कै लीका।।
verses.translation: 'Svayambhuva Manu and Shatarupa — from whom the matchless human creation arose — this couple practiced exemplary righteousness; even now the Vedas sing of their noble way of life.'

verses.original:
नृप उत्तानपाद सुत तासू। ध्रुव हरि भगत भयउ सुत जासू।।
लघु सुत नाम प्रिय्रब्रत ताही। बेद पुरान प्रसंसहि जाही।।
verses.translation: 'King Uttanapada was their son, and Dhruva, the great devotee of Hari, was born as his son. Their younger son was named Priyavrata, whom the Vedas and Puranas praise.'

verses.original:
देवहूति पुनि तासु कुमारी। जो मुनि कर्दम कै प्रिय नारी।।
आदिदेव प्रभु दीनदयाला। जठर धरेउ जेहिं कपिल कृपाला।।
verses.translation: 'Devahuti was his daughter, the beloved wife of the sage Kardama. The primeval, compassionate Lord — the merciful Kapila — was borne in her womb.'

verses.original:
सांख्य सास्त्र जिन्ह प्रगट बखाना। तत्व बिचार निपुन भगवाना।।
तेहिं मनु राज कीन्ह बहु काला। प्रभु आयसु सब बिधि प्रतिपाला।।
verses.translation: 'He who revealed the Sankhya philosophy, the Lord who is supremely skilled in the analysis of truth. That Manu ruled for a very long time, upholding the Lord's commands in every way.'

verses.original:
होइ न बिषय बिराग भवन बसत भा चौथपन।
हृदयँ बहुत दुख लाग जनम गयउ हरिभगति बिनु।।142।।
verses.translation: 'Living in a household, detachment from sense objects did not arise, and old age arrived. Great sorrow filled his heart that his life had passed without devotion to Hari.'

verses.original:
बरबस राज सुतहि तब दीन्हा। नारि समेत गवन बन कीन्हा।।
तीरथ बर नैमिष बिख्याता। अति पुनीत साधक सिधि दाता।।
verses.translation: 'He forcibly handed over the kingdom to his son and departed for the forest with his wife. The famous sacred site of Naimisha, supremely holy and granting success to spiritual seekers —'

verses.original:
बसहिं तहाँ मुनि सिद्ध समाजा। तहँ हियँ हरषि चलेउ मनु राजा।।
पंथ जात सोहहिं मतिधीरा। ग्यान भगति जनु धरें सरीरा।।
verses.translation: 'Hosts of sages and perfected beings dwelt there. There the king went with a glad heart. On the way, the steadfast couple shone like knowledge and devotion incarnate.'

verses.original:
पहुँचे जाइ धेनुमति तीरा। हरषि नहाने निरमल नीरा।।
आए मिलन सिद्ध मुनि ग्यानी। धरम धुरंधर नृपरिषि जानी।।
verses.translation: 'They reached the bank of the Dhenumati River and joyfully bathed in its pure waters. Sages, perfected beings, and men of knowledge came to meet them, recognizing them as royal sages and champions of righteousness.'

verses.original:
जहँ जँह तीरथ रहे सुहाए। मुनिन्ह सकल सादर करवाए।।
कृस सरीर मुनिपट परिधाना। सत समाज नित सुनहिं पुराना ।
verses.translation: 'They reverently visited all the beautiful sacred places there, guided by the sages. Their bodies were thin, they wore the garb of ascetics, and daily they listened to the Puranas in the company of saints.'

verses.original:
द्वादस अच्छर मंत्र पुनि जपहिं सहित अनुराग।
बासुदेव पद पंकरुह दंपति मन अति लाग।।143।।
verses.translation: 'They chanted the twelve-syllabled mantra with love, and the couple's minds were deeply attached to the lotus feet of Vasudeva.'

verses.original:
करहिं अहार साक फल कंदा। सुमिरहिं ब्रह्म सच्चिदानंदा।।
पुनि हरि हेतु करन तप लागे। बारि अधार मूल फल त्यागे।।
verses.translation: 'They ate only vegetables, fruits, and roots, meditating on Brahman — the truth, consciousness, and bliss. Then, to attain Hari, they began performing austerities, giving up roots and fruits and subsisting on water alone.'

verses.original:
उर अभिलाष निंरंतर होई। देखअ नयन परम प्रभु सोई।।
अगुन अखंड अनंत अनादी। जेहि चिंतहिं परमारथबादी।।
verses.translation: 'A constant longing filled their hearts — to behold with their own eyes that supreme Lord who is without attributes, indivisible, infinite, and beginningless — upon whom the seekers of the highest truth meditate.'

verses.original:
