नेति नेति जेहि बेद निरूपा। निजानंद निरुपाधि अनूपा।।
संभु बिरंचि बिष्नु भगवाना। उपजहिं जासु अंस तें नाना।।
verses.translation: 'The one whom the Vedas describe as "not this, not this" — self-blissful, without limitations, and incomparable. Shambhu, Brahma, Vishnu, and many other gods arise from a mere fraction of his being.'

verses.original:
ऐसेउ प्रभु सेवक बस अहई। भगत हेतु लीलातनु गहई।।
जौं यह बचन सत्य श्रुति भाषा। तौ हमार पूजहि अभिलाषा।।
verses.translation: 'Yet even such a Lord is under the control of his servants and assumes a body for the sake of his devotees. If the Vedas speak truly, then may our wish be fulfilled.'

verses.original:
एहि बिधि बीतें बरष षट सहस बारि आहार।
संबत सप्त सहस्त्र पुनि रहे समीर अधार।।144।।
verses.translation: 'In this way, six thousand years passed with water as their only sustenance. Then for another seven thousand years, they lived on air alone.'

verses.original:
बरष सहस दस त्यागेउ सोऊ। ठाढ़े रहे एक पद दोऊ।।
बिधि हरि तप देखि अपारा। मनु समीप आए बहु बारा।।
verses.translation: Manu renounced even that for ten thousand years, standing on one foot the whole time. Seeing his boundless austerity, Brahma and Vishnu came near him many times.

verses.original:
मागहु बर बहु भाँति लोभाए। परम धीर नहिं चलहिं चलाए।।
अस्थिमात्र होइ रहे सरीरा। तदपि मनाग मनहिं नहिं पीरा।।
verses.translation: They tempted him in many ways saying 'Ask for a boon,' but the supremely steadfast one would not be moved. His body was reduced to mere bones, yet he felt not the slightest pain in his mind.

verses.original:
प्रभु सर्बग्य दास निज जानी। गति अनन्य तापस नृप रानी।।
मागु मागु बरु भै नभ बानी। परम गभीर कृपामृत सानी।।
verses.translation: The omniscient Lord, recognizing them as His own devotees and knowing the unwavering devotion of the ascetic king and queen, spoke from the sky: 'Ask, ask for a boon!' — a voice supremely deep, steeped in the nectar of compassion.

verses.original:
मृतक जिआवनि गिरा सुहाई। श्रबन रंध्र होइ उर जब आई।।
ह्रष्टपुष्ट तन भए सुहाए। मानहुँ अबहिं भवन ते आए।।
verses.translation: That beautiful voice, capable of reviving the dead, when it entered through the ears into the heart, their bodies became healthy and radiant, as if they had just arrived from home.

verses.original:
श्रवन सुधा सम बचन सुनि पुलक प्रफुल्लित गात।
बोले मनु करि दंडवत प्रेम न हृदयँ समात।।145।।
verses.translation: Hearing words sweet as nectar, with thrilled and joyful bodies, Manu prostrated himself and spoke, his heart overflowing with love.

verses.original:
सुनु सेवक सुरतरु सुरधेनु। बिधि हरि हर बंदित पद रेनू।।
सेवत सुलभ सकल सुख दायक। प्रनतपाल सचराचर नायक।।
verses.translation: Listen, O Lord who is like the wish-fulfilling tree and divine cow for devotees, whose feet's dust is worshipped by Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva. You are easy to serve, bestower of all happiness, protector of the humble, and lord of all animate and inanimate creation.

verses.original:
जौं अनाथ हित हम पर नेहू। तौ प्रसन्न होइ यह बर देहू।।
जो सरूप बस सिव मन माहीं। जेहि कारन मुनि जतन कराहीं।।
verses.translation: If You have affection for us helpless ones, then be pleased and grant this boon: that form which dwells in Shiva's heart, for which sages strive with great effort—

verses.original:
जो भुसुंडि मन मानस हंसा। सगुन अगुन जेहि निगम प्रसंसा।।
देखहिं हम सो रूप भरि लोचन। कृपा करहु प्रनतारति मोचन।।
verses.translation: That form which is the swan in the Manasa lake of Bhushundi's mind, which the Vedas praise as both with and without attributes— let us behold that form with our eyes filled. Be gracious, O reliever of the devotees' distress.

verses.original:
दंपति बचन परम प्रिय लागे। मुदुल बिनीत प्रेम रस पागे।।
भगत बछल प्रभु कृपानिधाना। बिस्वबास प्रगटे भगवाना।।
verses.translation: The couple's words were supremely pleasing — gentle, humble, and steeped in the essence of love. The Lord, who is fond of devotees and an ocean of compassion, manifested before them with full faith.

verses.original:
नील सरोरुह नील मनि नील नीरधर स्याम।
लाजहिं तन सोभा निरखि कोटि कोटि सत काम।।146।।
verses.translation: Dark as the blue lotus, blue sapphire, and blue rain-cloud, millions upon millions of Kamadevas felt abashed beholding the beauty of His form.

verses.original:
सरद मयंक बदन छबि सींवा। चारु कपोल चिबुक दर ग्रीवा।।
अधर अरुन रद सुंदर नासा। बिधु कर निकर बिनिंदक हासा।।
verses.translation: His face was the very limit of the beauty of the autumn moon. Beautiful were His cheeks, chin, and graceful neck. His lips were red, teeth lovely, nose beautiful, and His smile put to shame the rays of the moon.

verses.original:
नव अबुंज अंबक छबि नीकी। चितवनि ललित भावँती जी की।।
भुकुटि मनोज चाप छबि हारी। तिलक ललाट पटल दुतिकारी।।
verses.translation: The beauty of His eyes was like fresh lotuses; His glance was charming and captivating to the heart. His eyebrows defeated the beauty of Kamadeva's bow; the tilak on His forehead enhanced its radiance.

verses.original:
कुंडल मकर मुकुट सिर भ्राजा। कुटिल केस जनु मधुप समाजा।।
उर श्रीबत्स रुचिर बनमाला। पदिक हार भूषन मनिजाला।।
verses.translation: Makara-shaped earrings and a crown shone on His head; His curly locks were like a swarm of bees. On His chest was the Shrivatsa mark, a beautiful garland of forest flowers, a pearl necklace, and ornaments studded with gems.

verses.original:
केहरि कंधर चारु जनेउ। बाहु बिभूषन सुंदर तेऊ।।
करि कर सरि सुभग भुजदंडा। कटि निषंग कर सर कोदंडा।।
verses.translation: His neck was like a lion's, adorned with a beautiful sacred thread; His arms too bore lovely ornaments. His mighty arms were like the trunk of an elephant; at His waist hung a quiver, and in His hands were arrows and a bow.

verses.original:
तडित बिनिंदक पीत पट उदर रेख बर तीनि।।
नाभि मनोहर लेति जनु जमुन भवँर छबि छीनि।।147।।
verses.translation: His yellow garment put lightning to shame; on His belly were three beautiful lines. His beautiful navel seemed to steal the charm of a whirlpool in the Yamuna.

verses.original:
पद राजीव बरनि नहि जाहीं। मुनि मन मधुप बसहिं जेन्ह माहीं।।
बाम भाग सोभति अनुकूला। आदिसक्ति छबिनिधि जगमूला।।
verses.translation: His lotus feet cannot be described — in them dwell the bee-like minds of sages. On His left side shone His consort, the primordial power, treasure-house of beauty, and root of the universe.

verses.original:
जासु अंस उपजहिं गुनखानी। अगनित लच्छि उमा ब्रह्मानी।।
भृकुटि बिलास जासु जग होई। राम बाम दिसि सीता सोई।।
verses.translation: From a fraction of whom are born all virtuous Lakshmis, Umas, and Brahmanis — by the play of whose brow the world comes into being — that Sita stood at Rama's left side.

verses.original:
छबिसमुद्र हरि रूप बिलोकी। एकटक रहे नयन पट रोकी।।
चितवहिं सादर रूप अनूपा। तृप्ति न मानहिं मनु सतरूपा।।
verses.translation: Beholding the ocean-of-beauty form of Hari, they gazed with unblinking eyes. They gazed reverently at that incomparable form, yet Manu and Shatarupa could not feel satiated.

verses.original:
हरष बिबस तन दसा भुलानी। परे दंड इव गहि पद पानी।।
सिर परसे प्रभु निज कर कंजा। तुरत उठाए करुनापुंजा।।
verses.translation: Overwhelmed with joy, they forgot the state of their bodies and fell like sticks, clasping His feet with their hands. The Lord, that mass of compassion, touched their heads with His lotus hands and quickly raised them up.

verses.original:
बोले कृपानिधान पुनि अति प्रसन्न मोहि जानि।
मागहु बर जोइ भाव मन महादानि अनुमानि।।148।।
verses.translation: The ocean of mercy spoke again: 'Know Me to be supremely pleased. Ask whatever boon your heart desires, regarding Me as the greatest of givers.'

verses.original:
सुनि प्रभु बचन जोरि जुग पानी। धरि धीरजु बोली मृदु बानी।।
नाथ देखि पद कमल तुम्हारे। अब पूरे सब काम हमारे।।
verses.translation: Hearing the Lord's words, joining both palms together and gathering courage, Manu spoke in gentle words: 'Lord, having seen Your lotus feet, all our desires are now fulfilled.'

verses.original:
एक लालसा बड़ि उर माही। सुगम अगम कहि जात सो नाहीं।।
तुम्हहि देत अति सुगम गोसाईं। अगम लाग मोहि निज कृपनाईं।।
verses.translation: 'Yet one great longing remains in my heart — one that is both easy and impossible to express. For You to give it is very easy, O Master, but my own meagreness makes it seem impossible.'

verses.original:
जथा दरिद्र बिबुधतरु पाई। बहु संपति मागत सकुचाई।।
तासु प्रभा जान नहिं सोई। तथा हृदयँ मम संसय होई।।
verses.translation: 'Just as a poor man who obtains the wish-fulfilling tree feels shy to ask for great wealth, not knowing its power — similarly, there is doubt in my heart.'

verses.original:
सो तुम्ह जानहु अंतरजामी। पुरवहु मोर मनोरथ स्वामी।।
सकुच बिहाइ मागु नृप मोहि। मोरें नहिं अदेय कछु तोही।।
verses.translation: 'You know it, O Indweller of all hearts! Fulfill my desire, O Lord!' The Lord replied: 'Set aside your hesitation and ask, O King — there is nothing that I cannot give you.'

verses.original:
दानि सिरोमनि कृपानिधि नाथ कहउँ सतिभाउ।।
चाहउँ तुम्हहि समान सुत प्रभु सन कवन दुराउ।।149।।
verses.translation: 'O crest-jewel of givers, O ocean of compassion, Lord, I speak my true feeling. I desire a son like You — what can one hide from the Lord?'

verses.original:
देखि प्रीति सुनि बचन अमोले। एवमस्तु करुनानिधि बोले।।
आपु सरिस खोजौं कहँ जाई। नृप तव तनय होब मैं आई।।
verses.translation: Seeing their love and hearing their priceless words, the ocean of compassion said 'So be it.' 'Where shall I go searching for one equal to Myself? O King, I shall Myself come and be born as your son.'

verses.original:
सतरूपहि बिलोकि कर जोरें। देबि मागु बरु जो रुचि तोरे।।
जो बरु नाथ चतुर नृप मागा। सोइ कृपाल मोहि अति प्रिय लागा।।
verses.translation: Looking at Shatarupa standing with folded hands, He said: 'O Queen, ask for whatever boon pleases you.' She replied: 'The boon that my wise husband asked for — that same one, O Merciful Lord, is supremely dear to me.'

verses.original:
प्रभु परंतु सुठि होति ढिठाई। जदपि भगत हित तुम्हहि सोहाई।।
तुम्ह ब्रह्मादि जनक जग स्वामी। ब्रह्म सकल उर अंतरजामी।।
verses.translation: 'But Lord, it is great boldness on my part to say more, even though such boldness pleases You for the sake of Your devotees. You are the father of Brahma and all, the Lord of the universe, the Supreme Spirit dwelling in every heart.'

verses.original:
अस समुझत मन संसय होई। कहा जो प्रभु प्रवान पुनि सोई।।
जे निज भगत नाथ तव अहहीं। जो सुख पावहिं जो गति लहहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Thinking thus, doubt arises in my mind. But whatever the Lord has promised, that shall come true. Those who are Your true devotees, Lord — whatever joy they obtain, whatever state they reach—'

verses.original:
सोइ सुख सोइ गति सोइ भगति सोइ निज चरन सनेहु।।
सोइ बिबेक सोइ रहनि प्रभु हमहि कृपा करि देहु।।150।।
verses.translation: 'That same joy, that same state, that same devotion, that same love for Your feet, that same wisdom, that same way of life — Lord, graciously grant all that to us.'

verses.original:
सुनु मृदु गूढ़ रुचिर बर रचना। कृपासिंधु बोले मृदु बचना।।
जो कछु रुचि तुम्हेर मन माहीं। मैं सो दीन्ह सब संसय नाहीं।।
verses.translation: Hearing her gentle, profound, and beautiful prayer, the ocean of compassion spoke soft words: 'Whatever is in your heart's desire, I have given it all — have no doubt.'

verses.original:
मातु बिबेक अलोकिक तोरें। कबहुँ न मिटिहि अनुग्रह मोरें ।
बंदि चरन मनु कहेउ बहोरी। अवर एक बिनति प्रभु मोरी।।
verses.translation: 'Mother, your extraordinary wisdom shall never fade by My grace.' Bowing at His feet, Manu spoke again: 'Lord, I have one more request.'

verses.original:
सुत बिषइक तव पद रति होऊ। मोहि बड़ मूढ़ कहै किन कोऊ।।
मनि बिनु फनि जिमि जल बिनु मीना। मम जीवन तिमि तुम्हहि अधीना।।
verses.translation: 'May I have devotion to Your feet even when I am blessed with sons — let anyone call me a great fool for it. Like a serpent without its gem, like a fish without water, my life is dependent on You.'

verses.original:
अस बरु मागि चरन गहि रहेऊ। एवमस्तु करुनानिधि कहेऊ।।
अब तुम्ह मम अनुसासन मानी। बसहु जाइ सुरपति रजधानी।।
verses.translation: Having asked this boon, he clung to the Lord's feet. The ocean of mercy said 'So be it.' 'Now, following My command, go and dwell in the capital of Indra.'

verses.original:
तहँ करि भोग बिसाल तात गउँ कछु काल पुनि।
होइहहु अवध भुआल तब मैं होब तुम्हार सुत।।151।।
verses.translation: 'There, enjoying great pleasures for some time, dear one, you shall then become the king of Ayodhya, and I shall become your son.'

verses.original:
इच्छामय नरबेष सँवारें। होइहउँ प्रगट निकेत तुम्हारे।।
अंसन्ह सहित देह धरि ताता। करिहउँ चरित भगत सुखदाता।।
verses.translation: 'Assuming a human form of My own will, I shall manifest in your home. Along with My partial incarnations, I shall take a body, dear one, and perform deeds that delight the devotees.'

verses.original:
जे सुनि सादर नर बड़भागी। भव तरिहहिं ममता मद त्यागी।।
आदिसक्ति जेहिं जग उपजाया। सोउ अवतरिहि मोरि यह माया।।
verses.translation: 'Those fortunate people who hear these deeds with reverence shall cross the ocean of worldly existence, abandoning attachment and pride. The primordial power who created the universe — she too, My Maya, shall incarnate.'

verses.original:
पुरउब मैं अभिलाष तुम्हारा। सत्य सत्य पन सत्य हमारा।।
पुनि पुनि अस कहि कृपानिधाना। अंतरधान भए भगवाना।।
verses.translation: 'I shall fulfill your desire — this is My true, true, and again true vow.' Saying this again and again, the ocean of mercy, the Lord, disappeared.

verses.original:
दंपति उर धरि भगत कृपाला। तेहिं आश्रम निवसे कछु काला।।
समय पाइ तनु तजि अनयासा। जाइ कीन्ह अमरावति बासा।।
verses.translation: The couple enshrined the gracious Lord in their hearts and lived in that hermitage for some time. When the right time came, they effortlessly gave up their bodies and went to dwell in Amaravati, Indra's city.

verses.original:
यह इतिहास पुनीत अति उमहि कही बृषकेतु।
भरद्वाज सुनु अपर पुनि राम जनम कर हेतु।।152।।
verses.translation: This most sacred story Shiva told to Uma. Now listen, Bharadvaja, to yet another cause of Rama's birth.

verses.original:
सुनु मुनि कथा पुनीत पुरानी। जो गिरिजा प्रति संभु बखानी।।
बिस्व बिदित एक कैकय देसू। सत्यकेतु तहँ बसइ नरेसू।।
verses.translation: Listen, O sage, to this ancient and sacred tale that Shambhu narrated to Girija. There is a well-known country called Kaikaya, where dwelt a king named Satyaketu.

verses.original:
धरम धुरंधर नीति निधाना। तेज प्रताप सील बलवाना।।
तेहि कें भए जुगल सुत बीरा। सब गुन धाम महा रनधीरा।।
verses.translation: He was a champion of righteousness, a treasure of wisdom, mighty in valor, glory, virtue, and strength. He had two heroic sons, repositories of all virtues and supremely brave in battle.

verses.original:
राज धनी जो जेठ सुत आही। नाम प्रतापभानु अस ताही।।
अपर सुतहि अरिमर्दन नामा। भुजबल अतुल अचल संग्रामा।।
verses.translation: The elder son was heir to the throne — his name was Pratapabhanu. The younger son was named Arimardana — of incomparable strength and immovable in war.

verses.original:
भाइहि भाइहि परम समीती। सकल दोष छल बरजित प्रीती।।
जेठे सुतहि राज नृप दीन्हा। हरि हित आपु गवन बन कीन्हा।।
verses.translation: The two brothers had supreme affection for each other — a love free from all faults and deceit. The king gave the kingdom to his elder son and himself retired to the forest for the sake of Hari.

verses.original:
जब प्रतापरबि भयउ नृप फिरी दोहाई देस।
प्रजा पाल अति बेदबिधि कतहुँ नहीं अघ लेस।।153।।
verses.translation: When Pratapabhanu became king, his fame spread throughout the land. He ruled his subjects strictly according to the Vedas, and there was not a trace of sin anywhere.

verses.original:
नृप हितकारक सचिव सयाना। नाम धरमरुचि सुक्र समाना।।
सचिव सयान बंधु बलबीरा। आपु प्रतापपुंज रनधीरा।।
verses.translation: The king had a wise minister who served his interests, named Dharmaruchi, equal to Shukra. With a clever minister, a brave brother, and himself being a mighty warrior in battle—

verses.original:
सेन संग चतुरंग अपारा। अमित सुभट सब समर जुझारा।।
सेन बिलोकि राउ हरषाना। अरु बाजे गहगहे निसाना।।
verses.translation: His army was vast, with all four divisions beyond measure — countless warriors, all fierce in combat. Seeing the army, the king was delighted, and war drums resounded mightily.

verses.original:
बिजय हेतु कटकई बनाई। सुदिन साधि नृप चलेउ बजाई।।
जँह तहँ परीं अनेक लराईं। जीते सकल भूप बरिआई।।
verses.translation: Having assembled his army for conquest, the king set out on an auspicious day with drums beating. Wherever they went, many battles were fought, and he defeated all kings by force.

verses.original:
सप्त दीप भुजबल बस कीन्हे। लै लै दंड छाड़ि नृप दीन्हें।।
सकल अवनि मंडल तेहि काला। एक प्रतापभानु महिपाला।।
verses.translation: He conquered the seven continents by the strength of his arms; exacting tribute, he released the kings. In that entire age, on all the earth, Pratapabhanu alone was the sovereign.

verses.original:
स्वबस बिस्व करि बाहुबल निज पुर कीन्ह प्रबेसु।
अरथ धरम कामादि सुख सेवइ समयँ नरेसु।।154।।
verses.translation: Having brought the whole world under his control by the strength of his arms, he returned to his own city. The king enjoyed the pleasures of wealth, duty, and desire, each in its proper time.

verses.original:
भूप प्रतापभानु बल पाई। कामधेनु भै भूमि सुहाई।।
सब दुख बरजित प्रजा सुखारी। धरमसील सुंदर नर नारी।।
verses.translation: Empowered by King Pratapabhanu, the earth became like the wish-fulfilling cow. The people were free from all sorrow and happy; men and women were virtuous and beautiful.

verses.original:
सचिव धरमरुचि हरि पद प्रीती। नृप हित हेतु सिखव नित नीती।।
गुर सुर संत पितर महिदेवा। करइ सदा नृप सब कै सेवा।।
verses.translation: Minister Dharmaruchi had devotion to Lord Hari's feet and would always teach the king wise counsel for his benefit. The king constantly served his guru, the gods, saints, ancestors, and brahmins.

verses.original:
भूप धरम जे बेद बखाने। सकल करइ सादर सुख माने।।
दिन प्रति देह बिबिध बिधि दाना। सुनहु सास्त्र बर बेद पुराना।।
verses.translation: Whatever duties of a king the Vedas prescribe, the king performed them all respectfully and joyfully. Daily he gave various kinds of charity; he listened to the scriptures, Vedas, and Puranas.

verses.original:
नाना बापीं कूप तड़ागा। सुमन बाटिका सुंदर बागा।।
बिप्रभवन सुरभवन सुहाए। सब तीरथन्ह बिचित्र बनाए।।
verses.translation: He built numerous wells, step-wells, tanks, flower gardens, and beautiful orchards. He constructed beautiful homes for brahmins and temples for the gods at all holy places.

verses.original:
जँह लगि कहे पुरान श्रुति एक एक सब जाग।
बार सहस्त्र सहस्त्र नृप किए सहित अनुराग।।155।।
verses.translation: Every sacrifice that the Puranas and Vedas mention, the king performed each one thousands upon thousands of times with great devotion.

verses.original:
हृदयँ न कछु फल अनुसंधाना। भूप बिबेकी परम सुजाना।।
करइ जे धरम करम मन बानी। बासुदेव अर्पित नृप ग्यानी।।
verses.translation: In his heart there was no desire for any reward — the king was supremely wise and discerning. Whatever righteous deeds he performed in mind, word, and deed, the enlightened king offered them all to Lord Vasudeva.

verses.original:
चढ़ि बर बाजि बार एक राजा। मृगया कर सब साजि समाजा।।
बिंध्याचल गभीर बन गयऊ। मृग पुनीत बहु मारत भयऊ।।
verses.translation: Once, mounting a fine horse, the king set out with full hunting equipment for the chase. He entered the deep forests of the Vindhya mountains, and slew many sanctified deer.

verses.original:
फिरत बिपिन नृप दीख बराहू। जनु बन दुरेउ ससिहि ग्रसि राहू।।
बड़ बिधु नहि समात मुख माहीं। मनहुँ क्रोधबस उगिलत नाहीं।।
verses.translation: While roaming the forest, the king spotted a wild boar — as if Rahu had hidden in the woods after swallowing the moon. The great moon would not fit in its mouth, as though it was spitting it out in rage.

verses.original:
कोल कराल दसन छबि गाई। तनु बिसाल पीवर अधिकाई।।
घुरुघुरात हय आरौ पाएँ। चकित बिलोकत कान उठाएँ।।
verses.translation: Its terrible tusks shone gloriously; its body was huge and exceedingly fat. It snorted as it came near the horse, which stood startled, ears pricked up.

verses.original:
नील महीधर सिखर सम देखि बिसाल बराहु।
चपरि चलेउ हय सुटुकि नृप हाँकि न होइ निबाहु।।156।।
verses.translation: Seeing the massive boar, like the peak of a dark mountain, the king spurred his horse and galloped after it — for he could not let it escape.

verses.original:
आवत देखि अधिक रव बाजी। चलेउ बराह मरुत गति भाजी।।
तुरत कीन्ह नृप सर संधाना। महि मिलि गयउ बिलोकत बाना।।
verses.translation: Seeing the horse approaching with great noise, the boar fled swift as the wind. The king immediately drew his bow, but as he watched, the arrow fell to the ground without hitting.

verses.original:
तकि तकि तीर महीस चलावा। करि छल सुअर सरीर बचावा।।
प्रगटत दुरत जाइ मृग भागा। रिस बस भूप चलेउ संग लागा।।
verses.translation: The king aimed and shot arrow after arrow, but the boar craftily dodged them all. The deer kept appearing and disappearing as it fled; in anger, the king followed relentlessly.

verses.original:
गयउ दूरि घन गहन बराहू। जहँ नाहिन गज बाजि निबाहू।।
अति अकेल बन बिपुल कलेसू। तदपि न मृग मग तजइ नरेसू।।
verses.translation: The boar went far into the dense, dark forest where neither elephant nor horse could follow. Utterly alone, the king suffered great distress in the forest, yet he would not abandon the chase.

verses.original:
कोल बिलोकि भूप बड़ धीरा। भागि पैठ गिरिगुहाँ गभीरा।।
अगम देखि नृप अति पछिताई। फिरेउ महाबन परेउ भुलाई।।
verses.translation: Seeing the brave king's resolve, the boar fled and hid in a deep mountain cave. Finding the cave inaccessible, the king deeply regretted, and turning back, he lost his way in the great forest.

verses.original:
खेद खिन्न छुद्धित तृषित राजा बाजि समेत।
खोजत ब्याकुल सरित सर जल बिनु भयउ अचेत।।157।।
verses.translation: Weary, dejected, hungry, and thirsty, the king along with his horse searched desperately for a river or lake, and fainted from lack of water.

verses.original:
फिरत बिपिन आश्रम एक देखा। तहँ बस नृपति कपट मुनिबेषा।।
जासु देस नृप लीन्ह छड़ाई। समर सेन तजि गयउ पराई।।
verses.translation: Wandering in the forest, he saw a hermitage where a king dwelt disguised as a sage. This was the king whose country Pratapabhanu had seized, whose army had fled in battle.

verses.original:
समय प्रतापभानु कर जानी। आपन अति असमय अनुमानी।।
गयउ न गृह मन बहुत गलानी। मिला न राजहि नृप अभिमानी।।
verses.translation: He recognized it was Pratapabhanu's time of fortune and deemed it his own time of misfortune. Full of shame, he had not returned home, and being too proud, he had not sought the king's pardon.

verses.original:
रिस उर मारि रंक जिमि राजा। बिपिन बसइ तापस कें साजा।।
तासु समीप गवन नृप कीन्हा। यह प्रतापरबि तेहि तब चीन्हा।।
verses.translation: Suppressing his rage like a pauper, the king lived in the forest in the guise of an ascetic. When the king approached him, the false ascetic recognized Pratapabhanu at once.

verses.original:
राउ तृषित नहि सो पहिचाना। देखि सुबेष महामुनि जाना।।
उतरि तुरग तें कीन्ह प्रनामा। परम चतुर न कहेउ निज नामा।।
verses.translation: But the thirsty king did not recognize him. Seeing his fine ascetic garb, he took him for a great sage. Dismounting, he bowed in reverence — shrewd as he was, he did not reveal his own name.

verses.original:
भूपति तृषित बिलोकि तेहिं सरबरु दीन्ह देखाइ।
मज्जन पान समेत हय कीन्ह नृपति हरषाइ।।158।।
verses.translation: Seeing the king was thirsty, the false sage showed him a lake. The king joyfully bathed and drank along with his horse.

verses.original:
गै श्रम सकल सुखी नृप भयऊ। निज आश्रम तापस लै गयऊ।।
आसन दीन्ह अस्त रबि जानी। पुनि तापस बोलेउ मृदु बानी।।
verses.translation: All fatigue left him, and the king felt refreshed. The ascetic took him to his hermitage. Seeing the sun about to set, he offered him a seat, and then the ascetic spoke in gentle words.

verses.original:
को तुम्ह कस बन फिरहु अकेलें। सुंदर जुबा जीव परहेलें।।
चक्रबर्ति के लच्छन तोरें। देखत दया लागि अति मोरें।।
verses.translation: 'Who are you, and why do you wander alone in the forest — young and handsome, risking your life? You bear the marks of an emperor; seeing you, I feel great compassion.'

verses.original:
नाम प्रतापभानु अवनीसा। तासु सचिव मैं सुनहु मुनीसा।।
फिरत अहेरें परेउँ भुलाई। बडे भाग देखउँ पद आई।।
verses.translation: The king replied: 'I am a minister of King Pratapabhanu. Listen, O great sage: I lost my way while hunting and by great fortune I have come to your feet.'

verses.original:
हम कहँ दुर्लभ दरस तुम्हारा। जानत हौं कछु भल होनिहारा।।
कह मुनि तात भयउ अँधियारा। जोजन सत्तरि नगरु तुम्हारा।।
verses.translation: 'Your sight is rare for the likes of me — I feel something good is about to happen.' The sage replied: 'Son, it has grown dark; your city is seventy yojanas away.'

verses.original:
निसा घोर गम्भीर बन पंथ न सुनहु सुजान।
बसहु आजु अस जानि तुम्ह जाएहु होत बिहान।।159(क)।।
तुलसी जसि भवतब्यता तैसी मिलइ सहाइ।
आपुनु आवइ ताहि पहिं ताहि तहाँ लै जाइ।।159(ख)।।
verses.translation: 'The night is dark, the forest deep, and there is no path — listen, wise one. Stay here tonight; you may leave at dawn.' Tulsidas says: as destiny decrees, so comes the helper — fate itself brings a person to the right place and takes them there.

verses.original:
भलेहिं नाथ आयसु धरि सीसा। बाँधि तुरग तरु बैठ महीसा।।
नृप बहु भाँती प्रसंसेउ ताही। चरन बंदी निज भाग्य सराही।।
verses.translation: 'Very well, my lord,' said the king, placing the command on his head. He tied his horse to a tree and sat down. He praised the sage in many ways, bowed at his feet, and counted his own fortune blessed.

verses.original:
पुनि बोले मृदु गिरा सुहाई। जानि पिता प्रभु करउँ ढिठाई।।
मोहि मुनीस सुत सेवक जानी। नाथ नाम निज कहहु बखानी।।
verses.translation: Then he spoke again in gentle, pleasant words: 'Knowing you to be like a father, Lord, I make bold. Considering me your son and servant, please tell me your revered name.'

verses.original:
तेहि न जान नृप नृपहि सो जाना। भूप सुह्रद सो कपट सयाना।।
बैरी पुनि छत्री पुनि राजा। छल बल कीन्ह चहइ निज काजा।।
verses.translation: The king did not know the ascetic, but the ascetic knew the king. The king was guileless, but the other was cunning. He was an enemy, moreover a Kshatriya, moreover a king — by deceit and force he wished to accomplish his purpose.

verses.original:
समुझि राजसुख दुखित अराती। अवाँ अनल इव सुलगइ छाती।।
सरल बचन नृप के सुनि काना। बयर सँभारि हृदयँ हरषाना।।
verses.translation: Brooding over his lost royal pleasures, the enemy's breast burned like a furnace of fire. Hearing the king's guileless words, he recalled his enmity and felt delighted within.

verses.original:
कपट बोरि बानी मृदुल बोलेउ जुगुति समेत।
नाम हमार भिखारि अब निर्धन रहित निकेति।।160।।
verses.translation: Steeping his words in deceit, he spoke gently and with cunning: 'My name is Bhikhari (beggar) — I am now a penniless, homeless wanderer.'

verses.original:
कह नृप जे बिग्यान निधाना। तुम्ह सारिखे गलित अभिमाना।।
सदा रहहि अपनपौ दुराएँ। सब बिधि कुसल कुबेष बनाएँ।।
verses.translation: The king said: 'Those who are treasure-houses of wisdom, who like you have dissolved all pride, always conceal their identity and remain hidden in every way, wearing the garb of the humble.'

verses.original:
तेहि तें कहहि संत श्रुति टेरें। परम अकिंचन प्रिय हरि केरें।।
तुम्ह सम अधन भिखारि अगेहा। होत बिरंचि सिवहि संदेहा।।
verses.translation: 'That is why the saints and Vedas declare that those who are utterly possessionless are most dear to the Lord. Even Brahma and Shiva would doubt whether someone could be as rich in renunciation as you — homeless and a beggar.'

verses.original:
जोसि सोसि तव चरन नमामी। मो पर कृपा करिअ अब स्वामी।।
सहज प्रीति भूपति कै देखी। आपु बिषय बिस्वास बिसेषी।।
verses.translation: 'Whatever you may be, I bow at your feet. Now be gracious to me, O master.' Seeing the king's natural affection and having great confidence in his own disguise,

verses.original:
सब प्रकार राजहि अपनाई। बोलेउ अधिक सनेह जनाई।।
सुनु सतिभाउ कहउँ महिपाला। इहाँ बसत बीते बहु काला।।
verses.translation: the false ascetic embraced the king in every way and spoke with heightened affection: 'Listen to the truth, O king — many ages have passed since I have been living here.'

verses.original:
अब लगि मोहि न मिलेउ कोउ मैं न जनावउँ काहु।
लोकमान्यता अनल सम कर तप कानन दाहु।।161(क)।।
तुलसी देखि सुबेषु भूलहिं मूढ़ न चतुर नर।
सुंदर केकिहि पेखु बचन सुधा सम असन अहि।।161(ख)।।
verses.translation: 'Until now no one has met me, nor do I reveal myself to anyone. Public fame is like fire that burns the forest of austerity.' Tulsidas says: seeing a fine appearance, not only the foolish but even the wise are deceived. Behold the beautiful peacock — its voice is sweet as nectar but it feeds on serpents.

verses.original:
तातें गुपुत रहउँ जग माहीं। हरि तजि किमपि प्रयोजन नाहीं।।
प्रभु जानत सब बिनहिं जनाएँ। कहहु कवनि सिधि लोक रिझाएँ।।
verses.translation: 'Therefore I remain hidden in the world — apart from Hari, I have no other purpose. The Lord knows everything without being told — what is gained by pleasing the world?'

verses.original:
तुम्ह सुचि सुमति परम प्रिय मोरें। प्रीति प्रतीति मोहि पर तोरें।।
अब जौं तात दुरावउँ तोही। दारुन दोष घटइ अति मोही।।
verses.translation: 'You are pure of heart, supremely dear to me; you have love and faith in me. If I now conceal anything from you, a terrible fault would attach to me.'

verses.original:
जिमि जिमि तापसु कथइ उदासा। तिमि तिमि नृपहि उपज बिस्वासा।।
देखा स्वबस कर्म मन बानी। तब बोला तापस बगध्यानी।।
verses.translation: The more the ascetic spoke of his indifference to the world, the more faith arose in the king's heart. Seeing the king completely under his spell in thought, word, and deed, the heron-like meditative impostor then spoke.

verses.original:
नाम हमार एकतनु भाई। सुनि नृप बोले पुनि सिरु नाई।।
कहहु नाम कर अरथ बखानी। मोहि सेवक अति आपन जानी।।
verses.translation: 'My name is Ekatanu, brother.' Hearing this, the king bowed his head and said: 'Please explain the meaning of your name, considering me your very own servant.'

verses.original:
आदिसृष्टि उपजी जबहिं तब उतपति भै मोरि।
नाम एकतनु हेतु तेहि देह न धरी बहोरि।।162।।
verses.translation: 'When the first creation arose, then was my birth. The name Ekatanu (one body) is because I have never taken another body since.'

verses.original:
जनि आचरुज करहु मन माहीं। सुत तप तें दुर्लभ कछु नाहीं।।
तपबल तें जग सृजइ बिधाता। तपबल बिष्नु भए परित्राता।।
verses.translation: 'Do not be amazed in your mind, son — nothing is impossible to obtain through austerity. By the power of austerity, Brahma creates the world; by the power of austerity, Vishnu became the preserver.'

verses.original:
तपबल संभु करहिं संघारा। तप तें अगम न कछु संसारा।।
भयउ नृपहि सुनि अति अनुरागा। कथा पुरातन कहै सो लागा।।
verses.translation: 'By the power of austerity, Shambhu destroys; nothing in the world is beyond the reach of penance.' The king became greatly enamored hearing this, and the sage began telling ancient stories.

verses.original:
करम धरम इतिहास अनेका। करइ निरूपन बिरति बिबेका।।
उदभव पालन प्रलय कहानी। कहेसि अमित आचरज बखानी।।
verses.translation: He narrated many tales of duty, karma, and history, and discoursed on renunciation and wisdom. He told stories of creation, preservation, and dissolution with infinite wonder.

verses.original:
सुनि महिप तापस बस भयऊ। आपन नाम कहत तब लयऊ।।
कह तापस नृप जानउँ तोही। कीन्हेहु कपट लाग भल मोही।।
verses.translation: Hearing all this, the king came under the ascetic's spell. Then the sage changed the subject. The sage said: 'King, I know who you are. You practiced deception — but I liked that.'

verses.original:
सुनु महीस असि नीति जहँ तहँ नाम न कहहिं नृप।
मोहि तोहि पर अति प्रीति सोइ चतुरता बिचारि तव।।163।।
verses.translation: 'Listen, O King, it is a wise policy that a king should not reveal his name everywhere. I have great affection for you — consider it your prudence that you concealed your identity.'

verses.original:
नाम तुम्हार प्रताप दिनेसा। सत्यकेतु तव पिता नरेसा।।
गुर प्रसाद सब जानिअ राजा। कहिअ न आपन जानि अकाजा।।
verses.translation: 'Your name is Pratapa Dinesa (sun of glory), and your father was King Satyaketu. O King, by the guru's grace one comes to know all things — do not consider it harmful that I know your secret.'

verses.original:
देखि तात तव सहज सुधाई। प्रीति प्रतीति नीति निपुनाई।।
उपजि परि ममता मन मोरें। कहउँ कथा निज पूछे तोरें।।
verses.translation: 'Seeing, dear son, your natural goodness, love, faith, and skill in conduct, affection has arisen in my heart. I have told you my story because you asked.'

verses.original:
अब प्रसन्न मैं संसय नाहीं। मागु जो भूप भाव मन माहीं।।
सुनि सुबचन भूपति हरषाना। गहि पद बिनय कीन्हि बिधि नाना।।
verses.translation: 'Now I am pleased, have no doubt — ask whatever you desire, O King.' Hearing these kind words, the king was overjoyed and grasped the sage's feet, entreating him in many ways.

verses.original:
कृपासिंधु मुनि दरसन तोरें। चारि पदारथ करतल मोरें।।
प्रभुहि तथापि प्रसन्न बिलोकी। मागि अगम बर होउँ असोकी।।
verses.translation: 'O ocean of mercy, O sage, by your sight, the four goals of life are in my grasp. Yet seeing the Lord so pleased, let me ask for an impossible boon and be free from sorrow.'

verses.original:
जरा मरन दुख रहित तनु समर जितै जनि कोउ।
एकछत्र रिपुहीन महि राज कलप सत होउ।।164।।
verses.translation: 'May my body be free from old age, death, and suffering; may no one defeat me in battle. May I reign as sole emperor over a rivalless earth for a hundred kalpas.'

verses.original:
कह तापस नृप ऐसेइ होऊ। कारन एक कठिन सुनु सोऊ।।
कालउ तुअ पद नाइहि सीसा। एक बिप्रकुल छाड़ि महीसा।।
verses.translation: The ascetic said: 'O King, so shall it be. But listen — there is one difficult condition. Even Death shall bow his head at your feet, O King — except from the curse of a Brahmin.'

verses.original:
तपबल बिप्र सदा बरिआरा। तिन्ह के कोप न कोउ रखवारा।।
जौं बिप्रन्ह सब करहु नरेसा। तौ तुअ बस बिधि बिष्नु महेसा।।
verses.translation: 'By the power of their austerity, Brahmins are always mighty; no one can protect you from their anger. But if you bring all Brahmins under your control, O King, then Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh themselves shall be in your power.'

verses.original:
चल न ब्रह्मकुल सन बरिआई। सत्य कहउँ दोउ भुजा उठाई।।
बिप्र श्राप बिनु सुनु महिपाला। तोर नास नहि कवनेहुँ काला।।
verses.translation: 'One cannot prevail against the Brahmin clan — I speak the truth, raising both arms. Listen, O King: without a Brahmin's curse, your destruction can never come by any means.'

verses.original:
हरषेउ राउ बचन सुनि तासू। नाथ न होइ मोर अब नासू।।
तव प्रसाद प्रभु कृपानिधाना। मो कहुँ सर्ब काल कल्याना।।
verses.translation: The king rejoiced hearing his words: 'Lord, now my destruction can never occur. By your grace, O merciful master, all times shall be auspicious for me.'

verses.original:
एवमस्तु कहि कपटमुनि बोला कुटिल बहोरि।
मिलब हमार भुलाब निज कहहु त हमहि न खोरि।।165।।
verses.translation: Saying 'So be it,' the deceitful sage spoke again with cunning: 'If you forget about our meeting, then do not blame me.'

verses.original:
तातें मै तोहि बरजउँ राजा। कहें कथा तव परम अकाजा।।
छठें श्रवन यह परत कहानी। नास तुम्हार सत्य मम बानी।।
verses.translation: 'Therefore I warn you, O King — if this tale is told, it would bring you supreme ruin. If this story reaches a sixth ear, your destruction is certain — my words are true.'

verses.original:
यह प्रगटें अथवा द्विजश्रापा। नास तोर सुनु भानुप्रतापा।।
आन उपायँ निधन तव नाहीं। जौं हरि हर कोपहिं मन माहीं।।
verses.translation: 'If this is revealed, or through a Brahmin's curse — listen, O Pratapabhanu — your destruction shall come. By no other means can your death occur, even if Hari and Hara become wrathful.'

verses.original:
सत्य नाथ पद गहि नृप भाषा। द्विज गुर कोप कहहु को राखा।।
राखइ गुर जौं कोप बिधाता। गुर बिरोध नहिं कोउ जग त्राता।।
verses.translation: Grasping the sage's feet, the king said truthfully: 'Tell me, who can save one from a Brahmin's or guru's wrath? If the guru is angry, even if the Creator protects, no savior exists; but if the guru is against you, no one in the world can protect you.'

verses.original:
जौं न चलब हम कहे तुम्हारें। होउ नास नहिं सोच हमारें।।
एकहिं डर डरपत मन मोरा। प्रभु महिदेव श्राप अति घोरा।।
verses.translation: 'If I do not follow your instructions, let me be destroyed — I care not. Only one fear haunts my mind — Lord, the terrible curse of the Brahmins.'

verses.original:
होहिं बिप्र बस कवन बिधि कहहु कृपा करि सोउ।
तुम्ह तजि दीनदयाल निज हितू न देखउँ कोउँ।।166।।
verses.translation: 'By what means can Brahmins be brought under control? Tell me, graciously. Other than you, O compassionate friend of the poor, I see no well-wisher.'

verses.original:
सुनु नृप बिबिध जतन जग माहीं। कष्टसाध्य पुनि होहिं कि नाहीं।।
अहइ एक अति सुगम उपाई। तहाँ परंतु एक कठिनाई।।
verses.translation: 'Listen, O King: there are many methods in the world, but they are difficult to accomplish and may or may not succeed. There is one very easy remedy, but it has one difficulty.'

verses.original:
मम आधीन जुगुति नृप सोई। मोर जाब तव नगर न होई।।
आजु लगें अरु जब तें भयऊँ। काहू के गृह ग्राम न गयऊँ।।
verses.translation: 'That device, O King, depends on me — but I cannot come to your city. From today, and ever since I have existed, I have never gone to anyone's house or village.'

verses.original:
जौं न जाउँ तव होइ अकाजू। बना आइ असमंजस आजू।।
सुनि महीस बोलेउ मृदु बानी। नाथ निगम असि नीति बखानी।।
verses.translation: 'Yet if I do not go, your purpose will fail. A dilemma has presented itself today.' The king spoke in gentle words: 'Lord, the Vedas proclaim this principle—'

verses.original:
बड़े सनेह लघुन्ह पर करहीं। गिरि निज सिरनि सदा तृन धरहीं।।
जलधि अगाध मौलि बह फेनू। संतत धरनि धरत सिर रेनू।।
verses.translation: 'The great always show kindness to the small. Mountains always bear grass on their peaks. The unfathomable ocean carries foam on its surface; the earth always bears dust upon its head.'

verses.original:
अस कहि गहे नरेस पद स्वामी होहु कृपाल।
मोहि लागि दुख सहिअ प्रभु सज्जन दीनदयाल।।167।।
verses.translation: So saying, the king grasped the sage's feet: 'Be gracious, O Master! For my sake, please endure this trouble, Lord — for the virtuous are compassionate to the humble.'

verses.original:
जानि नृपहि आपन आधीना। बोला तापस कपट प्रबीना।।
सत्य कहउँ भूपति सुनु तोही। जग नाहिन दुर्लभ कछु मोही।।
verses.translation: Knowing the king was completely under his control, the ascetic, skilled in deception, spoke: 'I tell you the truth, O King, listen — nothing in the world is impossible for me.'

verses.original:
अवसि काज मैं करिहउँ तोरा। मन तन बचन भगत तैं मोरा।।
जोग जुगुति तप मंत्र प्रभाऊ। फलइ तबहिं जब करिअ दुराऊ।।
verses.translation: 'I shall certainly accomplish your task — you are my devotee in mind, body, and speech. But yoga, technique, austerity, and the power of mantras bear fruit only when they are kept secret.'

verses.original:
जौं नरेस मैं करौं रसोई। तुम्ह परुसहु मोहि जान न कोई।।
अन्न सो जोइ जोइ भोजन करई। सोइ सोइ तव आयसु अनुसरई।।
verses.translation: 'If I, O King, prepare the food, and you serve it — with no one knowing about me — then whoever eats that food shall obey your every command.'

verses.original:
पुनि तिन्ह के गृह जेवँइ जोऊ। तव बस होइ भूप सुनु सोऊ।।
जाइ उपाय रचहु नृप एहू। संबत भरि संकलप करेहू।।
verses.translation: 'And further, whoever eats at their homes in turn shall also come under your control, O King. Go and arrange this plan — make a vow for an entire year.'

verses.original:
नित नूतन द्विज सहस सत बरेहु सहित परिवार।
मैं तुम्हरे संकलप लगि दिनहिंकरिब जेवनार।।168।।
verses.translation: 'Every day, invite a fresh hundred thousand Brahmins with their families. For the sake of your vow, I shall prepare the feast each day.'

verses.original:
एहि बिधि भूप कष्ट अति थोरें। होइहहिं सकल बिप्र बस तोरें।।
करिहहिं बिप्र होम मख सेवा। तेहिं प्रसंग सहजेहिं बस देवा।।
verses.translation: 'In this way, O King, with very little effort, all Brahmins shall come under your control. The Brahmins will perform fire-rituals, sacrifices, and worship — and through that, the gods will naturally come under your power too.'

verses.original:
और एक तोहि कहऊँ लखाऊ। मैं एहि बेष न आउब काऊ।।
तुम्हरे उपरोहित कहुँ राया। हरि आनब मैं करि निज माया।।
verses.translation: 'And one more thing I tell you as a hint: I shall not come in this guise ever. I shall bring away your family priest, O King, by my magical power.'

verses.original:
तपबल तेहि करि आपु समाना। रखिहउँ इहाँ बरष परवाना।।
मैं धरि तासु बेषु सुनु राजा। सब बिधि तोर सँवारब काजा।।
verses.translation: 'By the power of my austerity, I shall make him look like me and keep him here for a year. And I, assuming his appearance, O King, shall manage all your affairs in every way.'

verses.original:
गै निसि बहुत सयन अब कीजे। मोहि तोहि भूप भेंट दिन तीजे।।
मैं तपबल तोहि तुरग समेता। पहुँचेहउँ सोवतहि निकेता।।
verses.translation: 'Much of the night has passed — now sleep. O King, you and I shall meet on the third day. By my ascetic power, I shall transport you along with your horse to your palace while you sleep.'

verses.original:
मैं आउब सोइ बेषु धरि पहिचानेहु तब मोहि।
जब एकांत बोलाइ सब कथा सुनावौं तोहि।।169।।
verses.translation: 'I shall come in that same disguise; you will recognize me then. When you call me aside in private, I shall tell you everything.'

verses.original:
सयन कीन्ह नृप आयसु मानी। आसन जाइ बैठ छलग्यानी।।
श्रमित भूप निद्रा अति आई। सो किमि सोव सोच अधिकाई।।
verses.translation: The king obeyed and lay down to sleep; the cunning schemer sat on his seat. The weary king fell into deep slumber, but the other could not sleep due to his excessive scheming.

verses.original:
कालकेतु निसिचर तहँ आवा। जेहिं सूकर होइ नृपहि भुलावा।।
परम मित्र तापस नृप केरा। जानइ सो अति कपट घनेरा।।
verses.translation: Then came Kalanemi, the demon who had assumed the form of the boar that had led the king astray. He was the closest friend of the disguised king-turned-ascetic, and knew all his many intrigues.

verses.original:
तेहि के सत सुत अरु दस भाई। खल अति अजय देव दुखदाई।।
प्रथमहि भूप समर सब मारे। बिप्र संत सुर देखि दुखारे।।
verses.translation: He had a hundred sons and ten brothers — all wicked, invincible, and tormenting the gods. The king had earlier slain them all in battle, while Brahmins, saints, and gods looked on in distress.

verses.original:
तेहिं खल पाछिल बयरु सँभरा। तापस नृप मिलि मंत्र बिचारा।।
जेहि रिपु छय सोइ रचेन्हि उपाऊ। भावी बस न जान कछु राऊ।।
verses.translation: The wicked one recalled his old enmity; together with the ascetic-king, they hatched a plot. They devised a scheme to destroy their enemy — but the king, controlled by destiny, knew nothing of it.

verses.original:
रिपु तेजसी अकेल अपि लघु करि गनिअ न ताहु।
अजहुँ देत दुख रबि ससिहि सिर अवसेषित राहु।।170।।
verses.translation: An enemy should not be belittled even if he is powerful and alone. Even today, though reduced to just a head, Rahu continues to torment the sun and moon.

verses.original:
तापस नृप निज सखहि निहारी। हरषि मिलेउ उठि भयउ सुखारी।।
मित्रहि कहि सब कथा सुनाई। जातुधान बोला सुख पाई।।
verses.translation: The ascetic-king, seeing his friend, rose joyfully to embrace him. He told his friend the whole story, and the demon spoke with delight.

verses.original:
अब साधेउँ रिपु सुनहु नरेसा। जौं तुम्ह कीन्ह मोर उपदेसा।।
परिहरि सोच रहहु तुम्ह सोई। बिनु औषध बिआधि बिधि खोई।।
verses.translation: 'Now I have ensnared the enemy — listen, O King — if you have followed my advice. Set aside your worry and rest easy; without medicine, fate itself shall cure the disease.'

verses.original:
कुल समेत रिपु मूल बहाई। चौथे दिवस मिलब मैं आई।।
तापस नृपहि बहुत परितोषी। चला महाकपटी अतिरोषी।।
verses.translation: 'I shall uproot the enemy along with his entire clan. On the fourth day I shall return to meet you.' The demon greatly reassured the ascetic-king and departed — the great deceiver, filled with rage.

verses.original:
भानुप्रतापहि बाजि समेता। पहुँचाएसि छन माझ निकेता।।
नृपहि नारि पहिं सयन कराई। हयगृहँ बाँधेसि बाजि बनाई।।
verses.translation: He transported Pratapabhanu along with his horse to his palace in an instant. He put the king to sleep beside his queen and tied the horse carefully in the stable.

verses.original:
राजा के उपरोहितहि हरि लै गयउ बहोरि।
लै राखेसि गिरि खोह महुँ मायाँ करि मति भोरि।।171।।
verses.translation: Then he carried away the king's family priest, took him far off, and placed him in a mountain cave, confusing his mind with his magic.

verses.original:
आपु बिरचि उपरोहित रूपा। परेउ जाइ तेहि सेज अनूपा।।
जागेउ नृप अनभएँ बिहाना। देखि भवन अति अचरजु माना।।
verses.translation: Assuming the form of the family priest himself, the demon went and lay on that same splendid bed. The king awoke before dawn and was greatly amazed to find himself in his own palace.

verses.original:
मुनि महिमा मन महुँ अनुमानी। उठेउ गवँहि जेहि जान न रानी।।
कानन गयउ बाजि चढ़ि तेहीं। पुर नर नारि न जानेउ केहीं।।
verses.translation: Attributing it to the sage's miraculous power, he rose and left so quietly that even the queen did not know. He rode that same horse to the forest, and no man or woman in the city knew of his departure.

verses.original:
गएँ जाम जुग भूपति आवा। घर घर उत्सव बाज बधावा।।
उपरोहितहि देख जब राजा। चकित बिलोकि सुमिरि सोइ काजा।।
verses.translation: When two watches had passed, the king returned. Every home rang with festive music and celebrations. When the king saw his family priest, he was startled, remembering the whole scheme.

verses.original:
जुग सम नृपहि गए दिन तीनी। कपटी मुनि पद रह मति लीनी।।
समय जानि उपरोहित आवा। नृपहि मते सब कहि समुझावा।।
verses.translation: Those three days felt like ages to the king, whose mind was absorbed in thoughts of the deceitful sage. At the right time, the false priest came and explained the whole plan to the king.

verses.original:
नृप हरषेउ पहिचानि गुरु भ्रम बस रहा न चेत।
बरे तुरत सत सहस बर बिप्र कुटुंब समेत।।172।।
verses.translation: The king was overjoyed to recognize his so-called guru, but remained unaware due to his delusion. He immediately invited a hundred thousand fine Brahmins with their families.

verses.original:
उपरोहित जेवनार बनाई। छरस चारि बिधि जसि श्रुति गाई।।
मायामय तेहिं कीन्ह रसोई। बिंजन बहु गनि सकइ न कोई।।
verses.translation: The false priest prepared a feast — with six flavors and four kinds of food, as the scriptures prescribe. With his magic he prepared the meal; the dishes were so numerous that no one could count them.

verses.original:
बिबिध मृगन्ह कर आमिष राँधा। तेहि महुँ बिप्र माँसु खल साँधा।।
भोजन कहुँ सब बिप्र बोलाए। पद पखारि सादर बैठाए।।
verses.translation: He cooked the flesh of various animals, and among it the wicked one mixed the flesh of Brahmins. All the Brahmins were invited for the feast, their feet were washed, and they were seated with respect.

verses.original:
परुसन जबहिं लाग महिपाला। भै अकासबानी तेहि काला।।
बिप्रबृंद उठि उठि गृह जाहू। है बड़ि हानि अन्न जनि खाहू।।
verses.translation: Just as the king was about to serve the food, a voice came from the sky: 'O hosts of Brahmins, rise up and go to your homes! There is great harm — do not eat this food!'

verses.original:
भयउ रसोईं भूसुर माँसू। सब द्विज उठे मानि बिस्वासू।।
भूप बिकल मति मोहँ भुलानी। भावी बस आव मुख बानी।।
verses.translation: The food has been made with the flesh of Brahmins! All the Brahmins rose, believing the heavenly voice. The king was distressed, his mind bewildered by delusion — controlled by destiny, words came from his mouth unawares.

verses.original:
बोले बिप्र सकोप तब नहिं कछु कीन्ह बिचार।
जाइ निसाचर होहु नृप मूढ़ सहित परिवार।।173।।
verses.translation: Then the Brahmins spoke in anger without any further thought: 'Go, become a demon, O King — you fool — along with your entire family!'

verses.original:
छत्रबंधु तैं बिप्र बोलाई। घालै लिए सहित समुदाई।।
ईस्वर राखा धरम हमारा। जैहसि तैं समेत परिवारा।।
verses.translation: 'O degraded Kshatriya, you invited Brahmins and ruined them along with their families. God has protected our dharma. You shall perish along with your clan!'

verses.original:
संबत मध्य नास तव होऊ। जलदाता न रहिहि कुल कोऊ।।
नृप सुनि श्राप बिकल अति त्रासा। भै बहोरि बर गिरा अकासा।।
verses.translation: 'Within a year your destruction shall come, and no water-giver shall remain in your line.' Hearing the curse, the king trembled in extreme terror. Then again a noble voice came from the sky.

verses.original:
बिप्रहु श्राप बिचारि न दीन्हा। नहिं अपराध भूप कछु कीन्हा।।
चकित बिप्र सब सुनि नभबानी। भूप गयउ जहँ भोजन खानी।।
verses.translation: 'O Brahmins, you cursed without thinking — the king committed no offense.' All the Brahmins were startled hearing the heavenly voice. The king went to where the food was laid out.

verses.original:
तहँ न असन नहिं बिप्र सुआरा। फिरेउ राउ मन सोच अपारा।।
सब प्रसंग महिसुरन्ह सुनाई। त्रसित परेउ अवनीं अकुलाई।।
verses.translation: There was neither food nor any trace of the Brahmin cook. The king returned, his mind consumed with boundless anxiety. He told the whole story to the Brahmins, and in terror, fell prostrate on the ground in agony.

verses.original:
भूपति भावी मिटइ नहिं जदपि न दूषन तोर।
किएँ अन्यथा होइ नहिं बिप्रश्राप अति घोर।।174।।
verses.translation: 'O King, destiny cannot be averted, though the fault is not yours. What has been done cannot be undone — a Brahmin's curse is terribly potent.'

verses.original:
अस कहि सब महिदेव सिधाए। समाचार पुरलोगन्ह पाए।।
सोचहिं दूषन दैवहि देहीं। बिचरत हंस काग किय जेहीं।।
verses.translation: So saying, all the Brahmins departed. The townsfolk heard the news and grieved, blaming fate for the wrong — just as a swan is turned into a crow by the Creator.

verses.original:
उपरोहितहि भवन पहुँचाई। असुर तापसहि खबरि जनाई।।
तेहिं खल जहँ तहँ पत्र पठाए। सजि सजि सेन भूप सब धाए।।
verses.translation: After delivering the family priest back to his home, the demon informed the ascetic-king. That wicked one then sent messages far and wide, and kings everywhere mustered their armies and marched forth.

verses.original:
घेरेन्हि नगर निसान बजाई। बिबिध भाँति नित होई लराई।।
जूझे सकल सुभट करि करनी। बंधु समेत परेउ नृप धरनी।।
verses.translation: They besieged the city with drums beating; diverse battles raged day after day. All the brave warriors fought valiantly and fell; the king too fell along with his brother.

verses.original:
सत्यकेतु कुल कोउ नहिं बाँचा। बिप्रश्राप किमि होइ असाँचा।।
रिपु जिति सब नृप नगर बसाई। निज पुर गवने जय जसु पाई।।
verses.translation: Not one person of Satyaketu's line survived — for how can a Brahmin's curse prove false? Having defeated their foe, all the kings settled in the city, and then returned to their own cities with glory and triumph.

verses.original:
भरद्वाज सुनु जाहि जब होइ बिधाता बाम।
धूरि मेरुसम जनक जम ताहि ब्यालसम दाम।।।175।।
verses.translation: Listen, Bharadvaja — when fate turns hostile, dust becomes like Mount Meru, a protector becomes Death itself, and a garland becomes a serpent.

verses.original:
काल पाइ मुनि सुनु सोइ राजा। भयउ निसाचर सहित समाजा।।
दस सिर ताहि बीस भुजदंडा। रावन नाम बीर बरिबंडा।।
verses.translation: In due time, O sage, listen — that very king was reborn as a demon along with his retinue. He had ten heads and twenty arms — the mighty warrior named Ravana.

verses.original:
भूप अनुज अरिमर्दन नामा। भयउ सो कुंभकरन बलधामा।।
सचिव जो रहा धरमरुचि जासू। भयउ बिमात्र बंधु लघु तासू।।
verses.translation: The king's younger brother Arimardana became Kumbhakarna, an abode of strength. The minister who was named Dharmaruchi became the younger half-brother of Ravana.

verses.original:
नाम बिभीषन जेहि जग जाना। बिष्नुभगत बिग्यान निधाना।।
रहे जे सुत सेवक नृप केरे। भए निसाचर घोर घनेरे।।
verses.translation: He was known to the world as Vibhishana — a devotee of Vishnu and a treasure-house of wisdom. The sons, servants, and attendants of the former king became numerous and terrible demons.

verses.original:
कामरूप खल जिनस अनेका। कुटिल भयंकर बिगत बिबेका।।
कृपा रहित हिंसक सब पापी। बरनि न जाहिं बिस्व परितापी।।
verses.translation: Shape-shifters, wicked, of various species, crooked, fearful, devoid of discrimination — merciless, violent, all sinful — they cannot be described, those tormentors of the world.

verses.original:
उपजे जदपि पुलस्त्यकुल पावन अमल अनूप।
तदपि महीसुर श्राप बस भए सकल अघरूप।।176।।
verses.translation: Though born in the sacred and pure clan of Pulastya, by the force of the Brahmin's curse they all became embodiments of sin.

verses.original:
कीन्ह बिबिध तप तीनिहुँ भाई। परम उग्र नहिं बरनि सो जाई।।
गयउ निकट तप देखि बिधाता। मागहु बर प्रसन्न मैं ताता।।
verses.translation: All three brothers performed diverse and supremely fierce austerities that cannot be described. Seeing their penance, the Creator himself came near and said: 'I am pleased, dear sons — ask for a boon.'

verses.original:
करि बिनती पद गहि दससीसा। बोलेउ बचन सुनहु जगदीसा।।
हम काहू के मरहिं न मारें। बानर मनुज जाति दुइ बारें।।
verses.translation: With humble entreaty, Ravana grasped Brahma's feet and said: 'Listen, O Lord of the universe — let me not be slain by anyone, except perhaps by monkeys and humans, whom I count as nothing.'

verses.original:
एवमस्तु तुम्ह बड़ तप कीन्हा। मैं ब्रह्माँ मिलि तेहि बर दीन्हा।।
पुनि प्रभु कुंभकरन पहिं गयऊ। तेहि बिलोकि मन बिसमय भयऊ।।
verses.translation: 'So be it — you have performed great austerity.' Brahma gave him the boon. Then the Lord went to Kumbhakarna and was dismayed to see him. 'If this wretch eats daily, the whole world will be laid waste.'

verses.original:
जौं एहिं खल नित करब अहारू। होइहि सब उजारि संसारू।।
सारद प्रेरि तासु मति फेरी। मागेसि नीद मास षट केरी।।
verses.translation: He prompted Saraswati to confuse Kumbhakarna's mind, and so he asked for six months of sleep at a time.

verses.original:
गए बिभीषन पास पुनि कहेउ पुत्र बर मागु।
तेहिं मागेउ भगवंत पद कमल अमल अनुरागु।।177।।
verses.translation: Then Brahma went to Vibhishana and said: 'Ask for a boon, my son.' He asked for unwavering and pure devotion to the lotus feet of the Lord.

verses.original:
तिन्हि देइ बर ब्रह्म सिधाए। हरषित ते अपने गृह आए।।
मय तनुजा मंदोदरि नामा। परम सुंदरी नारि ललामा।।
verses.translation: Granting them all boons, Brahma departed, and they returned happily to their homes. Maya's daughter, named Mandodari, was the most beautiful jewel among women.

verses.original:
सोइ मयँ दीन्हि रावनहि आनी। होइहि जातुधानपति जानी।।
हरषित भयउ नारि भलि पाई। पुनि दोउ बंधु बिआहेसि जाई।।
verses.translation: Maya gave her in marriage to Ravana, knowing he would become the lord of the demons. Ravana rejoiced at obtaining such a fine wife, and then married off both his brothers as well.

verses.original:
गिरि त्रिकूट एक सिंधु मझारी। बिधि निर्मित दुर्गम अति भारी।।
सोइ मय दानवँ बहुरि सँवारा। कनक रचित मनिभवन अपारा।।
verses.translation: In the middle of the ocean stood a three-peaked mountain — a mighty fortress built by the Creator, exceedingly difficult to access. The demon Maya had further embellished it with countless golden palaces studded with gems.

verses.original:
भोगावति जसि अहिकुल बासा। अमरावति जसि सक्रनिवासा।।
तिन्ह तें अधिक रम्य अति बंका। जग बिख्यात नाम तेहि लंका।।
verses.translation: Like Bhogavati, the abode of serpents, and like Amaravati, the abode of Indra — even more beautiful and impregnable than these was Lanka, famed throughout the world.

verses.original:
खाईं सिंधु गभीर अति चारिहुँ दिसि फिरि आव।
कनक कोट मनि खचित दृढ़ बरनि न जाइ बनाव।।178(क)।।
हरिप्रेरित जेहिं कलप जोइ जातुधानपति होइ।
सूर प्रतापी अतुलबल दल समेत बस सोइ।।178(ख)।।
verses.translation: Around it flowed an exceedingly deep ocean moat on all four sides. Its golden ramparts were studded with gems — their beauty is beyond description. In each cosmic cycle, whoever is destined to be the lord of demons, being valorous, mighty, and of incomparable strength, occupies it with his army.

verses.original:
रहे तहाँ निसिचर भट भारे। ते सब सुरन्ह समर संघारे।।
अब तहँ रहहिं सक्र के प्रेरे। रच्छक कोटि जच्छपति केरे।।
verses.translation: Great demon warriors had lived there; they were all slain by the gods in battle. Now, at Indra's command, millions of Kubera's Yaksha guards dwelt there as protectors.

verses.original:
दसमुख कतहुँ खबरि असि पाई। सेन साजि गढ़ घेरेसि जाई।।
देखि बिकट भट बड़ि कटकाई। जच्छ जीव लै गए पराई।।
verses.translation: Ravana somehow received this intelligence and, marshalling his army, went and besieged the fortress. Seeing the terrible warriors and the huge army, the Yakshas fled for their lives.

verses.original:
फिरि सब नगर दसानन देखा। गयउ सोच सुख भयउ बिसेषा।।
सुंदर सहज अगम अनुमानी। कीन्हि तहाँ रावन रजधानी।।
verses.translation: Ravana inspected the entire city; his worries vanished and he felt great joy. Judging it to be naturally beautiful and impregnable, Ravana made it his capital.

verses.original:
जेहि जस जोग बाँटि गृह दीन्हे। सुखी सकल रजनीचर कीन्हे।।
एक बार कुबेर पर धावा। पुष्पक जान जीति लै आवा।।
verses.translation: He distributed houses to all according to their rank and made all the demons content. Once he attacked Kubera and captured the Pushpaka flying chariot in victory.

verses.original:
कौतुकहीं कैलास पुनि लीन्हेसि जाइ उठाइ।
मनहुँ तौलि निज बाहुबल चला बहुत सुख पाइ।।179।।
verses.translation: For sport, he went and lifted Mount Kailasa itself, as if to weigh his own arm-strength, and departed greatly pleased.

verses.original:
सुख संपति सुत सेन सहाई। जय प्रताप बल बुद्धि बड़ाई।।
नित नूतन सब बाढ़त जाई। जिमि प्रतिलाभ लोभ अधिकाई।।
verses.translation: Happiness, wealth, sons, armies, allies, victory, glory, strength, and intellect — all kept growing day by day, just as greed increases with every gain.

verses.original:
अतिबल कुंभकरन अस भ्राता। जेहि कहुँ नहिं प्रतिभट जग जाता।।
करइ पान सोवइ षट मासा। जागत होइ तिहुँ पुर त्रासा।।
verses.translation: He had a supremely powerful brother, Kumbhakarna, who had no rival in all the world. He would drink and sleep for six months; when he woke, all three worlds trembled in fear.

verses.original:
जौं दिन प्रति अहार कर सोई। बिस्व बेगि सब चौपट होई।।
समर धीर नहिं जाइ बखाना। तेहि सम अमित बीर बलवाना।।
verses.translation: If he were to eat daily, the whole world would quickly be destroyed. His courage in battle was beyond description; countless warriors of equal valor served under him.

verses.original:
बारिदनाद जेठ सुत तासू। भट महुँ प्रथम लीक जग जासू।।
जेहि न होइ रन सनमुख कोई। सुरपुर नितहिं परावन होई।।
verses.translation: Meghnad was Ravana's eldest son, renowned as the foremost among warriors in the world. None could stand before him in battle; the city of the gods was always under threat of invasion.

verses.original:
कुमुख अकंपन कुलिसरद धूमकेतु अतिकाय।
एक एक जग जीति सक ऐसे सुभट निकाय।।180।।
verses.translation: Kumukha, Akampana, Kulishrada, Dhumaketu, and Atikaya — each one alone could conquer the world. Such was his host of mighty warriors.

verses.original:
कामरूप जानहिं सब माया। सपनेहुँ जिन्ह कें धरम न दाया।।
दसमुख बैठ सभाँ एक बारा। देखि अमित आपन परिवारा।।
verses.translation: They could assume any form at will and knew all magic — beings who had no trace of righteousness or compassion even in their dreams. Once, the ten-headed Ravana sat in his court, surveying his vast family.

verses.original:
सुत समूह जन परिजन नाती। गे को पार निसाचर जाती।।
सेन बिलोकि सहज अभिमानी। बोला बचन क्रोध मद सानी।।
verses.translation: His sons, multitudes of servants, kinsmen, and grandchildren — who could count the endless demon race? Surveying his army, the naturally arrogant one spoke words steeped in anger and pride.

verses.original:
सुनहु सकल रजनीचर जूथा। हमरे बैरी बिबुध बरूथा।।
ते सनमुख नहिं करही लराई। देखि सबल रिपु जाहिं पराई।।
verses.translation: 'Listen, all you hosts of demons! The gods are our enemies, but they dare not fight us face to face — seeing their powerful foe, they flee away.'

verses.original:
तेन्ह कर मरन एक बिधि होई। कहउँ बुझाइ सुनहु अब सोई।।
द्विजभोजन मख होम सराधा।।सब कै जाइ करहु तुम्ह बाधा।।
verses.translation: 'There is but one way to destroy them, and I shall explain it — listen now. Wherever Brahmins are being fed, wherever sacrifices, fire-rituals, or shraddhas are being performed — go and obstruct them all.'

verses.original:
छुधा छीन बलहीन सुर सहजेहिं मिलिहहिं आइ।
तब मारिहउँ कि छाड़िहउँ भली भाँति अपनाइ।।181।।
verses.translation: 'Starved and weakened, the gods will come and surrender of their own accord. Then I shall decide whether to kill them or, showing them kindness, make them my servants.'

verses.original:
मेघनाद कहुँ पुनि हँकरावा। दीन्ही सिख बलु बयरु बढ़ावा।।
जे सुर समर धीर बलवाना। जिन्ह कें लरिबे कर अभिमाना।।
verses.translation: He then summoned Meghanada and instructed him, inflaming his valor and enmity: 'Those gods who are brave and powerful in battle, who pride themselves on their fighting prowess—'

verses.original:
तिन्हहि जीति रन आनेसु बाँधी। उठि सुत पितु अनुसासन काँधी।।
एहि बिधि सबही अग्या दीन्ही। आपुनु चलेउ गदा कर लीन्ही।।
verses.translation: 'Defeat them in combat and bring them bound as captives!' The son rose and shouldered his father's command. In this manner he gave orders to all, and himself set out with mace in hand.

verses.original:
चलत दसानन डोलति अवनी। गर्जत गर्भ स्त्रवहिं सुर रवनी।।
रावन आवत सुनेउ सकोहा। देवन्ह तके मेरु गिरि खोहा।।
verses.translation: As Ravana marched, the earth trembled; he roared so fiercely that the celestial women miscarried. Hearing of Ravana's furious advance, the gods hid in the caves of Mount Meru.

verses.original:
दिगपालन्ह के लोक सुहाए। सूने सकल दसानन पाए।।
पुनि पुनि सिंघनाद करि भारी। देइ देवतन्ह गारि पचारी।।
verses.translation: He found the beautiful realms of all the guardians of the directions lying deserted. Again and again he roared like a lion and challenged the gods with abuse.

verses.original:
रन मद मत्त फिरइ जग धावा। प्रतिभट खौजत कतहुँ न पावा।।
रबि ससि पवन बरुन धनधारी। अगिनि काल जम सब अधिकारी।।
verses.translation: Drunk with battle-frenzy, he roamed throughout the universe seeking a worthy opponent but finding none. The Sun, Moon, Wind-god, Varuna, Kubera, Agni, Time, Yama — all the rulers —

verses.original:
किंनर सिद्ध मनुज सुर नागा। हठि सबही के पंथहिं लागा।।
ब्रह्मसृष्टि जहँ लगि तनुधारी। दसमुख बसबर्ती नर नारी।।
verses.translation: Kinnaras, Siddhas, humans, gods, and serpents — he confronted them all stubbornly. Every embodied being in Brahma's creation — men and women — came under Ravana's sway.

verses.original: आयसु करहिं सकल भयभीता। नवहिं आइ नित चरन बिनीता।।
verses.translation: All obeyed his commands in fear, and came daily to bow humbly at his feet.

verses.original:
भुजबल बिस्व बस्य करि राखेसि कोउ न सुतंत्र।
मंडलीक मनि रावन राज करइ निज मंत्र।।182(क)।।
देव जच्छ गंधर्व नर किंनर नाग कुमारि।
जीति बरीं निज बाहुबल बहु सुंदर बर नारि।।182ख।।
verses.translation: Having conquered the world by the strength of his arms, he left none independent. The crest-jewel among emperors, Ravana ruled by his own counsel. Princesses of gods, Yakshas, Gandharvas, humans, Kinnaras, and Nagas — he conquered and took many beautiful and noble women by the force of his arms.

verses.original:
इंद्रजीत सन जो कछु कहेऊ। सो सब जनु पहिलेहिं करि रहेऊ।।
प्रथमहिं जिन्ह कहुँ आयसु दीन्हा। तिन्ह कर चरित सुनहु जो कीन्हा।।
verses.translation: Whatever Ravana had commanded Indrajit, he had already accomplished long before. Now hear what those to whom he gave orders first actually did.

verses.original:
देखत भीमरूप सब पापी। निसिचर निकर देव परितापी।।
करहि उपद्रव असुर निकाया। नाना रूप धरहिं करि माया।।
verses.translation: All those sinful demons, terrible in appearance — hosts of night-wanderers, tormentors of the gods — they caused havoc everywhere, assuming various forms through their magical powers.

verses.original:
जेहि बिधि होइ धर्म निर्मूला। सो सब करहिं बेद प्रतिकूला।।
जेहिं जेहिं देस धेनु द्विज पावहिं। नगर गाउँ पुर आगि लगावहिं।।
verses.translation: Whatever would uproot dharma, they did everything contrary to the Vedas. Wherever they found cows and Brahmins, they set fire to cities, villages, and towns.

verses.original:
सुभ आचरन कतहुँ नहिं होई। देव बिप्र गुरू मान न कोई।।
नहिं हरिभगति जग्य तप ग्याना। सपनेहुँ सुनिअ न बेद पुराना।।
verses.translation: Virtuous conduct ceased everywhere; no one honored gods, Brahmins, or gurus. There was no devotion to Hari, no sacrifices, austerity, or knowledge; the Vedas and Puranas could not even be heard in dreams.

verses.original:
जप जोग बिरागा तप मख भागा श्रवन सुनइ दससीसा।
आपुनु उठि धावइ रहै न पावइ धरि सब घालइ खीसा।।
अस भ्रष्ट अचारा भा संसारा धर्म सुनिअ नहि काना।
तेहि बहुबिधि त्रासइ देस निकासइ जो कह बेद पुराना।।
verses.translation: If Ravana heard of anyone practicing prayer, yoga, renunciation, austerity, or sacrifice, he would rise and rush there himself, and finding them, would destroy them in rage. Such corruption spread throughout the world that dharma could not even be heard of. In many ways he would terrorize and exile anyone who spoke of the Vedas and Puranas.

verses.original:
बरनि न जाइ अनीति घोर निसाचर जो करहिं।
हिंसा पर अति प्रीति तिन्ह के पापहि कवनि मिति।।183।।
verses.translation: The terrible injustices committed by the demons cannot be described. Their supreme delight was in violence — what limit could there be to their sins?

verses.original:
बाढ़े खल बहु चोर जुआरा। जे लंपट परधन परदारा।।
मानहिं मातु पिता नहिं देवा। साधुन्ह सन करवावहिं सेवा।।
verses.translation: Wicked men multiplied — thieves, gamblers, those who coveted others' wealth and others' wives. They showed no respect to mother, father, or gods, and forced even saints to serve them.

verses.original:
जिन्ह के यह आचरन भवानी। ते जानेहु निसिचर सब प्रानी।।
अतिसय देखि धर्म कै ग्लानी। परम सभीत धरा अकुलानी।।
verses.translation: Know, Bhavani, that all beings who act in this manner are verily demons. Seeing the extreme decline of dharma, the Earth became supremely frightened and distressed.

verses.original:
गिरि सरि सिंधु भार नहिं मोही। जस मोहि गरुअ एक परद्रोही।।
सकल धर्म देखइ बिपरीता। कहि न सकइ रावन भय भीता।।
verses.translation: 'Mountains, rivers, and oceans are no burden to me — what weighs on me most heavily is the malice of evildoers. Dharma is perverted in every way — but I cannot speak, terrified of Ravana.'

verses.original:
धेनु रूप धरि हृदयँ बिचारी। गई तहाँ जहँ सुर मुनि झारी।।
निज संताप सुनाएसि रोई। काहू तें कछु काज न होई।।
verses.translation: Taking the form of a cow, she went, pondering in her heart, to the place where gods and sages had gathered. Weeping, she told them of her suffering — but no one could do anything to help.

verses.original:
सुर मुनि गंधर्बा मिलि करि सर्बा गे बिरंचि के लोका।
सँग गोतनुधारी भूमि बिचारी परम बिकल भय सोका।।
ब्रह्माँ सब जाना मन अनुमाना मोर कछू न बसाई।
जा करि तैं दासी सो अबिनासी हमरेउ तोर सहाई।।
verses.translation: The gods, sages, and Gandharvas all assembled together and went to Brahma's realm. The Earth too went along in her cow form, utterly distressed with fear and sorrow. Brahma, knowing and considering everything in his mind, said: 'I have no power in this. The one whose servant you are, O Earth — that imperishable Lord is the helper of both you and us.'

verses.original:
धरनि धरहि मन धीर कह बिरंचि हरिपद सुमिरु।
जानत जन की पीर प्रभु भंजिहि दारुन बिपति।।184।।
verses.translation: 'O Earth, take heart and be firm,' said Brahma. 'Meditate on the feet of Hari. The Lord knows the pain of His devotees and will surely destroy this terrible calamity.'

verses.original:
बैठे सुर सब करहिं बिचारा। कहँ पाइअ प्रभु करिअ पुकारा।।
पुर बैकुंठ जान कह कोई। कोउ कह पयनिधि बस प्रभु सोई।।
verses.translation: The gods sat deliberating: 'Where can we find the Lord to make our appeal? Some said to go to Vaikuntha; others said the Lord dwells in the Ocean of Milk.'

verses.original:
जाके हृदयँ भगति जसि प्रीति। प्रभु तहँ प्रगट सदा तेहिं रीती।।
तेहि समाज गिरिजा मैं रहेऊँ। अवसर पाइ बचन एक कहेऊँ।।
verses.translation: 'Whoever has whatever devotion and love in their heart — the Lord manifests there, always, in that manner.' At that gathering, O Girija, I was present. Finding the right moment, I spoke one word:

verses.original:
हरि ब्यापक सर्बत्र समाना। प्रेम तें प्रगट होहिं मैं जाना।।
देस काल दिसि बिदिसिहु माहीं। कहहु सो कहाँ जहाँ प्रभु नाहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Hari is all-pervading, equally present everywhere; through love alone He manifests — this I know. In every direction, every quarter, and every place — tell me, where is the Lord not?'

verses.original:
अग जगमय सब रहित बिरागी। प्रेम तें प्रभु प्रगटइ जिमि आगी।।
मोर बचन सब के मन माना। साधु साधु करि ब्रह्म बखाना।।
verses.translation: 'The animate and inanimate are all pervaded by Him, yet He remains detached. Through love, the Lord manifests, just as fire emerges from within.' My words were accepted by all; Brahma praised them saying 'Well said, well said!'

verses.original:
सुनि बिरंचि मन हरष तन पुलकि नयन बह नीर।
अस्तुति करत जोरि कर सावधान मतिधीर।।185।।
verses.translation: Hearing this, joy arose in Brahma's heart; his body thrilled and tears flowed from his eyes. Joining his palms, the firm-minded one began to offer praise with full attention.

verses.original:
जय जय सुरनायक जन सुखदायक प्रनतपाल भगवंता।
गो द्विज हितकारी जय असुरारी सिधुंसुता प्रिय कंता।।
पालन सुर धरनी अद्भुत करनी मरम न जानइ कोई।
जो सहज कृपाला दीनदयाला करउ अनुग्रह सोई।।
जय जय अबिनासी  सब घट बासी ब्यापक परमानंदा।
अबिगत गोतीतं चरित पुनीतं मायारहित मुकुंदा।।
जेहि लागि बिरागी अति अनुरागी बिगतमोह मुनिबृंदा।
निसि बासर ध्यावहिं गुन गन गावहिं जयति सच्चिदानंदा।।
जेहिं सृष्टि उपाई त्रिबिध बनाई संग सहाय न दूजा।
सो करउ अघारी चिंत हमारी जानिअ भगति न पूजा।।
जो भव भय भंजन मुनि मन रंजन गंजन बिपति बरूथा।
मन बच क्रम बानी छाड़ि सयानी सरन सकल सुर जूथा।।
सारद श्रुति सेषा रिषय असेषा जा कहुँ कोउ नहि जाना।
जेहि दीन पिआरे बेद पुकारे द्रवउ सो श्रीभगवाना।।
भव बारिधि मंदर सब बिधि सुंदर गुनमंदिर सुखपुंजा।
मुनि सिद्ध सकल सुर परम भयातुर नमत नाथ पद कंजा।।
verses.translation: Glory, glory to the Lord of gods, giver of joy to devotees, protector of the humble, O Lord! Benefactor of cows and Brahmins, glory to the slayer of demons, beloved husband of Lakshmi! Protector and sustainer of gods and earth, performer of wondrous deeds whose mystery none can know — may that naturally compassionate, merciful-to-the-meek Lord bestow His grace! Glory, glory to the imperishable, all-pervading, infinite Lord!

verses.original:
जानि सभय सुरभूमि सुनि बचन समेत सनेह।
गगनगिरा गंभीर भइ हरनि सोक संदेह।।186।।
verses.translation: Knowing the gods and Earth to be afraid, and hearing their words full of love, a deep voice resounded from the sky, dispelling all grief and doubt.

verses.original:
जनि डरपहु मुनि सिद्ध सुरेसा। तुम्हहि लागि धरिहउँ नर बेसा।।
अंसन्ह सहित मनुज अवतारा। लेहउँ दिनकर बंस उदारा।।
verses.translation: 'Fear not, O sages, Siddhas, and gods — for your sake I shall assume a human form. Along with My partial incarnations, I shall take a human birth in the illustrious Solar dynasty.'

verses.original:
कस्यप अदिति महातप कीन्हा। तिन्ह कहुँ मैं पूरब बर दीन्हा।।
ते दसरथ कौसल्या रूपा। कोसलपुरीं प्रगट नरभूपा।।
verses.translation: 'Kashyapa and Aditi performed great austerities; to them I had previously given a boon. They have now taken birth as Dasharatha and Kausalya, manifest as king and queen in the city of Kosala.'

verses.original:
तिन्ह के गृह अवतरिहउँ जाई। रघुकुल तिलक सो चारिउ भाई।।
नारद बचन सत्य सब करिहउँ। परम सक्ति समेत अवतरिहउँ।।
verses.translation: 'I shall go and incarnate in their home — four brothers, the ornament of the Raghu dynasty. I shall make Narada's words come true and shall incarnate with My supreme power.'

verses.original:
हरिहउँ सकल भूमि गरुआई। निर्भय होहु देव समुदाई।।
गगन ब्रह्मबानी सुनी काना। तुरत फिरे सुर हृदय जुड़ाना।।
verses.translation: 'I shall remove all the burden of the Earth; be fearless, O host of gods.' The gods heard the divine voice of Brahma from the sky, and immediately returned with hearts comforted and cooled.

verses.original: तब ब्रह्मा धरनिहि समुझावा। अभय भई भरोस जियँ आवा।।
verses.translation: Then Brahma reassured the Earth; she became fearless and took comfort in her heart.

verses.original:
निज लोकहि बिरंचि गे देवन्ह इहइ सिखाइ।
बानर तनु धरि धरि महि हरि पद सेवहु जाइ।।187।।
verses.translation: Brahma returned to his own realm, having instructed the gods thus: 'Take the form of monkeys and go serve the feet of Hari on the earth.'

verses.original:
गए देव सब निज निज धामा। भूमि सहित मन कहुँ बिश्रामा ।
जो कछु आयसु ब्रह्माँ दीन्हा। हरषे देव बिलंब न कीन्हा।।
verses.translation: All the gods went to their respective abodes; the Earth too found peace of mind. Whatever instructions Brahma had given, the gods were delighted and did not delay.

verses.original:
बनचर देह धरि छिति माहीं। अतुलित बल प्रताप तिन्ह पाहीं।।
गिरि तरु नख आयुध सब बीरा। हरि मारग चितवहिं मतिधीरा।।
verses.translation: Taking the forms of forest-dwellers on the earth, they possessed incomparable strength and valor. With mountains, trees, and claws as their weapons, all these warriors fixed their eyes on Hari's path with firm minds.

verses.original:
गिरि कानन जहँ तहँ भरि पूरी। रहे निज निज अनीक रचि रूरी।।
यह सब रुचिर चरित मैं भाषा। अब सो सुनहु जो बीचहिं राखा।।
verses.translation: They filled the mountains and forests everywhere, forming their own beautiful armies. I have narrated all these delightful events — now listen to what I had set aside in the middle.

verses.original:
अवधपुरीं रघुकुलमनि राऊ। बेद बिदित तेहि दसरथ नाऊँ।।
धरम धुरंधर गुननिधि ग्यानी। हृदयँ भगति मति सारँगपानी।।
verses.translation: In the city of Ayodhya, there was a jewel of the Raghu dynasty — a king whose name was Dasharatha, known to the Vedas. He was a champion of dharma, a treasure of virtues, wise, and devoted in his heart to Lord Vishnu.

verses.original:
कौसल्यादि नारि प्रिय सब आचरन पुनीत।
पति अनुकूल प्रेम दृढ़ हरि पद कमल बिनीत।।188।।
verses.translation: Kausalya and his other dear wives were all of pure conduct, devoted to their husband, firm in love, and humble at the lotus feet of Hari.

verses.original:
एक बार भूपति मन माहीं। भै गलानि मोरें सुत नाहीं।।
गुर गृह गयउ तुरत महिपाला। चरन लागि करि बिनय बिसाला।।
verses.translation: Once the king felt sorrow in his heart that he had no sons. The king immediately went to his guru's house, touched his feet, and made humble entreaty at length.

verses.original:
निज दुख सुख सब गुरहि सुनायउ। कहि बसिष्ठ बहुबिधि समुझायउ।।
धरहु धीर होइहहिं सुत चारी। त्रिभुवन बिदित भगत भय हारी।।
verses.translation: He told his guru all his sorrows and joys. Vasishtha comforted him in many ways: 'Be patient — you shall have four sons, famed throughout the three worlds, who shall dispel the fear of devotees.'

verses.original:
सृंगी रिषहि बसिष्ठ बोलावा। पुत्रकाम सुभ जग्य करावा।।
भगति सहित मुनि आहुति दीन्हें। प्रगटे अगिनि चरू कर लीन्हें।।
verses.translation: Vasishtha summoned the sage Shringi and had him perform the auspicious Putrakameshti sacrifice (fire sacrifice for sons). When the sage offered oblations with devotion, the Fire-god appeared holding the sacred offering in his hands.

verses.original:
जो बसिष्ठ कछु हृदयँ बिचारा। सकल काजु भा सिद्ध तुम्हारा।।
यह हबि बाँटि देहु नृप जाई। जथा जोग जेहि भाग बनाई।।
verses.translation: 'Whatever Vasishtha conceived in his heart — O King, your entire purpose has been accomplished. Distribute this sacred food and give it to the queens, apportioning each her rightful share.'

verses.original:
तब अदृस्य भए पावक सकल सभहि समुझाइ।।
परमानंद मगन नृप हरष न हृदयँ समाइ।।189।।
verses.translation: Then the Fire-god became invisible, having explained everything to the assembly. The king was immersed in supreme bliss; his heart could not contain his joy.

verses.original:
तबहिं रायँ प्रिय नारि बोलाईं। कौसल्यादि तहाँ चलि आई।।
अर्ध भाग कौसल्याहि दीन्हा। उभय भाग आधे कर कीन्हा।।
verses.translation: The king then summoned his beloved wives; Kausalya and the others came. He gave half the sacred food to Kausalya and divided the remaining half into two equal portions.

verses.original:
कैकेई कहँ नृप सो दयऊ। रह्यो सो उभय भाग पुनि भयऊ।।
कौसल्या कैकेई हाथ धरि। दीन्ह सुमित्रहि मन प्रसन्न करि।।
verses.translation: He gave one portion to Kaikeyi; the remaining portion was again divided into two. Taking the hands of Kausalya and Kaikeyi, he gave both parts to Sumitra with a joyful heart.

verses.original:
एहि बिधि गर्भसहित सब नारी। भईं हृदयँ हरषित सुख भारी।।
जा दिन तें हरि गर्भहिं आए। सकल लोक सुख संपति छाए।।
verses.translation: Thus all the queens conceived, and their hearts were filled with joy and delight. From the day the Lord entered the womb, happiness and prosperity spread throughout all the worlds.

verses.original:
मंदिर महँ सब राजहिं रानी। सोभा सील तेज की खानीं।।
सुख जुत कछुक काल चलि गयऊ। जेहिं प्रभु प्रगट सो अवसर भयऊ।।
verses.translation: Within the palace, all the queens shone with glory — mines of beauty, virtue, and radiance. Some happy time passed, and then the moment arrived for the Lord to manifest.

verses.original:
जोग लगन ग्रह बार तिथि सकल भए अनुकूल।
चर अरु अचर हर्षजुत राम जनम सुखमूल।।190।।
verses.translation: The astrological conjunction, the auspicious planet, the day, and the lunar date — all became favorable. All animate and inanimate beings were filled with joy — the root of all happiness, the birth of Rama, was at hand.

verses.original:
नौमी तिथि मधु मास पुनीता। सुकल पच्छ अभिजित हरिप्रीता।।
मध्यदिवस अति सीत न घामा। पावन काल लोक बिश्रामा।।
verses.translation: It was the sacred ninth day of the bright fortnight in the month of Chaitra, under the beloved Abhijit constellation of Hari. It was midday — neither too cold nor too hot — a holy time that gives peace to the world.

verses.original:
सीतल मंद सुरभि बह बाऊ। हरषित सुर संतन मन चाऊ।।
बन कुसुमित गिरिगन मनिआरा। स्त्रवहिं सकल सरिताऽमृतधारा।।
verses.translation: A cool, gentle, and fragrant breeze was blowing; the gods and saints were delighted at heart. The forests blossomed, the mountains shone like jewels, and all the rivers flowed with streams of nectar.

verses.original:
सो अवसर बिरंचि जब जाना। चले सकल सुर साजि बिमाना।।
गगन बिमल सकुल सुर जूथा। गावहिं गुन गंधर्ब बरूथा।।
verses.translation: When Brahma perceived that auspicious moment, all the gods set forth in their celestial chariots. The sky was clear and filled with hosts of gods; troops of Gandharvas sang divine praises.

verses.original:
बरषहिं सुमन सुअंजलि साजी। गहगहि गगन दुंदुभी बाजी।।
अस्तुति करहिं नाग मुनि देवा। बहुबिधि लावहिं निज निज सेवा।।
verses.translation: They showered flowers with beautiful handfuls; drums resounded mightily in the sky. Nagas, sages, and gods offered praise, and all rendered their own particular service in many ways.

verses.original:
सुर समूह बिनती करि पहुँचे निज निज धाम।
जगनिवास प्रभु प्रगटे अखिल लोक बिश्राम।।191।।
verses.translation: The hosts of gods made their supplications and returned to their respective abodes. Then the Lord of the universe manifested — He who is the repose of all the worlds.

verses.original:
भए प्रगट कृपाला दीनदयाला कौसल्या हितकारी।
हरषित महतारी मुनि मन हारी अद्भुत रूप बिचारी।।
लोचन अभिरामा तनु घनस्यामा निज आयुध भुज चारी।
भूषन बनमाला नयन बिसाला सोभासिंधु खरारी।।
कह दुइ कर जोरी अस्तुति तोरी केहि बिधि करौं अनंता।
माया गुन ग्यानातीत अमाना बेद पुरान भनंता।।
करुना सुख सागर सब गुन आगर जेहि गावहिं श्रुति संता।
सो मम हित लागी जन अनुरागी भयउ प्रगट श्रीकंता।।
ब्रह्मांड निकाया निर्मित माया रोम रोम प्रति बेद कहै।
मम उर सो बासी यह उपहासी सुनत धीर पति थिर न रहै।।
उपजा जब ग्याना प्रभु मुसकाना चरित बहुत बिधि कीन्ह चहै।
कहि कथा सुहाई मातु बुझाई जेहि प्रकार सुत प्रेम लहै।।
माता पुनि बोली सो मति डौली तजहु तात यह रूपा।
कीजै सिसुलीला अति प्रियसीला यह सुख परम अनूपा।।
सुनि बचन सुजाना रोदन ठाना होइ बालक सुरभूपा।
यह चरित जे गावहिं हरिपद पावहिं ते न परहिं भवकूपा।।
verses.translation: The merciful Lord, compassionate to the lowly, benefactor of Kausalya, appeared. The mother was overjoyed, and sages' minds were captivated beholding His wondrous form. His eyes were enchanting, His body dark as a cloud, and He bore His own weapons in four arms. Adorned with ornaments and a garland of forest flowers, with large eyes, He was an ocean of beauty, the slayer of demon Khara. With joined palms, the mother spoke in praise...

verses.original:
बिप्र धेनु सुर संत हित लीन्ह मनुज अवतार।
निज इच्छा निर्मित तनु माया गुन गो पार।।192।।
verses.translation: For the sake of Brahmins, cows, gods, and saints, He took a human incarnation. He fashioned His body by His own will — beyond Maya and the three gunas.

verses.original:
सुनि सिसु रुदन परम प्रिय बानी। संभ्रम चलि आई सब रानी।।
हरषित जहँ तहँ धाईं दासी। आनँद मगन सकल पुरबासी।।
verses.translation: Hearing the supremely sweet cry of the newborn, all the queens came rushing in excitement. Delighted maidservants ran here and there; all the residents of the city were drowned in bliss.

verses.original:
दसरथ पुत्रजन्म सुनि काना। मानहुँ ब्रह्मानंद समाना।।
परम प्रेम मन पुलक सरीरा। चाहत उठत करत मति धीरा।।
