verses.translation: When Dasharatha heard of the birth of his son, it was as if he had attained the bliss of Brahman. With supreme love, his body thrilled; he wanted to rise but steadied himself with patience.

verses.original:
जाकर नाम सुनत सुभ होई। मोरें गृह आवा प्रभु सोई।।
परमानंद पूरि मन राजा। कहा बोलाइ बजावहु बाजा।।
verses.translation: 'He whose very name brings auspiciousness — that Lord has come to my home!' Filled with supreme bliss, the king commanded: 'Send for the musicians — let the instruments play!'

verses.original:
गुर बसिष्ठ कहँ गयउ हँकारा। आए द्विजन सहित नृपद्वारा।।
अनुपम बालक देखेन्हि जाई। रूप रासि गुन कहि न सिराई।।
verses.translation: A messenger was sent to summon Guru Vasishtha; he came to the king's gate with an assembly of Brahmins. Going inside, they beheld the incomparable child — a treasure of beauty, whose virtues cannot be fully described.

verses.original:
नंदीमुख सराध करि जातकरम सब कीन्ह।
हाटक धेनु बसन मनि नृप बिप्रन्ह कहँ दीन्ह।।193।।
verses.translation: After performing the Nandimukha Shraddha ceremony, all the birth rites were completed. The king gave gold, cows, garments, and gems to the Brahmins.

verses.original:
ध्वज पताक तोरन पुर छावा। कहि न जाइ जेहि भाँति बनावा।।
सुमनबृष्टि अकास तें होई। ब्रह्मानंद मगन सब लोई।।
verses.translation: The city was decorated with flags, banners, and festive arches — their beauty beyond description. Flowers rained from the sky; all the people were immersed in the bliss of Brahman.

verses.original:
बृंद बृंद मिलि चलीं लोगाई। सहज संगार किएँ उठि धाई।।
कनक कलस मंगल धरि थारा। गावत पैठहिं भूप दुआरा।।
verses.translation: Throngs upon throngs of women gathered and rushed out with their natural adornments. Carrying golden pitchers and auspicious trays, they sang as they entered the king's gate.

verses.original:
करि आरति नेवछावरि करहीं। बार बार सिसु चरनन्हि परहीं।।
मागध सूत बंदिगन गायक। पावन गुन गावहिं रघुनायक।।
verses.translation: They performed the arati ceremony and made offerings to avert the evil eye; again and again they touched the infant's feet. Bards, minstrels, heralds, and singers sang the holy praises of the Lord of the Raghus.

verses.original:
सर्बस दान दीन्ह सब काहू। जेहिं पावा राखा नहिं ताहू।।
मृगमद चंदन कुंकुम कीचा। मची सकल बीथिन्ह बिच बीचा।।
verses.translation: Everyone gave away all they had; whoever received anything did not keep it but gave it away in turn. A paste of musk, sandalwood, and saffron was smeared all over the streets.

verses.original:
गृह गृह बाज बधाव सुभ प्रगटे सुषमा कंद।
हरषवंत सब जहँ तहँ नगर नारि नर बृंद।।194।।
verses.translation: In every home auspicious music and celebrations resounded; the very source of grace had appeared. Everywhere, men, women, and throngs of people rejoiced.

verses.original:
कैकयसुता सुमित्रा दोऊ। सुंदर सुत जनमत भैं ओऊ।।
वह सुख संपति समय समाजा। कहि न सकइ सारद अहिराजा।।
verses.translation: Kaikeyi and both Sumitras also gave birth to beautiful sons. The joy, prosperity, and splendor of that occasion even Saraswati and Shesha could not describe.

verses.original:
अवधपुरी सोहइ एहि भाँती। प्रभुहि मिलन आई जनु राती।।
देखि भानू जनु मन सकुचानी। तदपि बनी संध्या अनुमानी।।
verses.translation: The city of Ayodhya looked as though Night itself had come to meet the Lord. Seeing the sun, she seemed to shrink back in shyness, yet she managed to appear as twilight.

verses.original:
अगर धूप बहु जनु अँधिआरी। उड़इ अभीर मनहुँ अरुनारी।।
मंदिर मनि समूह जनु तारा। नृप गृह कलस सो इंदु उदारा।।
verses.translation: The smoke of incense was like the darkness; the flying red powder was like the glow of dawn. The clusters of gems on the mansions were like stars; the golden pinnacle of the king's palace shone like the generous moon.

verses.original:
भवन बेदधुनि अति मृदु बानी। जनु खग मूखर समयँ जनु सानी।।
कौतुक देखि पतंग भुलाना। एक मास तेइँ जात न जाना।।
verses.translation: The recitation of the Vedas in the houses was like the sweet warbling of birds at the right hour. Seeing this spectacle, the sun himself was so enchanted that an entire month passed without him realizing it.

verses.original:
मास दिवस कर दिवस भा मरम न जानइ कोइ।
रथ समेत रबि थाकेउ निसा कवन बिधि होइ।।195।।
verses.translation: A month became a single day — the secret none could know. The sun and his chariot grew weary — how then could night ever come?

verses.original:
यह रहस्य काहू नहिं जाना। दिन मनि चले करत गुनगाना।।
देखि महोत्सव सुर मुनि नागा। चले भवन बरनत निज भागा।।
verses.translation: No one knew this mystery. The Sun proceeded on his way singing the Lord's praises. Seeing the great festival, gods, sages, and Nagas returned to their abodes, extolling their own good fortune.

verses.original:
औरउ एक कहउँ निज चोरी। सुनु गिरिजा अति दृढ़ मति तोरी।।
काक भुसुंडि संग हम दोऊ। मनुजरूप जानइ नहिं कोऊ।।
verses.translation: I shall tell you yet another secret of mine — listen, O Girija, with your firm mind. Kakabhushundi and I — we two were there together in human form, and no one recognized us.

verses.original:
परमानंद प्रेमसुख फूले। बीथिन्ह फिरहिं मगन मन भूले।।
यह सुभ चरित जान पै सोई। कृपा राम कै जापर होई।।
verses.translation: Blooming with supreme bliss and the joy of love, we wandered through the streets with minds entranced. Only that person knows this blessed story upon whom Rama's grace descends.

verses.original:
तेहि अवसर जो जेहि बिधि आवा। दीन्ह भूप जो जेहि मन भावा।।
गज रथ तुरग हेम गो हीरा। दीन्हे नृप नानाबिधि चीरा।।
verses.translation: At that time, whoever came in whatever manner, the king gave them whatever their heart desired. Elephants, chariots, horses, gold, cows, diamonds — the king gave garments of every kind.

verses.original:
मन संतोषे सबन्हि के जहँ तहँ देहि असीस।
सकल तनय चिर जीवहुँ तुलसिदास के ईस।।196।।
verses.translation: He satisfied the hearts of all, and from everywhere came blessings: 'May all the sons live long — they who are the lords of Tulsidas!'

verses.original:
कछुक दिवस बीते एहि भाँती। जात न जानिअ दिन अरु राती।।
नामकरन कर अवसरु जानी। भूप बोलि पठए मुनि ग्यानी।।
verses.translation: A few days passed in this manner; day and night went by unnoticed. Knowing the time had come for the naming ceremony, the king sent for the learned sage.

verses.original:
करि पूजा भूपति अस भाषा। धरिअ नाम जो मुनि गुनि राखा।।
इन्ह के नाम अनेक अनूपा। मैं नृप कहब स्वमति अनुरूपा।।
verses.translation: After performing the worship, the king said: 'Please give them the names that you, O wise sage, have determined.' The sage replied: 'These children have countless incomparable names; I shall suggest some according to my own understanding, O King.'

verses.original:
जो आनंद सिंधु सुखरासी। सीकर तें त्रैलोक सुपासी।।
सो सुख धाम राम अस नामा। अखिल लोक दायक बिश्रामा।।
verses.translation: 'He who is the ocean of bliss and the treasure-house of happiness, by a mere drop of whose joy the three worlds are sustained — His name is Rama, the bestower of rest to all the worlds.'

verses.original:
बिस्व भरन पोषन कर जोई। ताकर नाम भरत अस होई।।
जाके सुमिरन तें रिपु नासा। नाम सत्रुहन बेद प्रकासा।।
verses.translation: 'He who nourishes and sustains the universe — his name shall be Bharata. He by whose mere remembrance enemies are destroyed — the Vedas declare his name as Shatrughna.'

verses.original:
लच्छन धाम राम प्रिय सकल जगत आधार।
गुरु बसिष्ट तेहि राखा लछिमन नाम उदार।।197।।
verses.translation: 'The abode of all auspicious marks, dear to Rama, the support of the entire universe' — Guru Vasishtha named him Lakshmana, a noble name.

verses.original:
धरे नाम गुर हृदयँ बिचारी। बेद तत्व नृप तव सुत चारी।।
मुनि धन जन सरबस सिव प्राना। बाल केलि तेहिं सुख माना।।
verses.translation: The guru gave the names after deep contemplation and said: 'O King, your four sons are the very essence of the Vedas.' The treasure of sages, the everything of devotees, the very life-breath of Shiva — He delighted in childhood play.

verses.original:
बारेहि ते निज हित पति जानी। लछिमन राम चरन रति मानी।।
भरत सत्रुहन दूनउ भाई। प्रभु सेवक जसि प्रीति बड़ाई।।
verses.translation: From the very start, recognizing the Lord as his master and benefactor, Lakshmana developed devotion to Rama's feet. Bharata and Shatrughna — those two brothers — their love was like that of lord and servant.

verses.original:
स्याम गौर सुंदर दोउ जोरी। निरखहिं छबि जननीं तृन तोरी।।
चारिउ सील रूप गुन धामा। तदपि अधिक सुखसागर रामा।।
verses.translation: The two pairs — one dark and one fair — were beautiful to behold; the mothers gazed at them, plucking blades of grass to ward off the evil eye. All four were abodes of virtue, beauty, and good qualities, yet Rama, the ocean of delight, surpassed them all.

verses.original:
हृदयँ अनुग्रह इंदु प्रकासा। सूचत किरन मनोहर हासा।।
कबहुँ उछंग कबहुँ बर पलना। मातु दुलारइ कहि प्रिय ललना।।
verses.translation: In His heart shone the moonlight of grace, expressed through His enchanting smile. Sometimes on the lap, sometimes in a fine cradle, the mother would lovingly rock Him, calling Him her darling child.

verses.original:
ब्यापक ब्रह्म निरंजन निर्गुन बिगत बिनोद।
सो अज प्रेम भगति बस कौसल्या के गोद।।198।।
verses.translation: The all-pervading, pure, desireless, unborn, attributeless, nameless, and formless Brahman — that very Being, bound by the love of devotion, played in Kausalya's lap.

verses.original:
काम कोटि छबि स्याम सरीरा। नील कंज बारिद गंभीरा।।
अरुन चरन पकंज नख जोती। कमल दलन्हि बैठे जनु मोती।।
verses.translation: His dark body was lovely as millions of Kamadevas; like a blue lotus, dark cloud, deep and profound. The rosy glow of His lotus feet and nails was as if pearls sat upon lotus petals.

verses.original:
रेख कुलिस धवज अंकुर सोहे। नूपुर धुनि सुनि मुनि मन मोहे।।
कटि किंकिनी उदर त्रय रेखा। नाभि गभीर जान जेहि देखा।।
verses.translation: The auspicious marks of thunderbolt, flag, and goad shone on His soles; the tinkling of His anklets enchanted the minds of sages. At His waist hung a girdle with tiny bells; His belly bore three beautiful lines; His deep navel could be known only by one who has seen it.

verses.original:
भुज बिसाल भूषन जुत भूरी। हियँ हरि नख अति सोभा रूरी।।
उर मनिहार पदिक की सोभा। बिप्र चरन देखत मन लोभा।।
verses.translation: His broad arms were decked with many ornaments; the beauty of Hari's claw-mark on His chest was exquisite. The splendor of the jeweled necklace and crystal beads on His chest made the eyes of Brahmins covetous at the very sight.

verses.original:
कंबु कंठ अति चिबुक सुहाई। आनन अमित मदन छबि छाई।।
दुइ दुइ दसन अधर अरुनारे। नासा तिलक को बरनै पारे।।
verses.translation: His conch-like neck was graceful, His chin most lovely; His face overflowed with the beauty of countless Kamadevas. Two tiny teeth showed beneath His reddish lips; who can describe the beauty of the tilak on His nose?

verses.original:
सुंदर श्रवन सुचारु कपोला। अति प्रिय मधुर तोतरे बोला।।
चिक्कन कच कुंचित गभुआरे। बहु प्रकार रचि मातु सँवारे।।
verses.translation: Beautiful ears, charming cheeks, and supremely sweet, lisping baby-talk. His smooth, curly, dense locks were arranged by His mother in many styles.

verses.original:
पीत झगुलिआ तनु पहिराई। जानु पानि बिचरनि मोहि भाई।।
रूप सकहिं नहिं कहि श्रुति सेषा। सो जानइ सपनेहुँ जेहि देखा।।
verses.translation: Dressed in a little yellow frock, He crawled on hands and knees — this sight enchanted me. Even the Vedas and Shesha cannot describe His beauty — only one who has seen it, even in a dream, can know.

verses.original:
सुख संदोह मोहपर ग्यान गिरा गोतीत।
दंपति परम प्रेम बस कर सिसुचरित पुनीत।।199।।
verses.translation: An abode of joy, beyond delusion and knowledge, beyond speech and intellect — the divine couple, bound by supreme love, enact the holy play of their infant child.

verses.original:
एहि बिधि राम जगत पितु माता। कोसलपुर बासिन्ह सुखदाता।।
जिन्ह रघुनाथ चरन रति मानी। तिन्ह की यह गति प्रगट भवानी।।
verses.translation: Thus Rama, the father and mother of the universe, bestowed joy upon the residents of Ayodhya. Those who cherished devotion to the feet of the Lord of the Raghus — this is their manifest reward, O Bhavani.

verses.original:
रघुपति बिमुख जतन कर कोरी। कवन सकइ भव बंधन छोरी।।
जीव चराचर बस कै राखे। सो माया प्रभु सों भय भाखे।।
verses.translation: Whoever turns away from Rama — no matter what efforts they make — who can free them from the bonds of worldly existence? The beings, animate and inanimate, whom He keeps bound — that very Maya speaks with fear before the Lord.

verses.original:
भृकुटि बिलास नचावइ ताही। अस प्रभु छाड़ि भजिअ कहु काही।।
मन क्रम बचन छाड़ि चतुराई। भजत कृपा करिहहिं रघुराई।।
verses.translation: He makes even that Maya dance by a mere play of His eyebrows — tell me, whom else should one worship, forsaking such a Lord? Abandoning all cleverness of mind, word, and deed, if one worships Him, the Lord of the Raghus will be gracious.

verses.original:
एहि बिधि सिसुबिनोद प्रभु कीन्हा। सकल नगरबासिन्ह सुख दीन्हा।।
लै उछंग कबहुँक हलरावै। कबहुँ पालनें घालि झुलावै।।
verses.translation: Thus the Lord performed His childhood pastimes, giving joy to all the city's residents. Sometimes she would take Him in her lap and rock Him; sometimes she would lay Him in a cradle and swing Him.

verses.original:
प्रेम मगन कौसल्या निसि दिन जात न जान।
सुत सनेह बस माता बालचरित कर गान।।200।।
verses.translation: Drowned in love, Kausalya knew not the passing of day and night. Bound by affection for her son, the mother would sing of His childhood deeds.

verses.original:
एक बार जननीं अन्हवाए। करि सिंगार पलनाँ पौढ़ाए।।
निज कुल इष्टदेव भगवाना। पूजा हेतु कीन्ह अस्नाना।।
verses.translation: One day the mother bathed the child, adorned Him, and laid Him in His cradle. Then she bathed herself in preparation for the worship of her family deity, Lord Bhagavan.

verses.original:
करि पूजा नैबेद्य चढ़ावा। आपु गई जहँ पाक बनावा।।
बहुरि मातु तहवाँ चलि आई। भोजन करत देख सुत जाई।।
verses.translation: Having performed the worship and offered the food-offering, she went to the kitchen to prepare the meal. When the mother returned, she saw her child eating the offered food.

verses.original:
गै जननी सिसु पहिं भयभीता। देखा बाल तहाँ पुनि सूता।।
बहुरि आइ देखा सुत सोई। हृदयँ कंप मन धीर न होई।।
verses.translation: The mother went to the child in alarm — but found the baby sleeping there as before. She came back and again saw the same child eating. Her heart trembled and her mind could find no peace.

verses.original:
इहाँ उहाँ दुइ बालक देखा। मतिभ्रम मोर कि आन बिसेषा।।
देखि राम जननी अकुलानी। प्रभु हँसि दीन्ह मधुर मुसुकानी।।
verses.translation: 'I see two children here and there — is my mind confused, or is this something extraordinary?' Seeing His mother bewildered, the Lord laughed and gave a sweet smile.

verses.original:
देखरावा मातहि निज अदभुत रुप अखंड।
रोम रोम प्रति लागे कोटि कोटि ब्रह्मंड।। 201।।
verses.translation: He revealed to His mother His own wondrous, undivided cosmic form — in every pore of His body were lodged millions upon millions of universes.

verses.original:
अगनित रबि ससि सिव चतुरानन। बहु गिरि सरित सिंधु महि कानन।।
काल कर्म गुन ग्यान सुभाऊ। सोउ देखा जो सुना न काऊ।।
verses.translation: Countless suns, moons, Shivas, and Brahmas; innumerable mountains, rivers, oceans, earths, and forests. Time, karma, qualities, knowledge, and nature — she saw even that which she had never heard of.

verses.original:
देखी माया सब बिधि गाढ़ी। अति सभीत जोरें कर ठाढ़ी।।
देखा जीव नचावइ जाही। देखी भगति जो छोरइ ताही।।
verses.translation: She saw Maya, mighty in every way, standing nearby with folded hands, exceedingly frightened. She saw the individual soul whom Maya makes dance; she saw Devotion, who liberates the soul from Maya's grip.

verses.original:
तन पुलकित मुख बचन न आवा। नयन मूदि चरननि सिरु नावा।।
बिसमयवंत देखि महतारी। भए बहुरि सिसुरूप खरारी।।
verses.translation: Her body thrilled, no words came from her mouth. She closed her eyes and bowed her head at His feet. Seeing the mother overcome with wonder, the slayer of Khara resumed His infant form.

verses.original:
अस्तुति करि न जाइ भय माना। जगत पिता मैं सुत करि जाना।।
हरि जननि बहुबिधि समुझाई। यह जनि कतहुँ कहसि सुनु माई।।
verses.translation: 'I cannot praise You — I am seized with awe. I regarded the Father of the universe as my son!' Hari consoled His mother in many ways: 'Listen, Mother — never speak of this to anyone.'

verses.original:
बार बार कौसल्या बिनय करइ कर जोरि।।
अब जनि कबहूँ ब्यापै प्रभु मोहि माया तोरि।। 202।।
verses.translation: Again and again, Kausalya prayed with folded hands: 'Lord, may Your Maya never delude me again!'

verses.original:
बालचरित हरि बहुबिधि कीन्हा। अति अनंद दासन्ह कहँ दीन्हा।।
कछुक काल बीतें सब भाई। बड़े भए परिजन सुखदाई।।
verses.translation: Hari performed many kinds of childhood sports, giving immense joy to His servants. After some time, all four brothers grew up, delighting their family.

verses.original:
चूड़ाकरन कीन्ह गुरु जाई। बिप्रन्ह पुनि दछिना बहु पाई।।
परम मनोहर चरित अपारा। करत फिरत चारिउ सुकुमारा।।
verses.translation: The guru came and performed the Chudakarana (tonsure) ceremony; the Brahmins received abundant gifts. The four tender princes performed countless charming exploits as they roamed about.

verses.original:
मन क्रम बचन अगोचर जोई। दसरथ अजिर बिचर प्रभु सोई।।
भोजन करत बोल जब राजा। नहिं आवत तजि बाल समाजा।।
verses.translation: He who is beyond the grasp of mind, deed, and speech — that very Lord played in Dasharatha's courtyard. When the king called them for meals, they would not come, unwilling to leave their playmates.

verses.original:
कौसल्या जब बोलन जाई। ठुमकु ठुमकु प्रभु चलहिं पराई।।
निगम नेति सिव अंत न पावा। ताहि धरै जननी हठि धावा।।
verses.translation: When Kausalya went to fetch them, the Lord would toddle away from her. He whom the Vedas describe as 'not this, not this' and whose end even Shiva cannot find — that very Lord was chased and caught by His mother.

verses.original: धूरस धूरि भरें तनु आए। भूपति बिहसि गोद बैठाए।।
verses.translation: Covered with dust all over their bodies, they would come; the king would laugh and seat them in his lap.

verses.original:
भोजन करत चपल चित इत उत अवसरु पाइ।
भाजि चले किलकत मुख दधि ओदन लपटाइ।।203।।
verses.translation: While eating, with restless mind, finding the slightest opportunity, the Lord would run away laughing, His face smeared with curd and rice.

verses.original:
बालचरित अति सरल सुहाए। सारद सेष संभु श्रुति गाए।।
जिन कर मन इन्ह सन नहिं राता। ते जन बंचित किए बिधाता।।
verses.translation: These childhood sports were most simple and charming — they have been sung by Saraswati, Shesha, Shambhu, and the Vedas. Those whose minds do not delight in them — such persons have been cheated by the Creator.

verses.original:
भए कुमार जबहिं सब भ्राता। दीन्ह जनेऊ गुरु पितु माता।।
गुरगृहँ गए पढ़न रघुराई। अलप काल बिद्या सब आई।।
verses.translation: When all the brothers became young boys, their parents and guru invested them with the sacred thread. Rama went to the guru's house for study, and in a short time mastered all knowledge.

verses.original:
जाकी सहज स्वास श्रुति चारी। सो हरि पढ़ यह कौतुक भारी।।
बिद्या बिनय निपुन गुन सीला। खेलहिं खेल सकल नृपलीला।।
verses.translation: He whose natural breath constitutes the four Vedas — that Hari studying was itself a great marvel! Accomplished in learning, humility, virtue, and good conduct, they played all the games befitting princes.

verses.original:
करतल बान धनुष अति सोहा। देखत रूप चराचर मोहा।।
जिन्ह बीथिन्ह बिहरहिं सब भाई। थकित होहिं सब लोग लुगाई।।
verses.translation: Bow and arrow looked splendid in their hands; seeing their beauty, all creation — animate and inanimate — was enchanted. In whichever streets the brothers roamed, all the men and women would stand spellbound.

verses.original:
कोसलपुर बासी नर नारि बृद्ध अरु बाल।
प्रानहु ते प्रिय लागत सब कहुँ राम कृपाल।।204।।
verses.translation: To the residents of Ayodhya — men, women, old, and young — the gracious Rama was dearer than life itself.

verses.original:
बंधु सखा संग लेहिं बोलाई। बन मृगया नित खेलहिं जाई।।
पावन मृग मारहिं जियँ जानी। दिन प्रति नृपहि देखावहिं आनी।।
verses.translation: Calling their brothers and friends along, they would go hunting in the forest every day. Knowing which animals were fit to be killed, they would slay them and bring them daily to show the king.

verses.original:
जे मृग राम बान के मारे। ते तनु तजि सुरलोक सिधारे।।
अनुज सखा सँग भोजन करहीं। मातु पिता अग्या अनुसरहीं।।
verses.translation: Those deer that were slain by Rama's arrows gave up their bodies and went straight to heaven. The brothers would dine with their younger siblings and friends, and always followed their parents' commands.

verses.original:
जेहि बिधि सुखी होहिं पुर लोगा। करहिं कृपानिधि सोइ संजोगा।।
बेद पुरान सुनहिं मन लाई। आपु कहहिं अनुजन्ह समुझाई।।
verses.translation: Whatever would bring happiness to the people of the city, the all-merciful Lord would arrange just that. They listened to the Vedas and Puranas attentively and themselves explained them to their younger brothers.

verses.original:
प्रातकाल उठि कै रघुनाथा। मातु पिता गुरु नावहिं माथा।।
आयसु मागि करहिं पुर काजा। देखि चरित हरषइ मन राजा।।
verses.translation: Rising at dawn, the Lord of the Raghus would bow His head to His mother, father, and guru. Seeking their permission, He would attend to the city's affairs; seeing His conduct, the king's heart would rejoice.

verses.original:
ब्यापक अकल अनीह अज निर्गुन नाम न रूप।
भगत हेतु नाना बिधि करत चरित्र अनूप।।205।।
verses.translation: The all-pervading, immeasurable, desireless, unborn, attributeless Lord — without name or form — for the sake of His devotees, performed these incomparable deeds in countless ways.

verses.original:
यह सब चरित कहा मैं गाई। आगिलि कथा सुनहु मन लाई।।
बिस्वामित्र महामुनि ग्यानी। बसहि बिपिन सुभ आश्रम जानी।।
verses.translation: I have recounted all these delightful stories. Now listen attentively to the next part. The great and wise sage Vishvamitra dwelt in the forest at a beautiful hermitage.

verses.original:
जहँ जप जग्य मुनि करही। अति मारीच सुबाहुहि डरहीं।।
देखत जग्य निसाचर धावहि। करहि उपद्रव मुनि दुख पावहिं।।
verses.translation: Where sages performed prayer and sacrifice, they were greatly afraid of the demons Maricha and Subahu. Whenever they began a sacrifice, the demons would rush in, causing havoc, and the sages would suffer.

verses.original:
गाधितनय मन चिंता ब्यापी। हरि बिनु मरहि न निसिचर पापी।।
तब मुनिवर मन कीन्ह बिचारा। प्रभु अवतरेउ हरन महि भारा।।
verses.translation: Anxiety filled the mind of Vishvamitra, son of Gadhi: 'Without Hari, these sinful demons cannot be slain.' Then the great sage reflected in his mind: 'The Lord has incarnated to relieve the earth's burden.'

verses.original:
एहुँ मिस देखौं पद जाई। करि बिनती आनौ दोउ भाई।।
ग्यान बिराग सकल गुन अयना। सो प्रभु मै देखब भरि नयना।।
verses.translation: 'Using this occasion, I shall go and see His feet, and with humble request, I shall bring the two brothers with me. He who is the repository of knowledge, renunciation, and all virtues — that Lord I shall behold with my own eyes.'

verses.original:
बहुबिधि करत मनोरथ जात लागि नहिं बार।
करि मज्जन सरऊ जल गए भूप दरबार।।206।।
verses.translation: Making many such wishes in his heart, and without delay, the sage bathed in the waters of the Sarayu and went to the king's court.

verses.original:
मुनि आगमन सुना जब राजा। मिलन गयऊ लै बिप्र समाजा।।
करि दंडवत मुनिहि सनमानी। निज आसन बैठारेन्हि आनी।।
verses.translation: When the king heard that the sage had arrived, he went to meet him with an assembly of Brahmins. Prostrating himself, he honored the sage and brought him inside to be seated on his own throne.

verses.original:
चरन पखारि कीन्हि अति पूजा। मो सम आजु धन्य नहिं दूजा।।
बिबिध भाँति भोजन करवावा। मुनिवर हृदयँ हरष अति पावा।।
verses.translation: He washed the sage's feet and worshipped him lavishly, saying: 'No one is as blessed as I am today.' He had various delicacies served to the sage, and the great sage felt supreme delight in his heart.

verses.original:
पुनि चरननि मेले सुत चारी। राम देखि मुनि देह बिसारी।।
भए मगन देखत मुख सोभा। जनु चकोर पूरन ससि लोभा।।
verses.translation: Then the king brought all four sons and placed them at the sage's feet. Seeing Rama, the sage forgot all consciousness of his body. He became absorbed in gazing at His face's beauty, like a chakora bird captivated by the full moon.

verses.original:
तब मन हरषि बचन कह राऊ। मुनि अस कृपा न कीन्हिहु काऊ।।
केहि कारन आगमन तुम्हारा। कहहु सो करत न लावउँ बारा।।
verses.translation: Then the king spoke with a joyful heart: 'O sage, you have never shown me such kindness before. What is the purpose of your visit? Tell me, and I shall accomplish it without delay.'

verses.original:
असुर समूह सतावहिं मोही। मै जाचन आयउँ नृप तोही।।
अनुज समेत देहु रघुनाथा। निसिचर बध मैं होब सनाथा।।
verses.translation: 'Hosts of demons torment me; I have come to beg of you, O King. Give me Rama along with his younger brother; with them I shall be protected from the demons who obstruct my sacrifice.'

verses.original:
देहु भूप मन हरषित तजहु मोह अग्यान।
धर्म सुजस प्रभु तुम्ह कौं इन्ह कहँ अति कल्यान।।207।।
verses.translation: 'Give them joyfully, O King — abandon delusion and ignorance. It will bring you dharma and fair fame, and for them it will be supremely beneficial.'

verses.original:
सुनि राजा अति अप्रिय बानी। हृदय कंप मुख दुति कुमुलानी।।
चौथेंपन पायउँ सुत चारी। बिप्र बचन नहिं कहेहु बिचारी।।
verses.translation: Hearing these most unwelcome words, the king's heart trembled and his face lost its luster. 'In my old age I have obtained these four sons — O Brahmin, you have not spoken with due thought.'

verses.original:
मागहु भूमि धेनु धन कोसा। सर्बस देउँ आजु सहरोसा।।
देह प्रान तें प्रिय कछु नाही। सोउ मुनि देउँ निमिष एक माही।।
verses.translation: 'Ask for land, cows, wealth, and treasure — I shall give you everything this very moment, with enthusiasm. Nothing is dearer to me than my own life — even that, O sage, I shall give in an instant.'

verses.original:
सब सुत प्रिय मोहि प्रान कि नाईं। राम देत नहिं बनइ गोसाई।।
कहँ निसिचर अति घोर कठोरा। कहँ सुंदर सुत परम किसोरा।।
verses.translation: 'All my sons are dear to me as my very life. Giving up Rama is not possible, my Lord. On one hand are those terrible and fierce demons; on the other, my beautiful, tender young son.'

verses.original:
सुनि नृप गिरा प्रेम रस सानी। हृदयँ हरष माना मुनि ग्यानी।।
तब बसिष्ट बहु निधि समुझावा। नृप संदेह नास कहँ पावा।।
verses.translation: Hearing the king's words, steeped in the nectar of love, the wise sage was inwardly pleased. Then Vasishtha counseled the king at length, and the king's doubts were finally dispelled.

verses.original:
अति आदर दोउ तनय बोलाए। हृदयँ लाइ बहु भाँति सिखाए।।
मेरे प्रान नाथ सुत दोऊ। तुम्ह मुनि पिता आन नहिं कोऊ।।
verses.translation: With great affection, the king called both sons, embraced them, and instructed them in many ways: 'My sons are my very life, O Lord. You, O sage, are their father — there is none other.'

verses.original:
सौंपे भूप रिषिहि सुत बहु बिधि देइ असीस।
जननी भवन गए प्रभु चले नाइ पद सीस।।208(क)।।
पुरुषसिंह दोउ बीर हरषि चले मुनि भय हरन।।
कृपासिंधु मतिधीर अखिल बिस्व कारन करन।।208(ख)।।
verses.translation: The king entrusted his sons to the sage, blessing them in many ways. The Lord bowed at His mothers' feet and then departed with head bowed at the sage's feet. The two brothers, lions among men, set forth joyfully with the sage, dispellers of fear. The ocean of mercy, firm of mind — cause and creator of the entire universe.

verses.original:
अरुन नयन उर बाहु बिसाला। नील जलज तनु स्याम तमाला।।
कटि पट पीत कसें बर भाथा। रुचिर चाप सायक दुहुँ हाथा।।
verses.translation: With reddish eyes, broad chest and arms, bodies dark as blue lotuses and tamala trees — wearing yellow garments at the waist, with fine quivers strapped on, and beautiful bows and arrows in both hands.

verses.original:
स्याम गौर सुंदर दोउ भाई। बिस्बामित्र महानिधि पाई।।
प्रभु ब्रह्मन्यदेव मै जाना। मोहि निति पिता तजेहु भगवाना।।
verses.translation: The two brothers, one dark and one fair, were beautiful — Vishvamitra had obtained a supreme treasure. 'The Lord is the patron deity of Brahmins — I know this. Yet my father has always given me up, O Lord.'

verses.original:
चले जात मुनि दीन्हि दिखाई। सुनि ताड़का क्रोध करि धाई।।
एकहिं बान प्रान हरि लीन्हा। दीन जानि तेहि निज पद दीन्हा।।
verses.translation: As they walked, the sage pointed out the demoness Tadaka on the way. Hearing about her, she rushed at them in fury. With a single arrow, Rama took her life; knowing her to be pitiable, He granted her His own divine abode.

verses.original:
तब रिषि निज नाथहि जियँ चीन्ही। बिद्यानिधि कहुँ बिद्या दीन्ही।।
जाते लाग न छुधा पिपासा। अतुलित बल तनु तेज प्रकासा।।
verses.translation: Then the sage, recognizing his master in his heart, imparted divine knowledge and powers to the treasury of wisdom. Thereby neither hunger nor thirst could touch Him; His body gained incomparable strength, radiance, and splendor.

verses.original:
आयुष सब समर्पि कै प्रभु निज आश्रम आनि।
कंद मूल फल भोजन दीन्ह भगति हित जानि।।209।।
verses.translation: The sage offered all his spiritual powers and brought the Lord to his own hermitage. He served Him roots, tubers, and fruits as food, knowing Him to be devoted to His devotees' love.

verses.original:
प्रात कहा मुनि सन रघुराई। निर्भय जग्य करहु तुम्ह जाई।।
होम करन लागे मुनि झारी। आपु रहे मख कीं रखवारी।।
verses.translation: At dawn, the Lord of the Raghus said to the sage: 'Go and perform your sacrifice without fear.' The host of sages began the fire-ritual, while the Lord Himself stood guard over the sacrifice.

verses.original:
सुनि मारीच निसाचर क्रोही। लै सहाय धावा मुनिद्रोही।।
बिनु फर बान राम तेहि मारा। सत जोजन गा सागर पारा।।
verses.translation: Hearing this, the wrathful demon Maricha rushed forth with his allies to attack the sages. Rama struck him with a headless arrow that hurled him a hundred yojanas across the ocean.

verses.original:
पावक सर सुबाहु पुनि मारा। अनुज निसाचर कटकु सँघारा।।
मारि असुर द्विज निर्मयकारी। अस्तुति करहिं देव मुनि झारी।।
verses.translation: Then He slew Subahu with a fire-arrow, and His younger brother destroyed the entire demon army. Having slain the demons and freed the Brahmins from fear, gods and sages all joined in praise.

verses.original:
तहँ पुनि कछुक दिवस रघुराया। रहे कीन्हि बिप्रन्ह पर दाया।।
भगति हेतु बहु कथा पुराना। कहे बिप्र जद्यपि प्रभु जाना।।
verses.translation: The Lord of the Raghus stayed there for some more days, showing kindness to the Brahmins. Though the Lord already knew everything, the Brahmins narrated many Puranic stories for the sake of devotion.

verses.original:
तब मुनि सादर कहा बुझाई। चरित एक प्रभु देखिअ जाई।।
धनुषजग्य मुनि रघुकुल नाथा। हरषि चले मुनिबर के साथा।।
verses.translation: Then the sage respectfully said: 'Lord, let us go and witness an event. There is a bow-sacrifice, O Lord of the Raghu dynasty.' The Lord joyfully set out with the great sage.

verses.original:
आश्रम एक दीख मग माहीं। खग मृग जीव जंतु तहँ नाहीं।।
पूछा मुनिहि सिला प्रभु देखी। सकल कथा मुनि कहा बिसेषी।।
verses.translation: On the way, they saw a hermitage, but neither bird, deer, nor any creature was there. The Lord noticed a stone and asked the sage about it; the sage narrated the entire story in detail.

verses.original:
गौतम नारि श्राप बस उपल देह धरि धीर।
चरन कमल रज चाहति कृपा करहु रघुबीर।।210।।
verses.translation: 'She is Gautama's wife, who by the force of a curse endures patiently in this stone form. She longs for the dust of Your lotus feet — be gracious, O Hero of the Raghus.'

verses.original:
परसत पद पावन सोक नसावन प्रगट भई तपपुंज सही।
देखत रघुनायक जन सुख दायक सनमुख होइ कर जोरि रही।।
अति प्रेम अधीरा पुलक सरीरा मुख नहिं आवइ बचन कही।
अतिसय बड़भागी चरनन्हि लागी जुगल नयन जलधार बही।।
धीरजु मन कीन्हा प्रभु कहुँ चीन्हा रघुपति कृपाँ भगति पाई।
अति निर्मल बानीं अस्तुति ठानी ग्यानगम्य जय रघुराई।।
मै नारि अपावन प्रभु जग पावन रावन रिपु जन सुखदाई।
राजीव बिलोचन भव भय मोचन पाहि पाहि सरनहिं आई।।
मुनि श्राप जो दीन्हा अति भल कीन्हा परम अनुग्रह मैं माना।
देखेउँ भरि लोचन हरि भवमोचन इहइ लाभ संकर जाना।।
बिनती प्रभु मोरी मैं मति भोरी नाथ न मागउँ बर आना।
पद कमल परागा रस अनुरागा मम मन मधुप करै पाना।।
जेहिं पद सुरसरिता परम पुनीता प्रगट भई सिव सीस धरी।
सोइ पद पंकज जेहि पूजत अज मम सिर धरेउ कृपाल हरी।।
एहि भाँति सिधारी गौतम नारी बार बार हरि चरन परी।
जो अति मन भावा सो बरु पावा गै पतिलोक अनंद भरी।।
verses.translation: At the touch of His holy feet, destroyer of sorrow, Ahalya — a mass of accumulated austerity — was restored to her true form. Beholding the Lord of the Raghus, giver of joy to His devotees, she stood facing Him with folded hands. Overwhelmed with love, her body thrilling, no words would come from her mouth. Supremely blessed, she fell at His feet as streams of tears flowed from both eyes. Steadying herself, she began to offer praise...

verses.original:
अस प्रभु दीनबंधु हरि कारन रहित दयाल।
तुलसिदास सठ तेहि भजु छाड़ि कपट जंजाल।।211।।
verses.translation: Such is the Lord — friend of the lowly, Hari, compassionate without cause. Tulsidas says: O foolish one, worship Him and abandon the web of deceit!

verses.original:
चले राम लछिमन मुनि संगा। गए जहाँ जग पावनि गंगा।।
गाधिसूनु सब कथा सुनाई। जेहि प्रकार सुरसरि महि आई।।
verses.translation: Rama and Lakshmana proceeded with the sage and arrived where the sacred, world-purifying Ganga flowed. The son of Gadhi narrated the whole story of how the divine river had descended to earth.

verses.original:
तब प्रभु रिषिन्ह समेत नहाए। बिबिध दान महिदेवन्हि पाए।।
हरषि चले मुनि बृंद सहाया। बेगि बिदेह नगर निअराया।।
verses.translation: Then the Lord bathed there along with the sages, and the Brahmins received various gifts. The sage and his company joyfully set forth, and soon they drew near the city of Videha (Mithila).

verses.original:
पुर रम्यता राम जब देखी। हरषे अनुज समेत बिसेषी।।
बापीं कूप सरित सर नाना। सलिल सुधासम मनि सोपाना।।
verses.translation: When Rama beheld the beauty of the city, He and His younger brother were especially delighted. Numerous wells, step-wells, rivers, and lakes — their waters sweet as nectar, with steps made of jewels.

verses.original:
गुंजत मंजु मत्त रस भृंगा। कूजत कल बहुबरन बिहंगा।।
बरन बरन बिकसे बन जाता। त्रिबिध समीर सदा सुखदाता।।
verses.translation: Beautiful bees hummed, drunk on nectar; birds of many colors sang melodiously. Trees of every variety bloomed in the groves; breezes of three kinds always brought delight.

verses.original:
सुमन बाटिका बाग बन बिपुल बिहंग निवास।
फूलत फलत सुपल्लवत सोहत पुर चहुँ पास।।212।।
verses.translation: Flower gardens, orchards, and woods — abounding with countless birds — blooming, fruiting, and putting forth beautiful leaves, they adorned all four sides of the city.

verses.original:
बनइ न बरनत नगर निकाई। जहाँ जाइ मन तहँइँ लोभाई।।
चारु बजारु बिचित्र अँबारी। मनिमय बिधि जनु स्वकर सँवारी।।
verses.translation: The city's beauty cannot be described — wherever the mind goes, it is captivated there. The splendid marketplace had wonderful canopied structures, studded with gems, as if crafted by the Creator's own hand.

verses.original:
धनिक बनिक बर धनद समाना। बैठ सकल बस्तु लै नाना।।
चौहट सुंदर गलीं सुहाई। संतत रहहिं सुगंध सिंचाई।।
verses.translation: Wealthy and prosperous merchants, equal to Kubera, sat with all manner of wares. The crossroads were beautiful, the lanes charming, always sprinkled with fragrant water.

verses.original:
मंगलमय मंदिर सब केरें। चित्रित जनु रतिनाथ चितेरें।।
पुर नर नारि सुभग सुचि संता। धरमसील ग्यानी गुनवंता।।
verses.translation: Every home was auspicious, decorated as if painted by Kamadeva himself. The men and women of the city were handsome, pure, and saintly — righteous, wise, and virtuous.

verses.original:
अति अनूप जहँ जनक निवासू। बिथकहिं बिबुध बिलोकि बिलासू।।
होत चकित चित कोट बिलोकी। सकल भुवन सोभा जनु रोकी।।
verses.translation: Supremely beautiful was Janaka's palace; even the gods were dazzled gazing upon its splendor. The mind was awestruck beholding the fortress, as if the beauty of all the worlds were gathered there.

verses.original:
धवल धाम मनि पुरट पट सुघटित नाना भाँति।
सिय निवास सुंदर सदन सोभा किमि कहि जाति।।213।।
verses.translation: White mansions with gem-studded golden roofs, fashioned in myriad styles — the beauty of Sita's lovely abode, how can it be described?

verses.original:
सुभग द्वार सब कुलिस कपाटा। भूप भीर नट मागध भाटा।।
बनी बिसाल बाजि गज साला। हय गय रथ संकुल सब काला।।
verses.translation: The gates were magnificent with diamond-hard doors; crowds of kings, actors, bards, and minstrels thronged around. Vast stables for horses and elephants were built; they were always bustling with horses, elephants, and chariots.

verses.original:
सूर सचिव सेनप बहुतेरे। नृपगृह सरिस सदन सब केरे।।
पुर बाहेर सर सारित समीपा। उतरे जहँ तहँ बिपुल महीपा।।
verses.translation: Heroes, ministers, and generals were in plenty; their mansions rivaled the king's palace. Outside the city, near lakes and rivers, numerous kings had encamped.

verses.original:
देखि अनूप एक अँवराई। सब सुपास सब भाँति सुहाई।।
कौसिक कहेउ मोर मनु माना। इहाँ रहिअ रघुबीर सुजाना।।
verses.translation: They noticed a uniquely beautiful mango grove — pleasant in every way and delightful from all sides. Kaushika said: 'My heart is pleased — let us stay here, O wise hero of the Raghus.'

verses.original:
भलेहिं नाथ कहि कृपानिकेता। उतरे तहँ मुनिबृंद समेता।।
बिस्वामित्र महामुनि आए। समाचार मिथिलापति पाए।।
verses.translation: 'Very well, my Lord,' said the abode of mercy, and they alighted there along with the company of sages. When the great sage Vishvamitra's arrival was reported, King Janaka of Mithila received the news.

verses.original:
संग सचिव सुचि भूरि भट भूसुर बर गुर ग्याति।
चले मिलन मुनिनायकहि मुदित राउ एहि भाँति।।214।।
verses.translation: Accompanied by trusted ministers, numerous warriors, eminent Brahmins, and family elders and teachers, the delighted king set out in this manner to meet the chief of sages.

verses.original:
कीन्ह प्रनामु चरन धरि माथा। दीन्हि असीस मुदित मुनिनाथा।।
बिप्रबृंद सब सादर बंदे। जानि भाग्य बड़ राउ अनंदे।।
verses.translation: He bowed his head at the sage's feet; the happy sage blessed him. The king reverently saluted all the Brahmins and rejoiced, considering his fortune great.

verses.original:
कुसल प्रस्न कहि बारहिं बारा। बिस्वामित्र नृपहि बैठारा।।
तेहि अवसर आए दोउ भाई। गए रहे देखन फुलवाई।।
verses.translation: Exchanging cordial inquiries again and again, Vishvamitra seated the king. At that moment, the two brothers arrived — they had gone to see a flower garden.

verses.original:
स्याम गौर मृदु बयस किसोरा। लोचन सुखद बिस्व चित चोरा।।
उठे सकल जब रघुपति आए। बिस्वामित्र निकट बैठाए।।
verses.translation: One dark and one fair, of tender youthful age — a delight to the eyes and stealers of the hearts of all the world. All rose when the Lord of the Raghus approached; Vishvamitra seated them near himself.

verses.original:
भए सब सुखी देखि दोउ भ्राता। बारि बिलोचन पुलकित गाता।।
मूरति मधुर मनोहर देखी। भयउ बिदेहु बिदेहु बिसेषी।।
verses.translation: Everyone was filled with joy seeing the two brothers — their eyes streamed with tears and their bodies thrilled. Beholding their sweet and captivating forms, Videha (Janaka) became even more 'Videha' — bodiless with ecstasy.

verses.original:
प्रेम मगन मनु जानि नृपु करि बिबेकु धरि धीर।
बोलेउ मुनि पद नाइ सिरु गदगद गिरा गभीर।।215।।
verses.translation: Knowing his mind to be absorbed in love, the king summoned his discrimination and gathered his composure. He spoke with his head bowed at the sage's feet, his voice choked with emotion and deep.

verses.original:
कहहु नाथ सुंदर दोउ बालक। मुनिकुल तिलक कि नृपकुल पालक।।
ब्रह्म जो निगम नेति कहि गावा। उभय बेष धरि की सोइ आवा।।
verses.translation: 'Tell me, Lord, who are these two beautiful boys? Are they the glory of a sage's lineage or the guardians of a royal dynasty? Or is that Brahman whom the Vedas describe only by saying 'not this, not this' — has He come in these two forms?'

verses.original:
सहज बिरागरुप मनु मोरा। थकित होत जिमि चंद चकोरा।।
ताते प्रभु पूछउँ सतिभाऊ। कहहु नाथ जनि करहु दुराऊ।।
verses.translation: 'My mind, naturally disposed to detachment, is stunned like a chakora gazing at the moon. Therefore, Lord, I ask You truly — tell me, master, do not conceal anything.'

verses.original:
इन्हहि बिलोकत अति अनुरागा। बरबस ब्रह्मसुखहि मन त्यागा।।
कह मुनि बिहसि कहेहु नृप नीका। बचन तुम्हार न होइ अलीका।।
verses.translation: 'Gazing upon them, such intense love arises that my mind involuntarily abandons the bliss of Brahman.' The sage smiled and said: 'You have spoken well, O King — your words cannot be untrue.'

verses.original:
ए प्रिय सबहि जहाँ लगि प्रानी। मन मुसुकाहिं रामु सुनि बानी।।
रघुकुल मनि दसरथ के जाए। मम हित लागि नरेस पठाए।।
verses.translation: 'They are indeed dear to all living beings.' Rama smiled inwardly hearing these words. 'They are the jewels of the Raghu dynasty, born as sons of Dasharatha. The king sent them for my benefit.'

verses.original:
रामु लखनु दोउ बंधुबर रूप सील बल धाम।
मख राखेउ सबु साखि जगु जिते असुर संग्राम।।216।।
verses.translation: 'Rama and Lakshmana — these two excellent brothers are abodes of beauty, virtue, and strength. They protected my sacrifice — the whole world is witness — and conquered the demons in battle.'

verses.original:
मुनि तव चरन देखि कह राऊ। कहि न सकउँ निज पुन्य प्राभाऊ।।
सुंदर स्याम गौर दोउ भ्राता। आनँदहू के आनँद दाता।।
verses.translation: Seeing the sage's feet, the king said, 'I cannot express the glory of my good fortune. These two brothers, one dark and one fair, are givers of bliss to bliss itself.'

verses.original:
इन्ह कै प्रीति परसपर पावनि। कहि न जाइ मन भाव सुहावनि।।
सुनहु नाथ कह मुदित बिदेहू। ब्रह्म जीव इव सहज सनेहू।।
verses.translation: Their mutual love is so pure and holy that it cannot be described — the feeling in the heart is too beautiful for words. 'Listen, my lord,' said the delighted Videha (Janaka), 'their love is as natural as that between Brahman and the soul.'

verses.original:
पुनि पुनि प्रभुहि चितव नरनाहू। पुलक गात उर अधिक उछाहू।।
म्रुनिहि प्रसंसि नाइ पद सीसू। चलेउ लवाइ नगर अवनीसू।।
verses.translation: Again and again the king gazed at the Lord; his body thrilled with joy and his heart overflowed with delight. Praising the sage and bowing his head at his feet, the king led them toward the city.

verses.original:
सुंदर सदनु सुखद सब काला। तहाँ बासु लै दीन्ह भुआला।।
करि पूजा सब बिधि सेवकाई। गयउ राउ गृह बिदा कराई।।
verses.translation: He gave them lodging in a beautiful mansion that was pleasant in all seasons. After performing worship and rendering all kinds of service, the king took leave and returned to his own palace.

verses.original:
रिषय संग रघुबंस मनि करि भोजनु बिश्रामु।
बैठे प्रभु भ्राता सहित दिवसु रहा भरि जामु।।217।।
verses.translation: With the sages, the jewel of the Raghu dynasty took his meal and rested. The Lord sat with his brother as the remaining watch of the day passed.

verses.original:
लखन हृदयँ लालसा बिसेषी। जाइ जनकपुर आइअ देखी।।
प्रभु भय बहुरि मुनिहि सकुचाहीं। प्रगट न कहहिं मनहिं मुसुकाहीं।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana had a great longing in his heart to go and see the city of Janakpur. But out of awe for the Lord and hesitation before the sage, he did not speak openly but only smiled to himself.

verses.original:
राम अनुज मन की गति जानी। भगत बछलता हिंयँ हुलसानी।।
परम बिनीत सकुचि मुसुकाई। बोले गुर अनुसासन पाई।।
verses.translation: Rama perceived the desire in his younger brother's heart, and his love for his devotees welled up with delight. With utmost humility and a shy smile, he spoke after receiving the guru's permission.

verses.original:
नाथ लखनु पुरु देखन चहहीं। प्रभु सकोच डर प्रगट न कहहीं।।
जौं राउर आयसु मैं पावौं। नगर देखाइ तुरत लै आवौ।।
verses.translation: 'Lord, Lakshmana wishes to see the city, but out of awe and modesty he does not say so openly. If I have your permission, I shall show him the city and bring him back at once.'

verses.original:
सुनि मुनीसु कह बचन सप्रीती। कस न राम तुम्ह राखहु नीती।।
धरम सेतु पालक तुम्ह ताता। प्रेम बिबस सेवक सुखदाता।।
verses.translation: Hearing this, the great sage spoke with affection, 'How could it be otherwise, Rama? You always uphold propriety. You are the guardian of the bridge of dharma, dear son, and the giver of joy to your servants, being moved by love.'

verses.original:
जाइ देखी आवहु नगरु सुख निधान दोउ भाइ।
करहु सुफल सब के नयन सुंदर बदन देखाइ।।218।।
verses.translation: 'Go and see the city, O two brothers who are treasuries of happiness. Bless everyone's eyes by showing them your beautiful faces.'

verses.original:
मुनि पद कमल बंदि दोउ भ्राता। चले लोक लोचन सुख दाता।।
बालक बृंदि देखि अति सोभा। लगे संग लोचन मनु लोभा।।
verses.translation: Bowing at the lotus feet of the sage, the two brothers set forth — givers of delight to the eyes of the world. Seeing their extraordinary beauty, groups of children followed along, their eyes and minds captivated.

verses.original:
पीत बसन परिकर कटि भाथा। चारु चाप सर सोहत हाथा।।
तन अनुहरत सुचंदन खोरी। स्यामल गौर मनोहर जोरी।।
verses.translation: They wore yellow garments with quivers fastened at their waists; beautiful bows and arrows adorned their hands. Their bodies were anointed with lovely sandalwood paste — a captivating pair, one dark and one fair.

verses.original:
केहरि कंधर बाहु बिसाला। उर अति रुचिर नागमनि माला।।
सुभग सोन सरसीरुह लोचन। बदन मयंक तापत्रय मोचन।।
verses.translation: They had lion-like necks and mighty arms; on their broad chests gleamed beautiful necklaces of serpent-gems. Their eyes were like lovely golden lotuses; their faces were like the moon, dispelling the threefold afflictions.

verses.original:
कानन्हि कनक फूल छबि देहीं। चितवत चितहि चोरि जनु लेहीं।।
चितवनि चारु भृकुटि बर बाँकी। तिलक रेखा सोभा जनु चाँकी।।
verses.translation: Golden flowers in their ears added to their beauty; their glances seemed to steal away one's heart. Their charming gaze, their fine curved eyebrows, and the tilak marks on their foreheads shone like painted beauty.

verses.original:
रुचिर चौतनीं सुभग सिर मेचक कुंचित केस।
नख सिख सुंदर बंधु दोउ सोभा सकल सुदेस।।219।।
verses.translation: Beautiful diadems adorned their handsome heads with dark, curly hair. From head to toe, the two brothers were resplendent, beautifying every direction.

verses.original:
देखन नगरु भूपसुत आए। समाचार पुरबासिन्ह पाए।।
धाए धाम काम सब त्यागी। मनहु रंक निधि लूटन लागी।।
verses.translation: The princes had come to see the city, and the townsfolk received the news. They rushed from their homes, abandoning all their work, as if paupers had found a treasure to plunder.

verses.original:
निरखि सहज सुंदर दोउ भाई। होहिं सुखी लोचन फल पाई।।
जुबतीं भवन झरोखन्हि लागीं। निरखहिं राम रूप अनुरागीं।।
verses.translation: Gazing at the naturally beautiful pair of brothers, they became happy, having obtained the fruit of their eyes. Young women stood at the latticed windows of their homes, gazing at Rama's beauty with deep love.

verses.original:
कहहिं परसपर बचन सप्रीती। सखि इन्ह कोटि काम छबि जीती।।
सुर नर असुर नाग मुनि माहीं। सोभा असि कहुँ सुनिअति नाहीं।।
verses.translation: They spoke to one another with affection, 'O friend, their beauty has conquered millions of Kamadevas. Among gods, men, demons, nagas, and sages, such beauty has never been seen or heard of.'

verses.original:
बिष्नु चारि भुज बिघि मुख चारी। बिकट बेष मुख पंच पुरारी।।
अपर देउ अस कोउ न आही। यह छबि सखि पटतरिअ जाही।।
verses.translation: 'Vishnu has four arms, Brahma has four faces, and Shiva has a fearsome form with five faces. No other god compares — who can be set beside this beauty, dear friend?'

verses.original:
बय किसोर सुषमा सदन स्याम गौर सुख घाम।
अंग अंग पर वारिअहिं कोटि कोटि सत काम।।220।।
verses.translation: In the bloom of adolescence, abodes of loveliness, one dark and one fair, rays of bliss — upon each of their limbs one could sacrifice millions upon millions of Kamadevas.

verses.original:
कहहु सखी अस को तनुधारी। जो न मोह यह रूप निहारी।।
कोउ सप्रेम बोली मृदु बानी। जो मैं सुना सो सुनहु सयानी।।
verses.translation: 'Tell me, friend, who in this world could see such beauty and not be enchanted?' Another spoke in a gentle, loving voice, 'What I have heard, O wise one, listen now.'

verses.original:
ए दोऊ दसरथ के ढोटा। बाल मरालन्हि के कल जोटा।।
मुनि कौसिक मख के रखवारे। जिन्ह रन अजिर निसाचर मारे।।
verses.translation: 'These two are the sons of Dasharatha — a beautiful pair like young swans. They are protectors of Sage Kaushika's sacrifice, the ones who slew the demons on the battlefield.'

verses.original:
स्याम गात कल कंज बिलोचन। जो मारीच सुभुज मदु मोचन।।
कौसल्या सुत सो सुख खानी। नामु रामु धनु सायक पानी।।
verses.translation: 'The dark-complexioned one with beautiful lotus eyes, who vanquished the demon Maricha and Subahu — he is the son of Kausalya, that treasury of bliss. His name is Rama, and he holds a bow and arrows in his hands.'

verses.original:
गौर किसोर बेषु बर काछें। कर सर चाप राम के पाछें।।
लछिमनु नामु राम लघु भ्राता। सुनु सखि तासु सुमित्रा माता।।
verses.translation: 'The fair youth in fine attire, walking behind Rama with arrows and bow — his name is Lakshmana, Rama's younger brother. Know, O friend, that his mother is Sumitra.'

verses.original:
बिप्रकाजु करि बंधु दोउ मग मुनिबधू उधारि।
आए देखन चापमख सुनि हरषीं सब नारि।।221।।
verses.translation: 'Having accomplished the Brahmins' purpose, the two brothers liberated the sage's wife on the way, and have now come to see the bow-sacrifice.' Hearing this, all the women were delighted.

verses.original:
देखि राम छबि कोउ एक कहई। जोगु जानकिहि यह बरु अहई।।
जौ सखि इन्हहि देख नरनाहू। पन परिहरि हठि करइ बिबाहू।।
verses.translation: Seeing Rama's beauty, someone said, 'This groom is worthy of Janaki. If only the king could see them, he would abandon his vow and insist on arranging the marriage.'

verses.original:
कोउ कह ए भूपति पहिचाने। मुनि समेत सादर सनमाने।।
सखि परंतु पनु राउ न तजई। बिधि बस हठि अबिबेकहि भजई।।
verses.translation: Another said, 'The king has recognized these princes and has honored them along with the sage. But, friend, the king will not abandon his vow; under fate's sway, he stubbornly clings to folly.'

verses.original:
कोउ कह जौं भल अहइ बिधाता। सब कहँ सुनिअ उचित फलदाता।।
तौ जानकिहि मिलिहि बरु एहू। नाहिन आलि इहाँ संदेहू।।
verses.translation: Another said, 'If the Creator is good, and if he is heard to be the just giver of results to all, then Janaki will obtain this very groom. There is no doubt about this, dear friend.'

verses.original:
जौ बिधि बस अस बनै सँजोगू। तौ कृतकृत्य होइ सब लोगू।।
सखि हमरें आरति अति तातें। कबहुँक ए आवहिं एहि नातें।।
verses.translation: 'If by fate's will such a union comes to pass, then all the world will be fulfilled. Friend, our longing is so great — perhaps he has come for this very reason.'

verses.original:
नाहिं त हम कहुँ सुनहु सखि इन्ह कर दरसनु दूरि।
यह संघटु तब होइ जब पुन्य पुराकृत भूरि।।222।।
verses.translation: 'Otherwise, listen friend, the sight of these two will remain far from us. Such a meeting can only happen when one has an abundance of merit from past lives.'

verses.original:
बोली अपर कहेहु सखि नीका। एहिं बिआह अति हित सबहीं का।।
कोउ कह संकर चाप कठोरा। ए स्यामल मृदुगात किसोरा।।
verses.translation: Another spoke, 'You have said well, friend. This marriage would be in everyone's best interest.' Another said, 'Shiva's bow is exceedingly hard, and these are delicate-limbed dark youths.'

verses.original:
सबु असमंजस अहइ सयानी। यह सुनि अपर कहइ मृदु बानी।।
सखि इन्ह कहँ कोउ कोउ अस कहहीं। बड़ प्रभाउ देखत लघु अहहीं।।
verses.translation: 'The whole situation is perplexing, O wise one.' Hearing this, another spoke softly, 'Friend, some say about them — those who appear small can have great power.'

verses.original:
परसि जासु पद पंकज धूरी। तरी अहल्या कृत अघ भूरी।।
सो कि रहिहि बिनु सिवधनु तोरें। यह प्रतीति परिहरिअ न भोरें।।
verses.translation: 'By the touch of the dust of whose lotus feet, Ahalya was liberated from her heavy sins — can he possibly fail to break Shiva's bow? Never let go of this faith.'

verses.original:
जेहिं बिरंचि रचि सीय सँवारी। तेहिं स्यामल बरु रचेउ बिचारी।।
तासु बचन सुनि सब हरषानीं। ऐसेइ होउ कहहिं मुदु बानी।।
verses.translation: 'The Creator who fashioned and adorned Sita has thoughtfully created this dark-complexioned groom for her.' Hearing her words, all the women were delighted and said softly, 'May it be so indeed!'

verses.original:
हियँ हरषहिं बरषहिं सुमन सुमुखि सुलोचनि बृंद।
जाहिं जहाँ जहँ बंधु दोउ तहँ तहँ परमानंद।।223।।
verses.translation: With joy in their hearts, the beautiful, bright-eyed women showered flowers. Wherever the two brothers went, there was supreme bliss.

verses.original:
पुर पूरब दिसि गे दोउ भाई। जहँ धनुमख हित भूमि बनाई।।
अति बिस्तार चारु गच ढारी। बिमल बेदिका रुचिर सँवारी।।
verses.translation: The two brothers went to the eastern part of the city, where the ground had been prepared for the bow-sacrifice. It was vast and spacious, with a beautiful plastered floor and a splendid, spotless platform.

verses.original:
चहुँ दिसि कंचन मंच बिसाला। रचे जहाँ बेठहिं महिपाला।।
तेहि पाछें समीप चहुँ पासा। अपर मंच मंडली बिलासा।।
verses.translation: On all four sides were large golden galleries, built for the kings to sit upon. Behind these, nearby on all four sides, were other rows of galleries.

verses.original:
कछुक ऊँचि सब भाँति सुहाई। बैठहिं नगर लोग जहँ जाई।।
तिन्ह के निकट बिसाल सुहाए। धवल धाम बहुबरन बनाए।।
verses.translation: Somewhat elevated and beautiful in every way, these were where the townspeople could come and sit. Near these were large and handsome white mansions, decorated in many colors.

verses.original:
जहँ बैंठैं देखहिं सब नारी। जथा जोगु निज कुल अनुहारी।।
पुर बालक कहि कहि मृदु बचना। सादर प्रभुहि देखावहिं रचना।।
verses.translation: There the women could sit and watch, each according to her rank and family. The children of the city, speaking sweet words, respectfully showed the Lord the arrangements.

verses.original:
सब सिसु एहि मिस प्रेमबस परसि मनोहर गात।
तन पुलकहिं अति हरषु हियँ देखि देखि दोउ भ्रात।।224।।
verses.translation: Under the pretext of these activities, all the children, overwhelmed with love, touched the Lord's charming body. Their bodies thrilled with ecstasy in their hearts, gazing again and again at the two brothers.

verses.original:
सिसु सब राम प्रेमबस जाने। प्रीति समेत निकेत बखाने।।
निज निज रुचि सब लेंहिं बोलाई। सहित सनेह जाहिं दोउ भाई।।
verses.translation: Rama knew that all the children were captivated by love, and he lovingly described the different buildings to them. Each child, according to his own preference, would invite them, and the two brothers went with them affectionately.

verses.original:
राम देखावहिं अनुजहि रचना। कहि मृदु मधुर मनोहर बचना।।
लव निमेष महँ भुवन निकाया। रचइ जासु अनुसासन माया।।
verses.translation: Rama showed his younger brother the arrangements, speaking sweet, tender, and charming words. He whose command causes Maya to create multitudes of universes in the twinkling of an eye —

verses.original:
भगति हेतु सोइ दीनदयाला। चितवत चकित धनुष मखसाला।।
कौतुक देखि चले गुरु पाहीं। जानि बिलंबु त्रास मन माहीं।।
verses.translation: That same compassionate Lord, for the sake of devotion, gazed with wonder at the bow-sacrifice arena. After seeing the spectacle, they headed back to the guru, feeling anxious in their hearts at the thought of being late.

verses.original:
जासु त्रास डर कहुँ डर होई। भजन प्रभाउ देखावत सोई।।
कहि बातें मृदु मधुर सुहाईं। किए बिदा बालक बरिआई।।
verses.translation: He, the fear of whose very fear itself causes all fear, was demonstrating the power of devotion. Speaking sweet, gentle, and pleasant words, they bid farewell to the children with great effort.

verses.original:
सभय सप्रेम बिनीत अति सकुच सहित दोउ भाइ।
गुर पद पंकज नाइ सिर बैठे आयसु पाइ।।225।।
verses.translation: Timid, loving, and extremely humble, the two brothers bowed their heads at the guru's lotus feet with great modesty and sat down after receiving his permission.

verses.original:
निसि प्रबेस मुनि आयसु दीन्हा। सबहीं संध्याबंदनु कीन्हा।।
कहत कथा इतिहास पुरानी। रुचिर रजनि जुग जाम सिरानी।।
verses.translation: At nightfall, the sage gave his command, and all performed their evening worship. Telling stories from history and the Puranas, two watches of the delightful night passed.

verses.original:
मुनिबर सयन कीन्हि तब जाई। लगे चरन चापन दोउ भाई।।
जिन्ह के चरन सरोरुह लागी। करत बिबिध जप जोग बिरागी।।
verses.translation: The great sage then went to sleep. The two brothers began pressing his feet — those lotus feet for which ascetics practice various forms of japa, yoga, and renunciation.

verses.original:
तेइ दोउ बंधु प्रेम जनु जीते। गुर पद कमल पलोटत प्रीते।।
बारबार मुनि अग्या दीन्ही। रघुबर जाइ सयन तब कीन्ही।।
verses.translation: Those two brothers, as if conquered by love, lovingly massaged the guru's lotus feet. Again and again the sage ordered them to rest, and then the chief of the Raghus went and lay down.

verses.original:
चापत चरन लखनु उर लाएँ। सभय सप्रेम परम सचु पाएँ।।
पुनि पुनि प्रभु कह सोवहु ताता। पौढ़े धरि उर पद जलजाता।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana pressed Rama's feet, holding them to his chest, fearful yet loving, experiencing supreme joy. Again and again the Lord said, 'Sleep now, dear brother,' and Lakshmana lay down, clasping the Lord's lotus feet to his heart.

verses.original:
उठे लखन निसि बिगत सुनि अरुनसिखा धुनि कान।।
गुर तें पहिलेहिं जगतपति जागे रामु सुजान।।226।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana rose when the night was over, hearing the crowing of the rooster. But the all-knowing Lord of the universe, Rama, had already awakened even before the guru.

verses.original:
सकल सौच करि जाइ नहाए। नित्य निबाहि मुनिहि सिर नाए।।
समय जानि गुर आयसु पाई। लेन प्रसून चले दोउ भाई।।
verses.translation: After completing all their morning duties and bathing, they bowed their heads to the sage and carried out their daily rituals. Knowing the time was right and receiving the guru's permission, the two brothers set out to gather flowers.

verses.original:
भूप बागु बर देखेउ जाई। जहँ बसंत रितु रही लोभाई।।
लागे बिटप मनोहर नाना। बरन बरन बर बेलि बिताना।।
verses.translation: They went and saw a beautiful royal garden, where the spring season seemed to linger in delight. It was filled with many charming trees, with vines of various colors forming canopies.

verses.original:
नव पल्लव फल सुमान सुहाए। निज संपति सुर रूख लजाए।।
चातक कोकिल कीर चकोरा। कूजत बिहग नटत कल मोरा।।
verses.translation: The fresh leaves, fruits, and beautiful flowers put even the trees of heaven to shame with their splendor. Chataka birds, cuckoos, parrots, and chakora birds called out, while beautiful peacocks danced.

verses.original:
मध्य बाग सरु सोह सुहावा। मनि सोपान बिचित्र बनावा।।
बिमल सलिलु सरसिज बहुरंगा। जलखग कूजत गुंजत भृंगा।।
verses.translation: In the center of the garden sparkled a lovely lake with jeweled steps and wondrous design. Its clear waters held lotuses of many colors, while water-birds sang and bees hummed.

verses.original:
बागु तड़ागु बिलोकि प्रभु हरषे बंधु समेत।
परम रम्य आरामु यहु जो रामहि सुख देत।।227।।
verses.translation: The Lord was delighted to behold the garden and lake along with his brother. This grove was supremely beautiful — such as could give pleasure even to Rama himself.

verses.original:
चहुँ दिसि चितइ पूँछि मालिगन। लगे लेन दल फूल मुदित मन।।
तेहि अवसर सीता तहँ आई। गिरिजा पूजन जननि पठाई।।
verses.translation: Looking around in all four directions and asking the gardeners, they joyfully began gathering leaves and flowers. At that very time, Sita arrived there, sent by her mother to worship Goddess Girija (Parvati).

verses.original:
संग सखीं सब सुभग सयानी। गावहिं गीत मनोहर बानी।।
सर समीप गिरिजा गृह सोहा। बरनि न जाइ देखि मनु मोहा।।
verses.translation: With her were all her companions — beautiful, wise maidens — singing melodious songs in enchanting voices. Near the lake stood a beautiful temple of Girija; its beauty was indescribable and captivating to the mind.

verses.original:
मज्जनु करि सर सखिन्ह समेता। गई मुदित मन गौरि निकेता।।
पूजा कीन्हि अधिक अनुरागा। निज अनुरूप सुभग बरु मागा।।
verses.translation: After bathing in the lake with her companions, Sita went joyfully to Gauri's temple. She worshipped with great devotion and asked for a handsome groom worthy of herself.

verses.original:
एक सखी सिय संगु बिहाई। गई रही देखन फुलवाई।।
तेहि दोउ बंधु बिलोके जाई। प्रेम बिबस सीता पहिं आई।।
verses.translation: One companion, leaving Sita's side, had gone to see the flower garden. There she beheld the two brothers, and overwhelmed with love, she came back to Sita.

verses.original:
तासु दसा देखि सखिन्ह पुलक गात जलु नैन।
कहु कारनु निज हरष कर पूछहि सब मृदु बैन।।228।।
verses.translation: Seeing her condition — her body thrilling and tears in her eyes — the companions asked, 'Tell us the reason for your joy,' inquiring in gentle tones.

verses.original:
देखन बागु कुअँर दुइ आए। बय किसोर सब भाँति सुहाए।।
स्याम गौर किमि कहौं बखानी। गिरा अनयन नयन बिनु बानी।।
verses.translation: 'Two princes have come to see the garden — adolescents, beautiful in every way. One dark, one fair — how shall I describe them? Speech has no eyes, and eyes have no speech.'

verses.original:
सुनि हरषीं सब सखीं सयानी। सिय हियँ अति उतकंठा जानी।।
एक कहइ नृपसुत तेइ आली। सुने जे मुनि सँग आए काली।।
verses.translation: Hearing this, all the wise companions were delighted, knowing the great longing in Sita's heart. One said, 'Friend, those are the very princes we heard about, who came yesterday with the sage.'

verses.original:
जिन्ह निज रूप मोहनी डारी। कीन्ह स्वबस नगर नर नारी।।
बरनत छबि जहँ तहँ सब लोगू। अवसि देखिअहिं देखन जोगू।।
verses.translation: 'They have cast the spell of their beauty, bringing all the men and women of the city under their sway. Everywhere people are describing their beauty — they are certainly worth seeing.'

verses.original:
तासु वचन अति सियहि सुहाने। दरस लागि लोचन अकुलाने।।
चली अग्र करि प्रिय सखि सोई। प्रीति पुरातन लखइ न कोई।।
verses.translation: Her words were deeply pleasing to Sita; her eyes became restless with longing for a glimpse. She walked forward, led by that dear companion, and no one perceived the ancient love between them.

verses.original:
सुमिरि सीय नारद बचन उपजी प्रीति पुनीत।।
चकित बिलोकति सकल दिसि जनु सिसु मृगी सभीत।।229।।
verses.translation: Remembering Narada's words, pure love arose in Sita's heart. She looked about in all directions, startled, like a young doe full of fear.

verses.original:
कंकन किंकिनि नूपुर धुनि सुनि। कहत लखन सन रामु हृदयँ गुनि।।
मानहुँ मदन दुंदुभी दीन्ही।।मनसा बिस्व बिजय कहँ कीन्ही।।
verses.translation: Hearing the tinkling of bangles, anklets, and waist-bells, Rama reflected in his heart and said to Lakshmana, 'It seems as though Kamadeva has sounded his war-drum, resolving to conquer the entire world.'

verses.original:
अस कहि फिरि चितए तेहि ओरा। सिय मुख ससि भए नयन चकोरा।।
भए बिलोचन चारु अचंचल। मनहुँ सकुचि निमि तजे दिगंचल।।
verses.translation: Saying this, he turned and looked in that direction. His eyes became like chakora birds gazing upon the moon of Sita's face. His beautiful eyes became motionless, as if the eyelids, embarrassed, had abandoned their restless nature.

verses.original:
देखि सीय सोभा सुखु पावा। हृदयँ सराहत बचनु न आवा।।
जनु बिरंचि सब निज निपुनाई। बिरचि बिस्व कहँ प्रगटि देखाई।।
verses.translation: Beholding Sita's beauty, he felt supreme bliss; his heart praised her but no words would come. It was as though the Creator had gathered all his skill and displayed it for the world to see.

verses.original:
सुंदरता कहुँ सुंदर करई। छबिगृहँ दीपसिखा जनु बरई।।
सब उपमा कबि रहे जुठारी। केहिं पटतरौं बिदेहकुमारी।।
verses.translation: She who makes beauty itself beautiful — as if a lamp were burning in the very house of splendor. All comparisons that poets could make have been rendered stale; to whom can the princess of Videha be compared?

verses.original:
सिय सोभा हियँ बरनि प्रभु आपनि दसा बिचारि।
बोले सुचि मन अनुज सन बचन समय अनुहारि।।230।।
verses.translation: Having described Sita's beauty in his heart and reflecting on his own state, the Lord spoke to his younger brother with a pure mind, words befitting the occasion.

verses.original:
तात जनकतनया यह सोई। धनुषजग्य जेहि कारन होई।।
पूजन गौरि सखीं लै आई। करत प्रकासु फिरइ फुलवाई।।
verses.translation: 'Brother, this is the very daughter of Janaka, for whose sake the bow-sacrifice is being held. She has come with her companions to worship Gauri, and now walks through the garden, spreading radiance.'

verses.original:
जासु बिलोकि अलोकिक सोभा। सहज पुनीत मोर मनु छोभा।।
सो सबु कारन जान बिधाता। फरकहिं सुभद अंग सुनु भ्राता।।
verses.translation: 'Beholding her extraordinary beauty, my naturally pure mind is agitated. The Creator knows the cause of all this. Listen, brother — my auspicious limbs are throbbing.'

verses.original:
रघुबंसिन्ह कर सहज सुभाऊ। मनु कुपंथ पगु धरइ न काऊ।।
मोहि अतिसय प्रतीति मन केरी। जेहिं सपनेहुँ परनारि न हेरी।।
verses.translation: 'It is the innate nature of the Raghu dynasty that the mind never treads a wrong path. I have this absolute conviction in my heart — one who has never looked at another's wife, even in a dream.'

verses.original:
जिन्ह कै लहहिं न रिपु रन पीठी। नहिं पावहिं परतिय मनु डीठी।।
मंगन लहहि न जिन्ह कै नाहीं। ते नरबर थोरे जग माहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Those whose backs the enemy never sees in battle, who never cast their eyes or minds upon another's wife, from whom supplicants never go away empty-handed — such noble men are rare in this world.'

verses.original:
करत बतकहि अनुज सन मन सिय रूप लोभान।
मुख सरोज मकरंद छबि करइ मधुप इव पान।।231।।
verses.translation: While talking thus with his brother, Rama's mind was captivated by Sita's beauty. Like a bee, he drank in the nectar of the beauty of her lotus face.

verses.original:
चितवहि चकित चहूँ दिसि सीता। कहँ गए नृपकिसोर मनु चिंता।।
जहँ बिलोक मृग सावक नैनी। जनु तहँ बरिस कमल सित श्रेनी।।
verses.translation: Sita looked about in all directions, startled, wondering where the princes had gone. Wherever her doe-like eyes gazed, it was as though white lotuses rained down there.

verses.original:
लता ओट तब सखिन्ह लखाए। स्यामल गौर किसोर सुहाए।।
देखि रूप लोचन ललचाने। हरषे जनु निज निधि पहिचाने।।
verses.translation: Then her companions pointed out to her from behind a creeper the two charming youths, one dark and one fair. Seeing their beauty, her eyes were delighted, as though they had found their own lost treasure.

verses.original:
थके नयन रघुपति छबि देखें। पलकन्हिहूँ परिहरीं निमेषें।।
अधिक सनेहँ देह भै भोरी। सरद ससिहि जनु चितव चकोरी।।
verses.translation: Her eyes grew weary gazing upon Rama's beauty; her eyelids refused even to blink. Overwhelmed by deep love, she became oblivious of her body, like a chakori bird gazing at the autumn moon.

verses.original:
लोचन मग रामहि उर आनी। दीन्हे पलक कपाट सयानी।।
जब सिय सखिन्ह प्रेमबस जानी। कहि न सकहिं कछु मन सकुचानी।।
verses.translation: Through the pathway of her eyes, she drew Rama into her heart and then wisely closed the doors of her eyelids. When the companions saw Sita lost in love, they were too bashful to say anything, their minds full of modesty.

verses.original:
लताभवन तें प्रगट भे तेहि अवसर दोउ भाइ।
निकसे जनु जुग बिमल बिधु जलद पटल बिलगाइ।।232।।
verses.translation: At that moment, the two brothers emerged from the bower of creepers, appearing like two bright moons freed from a veil of clouds.

verses.original:
सोभा सीवँ सुभग दोउ बीरा। नील पीत जलजाभ सरीरा।।
मोरपंख सिर सोहत नीके। गुच्छ बीच बिच कुसुम कली के।।
verses.translation: The two heroes were the very limits of beauty, their bodies blue and golden like lotuses. Peacock plumes adorned their heads beautifully, with flower buds arranged here and there among them.

verses.original:
भाल तिलक श्रमबिंदु सुहाए। श्रवन सुभग भूषन छबि छाए।।
बिकट भृकुटि कच घूघरवारे। नव सरोज लोचन रतनारे।।
verses.translation: The tilak marks and drops of perspiration on their foreheads looked lovely; beautiful ornaments adorned their ears and added to their splendor. They had striking eyebrows, curly locks of hair, and reddish eyes like fresh lotuses.

verses.original:
चारु चिबुक नासिका कपोला। हास बिलास लेत मनु मोला।।
मुखछबि कहि न जाइ मोहि पाहीं। जो बिलोकि बहु काम लजाहीं।।
verses.translation: Their charming chins, noses, and cheeks, and their playful smiles captivated the heart. I cannot describe the beauty of their faces — faces whose sight puts countless Kamadevas to shame.

verses.original:
उर मनि माल कंबु कल गीवा। काम कलभ कर भुज बलसींवा।।
सुमन समेत बाम कर दोना। सावँर कुअँर सखी सुठि लोना।।
verses.translation: On their chests were jeweled necklaces, their necks were graceful like conch shells, and their arms were powerful as the trunks of young Kamadeva-elephants — the very boundaries of strength. With flower offerings in their left hands, the dark-complexioned prince was exceedingly charming, O friend.

verses.original:
केहरि कटि पट पीत धर सुषमा सील निधान।
देखि भानुकुलभूषनहि बिसरा सखिन्ह अपान।।233।।
verses.translation: With lion-like waists, wearing yellow garments, treasuries of beauty and virtue — seeing the ornament of the Solar dynasty, the companions forgot themselves.

verses.original:
धरि धीरजु एक आलि सयानी। सीता सन बोली गहि पानी।।
बहुरि गौरि कर ध्यान करेहू। भूपकिसोर देखि किन लेहू।।
verses.translation: Gathering her courage, one wise companion took Sita's hand and said, 'First complete your meditation on Gauri; then you can look at the princes.'

verses.original:
सकुचि सीयँ तब नयन उघारे। सनमुख दोउ रघुसिंघ निहारे।।
नख सिख देखि राम कै सोभा। सुमिरि पिता पनु मनु अति छोभा।।
verses.translation: Bashfully, Sita then opened her eyes and beheld the two lions of the Raghu clan standing before her. Seeing Rama's beauty from head to toe and remembering her father's vow, her mind was greatly troubled.

verses.original:
परबस सखिन्ह लखी जब सीता। भयउ गहरु सब कहहि सभीता।।
पुनि आउब एहि बेरिआँ काली। अस कहि मन बिहसी एक आली।।
verses.translation: When the companions saw Sita helplessly lost in love, they all said anxiously, 'It is getting late.' 'We shall come again at this very hour tomorrow,' said one companion, smiling inwardly.

verses.original:
गूढ़ गिरा सुनि सिय सकुचानी। भयउ बिलंबु मातु भय मानी।।
धरि बड़ि धीर रामु उर आने। फिरि अपनपउ पितुबस जाने।।
verses.translation: Hearing this veiled speech, Sita felt shy. Fearing her mother's displeasure at the delay, she summoned great courage, drew Rama into her heart, and turned away, knowing herself bound by her father's will.

verses.original:
देखन मिस मृग बिहग तरु फिरइ बहोरि बहोरि।
निरखि निरखि रघुबीर छबि बाढ़इ प्रीति न थोरि।। 234।।
verses.translation: Under the pretext of looking at deer, birds, and trees, she turned back again and again. Gazing repeatedly at the beauty of the hero of the Raghus, her love only kept growing.

verses.original:
जानि कठिन सिवचाप बिसूरति। चली राखि उर स्यामल मूरति।।
प्रभु जब जात जानकी जानी। सुख सनेह सोभा गुन खानी।।
verses.translation: Knowing Shiva's bow to be unyielding, she sighed, but departed carrying the dark image of the Lord in her heart. When the Lord saw Janaki departing — she who was a mine of happiness, love, beauty, and virtue —

verses.original:
परम प्रेममय मृदु मसि कीन्ही। चारु चित भीतीं लिख लीन्ही।।
गई भवानी भवन बहोरी। बंदि चरन बोली कर जोरी।।
verses.translation: He made supremely loving ink in his heart and painted her beautiful image on the canvas of his mind. Sita returned to Bhavani's temple, and bowing at her feet, she spoke with folded hands.

verses.original:
जय जय गिरिबरराज किसोरी। जय महेस मुख चंद चकोरी।।
जय गज बदन षड़ानन माता। जगत जननि दामिनि दुति गाता।।
verses.translation: 'Victory, victory to you, O daughter of the king of mountains! Victory to you, the chakori bird gazing at the moon-face of Lord Mahesha! Victory, O mother of Ganesha and Kartikeya! O mother of the world, whose body gleams like lightning!'

verses.original:
नहिं तव आदि मध्य अवसाना। अमित प्रभाउ बेदु नहिं जाना।।
भव भव बिभव पराभव कारिनि। बिस्व बिमोहनि स्वबस बिहारिनि।।
verses.translation: 'You have no beginning, middle, or end; the Vedas cannot comprehend your infinite power. You are the cause of creation, sustenance, and dissolution of worldly existence; you enchant the universe yet sport according to your own will.'

verses.original:
पतिदेवता सुतीय महुँ मातु प्रथम तव रेख।
महिमा अमित न सकहिं कहि सहस सारदा सेष।।235।।
verses.translation: 'Among devoted wives, O Mother, you hold the foremost place. Your glory is limitless — even a thousand Saraswatis and Sheshas cannot describe it.'

verses.original:
सेवत तोहि सुलभ फल चारी। बरदायनी पुरारि पिआरी।।
देबि पूजि पद कमल तुम्हारे। सुर नर मुनि सब होहिं सुखारे।।
verses.translation: 'By serving you, the four fruits of life are easily obtained, O bestower of boons, O beloved of Lord Shiva. O Goddess, by worshipping your lotus feet, gods, men, and sages all find happiness.'

verses.original:
मोर मनोरथु जानहु नीकें। बसहु सदा उर पुर सबही कें।।
कीन्हेउँ प्रगट न कारन तेहीं। अस कहि चरन गहे बैदेहीं।।
verses.translation: 'You know well the desire of my heart; may you dwell forever in the heart-city of all. I have not stated my wish openly for that very reason.' So saying, the princess of Videha clasped her feet.

verses.original:
बिनय प्रेम बस भई भवानी। खसी माल मूरति मुसुकानी।।
सादर सियँ प्रसादु सिर धरेऊ। बोली गौरि हरषु हियँ भरेऊ।।
verses.translation: Overcome by Sita's devotion and love, Bhavani was pleased — the garland fell from the image, and it smiled. Sita reverently placed the blessed offering on her head, and Gauri spoke, her heart brimming with joy.

verses.original:
सुनु सिय सत्य असीस हमारी। पूजिहि मन कामना तुम्हारी।।
नारद बचन सदा सुचि साचा। सो बरु मिलिहि जाहिं मनु राचा।।
verses.translation: 'Listen, Sita, my blessing is true: the desire of your heart shall be fulfilled. Narada's words are ever pure and true — you shall obtain the groom on whom your heart is set.'

verses.original:
मनु जाहिं राचेउ मिलिहि सो बरु सहज सुंदर साँवरो।
करुना निधान सुजान सीलु सनेहु जानत रावरो।।
एहि भाँति गौरि असीस सुनि सिय सहित हियँ हरषीं अली।
तुलसी भवानिहि पूजि पुनि पुनि मुदित मन मंदिर चली।।
verses.translation: The one on whom your heart is set — that naturally handsome, dark-complexioned groom — you shall obtain him. He is an ocean of mercy, wise, and knows well your virtue and love. Hearing Gauri's blessing thus, Sita and all her friends were delighted at heart. Tulsi says, after worshipping Bhavani again and again, she returned joyfully to her home.

verses.original:
जानि गौरि अनुकूल सिय हिय हरषु न जाइ कहि।
मंजुल मंगल मूल बाम अंग फरकन लगे।।236।।
verses.translation: Knowing Gauri to be favorable, Sita's heart was filled with inexpressible joy. Auspicious and blissful omens appeared — her left limbs began to throb.

verses.original:
हृदयँ सराहत सीय लोनाई। गुर समीप गवने दोउ भाई।।
राम कहा सबु कौसिक पाहीं। सरल सुभाउ छुअत छल नाहीं।।
verses.translation: Praising Sita's loveliness in his heart, the two brothers returned to their guru. Rama told Sage Kaushika everything — his simple nature knows no concealment.

verses.original:
सुमन पाइ मुनि पूजा कीन्ही। पुनि असीस दुहु भाइन्ह दीन्ही।।
सुफल मनोरथ होहुँ तुम्हारे। रामु लखनु सुनि भए सुखारे।।
verses.translation: Receiving the flowers, the sage performed his worship and then blessed both brothers. 'May your heart's desires be fulfilled.' Rama and Lakshmana were pleased to hear this.

verses.original:
करि भोजनु मुनिबर बिग्यानी। लगे कहन कछु कथा पुरानी।।
बिगत दिवसु गुरु आयसु पाई। संध्या करन चले दोउ भाई।।
verses.translation: After taking their meal, the wise sage began telling some ancient stories. When the day had passed and they received the guru's permission, the two brothers went to perform their evening worship.

verses.original:
प्राची दिसि ससि उयउ सुहावा। सिय मुख सरिस देखि सुखु पावा।।
बहुरि बिचारु कीन्ह मन माहीं। सीय बदन सम हिमकर नाहीं।।
verses.translation: In the east, the beautiful moon arose; seeing it resembling Sita's face, Rama felt delighted. But then he reflected in his mind — the moon does not truly compare to Sita's face.

verses.original:
जनमु सिंधु पुनि बंधु बिषु दिन मलीन सकलंक।
सिय मुख समता पाव किमि चंदु बापुरो रंक।।237।।
verses.translation: The moon was born from the ocean, and its brother is poison; it wanes day by day and is blemished. How can the poor, paltry moon ever equal Sita's face?

verses.original:
घटइ बढ़इ बिरहनि दुखदाई। ग्रसइ राहु निज संधिहिं पाई।।
कोक सिकप्रद पंकज द्रोही। अवगुन बहुत चंद्रमा तोही।।
verses.translation: It waxes and wanes, causing sorrow to those separated from their beloved; Rahu swallows it at every eclipse. It torments the chakravaka bird, is hostile to the lotus — O moon, you have many faults.

verses.original:
बैदेही मुख पटतर दीन्हे। होइ दोष बड़ अनुचित कीन्हे।।
सिय मुख छबि बिधु ब्याज बखानी। गुरु पहिं चले निसा बड़ि जानी।।
verses.translation: If Vaidehi's face were compared to you, it would be a great offense and most improper. Thus praising Sita's facial beauty under the pretext of the moon, and knowing the night was far advanced, Rama returned to the guru.

verses.original:
करि मुनि चरन सरोज प्रनामा। आयसु पाइ कीन्ह बिश्रामा।।
बिगत निसा रघुनायक जागे। बंधु बिलोकि कहन अस लागे।।
verses.translation: Bowing at the sage's lotus feet, he received permission and went to rest. When the night had passed, the Lord of the Raghus awoke, and seeing his brother, he began to speak.

verses.original:
उदउ अरुन अवलोकहु ताता। पंकज कोक लोक सुखदाता।।
बोले लखनु जोरि जुग पानी। प्रभु प्रभाउ सूचक मृदु बानी।।
verses.translation: 'Brother, behold the rising dawn — the sun that brings joy to the lotuses, the chakravaka birds, and all the world.' Lakshmana replied with folded hands, speaking gentle words that hinted at the Lord's glory.

verses.original:
अरुनोदयँ सकुचे कुमुद उडगन जोति मलीन।
जिमि तुम्हार आगमन सुनि भए नृपति बलहीन।।238।।
verses.translation: 'At sunrise the lilies close and the stars lose their light — just as upon hearing of your arrival, the kings have lost their strength.'

verses.original:
नृप सब नखत करहिं उजिआरी। टारि न सकहिं चाप तम भारी।।
कमल कोक मधुकर खग नाना। हरषे सकल निसा अवसाना।।
verses.translation: 'All the kings are like stars that give some light, but they cannot dispel the heavy darkness of the bow. The lotuses, chakravaka birds, bees, and many other birds all rejoice at the end of night.'

verses.original:
ऐसेहिं प्रभु सब भगत तुम्हारे। होइहहिं टूटें धनुष सुखारे।।
उयउ भानु बिनु श्रम तम नासा। दुरे नखत जग तेजु प्रकासा।।
verses.translation: 'Likewise, Lord, all your devotees will rejoice when the bow is broken. The sun rose, and without effort the darkness was destroyed; the stars hid and the sun's radiance lit up the world.'

verses.original:
रबि निज उदय ब्याज रघुराया। प्रभु प्रतापु सब नृपन्ह दिखाया।।
तव भुज बल महिमा उदघाटी। प्रगटी धनु बिघटन परिपाटी।।
verses.translation: 'Under the pretext of the sunrise, O Lord of the Raghus, you have shown all the kings the glory of your might. The fame of your arm-strength has been revealed — the manner in which the bow shall be broken is manifest.'

verses.original:
बंधु बचन सुनि प्रभु मुसुकाने। होइ सुचि सहज पुनीत नहाने।।
नित्यक्रिया करि गुरु पहिं आए। चरन सरोज सुभग सिर नाए।।
verses.translation: The Lord smiled at his brother's words. They bathed naturally in pure water and, having performed their daily rituals, came to the guru and bowed their beautiful heads at his lotus feet.

verses.original:
सतानंदु तब जनक बोलाए। कौसिक मुनि पहिं तुरत पठाए।।
जनक बिनय तिन्ह आइ सुनाई। हरषे बोलि लिए दोउ भाई।।
verses.translation: Then Janaka sent for Shatananda and dispatched him immediately to Sage Kaushika. Shatananda came and conveyed Janaka's request; the delighted sage called the two brothers.

verses.original:
सतानंदपद बंदि प्रभु बैठे गुर पहिं जाइ।
चलहु तात मुनि कहेउ तब पठवा जनक बोलाइ।।239।।
verses.translation: After bowing at Shatananda's feet, the Lord went and sat near his guru. 'Come, my sons,' said the sage, and then Janaka sent word inviting them.

verses.original:
सीय स्वयंबरु देखिअ जाई। ईसु काहि धौं देइ बड़ाई।।
लखन कहा जस भाजनु सोई। नाथ कृपा तव जापर होई।।
verses.translation: 'Let us go and see Sita's swayamvara. I wonder whom the Lord will bless with glory.' Lakshmana said, 'Only he is worthy of glory, Lord, upon whom your grace falls.'

verses.original:
हरषे मुनि सब सुनि बर बानी। दीन्हि असीस सबहिं सुखु मानी।।
पुनि मुनिबृंद समेत कृपाला। देखन चले धनुषमख साला।।
verses.translation: The sages were delighted to hear these fine words and gave blessings, and all felt happy. Then the compassionate Lord, accompanied by the assembly of sages, set forth to see the bow-sacrifice arena.

verses.original:
रंगभूमि आए दोउ भाई। असि सुधि सब पुरबासिन्ह पाई।।
चले सकल गृह काज बिसारी। बाल जुबान जरठ नर नारी।।
