verses.translation: When the two brothers arrived at the arena, all the townsfolk received the news. Everyone set out, forgetting their household tasks — children, youth, old men and women alike.

verses.original:
देखी जनक भीर भै भारी। सुचि सेवक सब लिए हँकारी।।
तुरत सकल लोगन्ह पहिं जाहू। आसन उचित देहू सब काहू।।
verses.translation: Seeing the enormous crowd, Janaka summoned his trusted servants and said, 'Go at once to all the people and give everyone an appropriate seat.'

verses.original:
कहि मृदु बचन बिनीत तिन्ह बैठारे नर नारि।
उत्तम मध्यम नीच लघु निज निज थल अनुहारि।।240।।
verses.translation: Speaking gentle, polite words, they seated all the men and women — high, middle, low, and humble — each in their proper place according to their station.

verses.original:
राजकुअँर तेहि अवसर आए। मनहुँ मनोहरता तन छाए।।
गुन सागर नागर बर बीरा। सुंदर स्यामल गौर सरीरा।।
verses.translation: At that moment the two princes arrived, as though beauty itself had taken bodily form. Oceans of virtue, urbane, heroic — their bodies one dark and one fair.

verses.original:
राज समाज बिराजत रूरे। उडगन महुँ जनु जुग बिधु पूरे।।
जिन्ह कें रही भावना जैसी। प्रभु मूरति तिन्ह देखी तैसी।।
verses.translation: They shone in the royal assembly like two full moons among the stars. Each person saw the Lord's form according to their own inner disposition.

verses.original:
देखहिं रूप महा रनधीरा। मनहुँ बीर रसु धरें सरीरा।।
डरे कुटिल नृप प्रभुहि निहारी। मनहुँ भयानक मूरति भारी।।
verses.translation: The great warriors saw in him the very embodiment of the heroic sentiment. The wicked kings trembled upon seeing the Lord, as though they beheld a terrifying form.

verses.original:
रहे असुर छल छोनिप बेषा। तिन्ह प्रभु प्रगट कालसम देखा।।
पुरबासिन्ह देखे दोउ भाई। नरभूषन लोचन सुखदाई।।
verses.translation: Demons who had assumed the guise of kings saw the Lord as Death itself. The townspeople saw the two brothers as the ornaments of humanity, givers of delight to the eyes.

verses.original:
नारि बिलोकहिं हरषि हियँ निज निज रुचि अनुरूप।
जनु सोहत सिंगार धरि मूरति परम अनूप।।241।।
verses.translation: The women gazed with joyful hearts, each seeing him according to her own taste — as though the very embodiment of beauty had taken a supremely matchless form.

verses.original:
बिदुषन्ह प्रभु बिराटमय दीसा। बहु मुख कर पग लोचन सीसा।।
जनक जाति अवलोकहिं कैसैं। सजन सगे प्रिय लागहिं जैसें।।
verses.translation: The learned ones saw the Lord in his cosmic form — with many mouths, hands, feet, eyes, and heads. The people of Janaka's family saw him as they would a dear, loving kinsman.

verses.original:
सहित बिदेह बिलोकहिं रानी। सिसु सम प्रीति न जाति बखानी।।
जोगिन्ह परम तत्वमय भासा। सांत सुद्ध सम सहज प्रकासा।।
verses.translation: The queens along with Videha beheld him with indescribable affection, as one feels for a child. The yogis saw him as the Supreme Reality — serene, pure, uniform, and naturally luminous.

verses.original:
हरिभगतन्ह देखे दोउ भ्राता। इष्टदेव इव सब सुख दाता।।
रामहि चितव भायँ जेहि सीया। सो सनेहु सुखु नहिं कथनीया।।
verses.translation: The devotees of Hari saw the two brothers as their cherished deity, the giver of all happiness. The love and joy with which Sita gazed at Rama cannot be expressed in words.

verses.original:
उर अनुभवति न कहि सक सोऊ। कवन प्रकार कहै कबि कोऊ।।
एहि बिधि रहा जाहि जस भाऊ। तेहिं तस देखेउ कोसलराऊ।।
verses.translation: Even she who experienced it in her heart could not articulate it — what poet could possibly describe it? In this way, each person saw the prince of Kosala according to their own inner feeling.

verses.original:
राजत राज समाज महुँ कोसलराज किसोर।
सुंदर स्यामल गौर तन बिस्व बिलोचन चोर।।242।।
verses.translation: The prince of the Kosala kingdom shone in that royal assembly — beautiful, one dark and one fair, the captivator of the eyes of the entire universe.

verses.original:
सहज मनोहर मूरति दोऊ। कोटि काम उपमा लघु सोऊ।।
सरद चंद निंदक मुख नीके। नीरज नयन भावते जी के।।
verses.translation: Both forms were naturally enchanting; even comparing them to millions of Kamadevas would be inadequate. Their beautiful faces shamed the autumn moon; their lotus eyes captivated the heart.

verses.original:
चितवत चारु मार मनु हरनी। भावति हृदय जाति नहीं बरनी।।
कल कपोल श्रुति कुंडल लोला। चिबुक अधर सुंदर मृदु बोला।।
verses.translation: Their charming glances stole the mind of Kamadeva himself; the feeling they evoked in the heart defied description. They had graceful cheeks, pendulous earrings at their ears, and beautiful chins with sweet, gentle speech.

verses.original:
कुमुदबंधु कर निंदक हाँसा। भृकुटी बिकट मनोहर नासा।।
भाल बिसाल तिलक झलकाहीं। कच बिलोकि अलि अवलि लजाहीं।।
verses.translation: Their smiles mocked the moon; their brows were striking and their noses beautiful. Their broad foreheads gleamed with tilak marks, and their hair put rows of bees to shame.

verses.original:
पीत चौतनीं सिरन्हि सुहाई। कुसुम कलीं बिच बीच बनाईं।।
रेखें रुचिर कंबु कल गीवाँ। जनु त्रिभुवन सुषमा की सीवाँ।।
verses.translation: Beautiful yellow diadems adorned their heads, with flower buds arranged here and there. Graceful lines adorned their conch-like necks — as though they were the very boundary of the beauty of the three worlds.

verses.original:
कुंजर मनि कंठा कलित उरन्हि तुलसिका माल।
बृषभ कंध केहरि ठवनि बल निधि बाहु बिसाल।।243।।
verses.translation: On their necks hung beautiful necklaces of elephant-pearls, and on their chests were garlands of tulsi. With bull-like shoulders, lion-like bearing — those treasuries of strength had mighty arms.

verses.original:
कटि तूनीर पीत पट बाँधे। कर सर धनुष बाम बर काँधे।।
पीत जग्य उपबीत सुहाए। नख सिख मंजु महाछबि छाए।।
verses.translation: Quivers were fastened at their waists with yellow cloth; they held arrows and bows, with bows also resting on their left shoulders. Their beautiful yellow sacred threads gleamed; from head to toe they radiated supreme splendor.

verses.original:
देखि लोग सब भए सुखारे। एकटक लोचन चलत न तारे।।
हरषे जनकु देखि दोउ भाई। मुनि पद कमल गहे तब जाई।।
verses.translation: Seeing them, all the people were delighted; their unblinking eyes could not look away. Janaka was overjoyed to see the two brothers and went and clasped the sage's lotus feet.

verses.original:
करि बिनती निज कथा सुनाई। रंग अवनि सब मुनिहि देखाई।।
जहँ जहँ जाहि कुअँर बर दोऊ। तहँ तहँ चकित चितव सबु कोऊ।।
verses.translation: Making his request and telling his story, he showed the sage the entire arena. Wherever the two fine princes went, everyone gazed at them in wonder.

verses.original:
निज निज रुख रामहि सबु देखा। कोउ न जान कछु मरमु बिसेषा।।
भलि रचना मुनि नृप सन कहेऊ। राजाँ मुदित महासुख लहेऊ।।
verses.translation: Everyone saw Rama according to their own disposition, but no one knew the special secret. The sage praised the arrangements to the king, and the king was greatly pleased.

verses.original:
सब मंचन्ह ते मंचु एक सुंदर बिसद बिसाल।
मुनि समेत दोउ बंधु तहँ बैठारे महिपाल।।244।।
verses.translation: Among all the galleries, one was particularly beautiful, bright, and spacious. There the king seated the two brothers along with the sage.

verses.original:
प्रभुहि देखि सब नृप हिंयँ हारे। जनु राकेस उदय भएँ तारे।।
असि प्रतीति सब के मन माहीं। राम चाप तोरब सक नाहीं।।
verses.translation: Seeing the Lord, all the kings lost heart — like stars when the full moon rises. Everyone felt in their hearts that Rama could not possibly fail to break the bow.

verses.original:
बिनु भंजेहुँ भव धनुषु बिसाला। मेलिहि सीय राम उर माला।।
अस बिचारि गवनहु घर भाई। जसु प्रतापु बलु तेजु गवाँई।।
verses.translation: 'Even without breaking this great bow of Shiva, Sita will place the victory garland around Rama's neck. Thinking thus, go home, brothers, having lost your fame, valor, strength, and glory.'

verses.original:
बिहसे अपर भूप सुनि बानी। जे अबिबेक अंध अभिमानी।।
तोरेहुँ धनुषु ब्याहु अवगाहा। बिनु तोरें को कुअँरि बिआहा।।
verses.translation: Other kings who were blindly proud and ignorant laughed upon hearing these words. 'The marriage depends on breaking the bow — without breaking it, who can marry the princess?'

verses.original:
एक बार कालउ किन होऊ। सिय हित समर जितब हम सोऊ।।
यह सुनि अवर महिप मुसकाने। धरमसील हरिभगत सयाने।।
verses.translation: 'Let Death himself come if he will; we shall conquer even him in battle for Sita's sake.' Hearing this, other kings — righteous, devoted to Hari, and wise — only smiled.

verses.original:
सीय बिआहबि राम गरब दूरि करि नृपन्ह के।।
जीति को सक संग्राम दसरथ के रन बाँकुरे।।245।।
verses.translation: 'Rama shall marry Sita, removing the pride of all the kings. Who can defeat in battle the valiant warriors of Dasharatha?'

verses.original:
ब्यर्थ मरहु जनि गाल बजाई। मन मोदकन्हि कि भूख बुताई।।
सिख हमारि सुनि परम पुनीता। जगदंबा जानहु जियँ सीता।।
verses.translation: 'Do not die in vain boasting empty words; can imaginary sweets satisfy hunger? Hear our supremely holy advice: know in your heart that Sita is the Mother of the Universe.'

verses.original:
जगत पिता रघुपतिहि बिचारी। भरि लोचन छबि लेहु निहारी।।
सुंदर सुखद सकल गुन रासी। ए दोउ बंधु संभु उर बासी।।
verses.translation: 'Consider Raghupati as the Father of the World, and fill your eyes beholding his beauty. These two brothers are beautiful, bliss-giving, repositories of all virtues, and they dwell in Shambhu's heart.'

verses.original:
सुधा समुद्र समीप बिहाई। मृगजलु निरखि मरहु कत धाई।।
करहु जाइ जा कहुँ जोई भावा। हम तौ आजु जनम फलु पावा।।
verses.translation: 'Having the ocean of nectar nearby, why do you run toward a mirage to your death? Let each one go and do as he pleases — as for us, today we have found the fruit of our lives.'

verses.original:
अस कहि भले भूप अनुरागे। रूप अनूप बिलोकन लागे।।
देखहिं सुर नभ चढ़े बिमाना। बरषहिं सुमन करहिं कल गाना।।
verses.translation: So saying, the good kings, filled with devotion, began gazing at Rama's matchless beauty. The gods watched from their celestial chariots in the sky, showering flowers and singing melodious songs.

verses.original:
जानि सुअवसरु सीय तब पठई जनक बोलाई।
चतुर सखीं सुंदर सकल सादर चलीं लवाईं।।246।।
verses.translation: Recognizing the auspicious moment, Janaka then sent for Sita. Surrounded by clever, beautiful companions, she was led forth with great respect.

verses.original:
सिय सोभा नहिं जाइ बखानी। जगदंबिका रूप गुन खानी।।
उपमा सकल मोहि लघु लागीं। प्राकृत नारि अंग अनुरागीं।।
verses.translation: Sita's beauty cannot be described — she is the Mother of the Universe, a mine of beauty and virtue. All comparisons seem trivial to me, as they relate to ordinary women's physical features.

verses.original:
सिय बरनिअ तेइ उपमा देई। कुकबि कहाइ अजसु को लेई।।
जौ पटतरिअ तीय सम सीया। जग असि जुबति कहाँ कमनीया।।
verses.translation: If one were to describe Sita using such comparisons, one would only earn the reputation of a bad poet and invite disgrace. If Sita is compared to ordinary women, where in the world is there such a beautiful maiden?

verses.original:
गिरा मुखर तन अरध भवानी। रति अति दुखित अतनु पति जानी।।
बिष बारुनी बंधु प्रिय जेही। कहिअ रमासम किमि बैदेही।।
verses.translation: Speech is garrulous but Bhavani's body is only half of Shiva's; Rati is deeply sorrowful, knowing her husband (Kama) is bodiless. The one whose dear brother is poison and wine — how can Vaidehi be called equal to Lakshmi?

verses.original:
जौ छबि सुधा पयोनिधि होई। परम रूपमय कच्छप सोई।।
सोभा रजु मंदरु सिंगारू। मथै पानि पंकज निज मारू।।
verses.translation: If beauty itself were the milky ocean, and supreme loveliness the tortoise, and splendor the churning rope, and adornment the Mandara mountain, and Kamadeva himself churned with his own lotus-hands —

verses.original:
एहि बिधि उपजै लच्छि जब सुंदरता सुख मूल।
तदपि सकोच समेत कबि कहहिं सीय समतूल।।247।।
verses.translation: If in this way a Lakshmi of beauty were to arise — the root of loveliness and bliss — even then, poets would hesitantly say she merely equals Sita.

verses.original:
चलिं संग लै सखीं सयानी। गावत गीत मनोहर बानी।।
सोह नवल तनु सुंदर सारी। जगत जननि अतुलित छबि भारी।।
verses.translation: Sita walked forth, accompanied by wise companions singing melodious songs in enchanting voices. Her youthful body was adorned with a beautiful sari — the Mother of the Universe, with incomparable splendor.

verses.original:
भूषन सकल सुदेस सुहाए। अंग अंग रचि सखिन्ह बनाए।।
रंगभूमि जब सिय पगु धारी। देखि रूप मोहे नर नारी।।
verses.translation: Every ornament suited her perfectly; her companions had adorned each limb with care. When Sita set foot on the arena, men and women alike were entranced by her beauty.

verses.original:
हरषि सुरन्ह दुंदुभीं बजाई। बरषि प्रसून अपछरा गाई।।
पानि सरोज सोह जयमाला। अवचट चितए सकल भुआला।।
verses.translation: The gods joyfully beat their drums, rained flowers, and celestial nymphs sang. In her lotus hands she held the beautiful victory garland. Inadvertently, her eyes fell upon all the assembled kings.

verses.original:
सीय चकित चित रामहि चाहा। भए मोहबस सब नरनाहा।।
मुनि समीप देखे दोउ भाई। लगे ललकि लोचन निधि पाई।।
verses.translation: Sita's bewildered heart longed for Rama; all the kings had fallen under the spell of her beauty. Seeing the two brothers near the sage, her eyes clung to them eagerly, having found their treasure.

verses.original:
गुरजन लाज समाजु बड़ देखि सीय सकुचानि।।
लागि बिलोकन सखिन्ह तन रघुबीरहि उर आनि।।248।।
verses.translation: Feeling the modesty of being before the elders in that great assembly, Sita grew shy. She began looking at her companions' forms instead, having drawn the hero of the Raghus into her heart.

verses.original:
राम रूपु अरु सिय छबि देखें। नर नारिन्ह परिहरीं निमेषें।।
सोचहिं सकल कहत सकुचाहीं। बिधि सन बिनय करहिं मन माहीं।।
verses.translation: Seeing Rama's beauty and Sita's grace, men and women alike stopped blinking. Everyone worried but was too shy to speak openly; in their hearts they prayed to the Creator.

verses.original:
हरु बिधि बेगि जनक जड़ताई। मति हमारि असि देहि सुहाई।।
बिनु बिचार पनु तजि नरनाहु। सीय राम कर करै बिबाहू।।
verses.translation: 'O Creator, quickly remove Janaka's stubbornness and grant us this happy wisdom: let the king abandon his vow without further thought and arrange the marriage of Sita and Rama.'

verses.original:
जग भल कहहि भाव सब काहू। हठ कीन्हे अंतहुँ उर दाहू।।
एहिं लालसाँ मगन सब लोगू। बरु साँवरो जानकी जोगू।।
verses.translation: 'The world will call it good, and it is everyone's wish. Stubbornness in the end only brings heartache.' All the people were immersed in this longing: 'The dark-complexioned groom is worthy of Janaki.'

verses.original:
तब बंदीजन जनक बौलाए। बिरिदावली कहत चलि आए।।
कह नृप जाइ कहहु पन मोरा। चले भाट हियँ हरषु न थोरा।।
verses.translation: Then Janaka summoned his heralds. They came forward reciting his glories. The king said, 'Go and proclaim my vow.' The bards departed, their hearts filled with no little delight.

verses.original:
बोले बंदी बचन बर सुनहु सकल महिपाल।
पन बिदेह कर कहहिं हम भुजा उठाइ बिसाल।।249।।
verses.translation: The heralds proclaimed in fine words, 'Hear, all ye kings! We declare the vow of Videha, raising our mighty arms.'

verses.original:
नृप भुजबल बिधु सिवधनु राहू। गरुअ कठोर बिदित सब काहू।।
रावनु बानु महाभट भारे। देखि सरासन गवँहिं सिधारे।।
verses.translation: 'The arm-strength of kings is like the moon, and Shiva's bow is like Rahu — heavy, hard, and known to all. Even Ravana and Banasura, those mighty warriors, turned away upon seeing the bow.'

verses.original:
सोइ पुरारि कोदंडु कठोरा। राज समाज आजु जोइ तोरा।।
त्रिभुवन जय समेत बैदेही।।बिनहिं बिचार बरइ हठि तेही।।
verses.translation: 'That same formidable bow of the Destroyer of Tripura — whoever in this royal assembly today breaks it shall win the three worlds along with Vaidehi (Sita). He who does so shall marry her without further deliberation.'

verses.original:
सुनि पन सकल भूप अभिलाषे। भटमानी अतिसय मन माखे।।
परिकर बाँधि उठे अकुलाई। चले इष्टदेवन्ह सिर नाई।।
verses.translation: Hearing the challenge, all the kings were eager; the proud warriors were greatly provoked. They girded their loins and rose in agitation, bowing to their patron deities.

verses.original:
तमकि ताकि तकि सिवधनु धरहीं। उठइ न कोटि भाँति बलु करहीं।।
जिन्ह के कछु बिचारु मन माहीं। चाप समीप महीप न जाहीं।।
verses.translation: Furiously they seized Shiva's bow and strained, but it would not budge despite all their efforts. Those who had any sense in their minds did not even approach the bow.

verses.original:
तमकि धरहिं धनु मूढ़ नृप उठइ न चलहिं लजाइ।
मनहुँ पाइ भट बाहुबलु अधिकु अधिकु गरुआइ।।250।।
verses.translation: The foolish kings furiously grasped the bow, but it would not budge, and they walked away in shame — as though the bow had gained even more weight from the strength of their arms.

verses.original:
भूप सहस दस एकहि बारा। लगे उठावन टरइ न टारा।।
डगइ न संभु सरासन कैसें। कामी बचन सती मनु जैसें।।
verses.translation: Ten thousand kings tried all at once to lift it, but it would not move an inch. Shambhu's bow did not budge at all — like the mind of a chaste woman unmoved by a lustful man's words.

verses.original:
सब नृप भए जोगु उपहासी। जैसें बिनु बिराग संन्यासी।।
कीरति बिजय बीरता भारी। चले चाप कर बरबस हारी।।
verses.translation: All the kings became objects of ridicule — like a sannyasi without true renunciation. Having lost their great fame, prowess, and heroism, they departed with the bow having forcefully defeated them.

verses.original:
श्रीहत भए हारि हियँ राजा। बैठे निज निज जाइ समाजा।।
नृपन्ह बिलोकि जनकु अकुलाने। बोले बचन रोष जनु साने।।
verses.translation: Shorn of glory, the dejected kings returned and sat down in their respective camps. Looking at the kings, Janaka grew distressed and spoke words as if mixed with indignation.

verses.original:
दीप दीप के भूपति नाना। आए सुनि हम जो पनु ठाना।।
देव दनुज धरि मनुज सरीरा। बिपुल बीर आए रनधीरा।।
verses.translation: 'Kings from every land came when they heard the vow I had made. Gods and demons in human guise, many mighty warriors came, ready for battle.'

verses.original:
कुअँरि मनोहर बिजय बड़ि कीरति अति कमनीय।
पावनिहार बिरंचि जनु रचेउ न धनु दमनीय।।251।।
verses.translation: 'A beautiful bride, great victory, and most glorious fame — yet it seems the Creator has made no one worthy to claim them, for the bow is indomitable.'

verses.original:
कहहु काहि यहु लाभु न भावा। काहुँ न संकर चाप चढ़ावा।।
रहउ चढ़ाउब तोरब भाई। तिलु भरि भूमि न सके छड़ाई।।
verses.translation: 'Tell me, who would not want this prize? Yet no one could even string Shankara's bow. Forget stringing or breaking it, brothers — no one could lift it even a hair's breadth from the ground.'

verses.original:
अब जनि कोउ माखै भट मानी। बीर बिहीन मही मैं जानी।।
तजहु आस निज निज गृह जाहू। लिखा न बिधि बैदेहि बिबाहू।।
verses.translation: 'Now let no proud warrior take offense, for I have concluded that the earth is devoid of heroes. Give up hope and return to your homes; it is not written in fate that Vaidehi should be married.'

verses.original:
सुकृत जाइ जौं पनु परिहरऊँ। कुअँरि कुआरि रहउ का करऊँ।।
जो जनतेउँ बिनु भट भुबि भाई। तौ पनु करि होतेउँ न हँसाई।।
verses.translation: 'My accumulated merit will be lost if I abandon my vow; let the princess remain unwed — what can I do? Had I known the earth was bereft of warriors, I would not have made this vow and become a laughingstock.'

verses.original:
जनक बचन सुनि सब नर नारी। देखि जानकिहि भए दुखारी।।
माखे लखनु कुटिल भइँ भौंहें। रदपट फरकत नयन रिसौंहें।।
verses.translation: Hearing Janaka's words, all the men and women were grieved, looking at Janaki. Lakshmana was enraged — his brows became crooked, his lips quivered, and his eyes blazed with anger.

verses.original:
कहि न सकत रघुबीर डर लगे बचन जनु बान।
नाइ राम पद कमल सिरु बोले गिरा प्रमान।।252।।
verses.translation: Restrained by his awe of the hero of the Raghus, his words flew like arrows. Bowing his head at Rama's lotus feet, he spoke in a voice of authority.

verses.original:
रघुबंसिन्ह महुँ जहँ कोउ होई। तेहिं समाज अस कहइ न कोई।।
कही जनक जसि अनुचित बानी। बिद्यमान रघुकुल मनि जानी।।
verses.translation: 'In any assembly where a member of the Raghu dynasty is present, no one would speak thus. Janaka has uttered such unseemly words while the jewel of the Raghu clan is here!'

verses.original:
सुनहु भानुकुल पंकज भानू। कहउँ सुभाउ न कछु अभिमानू।।
जौ तुम्हारि अनुसासन पावौं। कंदुक इव ब्रह्मांड उठावौं।।
verses.translation: 'Listen, O sun to the lotus of the Solar dynasty! I speak my nature, not out of pride. If I have your command, I can lift the entire universe like a ball.'

verses.original:
काचे घट जिमि डारौं फोरी। सकउँ मेरु मूलक जिमि तोरी।।
तव प्रताप महिमा भगवाना। को बापुरो पिनाक पुराना।।
verses.translation: 'I can smash it to pieces like an unbaked clay pot; I can break Mount Meru like a radish. By your glory, my Lord, what is this wretched old bow of Pinaka?'

verses.original:
नाथ जानि अस आयसु होऊ। कौतुकु करौं बिलोकिअ सोऊ।।
कमल नाल जिमि चाफ चढ़ावौं। जोजन सत प्रमान लै धावौं।।
verses.translation: 'Lord, knowing this, just give the command, and watch the sport I shall perform. I shall string the bow as easily as threading a lotus stem, and then run a hundred yojanas carrying it.'

verses.original:
तोरौं छत्रक दंड जिमि तव प्रताप बल नाथ।
जौं न करौं प्रभु पद सपथ कर न धरौं धनु भाथ।।253।।
verses.translation: 'I shall break it like snapping a mushroom stem, by your power and might, Lord. If I fail to do so, I swear by the Lord's feet, I shall never again take up bow, arrow, or quiver.'

verses.original:
लखन सकोप बचन जे बोले। डगमगानि महि दिग्गज डोले।।
सकल लोक सब भूप डेराने। सिय हियँ हरषु जनकु सकुचाने।।
verses.translation: The words that Lakshmana spoke in anger made the earth shake and the elephants of the directions tremble. All the worlds and all the kings were frightened; Sita's heart rejoiced, and Janaka was abashed.

verses.original:
गुर रघुपति सब मुनि मन माहीं। मुदित भए पुनि पुनि पुलकाहीं।।
सयनहिं रघुपति लखनु नेवारे। प्रेम समेत निकट बैठारे।।
verses.translation: The guru, Raghupati, and all the sages were inwardly delighted, thrilling again and again. Rama gently restrained Lakshmana with a glance and seated him near with affection.

verses.original:
बिस्वामित्र समय सुभ जानी। बोले अति सनेहमय बानी।।
उठहु राम भंजहु भवचापा। मेटहु तात जनक परितापा।।
verses.translation: Then Vishwamitra, knowing the moment to be auspicious, spoke in a most affectionate voice, 'Rise, Rama, and break Shiva's bow. Remove Janaka's anguish, dear son.'

verses.original:
सुनि गुरु बचन चरन सिरु नावा। हरषु बिषादु न कछु उर आवा।।
ठाढ़े भए उठि सहज सुभाएँ। ठवनि जुबा मृगराजु लजाएँ।।
verses.translation: Hearing the guru's words, Rama bowed his head at his feet. No joy or sorrow arose in his heart. He stood up with natural ease — his stance would put a young lion to shame.

verses.original:
उदित उदयगिरि मंच पर रघुबर बालपतंग।
बिकसे संत सरोज सब हरषे लोचन भृंग।।254।।
verses.translation: Like the young sun rising on the eastern mountain, Rama appeared on the high platform. The saints blossomed like lotuses, and the eyes of all were delighted like bees.

verses.original:
नृपन्ह केरि आसा निसि नासी। बचन नखत अवली न प्रकासी।।
मानी महिप कुमुद सकुचाने। कपटी भूप उलूक लुकाने।।
verses.translation: The night of the kings' hopes was destroyed; the star-like glory of their words faded away. The proud kings shrank like water-lilies, and the deceitful kings hid like owls.

verses.original:
भए बिसोक कोक मुनि देवा। बरिसहिं सुमन जनावहिं सेवा।।
गुर पद बंदि सहित अनुरागा। राम मुनिन्ह सन आयसु मागा।।
verses.translation: The gods and sages, like chakravaka birds, were freed from sorrow. They rained flowers and expressed their devotion. Bowing at the guru's feet with deep love, Rama sought the sages' permission.

verses.original:
सहजहिं चले सकल जग स्वामी। मत्त मंजु बर कुंजर गामी।।
चलत राम सब पुर नर नारी। पुलक पूरि तन भए सुखारी।।
verses.translation: The Lord of all the worlds walked naturally with the graceful gait of a majestic young elephant. As Rama walked, all the men and women of the city were filled with joy, their bodies thrilling with delight.

verses.original:
बंदि पितर सुर सुकृत सँभारे। जौं कछु पुन्य प्रभाउ हमारे।।
तौ सिवधनु मृनाल की नाईं। तोरहुँ राम गनेस गोसाईं।।
verses.translation: Remembering their ancestors, the gods, and their own good deeds, they prayed, 'If we have any merit, then let Rama break Shiva's bow as easily as a lotus stem, O Lord Ganesha!'

verses.original:
रामहि प्रेम समेत लखि सखिन्ह समीप बोलाइ।
सीता मातु सनेह बस बचन कहइ बिलखाइ।।255।।
verses.translation: Seeing Rama with love, Sita's mother called her companions near and, overcome with maternal affection, spoke with anguish.

verses.original:
सखि सब कौतुक देखनिहारे। जेठ कहावत हितू हमारे।।
कोउ न बुझाइ कहइ गुर पाहीं। ए बालक असि हठ भलि नाहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Friends, everyone here is a mere spectator. Those who call themselves our well-wishers — not one of them advises the guru, saying that such stubbornness is not right for these young boys.'

verses.original:
रावन बान छुआ नहिं चापा। हारे सकल भूप करि दापा।।
सो धनु राजकुअँर कर देहीं। बाल मराल कि मंदर लेहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Even Ravana and Banasura could not touch the bow; all the kings have failed despite their boasting. And now they give this bow to a young prince — can baby swans lift Mount Mandara?'

verses.original:
भूप सयानप सकल सिरानी। सखि बिधि गति कछु जाति न जानी।।
बोली चतुर सखी मृदु बानी। तेजवंत लघु गनिअ न रानी।।
verses.translation: 'The wisdom of all the kings has come to nothing; friend, the ways of fate are unknowable.' A clever companion spoke in gentle words, 'O Queen, do not judge the mighty by their size.'

verses.original:
कहँ कुंभज कहँ सिंधु अपारा। सोषेउ सुजसु सकल संसारा।।
रबि मंडल देखत लघु लागा। उदयँ तासु तिभुवन तम भागा।।
verses.translation: 'Where was the tiny sage Agastya, and where the boundless ocean? Yet he drank it dry, and his fame fills the whole world. The orb of the sun appears small to look at, but at its rising, the darkness of all three worlds flees.'

verses.original:
मंत्र परम लघु जासु बस बिधि हरि हर सुर सर्ब।
महामत्त गजराज कहुँ बस कर अंकुस खर्ब।।256।।
verses.translation: 'The mightiest mantras are the shortest, yet they control Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva, and all the gods. Even the mightiest of mad elephants is controlled by a tiny goad.'

verses.original:
काम कुसुम धनु सायक लीन्हे। सकल भुवन अपने बस कीन्हे।।
देबि तजिअ संसउ अस जानी। भंजब धनुष रामु सुनु रानी।।
verses.translation: 'Kamadeva has taken a bow of flowers and flower-arrows, yet has brought all the worlds under his sway. O Queen, abandon doubt knowing this — listen, Rama shall break the bow.'

verses.original:
सखी बचन सुनि भै परतीती। मिटा बिषादु बढ़ी अति प्रीती।।
तब रामहि बिलोकि बैदेही। सभय हृदयँ बिनवति जेहि तेही।।
verses.translation: Hearing her companion's words, faith arose in the queen's heart; her sorrow departed and her love grew greatly. Then Vaidehi, gazing at Rama, prayed anxiously in her heart to every deity.

verses.original:
मनहीं मन मनाव अकुलानी। होहु प्रसन्न महेस भवानी।।
करहु सफल आपनि सेवकाई। करि हितु हरहु चाप गरुआई।।
verses.translation: Deeply agitated, she prayed in her mind, 'Be gracious, O Mahesha and Bhavani! Make your servant's devotion fruitful; do me this favor and remove the heaviness of the bow.'

verses.original:
गननायक बरदायक देवा। आजु लगें कीन्हिउँ तुअ सेवा।।
बार बार बिनती सुनि मोरी। करहु चाप गुरुता अति थोरी।।
verses.translation: 'O Ganesha, O boon-granting god! I have served you since my very birth. Hear my repeated prayer — make the bow exceedingly light!'

verses.original:
देखि देखि रघुबीर तन सुर मनाव धरि धीर।।
भरे बिलोचन प्रेम जल पुलकावली सरीर।।257।।
verses.translation: Gazing at the hero of the Raghus, Sita gathered courage and prayed to the gods. Her eyes filled with tears of love, and her body was covered with a thrill of joy.

verses.original:
नीकें निरखि नयन भरि सोभा। पितु पनु सुमिरि बहुरि मनु छोभा।।
अहह तात दारुनि हठ ठानी। समुझत नहिं कछु लाभु न हानी।।
verses.translation: Gazing intently to fill her eyes with his beauty, she then remembered her father's vow and her mind was troubled again. 'Alas, father has made such a cruel vow, understanding neither gain nor loss.'

verses.original:
सचिव सभय सिख देइ न कोई। बुध समाज बड़ अनुचित होई।।
कहँ धनु कुलिसहु चाहि कठोरा। कहँ स्यामल मृदुगात किसोरा।।
verses.translation: 'The ministers are all too afraid to advise him; in a learned assembly, this is a great impropriety. On one hand the bow, harder even than a thunderbolt — on the other, this delicate, dark-limbed youth.'

verses.original:
बिधि केहि भाँति धरौं उर धीरा। सिरस सुमन कन बेधिअ हीरा।।
सकल सभा कै मति भै भोरी। अब मोहि संभुचाप गति तोरी।।
verses.translation: 'How can I find courage in my heart, O Creator? Can a diamond be pierced with the petal of a shirisha flower? The understanding of the entire assembly has been dulled; now only Shiva's bow knows my fate.'

verses.original:
निज जड़ता लोगन्ह पर डारी। होहि हरुअ रघुपतिहि निहारी।।
अति परिताप सीय मन माही। लव निमेष जुग सब सय जाहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Let them blame their own dullness on others — may the bow become light as they behold Raghupati.' The anguish in Sita's heart was extreme; each moment passed like an age.

verses.original:
प्रभुहि चितइ पुनि चितव महि राजत लोचन लोल।
खेलत मनसिज मीन जुग जनु बिधु मंडल डोल।।258।।
verses.translation: She gazed at the Lord and then looked down at the ground, her eyes restlessly moving — as though two fish of Kamadeva were playing in the pool of the moon.

verses.original:
गिरा अलिनि मुख पंकज रोकी। प्रगट न लाज निसा अवलोकी।।
लोचन जलु रह लोचन कोना। जैसे परम कृपन कर सोना।।
verses.translation: Her bee-like voice was sealed within the lotus of her lips; her modesty, seeing the night of shyness, would not come forth openly. Tears lingered in the corners of her eyes, like gold hoarded by an extreme miser.

verses.original:
सकुची ब्याकुलता बड़ि जानी। धरि धीरजु प्रतीति उर आनी।।
तन मन बचन मोर पनु साचा। रघुपति पद सरोज चितु राचा।।
verses.translation: Knowing her great agitation, she suppressed her shyness and drew courage and faith into her heart. 'If my body, mind, and speech are truly devoted, and my heart is fixed on Raghupati's lotus feet —'

verses.original:
तौ भगवानु सकल उर बासी। करिहिं मोहि रघुबर कै दासी।।
जेहि कें जेहि पर सत्य सनेहू। सो तेहि मिलइ न कछु संहेहू।।
verses.translation: 'Then the Lord who dwells in all hearts will make me the servant of the chief of the Raghus. Whoever has true love for whomever — they will meet that person, without any doubt.'

verses.original:
प्रभु तन चितइ प्रेम तन ठाना। कृपानिधान राम सबु जाना।।
सियहि बिलोकि तकेउ धनु कैसे। चितव गरुरु लघु ब्यालहि जैसे।।
verses.translation: She gazed at the Lord's form and love filled her body. The treasure-house of mercy, all-knowing Rama, understood everything. He looked at Sita and then glanced at the bow — as Garuda would look at a small serpent.

verses.original:
लखन लखेउ रघुबंसमनि ताकेउ हर कोदंडु।
पुलकि गात बोले बचन चरन चापि ब्रह्मांडु।।259।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana saw that the jewel of the Raghu dynasty had fixed his gaze on Shiva's bow. His body thrilling with joy, he spoke, stamping his foot so as to shake the universe.

verses.original:
दिसकुंजरहु कमठ अहि कोला। धरहु धरनि धरि धीर न डोला।।
रामु चहहिं संकर धनु तोरा। होहु सजग सुनि आयसु मोरा।।
verses.translation: 'O elephants of the directions, O tortoise, O serpent and boar — hold the earth steady and firm, do not waver! Rama wishes to break Shankara's bow; hear my command and be alert!'

verses.original:
चाप सपीप रामु जब आए। नर नारिन्ह सुर सुकृत मनाए।।
सब कर संसउ अरु अग्यानू। मंद महीपन्ह कर अभिमानू।।
verses.translation: When Rama drew near the bow, men, women, and gods all invoked their good deeds. Everyone's doubt and ignorance, the foolish pride of the kings,

verses.original:
भृगुपति केरि गरब गरुआई। सुर मुनिबरन्ह केरि कदराई।।
सिय कर सोचु जनक पछितावा। रानिन्ह कर दारुन दुख दावा।।
verses.translation: The heavy pride of Bhrigu's descendant (Parashurama), the timidity of the gods and great sages, Sita's anxiety, Janaka's regret, and the terrible anguish of the queens —

verses.original:
संभुचाप बड बोहितु पाई। चढे जाइ सब संगु बनाई।।
राम बाहुबल सिंधु अपारू। चहत पारु नहि कोउ कड़हारू।।
verses.translation: All these found Shiva's bow a great ship to board, and they all climbed aboard together. The ocean was the boundless might of Rama's arms — all wished to cross, but there was no helmsman.

verses.original:
राम बिलोके लोग सब चित्र लिखे से देखि।
चितई सीय कृपायतन जानी बिकल बिसेषि।।260।।
verses.translation: Rama looked at everyone and saw them motionless like figures in a painting. Then he looked at Sita, that abode of compassion, knowing her to be especially distressed.

verses.original:
देखी बिपुल बिकल बैदेही। निमिष बिहात कलप सम तेही।।
तृषित बारि बिनु जो तनु त्यागा। मुएँ करइ का सुधा तड़ागा।।
verses.translation: He saw Vaidehi in great distress; each moment felt to her like an age. If a thirsty person dies without water, what use is a lake of nectar afterwards?

verses.original:
का बरषा सब कृषी सुखानें। समय चुकें पुनि का पछितानें।।
अस जियँ जानि जानकी देखी। प्रभु पुलके लखि प्रीति बिसेषी।।
verses.translation: What good is rain after all the crops have withered? Why lament when the right moment has passed? Knowing this in his heart and seeing Janaki, the Lord thrilled with joy upon seeing her profound love.

verses.original:
गुरहि प्रनामु मनहि मन कीन्हा। अति लाघवँ उठाइ धनु लीन्हा।।
दमकेउ दामिनि जिमि जब लयऊ। पुनि नभ धनु मंडल सम भयऊ।।
verses.translation: He mentally bowed to his guru, and with supreme ease, he lifted the bow. It flashed like lightning when he took it up, and then it appeared like a rainbow in the sky.

verses.original:
लेत चढ़ावत खैंचत गाढ़ें। काहुँ न लखा देख सबु ठाढ़ें।।
तेहि छन राम मध्य धनु तोरा। भरे भुवन धुनि घोर कठोरा।।
verses.translation: He took it up, strung it, and drew it taut so effortlessly that no one could follow his movements — everyone just stood staring. In that instant, Rama snapped the bow in two, and a terrible, thunderous sound filled the universe.

verses.original:
भरे भुवन घोर कठोर रव रबि बाजि तजि मारगु चले।
चिक्करहिं दिग्गज डोल महि अहि कोल कूरुम कलमले।।
सुर असुर मुनि कर कान दीन्हें सकल बिकल बिचारहीं।
कोदंड खंडेउ राम तुलसी जयति बचन उचारही।।
verses.translation: The terrible, harsh sound filled the worlds; the horses of the sun left their path. The elephants of the directions trumpeted, the earth shook, and the serpent, boar, and tortoise trembled. Gods, demons, and sages covered their ears, all bewildered and wondering. Tulsi says they exclaimed, 'Rama has broken the bow! Victory! Victory!'

verses.original:
संकर चापु जहाजु सागरु रघुबर बाहुबलु।
बूड़ सो सकल समाजु चढ़ा जो प्रथमहिं मोह बस।।261।।
verses.translation: Shankara's bow was the ship, and the ocean was the might of Rama's arms. The whole company that had boarded in delusion was drowned, while Rama emerged triumphant.

verses.original:
प्रभु दोउ चापखंड महि डारे। देखि लोग सब भए सुखारे।।
कोसिकरुप पयोनिधि पावन। प्रेम बारि अवगाहु सुहावन।।
verses.translation: The Lord cast the two pieces of the bow upon the ground, and all the people were overjoyed. Sage Kaushika's love was like a holy ocean, and the beautiful waters of love were its bathing pool.

verses.original:
रामरूप राकेसु निहारी। बढ़त बीचि पुलकावलि भारी।।
बाजे नभ गहगहे निसाना। देवबधू नाचहिं करि गाना।।
verses.translation: Seeing the full moon of Rama's beauty, great waves of thrilling joy surged forth. Drums resounded in the sky, and celestial maidens danced and sang.

verses.original:
ब्रह्मादिक सुर सिद्ध मुनीसा। प्रभुहि प्रसंसहि देहिं असीसा।।
बरिसहिं सुमन रंग बहु माला। गावहिं किंनर गीत रसाला।।
verses.translation: Brahma and all the gods, siddhas, and great sages praised the Lord and gave their blessings. They rained flowers and garlands of many colors, and Kinnaras sang delightful songs.

verses.original:
रही भुवन भरि जय जय बानी। धनुषभंग धुनि जात न जानी।।
मुदित कहहिं जहँ तहँ नर नारी। भंजेउ राम संभुधनु भारी।।
verses.translation: Cries of 'Victory! Victory!' filled the worlds, drowning out even the sound of the bow breaking. Everywhere men and women joyfully said, 'Rama has broken Shambhu's mighty bow!'

verses.original:
बंदी मागध सूतगन बिरुद बदहिं मतिधीर।
करहिं निछावरि लोग सब हय गय धन मनि चीर।।262।।
verses.translation: Bards, panegyrists, and minstrels recited the Lord's praises with great wisdom. Everyone made offerings of horses, elephants, wealth, jewels, and garments.

verses.original:
झाँझि मृदंग संख सहनाई। भेरि ढोल दुंदुभी सुहाई।।
बाजहिं बहु बाजने सुहाए। जहँ तहँ जुबतिन्ह मंगल गाए।।
verses.translation: Cymbals, mridangas, conches, shehnais, bheris, drums, and kettledrums — many beautiful instruments played, and women everywhere sang auspicious songs.

verses.original:
सखिन्ह सहित हरषी अति रानी। सूखत धान परा जनु पानी।।
जनक लहेउ सुखु सोचु बिहाई। पैरत थकें थाह जनु पाई।।
verses.translation: The queen along with her companions was supremely delighted, as though a withered crop had suddenly received rain. Janaka found happiness and was freed from worry, like one who, exhausted from swimming, finally touches ground.

verses.original:
श्रीहत भए भूप धनु टूटे। जैसें दिवस दीप छबि छूटे।।
सीय सुखहि बरनिअ केहि भाँती। जनु चातकी पाइ जलु स्वाती।।
verses.translation: The kings who had lost their glory when the bow broke were dimmed like lamps at daybreak. As for Sita's happiness — how can it be described? It was like a chakori bird receiving drops of the Swati constellation.

verses.original:
रामहि लखनु बिलोकत कैसें। ससिहि चकोर किसोरकु जैसें।।
सतानंद तब आयसु दीन्हा। सीताँ गमनु राम पहिं कीन्हा।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana gazed at Rama as a young chakora bird gazes at the moon. Then Shatananda gave the command, and Sita proceeded toward Rama.

verses.original:
संग सखीं सुदंर चतुर गावहिं मंगलचार।
गवनी बाल मराल गति सुषमा अंग अपार।।263।।
verses.translation: Accompanied by beautiful, clever companions singing auspicious songs, she walked with the graceful gait of a young swan, her limbs radiant with boundless beauty.

verses.original:
सखिन्ह मध्य सिय सोहति कैसे। छबिगन मध्य महाछबि जैसें।।
कर सरोज जयमाल सुहाई। बिस्व बिजय सोभा जेहिं छाई।।
verses.translation: Sita among her companions was like supreme beauty itself amidst a host of beauties. In her lotus hands she held the lovely victory garland, resplendent with the glory of conquering the world.

verses.original:
तन सकोचु मन परम उछाहू। गूढ़ प्रेमु लखि परइ न काहू।।
जाइ समीप राम छबि देखी। रहि जनु कुँअरि चित्र अवरेखी।।
verses.translation: Her body was shy, but her heart was brimming with supreme joy. Her deep love was hidden; no one could detect it. Coming near and beholding Rama's beauty, the princess stood motionless, as if painted in a picture.

verses.original:
चतुर सखीं लखि कहा बुझाई। पहिरावहु जयमाल सुहाई।।
सुनत जुगल कर माल उठाई। प्रेम बिबस पहिराइ न जाई।।
verses.translation: Her clever companions observed this and gently advised, 'Place the beautiful victory garland upon him.' Hearing this, she raised the garland with both hands, but was so overwhelmed with love that she could not place it.

verses.original:
सोहत जनु जुग जलज सनाला। ससिहि सभीत देत जयमाला।।
गावहिं छबि अवलोकि सहेली। सियँ जयमाल राम उर मेली।।
verses.translation: Her two hands holding the garland looked like lotus stems, as though the moon were shyly offering a victory garland to Rama. As her companions sang and admired the scene, Sita placed the victory garland around Rama's neck.

verses.original:
रघुबर उर जयमाल देखि देव बरिसहिं सुमन।
सकुचे सकल भुआल जनु बिलोकि रबि कुमुदगन।।264।।
verses.translation: Seeing the victory garland on the chest of the chief of the Raghus, the gods rained flowers. All the kings shrank in dismay, like water-lilies at the sight of the sun.

verses.original:
पुर अरु ब्योम बाजने बाजे। खल भए मलिन साधु सब राजे।।
सुर किंनर नर नाग मुनीसा। जय जय जय कहि देहिं असीसा।।
verses.translation: Instruments played in the city and in the heavens. The wicked were disheartened while all the virtuous rejoiced. Gods, Kinnaras, men, nagas, and great sages all cried 'Victory! Victory!' and gave their blessings.

verses.original:
नाचहिं गावहिं बिबुध बधूटीं। बार बार कुसुमांजलि छूटीं।।
जहँ तहँ बिप्र बेदधुनि करहीं। बंदी बिरदावलि उच्चरहीं।।
verses.translation: Celestial women danced and sang, and showers of flower-offerings fell again and again. Here and there, Brahmins chanted Vedic hymns, and bards recited the praises of the Lord.

verses.original:
महि पाताल नाक जसु ब्यापा। राम बरी सिय भंजेउ चापा।।
करहिं आरती पुर नर नारी। देहिं निछावरि बित्त बिसारी।।
verses.translation: The glory spread through earth, the netherworld, and heaven: 'Rama has won Sita and broken the bow.' The men and women of the city performed arati and made lavish offerings, forgetting their wealth.

verses.original:
सोहति सीय राम कै जौरी। छबि सिंगारु मनहुँ एक ठोरी।।
सखीं कहहिं प्रभुपद गहु सीता। करति न चरन परस अति भीता।।
verses.translation: The pair of Sita and Rama shone as though Beauty and Adornment had come together in one place. Her companions said, 'Sita, clasp the Lord's feet,' but she was too afraid to touch them.

verses.original:
गौतम तिय गति सुरति करि नहिं परसति पग पानि।
मन बिहसे रघुबंसमनि प्रीति अलौकिक जानि।।265।।
verses.translation: Remembering the fate of Gautama's wife (Ahalya), she would not touch his feet with her hands. The jewel of the Raghu dynasty smiled in his heart, recognizing her extraordinary love.

verses.original:
तब सिय देखि भूप अभिलाषे। कूर कपूत मूढ़ मन माखे।।
उठि उठि पहिरि सनाह अभागे। जहँ तहँ गाल बजावन लागे।।
verses.translation: Then the wicked kings, seeing Sita, became covetous. Those accursed, worthless fools rose and began boasting and threatening everywhere.

verses.original:
लेहु छड़ाइ सीय कह कोऊ। धरि बाँधहु नृप बालक दोऊ।।
तोरें धनुषु चाड़ नहिं सरई। जीवत हमहि कुअँरि को बरई।।
verses.translation: One said, 'Snatch Sita away!' Another said, 'Seize and bind the two royal boys! Breaking the bow does not settle the matter — who shall marry the princess while we are alive?'

verses.original:
जौं बिदेहु कछु करै सहाई। जीतहु समर सहित दोउ भाई।।
साधु भूप बोले सुनि बानी। राजसमाजहि लाज लजानी।।
verses.translation: 'If Videha takes their side, we shall defeat them all in battle, along with both brothers.' The virtuous kings heard their words and spoke — words that would put even shame itself to shame.

verses.original:
बलु प्रतापु बीरता बड़ाई। नाक पिनाकहि संग सिधाई।।
सोइ सूरता कि अब कहुँ पाई। असि बुधि तौ बिधि मुहँ मसि लाई।।
verses.translation: 'Your strength, valor, bravery, and honor departed along with the Pinaka bow. Where will you find that courage now? Such foolishness means the Creator has blackened your faces.'

verses.original:
देखहु रामहि नयन भरि तजि इरिषा मदु कोहु।
लखन रोषु पावकु प्रबल जानि सलभ जनि होहु।।266।।
verses.translation: 'Behold Rama with your eyes to your heart's content, and abandon envy, pride, and anger. Lakshmana's rage is like a fierce fire — knowing this, do not become moths rushing to destruction.'

verses.original:
बैनतेय बलि जिमि चह कागू। जिमि ससु चहै नाग अरि भागू।।
जिमि चह कुसल अकारन कोही। सब संपदा चहै सिवद्रोही।।
verses.translation: 'A crow coveting the prey of Garuda, a hare challenging the enemy of serpents, a perpetually angry person seeking well-being, an enemy of Shiva desiring all prosperity —'

verses.original:
लोभी लोलुप कल कीरति चहई। अकलंकता कि कामी लहई।।
हरि पद बिमुख परम गति चाहा। तस तुम्हार लालचु नरनाहा।।
verses.translation: 'A greedy, lustful man desiring noble fame, a sensualist hoping for spotless honor, one hostile to Hari's feet yet craving the highest state — such, O kings, is your vain desire.'

verses.original:
कोलाहलु सुनि सीय सकानी। सखीं लवाइ गईं जहँ रानी।।
रामु सुभायँ चले गुरु पाहीं। सिय सनेहु बरनत मन माहीं।।
verses.translation: Sita was frightened by the uproar, and her companions led her away to the queen. Rama walked calmly back toward the guru, describing Sita's love within his heart.

verses.original:
रानिन्ह सहित सोचबस सीया। अब धौं बिधिहि काह करनीया।।
भूप बचन सुनि इत उत तकहीं। लखनु राम डर बोलि न सकहीं।।
verses.translation: Sita along with the queens was lost in worry — 'What will fate do now?' The kings heard the threats and looked this way and that; awed by Lakshmana and Rama, they dared not speak.

verses.original:
अरुन नयन भृकुटी कुटिल चितवत नृपन्ह सकोप।
मनहुँ मत्त गजगन निरखि सिंघकिसोरहि चोप।।267।।
verses.translation: With red eyes and knitted brows, Lakshmana glared at the kings in anger — like a young lion cub eagerly watching a herd of maddened elephants.

verses.original:
खरभरु देखि बिकल पुर नारीं। सब मिलि देहिं महीपन्ह गारीं।।
तेहिं अवसर सुनि सिव धनु भंगा। आयसु भृगुकुल कमल पतंगा।।
verses.translation: Seeing the commotion, the women of the city were distressed; they all cursed the kings. At that very moment, hearing of Shiva's bow being broken, came the sun of the Bhrigu clan — Parashurama.

verses.original:
देखि महीप सकल सकुचाने। बाज झपट जनु लवा लुकाने।।
गौरि सरीर भूति भल भ्राजा। भाल बिसाल त्रिपुंड बिराजा।।
verses.translation: Seeing him, all the kings cowered in fear — like quails hiding at the swoop of a hawk. His fair body was beautifully smeared with sacred ash, and a large triple mark of Tripundra adorned his broad forehead.

verses.original:
सीस जटा ससिबदनु सुहावा। रिसबस कछुक अरुन होइ आवा।।
भृकुटी कुटिल नयन रिस राते। सहजहुँ चितवत मनहुँ रिसाते।।
verses.translation: He had matted locks on his head and a handsome moon-like face, which had turned slightly red under the sway of anger. His brows were knitted, his eyes red with fury — even his natural glance seemed angry.

verses.original:
बृषभ कंध उर बाहु बिसाला। चारु जनेउ माल मृगछाला।।
कटि मुनि बसन तून दुइ बाँधें। धनु सर कर कुठारु कल काँधें।।
verses.translation: He had bull-like shoulders, a broad chest, and mighty arms. A beautiful sacred thread, garland, and deerskin adorned him. At his waist was a sage's garment; two quivers were fastened there. In his hand was a bow and arrow, and a fine battle-axe rested on his shoulder.

verses.original:
सांत बेषु करनी कठिन बरनि न जाइ सरुप।
धरि मुनितनु जनु बीर रसु आयउ जहँ सब भूप।।268।।
verses.translation: His garb was that of a peaceful ascetic, but his deeds were fierce — his form was beyond description. It was as though the heroic sentiment itself had taken the form of a sage and come to where all the kings were assembled.

verses.original:
देखत भृगुपति बेषु कराला। उठे सकल भय बिकल भुआला।।
पितु समेत कहि कहि निज नामा। लगे करन सब दंड प्रनामा।।
verses.translation: Seeing Bhrigu's descendant in his fierce form, all the kings rose up, trembling with fear. Each one, together with their fathers, gave their names and began prostrating themselves.

verses.original:
जेहि सुभायँ चितवहिं हितु जानी। सो जानइ जनु आइ खुटानी।।
जनक बहोरि आइ सिरु नावा। सीय बोलाइ प्रनामु करावा।।
verses.translation: Whomever he glanced at with seeming favor, that person felt as if his end had come. Then Janaka came and bowed his head, and had Sita brought forward to pay her respects.

verses.original:
आसिष दीन्हि सखीं हरषानीं। निज समाज लै गई सयानीं।।
बिस्वामित्रु मिले पुनि आई। पद सरोज मेले दोउ भाई।।
verses.translation: Parashurama gave his blessing, and the happy companions led her back to their group. Then Vishwamitra came and met him, and brought the two brothers to touch his lotus feet.

verses.original:
रामु लखनु दसरथ के ढोटा। दीन्हि असीस देखि भल जोटा।।
रामहि चितइ रहे थकि लोचन। रूप अपार मार मद मोचन।।
verses.translation: 'These are Rama and Lakshmana, sons of Dasharatha.' Parashurama blessed them, seeing the fine pair. He stared at Rama, his eyes unable to turn away — Rama's beauty was limitless, dispelling the intoxication of Kamadeva.

verses.original:
बहुरि बिलोकि बिदेह सन कहहु काह अति भीर।।
पूछत जानि अजान जिमि ब्यापेउ कोपु सरीर।।269।।
verses.translation: Then, looking again at Videha, he said, 'Tell me, what is this great commotion about?' His questioning was like that of one who pretends not to know, and anger pervaded his body.

verses.original:
समाचार कहि जनक सुनाए। जेहि कारन महीप सब आए।।
सुनत बचन फिरि अनत निहारे। देखे चापखंड महि डारे।।
verses.translation: Janaka told him the full story — the reason why all the kings had assembled. Hearing the words, Parashurama looked around and saw the pieces of the broken bow lying on the ground.

verses.original:
अति रिस बोले बचन कठोरा। कहु जड़ जनक धनुष कै तोरा।।
बेगि देखाउ मूढ़ न त आजू। उलटउँ महि जहँ लहि तव राजू।।
verses.translation: In extreme fury, he spoke harsh words, 'Tell me, fool Janaka, who broke the bow? Show me quickly, you dolt, or I shall turn the earth upside down as far as your kingdom extends!'

verses.original:
अति डरु उतरु देत नृपु नाहीं। कुटिल भूप हरषे मन माहीं।।
सुर मुनि नाग नगर नर नारी।।सोचहिं सकल त्रास उर भारी।।
verses.translation: The king was too terrified to answer. The wicked kings secretly rejoiced in their hearts. Gods, sages, nagas, and the men and women of the city — all were worried, with a heavy dread in their hearts.

verses.original:
मन पछिताति सीय महतारी। बिधि अब सँवरी बात बिगारी।।
भृगुपति कर सुभाउ सुनि सीता। अरध निमेष कलप सम बीता।।
verses.translation: Sita's mother bitterly repented in her mind, 'The Creator has now ruined what was going so well.' Hearing of Parashurama's nature, Sita grew anxious — each half-moment felt like an age.

verses.original:
सभय बिलोके लोग सब जानि जानकी भीरु।
हृदयँ न हरषु बिषादु कछु बोले श्रीरघुबीरु।।270।।
verses.translation: All the people looked on in fear; knowing Janaki's terror, the hero of the Raghus — with neither joy nor sorrow in his heart — spoke thus.

verses.original:
नाथ संभुधनु भंजनिहारा। होइहि केउ एक दास तुम्हारा।।
आयसु काह कहिअ किन मोही। सुनि रिसाइ बोले मुनि कोही।।
verses.translation: 'Lord, the one who broke Shambhu's bow must be some servant of yours. What is your command? Why not tell me?' Hearing this, the wrathful sage grew angrier still.

verses.original:
सेवकु सो जो करै सेवकाई। अरि करनी करि करिअ लराई।।
सुनहु राम जेहिं सिवधनु तोरा। सहसबाहु सम सो रिपु मोरा।।
verses.translation: 'A servant is one who serves; for hostile acts, one wages war. Listen, Rama — whoever broke Shiva's bow is my enemy, as was Sahasrabahu (Kartavirya).'

verses.original:
सो बिलगाउ बिहाइ समाजा। न त मारे जैहहिं सब राजा।।
सुनि मुनि बचन लखन मुसुकाने। बोले परसुधरहि अपमाने।।
verses.translation: 'Let that person come forward, apart from this assembly, or else all the kings shall die.' Hearing the sage's words, Lakshmana smiled and spoke, feeling slighted by the axe-bearer.

verses.original:
बहु धनुहीं तोरीं लरिकाईं। कबहुँ न असि रिस कीन्हि गोसाईं।।
एहि धनु पर ममता केहि हेतू। सुनि रिसाइ कह भृगुकुलकेतू।।
verses.translation: 'We have broken many little bows in our childhood, but you never showed such anger then, my lord. Why this special attachment to this particular bow?' Hearing this, the banner of the Bhrigu clan spoke in fury.

verses.original:
रे नृप बालक कालबस बोलत तोहि न सँमार।।
धनुही सम तिपुरारि धनु बिदित सकल संसार।।271।।
verses.translation: 'O foolish prince, child in the grip of Death — you speak without any sense. This bow of the Destroyer of Tripura is famed throughout the world — do you compare it to some toy bow?'

verses.original:
लखन कहा हँसि हमरें जाना। सुनहु देव सब धनुष समाना।।
का छति लाभु जून धनु तौरें। देखा राम नयन के भोरें।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana laughed and said, 'In our view — listen, my lord — all bows are alike. What gain or loss in breaking an old bow? Rama merely glanced at it with his innocent eyes.'

verses.original:
छुअत टूट रघुपतिहु न दोसू। मुनि बिनु काज करिअ कत रोसू ।
बोले चितइ परसु की ओरा। रे सठ सुनेहि सुभाउ न मोरा।।
verses.translation: 'It broke at his mere touch — Raghupati is not at fault. O sage, why be angry without cause?' Parashurama looked menacingly at his axe and said, 'You impudent fool, have you not heard my nature?'

verses.original:
बालकु बोलि बधउँ नहिं तोही। केवल मुनि जड़ जानहि मोही।।
बाल ब्रह्मचारी अति कोही। बिस्व बिदित छत्रियकुल द्रोही।।
verses.translation: 'I do not kill you, calling you a mere child; but know me not merely as a mild sage. I am a lifelong celibate, exceedingly wrathful, known throughout the world as the scourge of the Kshatriya race.'

verses.original:
भुजबल भूमि भूप बिनु कीन्ही। बिपुल बार महिदेवन्ह दीन्ही।।
सहसबाहु भुज छेदनिहारा। परसु बिलोकु महीपकुमारा।।
verses.translation: 'By the strength of my arms I cleared the earth of kings and gave it to the Brahmins many times over. Behold this axe that severed the thousand arms of Sahasrabahu, O prince!'

verses.original:
मातु पितहि जनि सोचबस करसि महीसकिसोर।
गर्भन्ह के अर्भक दलन परसु मोर अति घोर।।272।।
verses.translation: 'Do not cause your mother and father grief, O young prince. My terrible axe has destroyed even the unborn children within wombs.'

verses.original:
बिहसि लखनु बोले मृदु बानी। अहो मुनीसु महा भटमानी।।
पुनि पुनि मोहि देखाव कुठारू। चहत उड़ावन फूँकि पहारू।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana laughed and replied gently, 'Ah, great sage, what a proud warrior you are! Again and again you show me your axe — do you hope to blow away a mountain with your breath?'

verses.original:
इहाँ कुम्हड़बतिया कोउ नाहीं। जे तरजनी देखि मरि जाहीं।।
देखि कुठारु सरासन बाना। मैं कछु कहा सहित अभिमाना।।
verses.translation: 'There are no pumpkin-vines here that would wilt at the pointing of a finger. Seeing your axe, bow, and arrows, I did speak with some pride.'

verses.original:
भृगुसुत समुझि जनेउ बिलोकी। जो कछु कहहु सहउँ रिस रोकी।।
सुर महिसुर हरिजन अरु गाई। हमरें कुल इन्ह पर न सुराई।।
verses.translation: 'Recognizing you as a son of the Bhrigu clan and seeing your sacred thread, I suppress my anger and endure whatever you say. Gods, Brahmins, devotees of Hari, and cows — our clan does not make war upon these.'

verses.original:
बधें पापु अपकीरति हारें। मारतहूँ पा परिअ तुम्हारें।।
कोटि कुलिस सम बचनु तुम्हारा। ब्यर्थ धरहु धनु बान कुठारा।।
verses.translation: 'Killing them would be sinful; losing to them would be disgraceful. Even if you strike us, we touch your feet. Your every word strikes like a million thunderbolts — you carry your bow, arrows, and axe in vain.'

verses.original:
जो बिलोकि अनुचित कहेउँ छमहु महामुनि धीर।
सुनि सरोष भृगुबंसमनि बोले गिरा गभीर।।273।।
verses.translation: 'If I have said anything improper, please forgive me, O great and patient sage.' Hearing this, the scion of the Bhrigu clan spoke in a deep, resonant voice.

verses.original:
कौसिक सुनहु मंद यहु बालकु। कुटिल कालबस निज कुल घालकु।।
भानु बंस राकेस कलंकू। निपट निरंकुस अबुध असंकू।।
verses.translation: 'Kaushika, listen — this boy is wicked, perverse, in the grip of Death, the destroyer of his own clan. He is a blot on the moon of the Solar dynasty — utterly unrestrained, ignorant, and fearless.'

verses.original:
काल कवलु होइहि छन माहीं। कहउँ पुकारि खोरि मोहि नाहीं।।
तुम्ह हटकउ जौं चहहु उबारा। कहि प्रतापु बलु रोषु हमारा।।
verses.translation: 'He will be swallowed by Death in an instant. I proclaim this — the fault will not be mine. You had better restrain him if you want to save him.' So saying, he declared his valor, strength, and wrath.

verses.original:
लखन कहेउ मुनि सुजस तुम्हारा। तुम्हहि अछत को बरनै पारा।।
अपने मुँह तुम्ह आपनि करनी। बार अनेक भाँति बहु बरनी।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana said, 'O sage, who can possibly describe your famous glory while you yourself are present? With your own mouth, you have narrated your own deeds many a time and in many a way.'

verses.original:
नहिं संतोषु त पुनि कछु कहहू। जनि रिस रोकि दुसह दुख सहहू।।
बीरब्रती तुम्ह धीर अछोभा। गारी देत न पावहु सोभा।।
verses.translation: 'If you are still not satisfied, then say some more — do not suppress your anger and suffer unbearable pain. You claim to be a brave warrior, calm and unshakable — yet scolding and abusing does not befit you.'

verses.original:
सूर समर करनी करहिं कहि न जनावहिं आपु।
बिद्यमान रन पाइ रिपु कायर कथहिं प्रतापु।।274।।
verses.translation: 'True heroes prove themselves through deeds in battle, never boasting about themselves. When a warrior finds his foe present on the battlefield, it is only the coward who talks about his prowess.'

verses.original:
तुम्ह तौ कालु हाँक जनु लावा। बार बार मोहि लागि बोलावा।।
सुनत लखन के बचन कठोरा। परसु सुधारि धरेउ कर घोरा।।
verses.translation: 'You have been beckoning Death itself, calling out to me again and again.' Hearing Lakshmana's harsh words, Parashurama gripped his terrible axe firmly in his hand.

verses.original:
अब जनि देइ दोसु मोहि लोगू। कटुबादी बालकु बधजोगू।।
बाल बिलोकि बहुत मैं बाँचा। अब यहु मरनिहार भा साँचा।।
verses.translation: 'Now let no one blame me — this sharp-tongued boy deserves to be killed. I spared him many times seeing he is a child, but now he truly courts death.'

verses.original:
कौसिक कहा छमिअ अपराधू। बाल दोष गुन गनहिं न साधू।।
खर कुठार मैं अकरुन कोही। आगें अपराधी गुरुद्रोही।।
verses.translation: Kaushika (Vishwamitra) said, 'Forgive his offense; the wise do not count the faults and virtues of children.' 'But my sharp axe is merciless and wrathful, and before me stands one who offends his guru.'

verses.original:
उतर देत छोड़उँ बिनु मारें। केवल कौसिक सील तुम्हारें।।
न त एहि काटि कुठार कठोरें। गुरहि उरिन होतेउँ श्रम थोरें।।
verses.translation: 'I release him without striking only because of your gentle nature, Kaushika. Otherwise, with this harsh axe I would have cut him down and easily repaid my debt to my guru.'

verses.original:
गाधिसूनु कह हृदयँ हँसि मुनिहि हरिअरइ सूझ।
अयमय खाँड न ऊखमय अजहुँ न बूझ अबूझ।।275।।
verses.translation: The son of Gadhi (Vishwamitra) laughed inwardly and thought, 'The sage sees only green all around. This is a blade of steel, not of sugarcane — the foolish one still does not understand.'

verses.original:
कहेउ लखन मुनि सीलु तुम्हारा। को नहि जान बिदित संसारा।।
माता पितहि उरिन भए नीकें। गुर रिनु रहा सोचु बड़ जीकें।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana said, 'O sage, your gentle nature is well known to the world. You have repaid your parents' debt handsomely. Your debt to your guru remains — a great weight on your mind.'

verses.original:
सो जनु हमरेहि माथे काढ़ा। दिन चलि गए ब्याज बड़ बाढ़ा।।
अब आनिअ ब्यवहरिआ बोली। तुरत देउँ मैं थैली खोली।।
verses.translation: 'It seems that debt has been placed upon our heads. Many days have passed and the interest has grown large. Now call a broker and settle accounts — I shall pay at once, opening my purse.'

verses.original:
सुनि कटु बचन कुठार सुधारा। हाय हाय सब सभा पुकारा।।
भृगुबर परसु देखावहु मोही। बिप्र बिचारि बचउँ नृपद्रोही।।
verses.translation: Hearing these cutting words, Parashurama brandished his axe. The whole assembly cried, 'Alas! Alas!' 'O great Bhrigu, you show me your axe! Considering you a Brahmin, I spare you, O enemy of kings.'

verses.original:
मिले न कबहुँ सुभट रन गाढ़े। द्विज देवता घरहि के बाढ़े।।
अनुचित कहि सब लोग पुकारे। रघुपति सयनहिं लखनु नेवारे।।
verses.translation: 'You have never met true warriors in battle; you are a Brahmin, brave only at home.' Everyone cried out, 'This is wrong!' Raghupati restrained Lakshmana with a glance.

verses.original:
लखन उतर आहुति सरिस भृगुबर कोपु कृसानु।
बढ़त देखि जल सम बचन बोले रघुकुलभानु।।276।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana's retorts were like fuel, and Bhrigu's descendant's wrath was the fire. Seeing it blaze, the sun of the Raghu dynasty spoke words like water to quench it.

verses.original:
नाथ करहु बालक पर छोहू। सूध दूधमुख करिअ न कोहू।।
जौं पै प्रभु प्रभाउ कछु जाना। तौ कि बराबरि करत अयाना।।
verses.translation: 'Lord, have compassion on this child. Do not be angry with this innocent, milk-faced boy. If you had any knowledge of the Lord's power, would this ignorant one dare challenge you?'

verses.original:
जौं लरिका कछु अचगरि करहीं। गुर पितु मातु मोद मन भरहीं।।
करिअ कृपा सिसु सेवक जानी। तुम्ह सम सील धीर मुनि ग्यानी।।
verses.translation: 'When children commit mischief, the guru, father, and mother are only pleased at heart. Please show him mercy, knowing him as a child and your servant. You are a sage of great patience, forbearance, and wisdom.'

verses.original:
राम बचन सुनि कछुक जुड़ाने। कहि कछु लखनु बहुरि मुसकाने।।
हँसत देखि नख सिख रिस ब्यापी। राम तोर भ्राता बड़ पापी।।
verses.translation: Hearing Rama's words, Parashurama cooled somewhat. But then Lakshmana said something again with a smile. Seeing him laugh, fury pervaded Parashurama from head to toe. 'Rama, your brother is a great sinner!'

verses.original:
गौर सरीर स्याम मन माहीं। कालकूटमुख पयमुख नाहीं।।
सहज टेढ़ अनुहरइ न तोही। नीचु मीचु सम देख न मौहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Fair of body but dark within — not honey-mouthed but venom-mouthed. Naturally crooked, he does not take after you. He is base and like death itself — I cannot bear the sight of him.'

verses.original:
लखन कहेउ हँसि सुनहु मुनि क्रोधु पाप कर मूल।
जेहि बस जन अनुचित करहिं चरहिं बिस्व प्रतिकूल।।277।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana laughed and said, 'Listen, O sage — anger is the root of sin. Under its sway, people do wrong and act against the whole world.'

verses.original:
मैं तुम्हार अनुचर मुनिराया। परिहरि कोपु करिअ अब दाया।।
टूट चाप नहिं जुरहि रिसाने। बैठिअ होइहिं पाय पिराने।।
verses.translation: 'I am your servant, O great sage. Abandon your anger and show mercy now. The broken bow cannot be joined again by being angry; please sit down, or your feet will ache from standing.'

verses.original:
जौ अति प्रिय तौ करिअ उपाई। जोरिअ कोउ बड़ गुनी बोलाई।।
बोलत लखनहिं जनकु डेराहीं। मष्ट करहु अनुचित भल नाहीं।।
verses.translation: 'If the bow is so dear to you, then arrange a remedy — summon some great craftsman to repair it.' At Lakshmana's words, Janaka trembled and said, 'Be quiet — this is most improper.'

verses.original:
थर थर कापहिं पुर नर नारी। छोट कुमार खोट बड़ भारी।।
भृगुपति सुनि सुनि निरभय बानी। रिस तन जरइ होइ बल हानी।।
verses.translation: The men and women of the city trembled in terror. 'This little prince is terribly naughty.' Hearing the fearless words again and again, Parashurama burned with rage while his strength ebbed away.

verses.original:
बोले रामहि देइ निहोरा। बचउँ बिचारि बंधु लघु तोरा।।
मनु मलीन तनु सुंदर कैसें। बिष रस भरा कनक घटु जैसैं।।
verses.translation: He addressed Rama with an appeal, 'I spare your brother only because he is young. His mind is foul though his body is fair — like a golden pot filled with poison.'

verses.original:
सुनि लछिमन बिहसे बहुरि नयन तरेरे राम।
गुर समीप गवने सकुचि परिहरि बानी बाम।।278।।
verses.translation: Hearing this, Lakshmana laughed; then Rama glared at him sternly. Abashed, Lakshmana went and sat near the guru, abandoning his provocative speech.

verses.original:
अति बिनीत मृदु सीतल बानी। बोले रामु जोरि जुग पानी।।
सुनहु नाथ तुम्ह सहज सुजाना। बालक बचनु करिअ नहिं काना।।
verses.translation: With utmost humility and cool, gentle words, Rama spoke with folded hands, 'Listen, lord — you are naturally wise. A child's words should not be taken to heart.'

verses.original:
बररै बालक एकु सुभाऊ। इन्हहि न संत बिदूषहिं काऊ।।
तेहिं नाहीं कछु काज बिगारा। अपराधी में नाथ तुम्हारा।।
verses.translation: 'It is the one nature of all children to prattle thus; even saints never take offense at them. He has done no real harm; I, my lord, am the offender before you.'

verses.original:
कृपा कोपु बधु बँधब गोसाईं। मो पर करिअ दास की नाई।।
कहिअ बेगि जेहि बिधि रिस जाई। मुनिनायक सोइ करौं उपाई।।
verses.translation: 'Grace or anger, death or bondage, my master — deal with me as you would with a slave. Tell me quickly how your anger may be appeased, O chief of sages, and I shall do accordingly.'

verses.original:
कह मुनि राम जाइ रिस कैसें। अजहुँ अनुज तव चितव अनैसें।।
एहि के कंठ कुठारु न दीन्हा। तौ मैं काह कोपु करि कीन्हा।।
verses.translation: The sage said, 'Rama, how can my anger be appeased? Even now your brother glares at me defiantly. Unless I put my axe to his throat, what meaning has my wrath?'

verses.original:
गर्भ स्त्रवहिं अवनिप रवनि सुनि कुठार गति घोर।
परसु अछत देखउँ जिअत बैरी भूपकिसोर।।279।।
verses.translation: 'The very mention of my axe's terrible power causes pregnant queens to miscarry, and yet this princeling stands alive before me, with the axe right here!'

verses.original:
बहइ न हाथु दहइ रिस छाती। भा कुठारु कुंठित नृपघाती।।
भयउ बाम बिधि फिरेउ सुभाऊ। मोरे हृदयँ कृपा कसि काऊ।।
verses.translation: 'My hand does not move to strike, yet my heart burns with rage. This axe that slays kings has become blunt. Fate has turned against me; my nature has reversed. When has my heart ever known compassion?'

verses.original:
आजु दया दुखु दुसह सहावा। सुनि सौमित्र बिहसि सिरु नावा।।
बाउ कृपा मूरति अनुकूला। बोलत बचन झरत जनु फूला।।
verses.translation: 'Today compassion has forced upon me unbearable suffering.' Hearing this, Lakshmana smiled and bowed his head. 'The Lord's grace is most favorable to you, sir — your words are dropping like flowers.'

verses.original:
जौं पै कृपाँ जरिहिं मुनि गाता। क्रोध भएँ तनु राख बिधाता।।
देखु जनक हठि बालक एहू। कीन्ह चहत जड़ जमपुर गेहू।।
verses.translation: 'If mercy burns one's body, O sage, then when anger comes, may the Creator preserve you. Look, Janaka — this stubborn child wants foolishly to make his home in Yama's city.'

verses.original:
बेगि करहु किन आँखिन्ह ओटा। देखत छोट खोट नृप ढोटा।।
बिहसे लखनु कहा मन माहीं। मूदें आँखि कतहुँ कोउ नाहीं।।
verses.translation: 'Quickly remove him from my sight — the king's boy looks small but is very mischievous.' Lakshmana laughed and thought to himself, 'Closing one's eyes does not make anyone disappear.'

verses.original:
परसुरामु तब राम प्रति बोले उर अति क्रोधु।
संभु सरासनु तोरि सठ करसि हमार प्रबोधु।।280।।
verses.translation: Then Parashurama spoke to Rama, his heart full of rage, 'Having broken Shambhu's bow, fool, you now try to counsel me?'

verses.original:
बंधु कहइ कटु संमत तोरें। तू छल बिनय करसि कर जोरें।।
करु परितोषु मोर संग्रामा। नाहिं त छाड़ कहाउब रामा।।
verses.translation: 'Your brother speaks rudely with your approval, while you make a show of humility with folded hands. Satisfy me in combat, or else give up the name of Rama.'

verses.original:
छलु तजि करहि समरु सिवद्रोही। बंधु सहित न त मारउँ तोही।।
भृगुपति बकहिं कुठार उठाएँ। मन मुसकाहिं रामु सिर नाएँ।।
verses.translation: 'Abandon your deceit and fight, O enemy of Shiva, or I shall slay you along with your brother.' Bhrigu's lord railed on, axe raised; Rama smiled within and bowed his head.

verses.original:
गुनह लखन कर हम पर रोषू। कतहुँ सुधाइहु ते बड़ दोषू।।
टेढ़ जानि सब बंदइ काहू। बक्र चंद्रमहि ग्रसइ न राहू।।
verses.translation: 'You are angry at me for Lakshmana's fault. If you ever find any fault anywhere, it is a great failing. Who respects one known to be crooked? Even Rahu does not devour the crescent moon.'

verses.original:
राम कहेउ रिस तजिअ मुनीसा। कर कुठारु आगें यह सीसा।।
जेंहिं रिस जाइ करिअ सोइ स्वामी। मोहि जानि आपन अनुगामी।।
verses.translation: Rama said, 'Cast aside your anger, O lord of sages. Here is your axe, and here is my head. Do whatever will make your anger depart, my master. Consider me your own follower.'

verses.original:
प्रभुहि सेवकहि समरु कस तजहु बिप्रबर रोसु।
बेषु बिलोकें कहेसि कछु बालकहू नहिं दोसु।।281।।
verses.translation: 'How can there be battle between a master and his servant? Abandon your wrath, O best of Brahmins. Seeing my appearance, the boy said some things — even a child cannot be blamed for that.'

verses.original:
देखि कुठार बान धनु धारी। भै लरिकहि रिस बीरु बिचारी।।
नामु जान पै तुम्हहि न चीन्हा। बंस सुभायँ उतरु तेंहिं दीन्हा।।
verses.translation: 'Seeing you bearing an axe, arrows, and bow, the child thought you a warrior, not a sage. He knows your name but did not recognize you — it was the natural manner of his clan that prompted his reply.'

verses.original:
जौं तुम्ह औतेहु मुनि की नाईं। पद रज सिर सिसु धरत गोसाईं।।
छमहु चूक अनजानत केरी। चहिअ बिप्र उर कृपा घनेरी।।
verses.translation: 'If you had come in the manner of a sage, this child would have placed the dust of your feet upon his head. Forgive the error of one who did not know — a Brahmin's heart should hold abundant mercy.'

verses.original:
हमहि तुम्हहि सरिबरि कसि नाथा।।कहहु न कहाँ चरन कहँ माथा।।
राम मात्र लघु नाम हमारा। परसु सहित बड़ नाम तोहारा।।
verses.translation: 'How can there be any comparison between us, my lord? You tell me — where are the feet, and where is the forehead? Our name is merely "Rama" — small and short. Your name, with "Parashu" attached, is great and grand.'

verses.original:
देव एकु गुनु धनुष हमारें। नव गुन परम पुनीत तुम्हारें।।
सब प्रकार हम तुम्ह सन हारे। छमहु बिप्र अपराध हमारे।।
verses.translation: 'My lord, our bow has but one virtue; your bow has nine supreme and holy virtues. In every way we are defeated by you. Forgive our offenses, O Brahmin.'

verses.original:
बार बार मुनि बिप्रबर कहा राम सन राम।
बोले भृगुपति सरुष हसि तहूँ बंधु सम बाम।।282।।
verses.translation: Again and again, the great Brahmin sage called Rama by name. Bhrigu's lord replied with an angry smile, 'Even you are as perverse as your brother.'

verses.original:
निपटहिं द्विज करि जानहि मोही। मैं जस बिप्र सुनावउँ तोही।।
चाप स्त्रुवा सर आहुति जानू। कोप मोर अति घोर कृसानु।।
verses.translation: 'You take me for a mere Brahmin — let me tell you what kind of Brahmin I am. Know the bow as my sacrificial ladle, the arrows as my offerings, and my wrath as the fiercest sacrificial fire.'

verses.original:
समिधि सेन चतुरंग सुहाई। महा महीप भए पसु आई।।
मै एहि परसु काटि बलि दीन्हे। समर जग्य जप कोटिन्ह कीन्हे।।
verses.translation: 'The four-fold armies are the fine sacrificial fuel, and the great kings are the sacrificial animals. I have offered them with this axe as oblations and performed millions of battle-sacrifices.'

verses.original:
मोर प्रभाउ बिदित नहिं तोरें। बोलसि निदरि बिप्र के भोरें।।
भंजेउ चापु दापु बड़ बाढ़ा। अहमिति मनहुँ जीति जगु ठाढ़ा।।
verses.translation: 'My power is not known to you; you dare speak slightingly, presuming on the Brahmin's meekness. You broke the bow and your pride has swelled — as though you stand having conquered the world through arrogance.'

verses.original:
राम कहा मुनि कहहु बिचारी। रिस अति बड़ि लघु चूक हमारी।।
छुअतहिं टूट पिनाक पुराना। मैं कहि हेतु करौं अभिमाना।।
verses.translation: Rama said, 'Sage, speak with reflection — your anger is very great, and my fault is small. The ancient Pinaka broke at a mere touch — on what basis should I claim any pride?'

verses.original:
जौं हम निदरहिं बिप्र बदि सत्य सुनहु भृगुनाथ।
तौ अस को जग सुभटु जेहि भय बस नावहिं माथ।।283।।
verses.translation: 'If we disrespect Brahmins as you say, O lord of the Bhrigus, hear the truth — who then is such a warrior in this world that he could make us bow our heads in fear?'

verses.original:
देव दनुज भूपति भट नाना। समबल अधिक होउ बलवाना।।
जौं रन हमहि पचारै कोऊ। लरहिं सुखेन कालु किन होऊ।।
verses.translation: 'Among gods, demons, kings, and warriors of every kind — be they equal in strength or even mightier — if anyone challenges us in battle, we shall gladly fight, even if Death himself comes.'

verses.original:
छत्रिय तनु धरि समर सकाना। कुल कलंकु तेहिं पावँर आना।।
कहउँ सुभाउ न कुलहि प्रसंसी। कालहु डरहिं न रन रघुबंसी।।
verses.translation: 'A Kshatriya who shrinks from battle in his warrior's body brings disgrace upon his clan — such a one is wretched. I speak my nature, not to boast of my lineage — the Raghus do not fear even Death in battle.'

verses.original:
बिप्रबंस कै असि प्रभुताई। अभय होइ जो तुम्हहि डेराई।।
सुनु मृदु गूढ़ बचन रघुपति के। उघरे पटल परसुधर मति के।।
verses.translation: 'Such is the glory of the Brahmin race that even one who is terrified of you remains safe from harm.' Hearing Rama's gentle yet profound words, the veil over Parashurama's understanding was lifted.

verses.original:
राम रमापति कर धनु लेहू। खैंचहु मिटै मोर संदेहू।।
देत चापु आपुहिं चलि गयऊ। परसुराम मन बिसमय भयऊ।।
verses.translation: 'Rama, take this bow of Vishnu. Draw it, and my doubt will be dispelled.' As he offered the bow, it went to Rama of its own accord. Parashurama was struck with wonder.

verses.original:
जाना राम प्रभाउ तब पुलक प्रफुल्लित गात।
जोरि पानि बोले बचन ह्दयँ न प्रेमु अमात।।284।।
verses.translation: Then he recognized Rama's divine power; his body thrilled and blossomed with joy. With folded hands he spoke, his heart brimming with love that could not be contained.

verses.original:
जय रघुबंस बनज बन भानू। गहन दनुज कुल दहन कृसानु।।
जय सुर बिप्र धेनु हितकारी। जय मद मोह कोह भ्रम हारी।।
verses.translation: 'Victory to you, O sun to the lotus-forest of the Raghu dynasty! O blazing fire consuming the dense forest of the demon race! Victory, O benefactor of gods, Brahmins, and cows! Victory, O destroyer of pride, delusion, anger, and confusion!'

verses.original:
बिनय सील करुना गुन सागर। जयति बचन रचना अति नागर।।
सेवक सुखद सुभग सब अंगा। जय सरीर छबि कोटि अनंगा।।
verses.translation: 'O ocean of humility, virtue, compassion, and goodness! Victory! Your speech is supremely eloquent. O giver of happiness to your servants, beautiful in every limb! Victory, whose bodily splendor surpasses millions of Kamadevas!'

verses.original:
करौं काह मुख एक प्रसंसा। जय महेस मन मानस हंसा।।
अनुचित बहुत कहेउँ अग्याता। छमहु छमामंदिर दोउ भ्राता।।
verses.translation: 'With but one mouth, what praise can I offer? Victory, O swan sporting on the Manasa lake of Mahesha's mind! I have spoken much that was unseemly in my ignorance — forgive me, O two brothers, abode of forgiveness.'

verses.original:
कहि जय जय जय रघुकुलकेतू। भृगुपति गए बनहि तप हेतू।।
अपभयँ कुटिल महीप डेराने। जहँ तहँ कायर गवँहिं पराने।।
verses.translation: Crying 'Victory, victory, victory to the banner of the Raghu clan!' Bhrigu's lord departed for the forest to perform penance. The wicked, scheming kings were now terrified; cowards fled here and there in all directions.

verses.original:
देवन्ह दीन्हीं दुंदुभीं प्रभु पर बरषहिं फूल।
हरषे पुर नर नारि सब मिटी मोहमय सूल।।285।।
verses.translation: The gods beat their drums and rained flowers upon the Lord. All the men and women of the city rejoiced; the thorn of delusion-born anguish was removed.

verses.original:
अति गहगहे बाजने बाजे। सबहिं मनोहर मंगल साजे।।
जूथ जूथ मिलि सुमुख सुनयनीं। करहिं गान कल कोकिलबयनी।।
verses.translation: Many instruments played with great fanfare; everyone donned beautiful festive attire. Groups of sweet-voiced women, fair-faced and bright-eyed, gathered together and sang melodious songs like cuckoos.

verses.original:
सुखु बिदेह कर बरनि न जाई। जन्मदरिद्र मनहुँ निधि पाई।।
गत त्रास भइ सीय सुखारी। जनु बिधु उदयँ चकोरकुमारी।।
verses.translation: Videha's joy was beyond description — as though one born in poverty had found a treasure. Freed from fear, Sita became happy — like a young chakori bird at the rising of the moon.

verses.original:
जनक कीन्ह कौसिकहि प्रनामा। प्रभु प्रसाद धनु भंजेउ रामा।।
मोहि कृतकृत्य कीन्ह दुहुँ भाईं। अब जो उचित सो कहिअ गोसाई।।
verses.translation: Janaka bowed to Kaushika and said, 'By the Lord's grace, Rama has broken the bow. Both brothers have fulfilled my life. Now tell me what is proper to do, my lord.'

verses.original:
कह मुनि सुनु नरनाथ प्रबीना। रहा बिबाहु चाप आधीना।।
टूटतहीं धनु भयउ बिबाहू। सुर नर नाग बिदित सब काहु।।
verses.translation: The sage said, 'Listen, O wise king — the marriage depended on the bow. The moment the bow broke, the marriage was accomplished — this is known to gods, men, and nagas alike.'

verses.original:
तदपि जाइ तुम्ह करहु अब जथा बंस ब्यवहारु।
बूझि बिप्र कुलबृद्ध गुर बेद बिदित आचारु।।286।।
verses.translation: 'Nevertheless, go now and follow the customary practices of your family. Consult the Brahmins, the family elders, the guru, and the procedures prescribed by the Vedas.'

verses.original:
दूत अवधपुर पठवहु जाई। आनहिं नृप दसरथहि बोलाई।।
मुदित राउ कहि भलेहिं कृपाला। पठए दूत बोलि तेहि काला।।
verses.translation: 'Send a messenger to Ayodhya at once and bring King Dasharatha here.' The delighted king said, 'Very well, O gracious one,' and immediately summoned and dispatched messengers.

verses.original:
बहुरि महाजन सकल बोलाए। आइ सबन्हि सादर सिर नाए।।
हाट बाट मंदिर सुरबासा। नगरु सँवारहु चारिहुँ पासा।।
verses.translation: Then he called together all the leading citizens. They all came and respectfully bowed. 'Decorate the markets, streets, temples, and shrines — beautify the city on all four sides.'

verses.original:
हरषि चले निज निज गृह आए। पुनि परिचारक बोलि पठाए।।
रचहु बिचित्र बितान बनाई। सिर धरि बचन चले सचु पाई।।
verses.translation: They departed joyfully to their respective homes, and servants were also sent with instructions. 'Erect wonderful canopies and awnings.' They placed the command on their heads and left happily.

verses.original:
पठए बोलि गुनी तिन्ह नाना। जे बितान बिधि कुसल सुजाना।।
बिधिहि बंदि तिन्ह कीन्ह अरंभा। बिरचे कनक कदलि के खंभा।।
verses.translation: Many skilled artisans who were expert in constructing canopies were summoned. After invoking the Creator, they began their work, fashioning pillars of golden plantain trees.

verses.original:
हरित मनिन्ह के पत्र फल पदुमराग के फूल।
रचना देखि बिचित्र अति मनु बिरंचि कर भूल।।287।।
verses.translation: The leaves and fruits were made of emeralds, and the flowers of rubies. The artistry was so marvelous that it bewildered even the mind of Brahma himself.

verses.original:
बेनि हरित मनिमय सब कीन्हे। सरल सपरब परहिं नहिं चीन्हे।।
कनक कलित अहिबेल बनाई। लखि नहि परइ सपरन सुहाई।।
verses.translation: All the tendrils were made of green gems — so smooth and jointed that they could not be told from real ones. Creeper-vines of gold were exquisitely wrought, so lifelike with their leaves that one could not tell they were artificial.

verses.original:
तेहि के रचि पचि बंध बनाए। बिच बिच मुकता दाम सुहाए।।
मानिक मरकत कुलिस पिरोजा। चीरि कोरि पचि रचे सरोजा।।
verses.translation: Around these were fashioned beautiful bows and loops, with strings of lovely pearls set here and there. Rubies, emeralds, diamonds, and turquoise were carved and shaped and crafted into lotuses.

verses.original:
किए भृंग बहुरंग बिहंगा। गुंजहिं कूजहिं पवन प्रसंगा।।
सुर प्रतिमा खंभन गढ़ी काढ़ी। मंगल द्रब्य लिएँ सब ठाढ़ी।।
verses.translation: Bees and colorful birds were fashioned; they buzzed and sang when stirred by the breeze. Images of gods were carved and set upon the pillars, each holding auspicious objects in their hands.

verses.original: चौंकें भाँति अनेक पुराईं। सिंधुर मनिमय सहज सुहाई।।
verses.translation: The courtyard was filled with many kinds of beautiful designs, naturally lovely, made of vermilion and gems.

verses.original:
सौरभ पल्लव सुभग सुठि किए नीलमनि कोरि।।
हेम बौर मरकत घवरि लसत पाटमय डोरि।।288।।
verses.translation: Exquisite leaves were carved from sapphires, blossoms fashioned from gold, and clusters of emeralds were strung on silk threads, all shining beautifully.

verses.original:
रचे रुचिर बर बंदनिबारे। मनहुँ मनोभवँ फंद सँवारे।।
मंगल कलस अनेक बनाए। ध्वज पताक पट चमर सुहाए।।
verses.translation: Beautiful and elegant wedding arches were erected, as if Kamadeva himself had fashioned his snares. Numerous auspicious pots were prepared, and flags, banners, fine cloths, and whisks adorned everything beautifully.

verses.original:
दीप मनोहर मनिमय नाना। जाइ न बरनि बिचित्र बिताना।।
जेहिं मंडप दुलहिनि बैदेही। सो बरनै असि मति कबि केही।।
verses.translation: Enchanting lamps of many kinds studded with gems—the wondrous canopy cannot be described. The wedding pavilion where the bride Vaidehi would sit—what poet has the wit to describe it?

verses.original:
दूलहु रामु रूप गुन सागर। सो बितानु तिहुँ लोक उजागर।।
जनक भवन कै सौभा जैसी। गृह गृह प्रति पुर देखिअ तैसी।।
verses.translation: The groom is Rama, an ocean of beauty and virtue; the canopy shines gloriously across the three worlds. The splendor that adorned Janaka's palace was mirrored in every home throughout the city.

verses.original:
जेहिं तेरहुति तेहि समय निहारी। तेहि लघु लगहिं भुवन दस चारी।।
जो संपदा नीच गृह सोहा। सो बिलोकि सुरनायक मोहा।।
verses.translation: Whoever beheld Tirhut (Mithila) at that time felt the fourteen worlds were insignificant in comparison. The wealth that graced even the humblest homes was enough to enchant the lord of the gods.

verses.original:
बसइ नगर जेहि लच्छ करि कपट नारि बर बेषु।।
तेहि पुर कै सोभा कहत सकुचहिं सारद सेषु।।289।।
verses.translation: In the city where Lakshmi herself dwells, having assumed the disguise of a beautiful woman—even Sharada and Shesha hesitate to describe the splendor of that city.

verses.original:
पहुँचे दूत राम पुर पावन। हरषे नगर बिलोकि सुहावन।।
भूप द्वार तिन्ह खबरि जनाई। दसरथ नृप सुनि लिए बोलाई।।
verses.translation: The messengers arrived at the sacred city of Rama and were delighted to see the beautiful town. They brought the news to the king's gate, and King Dasharatha, hearing of them, summoned them.

verses.original:
करि प्रनामु तिन्ह पाती दीन्ही। मुदित महीप आपु उठि लीन्ही।।
बारि बिलोचन बाचत पाँती। पुलक गात आई भरि छाती।।
