verses.translation: Again and again, bowing at Kaushika's feet, the king said: "All this happiness, O King of Sages, is the gift of your gracious glance."

verses.original:
जनक सनेहु सीलु करतूती। नृपु सब भाँति सराह बिभूती।।
दिन उठि बिदा अवधपति मागा। राखहिं जनकु सहित अनुरागा।।
verses.translation: The king praised Janaka's affection, virtue, and munificence in every way. Each day, the King of Ayodhya would ask leave to depart, but Janaka would lovingly hold him back.

verses.original:
नित नूतन आदरु अधिकाई। दिन प्रति सहस भाँति पहुनाई।।
नित नव नगर अनंद उछाहू। दसरथ गवनु सोहाइ न काहू।।
verses.translation: Each day brought new and greater hospitality, with a thousand forms of entertainment daily. Each day brought new joy and celebration to the city; no one wanted Dasharatha to leave.

verses.original:
बहुत दिवस बीते एहि भाँती। जनु सनेह रजु बँधे बराती।।
कौसिक सतानंद तब जाई। कहा बिदेह नृपहि समुझाई।।
verses.translation: Many days passed in this manner; the wedding party seemed bound by cords of affection. Then Kaushika and Shatananda went and counseled King Videha.

verses.original:
अब दसरथ कहँ आयसु देहू। जद्यपि छाड़ि न सकहु सनेहू।।
भलेहिं नाथ कहि सचिव बोलाए। कहि जय जीव सीस तिन्ह नाए।।
verses.translation: "Now give Dasharatha leave to depart, though you cannot let go of your love." "Very well, my lord," said the king, and summoned his ministers. They came and bowed, saying "Victory and long life!"

verses.original:
अवधनाथु चाहत चलन भीतर करहु जनाउ।
भए प्रेमबस सचिव सुनि बिप्र सभासद राउ।।332।।
verses.translation: "The Lord of Ayodhya wishes to depart; make the announcement within." Hearing this, the ministers, the Brahmins, the courtiers, and the king himself became overwhelmed with emotion.

verses.original:
पुरबासी सुनि चलिहि बराता। बूझत बिकल परस्पर बाता।।
सत्य गवनु सुनि सब बिलखाने। मनहुँ साँझ सरसिज सकुचाने।।
verses.translation: The citizens, hearing the wedding party would depart, anxiously asked each other. When the departure was confirmed, all were grieved—like lotuses wilting at dusk.

verses.original:
जहँ जहँ आवत बसे बराती। तहँ तहँ सिद्ध चला बहु भाँती।।
बिबिध भाँति मेवा पकवाना। भोजन साजु न जाइ बखाना।।
verses.translation: Wherever the wedding guests had stayed, provisions of many kinds were sent. All manner of sweets and delicacies were prepared; the feast arrangements defy description.

verses.original:
भरि भरि बसहँ अपार कहारा। पठई जनक अनेक सुसारा।।
तुरग लाख रथ सहस पचीसा। सकल सँवारे नख अरु सीसा।।
verses.translation: Countless porters carried loads on shoulder-poles; Janaka sent abundant supplies. A hundred thousand horses, twenty-five thousand chariots—all decorated from hoof to head.

verses.original:
मत्त सहस दस सिंधुर साजे। जिन्हहि देखि दिसिकुंजर लाजे।।
कनक बसन मनि भरि भरि जाना। महिषीं धेनु बस्तु बिधि नाना।।
verses.translation: Ten thousand stately elephants were caparisoned, whose sight put the elephants of the quarters to shame. Carriages filled with gold, garments, and gems; buffaloes, cows, and goods of every kind.

verses.original:
दाइज अमित न सकिअ कहि दीन्ह बिदेहँ बहोरि।
जो अवलोकत लोकपति लोक संपदा थोरि।।333।।
verses.translation: Immeasurable dowry that cannot be described—Videha gave yet more. Looking at it, even the world-guardians would consider their own wealth insignificant.

verses.original:
सबु समाजु एहि भाँति बनाई। जनक अवधपुर दीन्ह पठाई।।
चलिहि बरात सुनत सब रानीं। बिकल मीनगन जनु लघु पानीं।।
verses.translation: Having prepared the entire retinue in this manner, Janaka dispatched it toward Ayodhya. Hearing that the procession was about to leave, all the queens were distraught—like fish in diminishing water.

verses.original:
पुनि पुनि सीय गोद करि लेहीं। देइ असीस सिखावनु देहीं।।
होएहु संतत पियहि पिआरी। चिरु अहिबात असीस हमारी।।
verses.translation: Again and again they took Sita in their laps, giving blessings and instructions: "May you always be dear to your husband! May you forever remain a married woman—this is our blessing."

verses.original:
सासु ससुर गुर सेवा करेहू। पति रुख लखि आयसु अनुसरेहू।।
अति सनेह बस सखीं सयानी। नारि धरम सिखवहिं मृदु बानी।।
verses.translation: "Serve your mother-in-law, father-in-law, and guru. Study your husband's wishes and follow his commands." With great affection, the wise female companions taught the brides the duties of a wife in gentle words.

verses.original:
सादर सकल कुअँरि समुझाई। रानिन्ह बार बार उर लाई।।
बहुरि बहुरि भेटहिं महतारीं। कहहिं बिरंचि रचीं कत नारीं।।
verses.translation: All the princesses were respectfully counseled, and the queens embraced them again and again. The mothers embraced them over and over, saying: "Why did Brahma create women?"

verses.original:
तेहि अवसर भाइन्ह सहित रामु भानु कुल केतु।
चले जनक मंदिर मुदित बिदा करावन हेतु।।334।।
verses.translation: At that time, Rama, the banner of the Solar Race, accompanied by his brothers, went happily to Janaka's palace to take his formal leave.

verses.original:
चारिअ भाइ सुभायँ सुहाए। नगर नारि नर देखन धाए।।
कोउ कह चलन चहत हहिं आजू। कीन्ह बिदेह बिदा कर साजू।।
verses.translation: The four brothers, naturally charming, walked through the city; men and women rushed to see them. Someone said: "They wish to depart today; Videha has prepared the farewell arrangements."

verses.original:
लेहु नयन भरि रूप निहारी। प्रिय पाहुने भूप सुत चारी।।
को जानै केहि सुकृत सयानी। नयन अतिथि कीन्हे बिधि आनी।।
verses.translation: "Fill your eyes and gaze upon their beauty—these four dear royal guests. Who knows by whose wise merit the Creator brought these guests for our eyes?"

verses.original:
मरनसीलु जिमि पाव पिऊषा। सुरतरु लहै जनम कर भूखा।।
पाव नारकी हरिपदु जैसें। इन्ह कर दरसनु हम कहँ तैसे।।
verses.translation: "It is as if a mortal found the elixir of immortality, or a pauper found a wish-fulfilling tree, or a denizen of hell attained the abode of Hari. Such is the sight of these princes to us."

verses.original:
निरखि राम सोभा उर धरहू। निज मन फनि मूरति मनि करहू।।
एहि बिधि सबहि नयन फलु देता। गए कुअँर सब राज निकेता।।
verses.translation: "Gaze upon Rama's beauty and treasure it in your hearts; make it the jewel upon the serpent-head of your minds." In this way, giving the reward of sight to all, the princes arrived at the royal palace.

verses.original:
रूप सिंधु सब बंधु लखि हरषि उठा रनिवासु।
करहि निछावरि आरती महा मुदित मन सासु।।335।।
verses.translation: Seeing the ocean of beauty that was the four brothers, the inner court rose with joy. The mothers-in-law, supremely delighted, waved the auspicious arati and showered gifts.

verses.original:
देखि राम छबि अति अनुरागीं। प्रेमबिबस पुनि पुनि पद लागीं।।
रही न लाज प्रीति उर छाई। सहज सनेहु बरनि किमि जाई।।
verses.translation: Seeing Rama's beauty, the queens were filled with deep love and again and again fell at his feet. All modesty vanished, overwhelmed by affection—how can such natural love be described?

verses.original:
भाइन्ह सहित उबटि अन्हवाए। छरस असन अति हेतु जेवाँए।।
बोले रामु सुअवसरु जानी। सील सनेह सकुचमय बानी।।
verses.translation: They bathed the brothers with turmeric paste and lovingly fed them a six-course meal. Then Rama, knowing the right moment, spoke with words of courtesy, affection, and gentle shyness.

verses.original:
राउ अवधपुर चहत सिधाए। बिदा होन हम इहाँ पठाए।।
मातु मुदित मन आयसु देहू। बालक जानि करब नित नेहू।।
verses.translation: "The king wishes to proceed to Ayodhya; we have been sent here to take leave. O mothers, kindly give us your joyful permission. Knowing us as children, always shower us with your love."

verses.original:
सुनत बचन बिलखेउ रनिवासू। बोलि न सकहिं प्रेमबस सासू।।
हृदयँ लगाइ कुअँरि सब लीन्ही। पतिन्ह सौंपि बिनती अति कीन्ही।।
verses.translation: Hearing these words, the inner court was grieved. The mothers-in-law, overcome with love, could not speak. They embraced all the princesses to their hearts and, entrusting them to their husbands, made earnest entreaties.

verses.original:
करि बिनय सिय रामहि समरपी जोरि कर पुनि पुनि कहै।
बलि जाँउ तात सुजान तुम्ह कहुँ बिदित गति सब की अहै।।
परिवार पुरजन मोहि राजहि प्रानप्रिय सिय जानिबी।
तुलसीस सीलु सनेहु लखि निज किंकरी करि मानिबी।।
verses.translation: Making humble entreaty and entrusting Sita to Rama, she said again and again with folded hands: "I bow to you, O wise one! You know the state of everyone's heart. The family, the citizens, and I—all consider Sita as dear as our own life. O Lord of Tulasi, seeing your grace and affection, please accept her as your humble servant."

verses.original:
तुम्ह परिपूरन काम जान सिरोमनि भावप्रिय।
जन गुन गाहक राम दोष दलन करुनायतन।।336।।
verses.translation: "You are complete in all desires, O crest-jewel of the wise, dear to devotion. O Rama, you appreciate the virtues of your servants, you destroy their faults, you are the abode of compassion."

verses.original:
अस कहि रही चरन गहि रानी। प्रेम पंक जनु गिरा समानी।।
सुनि सनेहसानी बर बानी। बहुबिधि राम सासु सनमानी।।
verses.translation: Having said this, the queen clasped his feet, her voice drowning in the mire of emotion. Hearing her words steeped in love, Rama honored his mother-in-law in many ways.

verses.original:
राम बिदा मागत कर जोरी। कीन्ह प्रनामु बहोरि बहोरी।।
पाइ असीस बहुरि सिरु नाई। भाइन्ह सहित चले रघुराई।।
verses.translation: Rama asked for leave with folded hands, prostrating again and again. Receiving blessings and bowing his head once more, the Lord of the Raghus departed with all his brothers.

verses.original:
मंजु मधुर मूरति उर आनी। भई सनेह सिथिल सब रानी।।
पुनि धीरजु धरि कुअँरि हँकारी। बार बार भेटहिं महतारीं।।
verses.translation: Holding Rama's sweet and lovely image in their hearts, all the queens became weak with emotion. Then gathering courage, they called the princesses and the mothers embraced them again and again.

verses.original:
पहुँचावहिं फिरि मिलहिं बहोरी। बढ़ी परस्पर प्रीति न थोरी।।
पुनि पुनि मिलत सखिन्ह बिलगाई। बाल बच्छ जिमि धेनु लवाई।।
verses.translation: They would accompany them a little way, then return and meet again; the mutual love grew ever greater. Meeting again and again, the friends had to separate the princesses from their mothers, as one separates a cow from her calf.

verses.original:
प्रेमबिबस नर नारि सब सखिन्ह सहित रनिवासु।
मानहुँ कीन्ह बिदेहपुर करुनाँ बिरहँ निवासु।।337।।
verses.translation: Overcome with love, all the men and women, the friends and the entire inner court—as if Videha's city had been made a dwelling of sorrow and separation.

verses.original:
सुक सारिका जानकी ज्याए। कनक पिंजरन्हि राखि पढ़ाए।।
ब्याकुल कहहिं कहाँ बैदेही। सुनि धीरजु परिहरइ न केही।।
verses.translation: Parrots and mynas that Janaki had lovingly reared in golden cages called out distractedly: "Where is Vaidehi?" Hearing them, who could keep their composure?

verses.original:
भए बिकल खग मृग एहि भाँति। मनुज दसा कैसें कहि जाती।।
बंधु समेत जनकु तब आए। प्रेम उमगि लोचन जल छाए।।
verses.translation: When even birds and beasts were so distressed, how can the condition of humans be described? Then Janaka came with his brothers, love swelling and tears filling his eyes.

verses.original:
सीय बिलोकि धीरता भागी। रहे कहावत परम बिरागी।।
लीन्हि राँय उर लाइ जानकी। मिटी महामरजाद ग्यान की।।
verses.translation: Seeing Sita, his composure fled; he who was called the supreme renunciant could not control himself. The king pressed Janaki to his heart; the grand boundary of wisdom crumbled.

verses.original:
समुझावत सब सचिव सयाने। कीन्ह बिचारु न अवसर जाने।।
बारहिं बार सुता उर लाई। सजि सुंदर पालकीं मगाई।।
verses.translation: All the wise ministers tried to console him, but he heeded no counsel, not recognizing the moment. Again and again he pressed his daughter to his chest. Then beautiful palanquins were sent for.

verses.original:
प्रेमबिबस परिवारु सबु जानि सुलगन नरेस।
कुँअरि चढ़ाई पालकिन्ह सुमिरे सिद्धि गनेस।।338।।
verses.translation: The entire family was overwhelmed with love. Knowing it was the auspicious hour, the king placed the princesses in the palanquins, invoking Siddhi and Ganesha.

verses.original:
बहुबिधि भूप सुता समुझाई। नारिधरमु कुलरीति सिखाई।।
दासीं दास दिए बहुतेरे। सुचि सेवक जे प्रिय सिय केरे।।
verses.translation: In many ways, the king counseled his daughters, teaching them the duties of a wife and family customs. He gave them many male and female servants—devoted attendants dear to Sita.

verses.original:
सीय चलत ब्याकुल पुरबासी। होहिं सगुन सुभ मंगल रासी।।
भूसुर सचिव समेत समाजा। संग चले पहुँचावन राजा।।
verses.translation: As Sita departed, the citizens were distraught, yet omens appeared that were auspicious and full of blessings. The Brahmins, ministers, and the entire court accompanied the king to see them off.

verses.original:
समय बिलोकि बाजने बाजे। रथ गज बाजि बरातिन्ह साजे।।
दसरथ बिप्र बोलि सब लीन्हे। दान मान परिपूरन कीन्हे।।
verses.translation: Seeing the right time, the instruments began to play; the chariots, elephants, and horses of the wedding party were readied. Dasharatha summoned all the Brahmins and fulfilled their wishes with gifts and honors.

verses.original:
चरन सरोज धूरि धरि सीसा। मुदित महीपति पाइ असीसा।।
सुमिरि गजाननु कीन्ह पयाना। मंगलमूल सगुन भए नाना।।
verses.translation: Placing the dust of their lotus feet upon his head, the joyful king received their blessings. Invoking Lord Ganesha, the departure began, and many auspicious omens appeared.

verses.original:
सुर प्रसून बरषहि हरषि करहिं अपछरा गान।
चले अवधपति अवधपुर मुदित बजाइ निसान।।339।।
verses.translation: The gods joyfully showered flowers, and celestial nymphs sang. The King of Ayodhya set out for Ayodhya, happily sounding the drums.

verses.original:
नृप करि बिनय महाजन फेरे। सादर सकल मागने टेरे।।
भूषन बसन बाजि गज दीन्हे। प्रेम पोषि ठाढ़े सब कीन्हे।।
verses.translation: With humble entreaty, the king turned back the dignitaries. He respectfully called all the dependents and gave them ornaments, garments, horses, and elephants, nourishing them with love and making them stand firm.

verses.original:
बार बार बिरिदावलि भाषी। फिरे सकल रामहि उर राखी।।
बहुरि बहुरि कोसलपति कहहीं। जनकु प्रेमबस फिरै न चहहीं।।
verses.translation: Reciting their titles of honor again and again, they all turned back, treasuring Rama in their hearts. Again and again the King of Kosala urged Janaka to return, but Janaka, overcome with love, would not turn back.

verses.original:
पुनि कह भूपति बचन सुहाए। फिरिअ महीस दूरि बड़ि आए।।
राउ बहोरि उतरि भए ठाढ़े। प्रेम प्रबाह बिलोचन बाढ़े।।
verses.translation: Then the king spoke pleasant words: "Please return, O great king; you have come a very long distance." Janaka then dismounted and stood still; streams of love overflowed from his eyes.

verses.original:
तब बिदेह बोले कर जोरी। बचन सनेह सुधाँ जनु बोरी।।
करौ कवन बिधि बिनय बनाई। महाराज मोहि दीन्हि बड़ाई।।
verses.translation: Then Videha spoke with folded hands, his words drenched in the nectar of affection: "In what way can I compose my humble entreaty? O Great King, you have shown me such honor."

verses.original:
कोसलपति समधी सजन सनमाने सब भाँति।
मिलनि परसपर बिनय अति प्रीति न हृदयँ समाति।।340।।
verses.translation: The King of Kosala honored his in-law and dear relative in every way. Their mutual courtesies at parting were so full of love that their hearts could not contain it.

verses.original:
मुनि मंडलिहि जनक सिरु नावा। आसिरबादु सबहि सन पावा।।
सादर पुनि भेंटे जामाता। रूप सील गुन निधि सब भ्राता।।
verses.translation: Janaka bowed before the assembly of sages and received blessings from all. Then he affectionately embraced his sons-in-law—all four brothers, treasuries of beauty, virtue, and goodness.

verses.original:
जोरि पंकरुह पानि सुहाए। बोले बचन प्रेम जनु जाए।।
राम करौ केहि भाँति प्रसंसा। मुनि महेस मन मानस हंसा।।
verses.translation: With beautiful lotus-hands joined together, he spoke words as if born of pure love: "O Rama, how can I praise you—you, the swan in the lake of Shiva's mind?"

verses.original:
करहिं जोग जोगी जेहि लागी। कोहु मोहु ममता मदु त्यागी।।
ब्यापकु ब्रह्मु अलखु अबिनासी। चिदानंदु निरगुन गुनरासी।।
verses.translation: "For whose sake yogis practice yoga, renouncing anger, delusion, attachment, and pride. The all-pervading Brahman, invisible and imperishable—pure consciousness and bliss, formless yet the treasure of all virtues."

verses.original:
मन समेत जेहि जान न बानी। तरकि न सकहिं सकल अनुमानी।।
महिमा निगमु नेति कहि कहई। जो तिहुँ काल एकरस रहई।।
verses.translation: "Whom even the mind combined with speech cannot know, whom all logic and inference cannot fathom. Whose glory even the Vedas describe only by saying 'not this, not this'; who remains the same through all three times."

verses.original:
नयन बिषय मो कहुँ भयउ सो समस्त सुख मूल।
सबइ लाभु जग जीव कहँ भएँ ईसु अनुकुल।।341।।
verses.translation: "That Supreme Being has become visible to my eyes—the root of all happiness. All gain in the world comes to a soul when the Lord becomes favorable."

verses.original:
सबहि भाँति मोहि दीन्हि बड़ाई। निज जन जानि लीन्ह अपनाई।।
होहिं सहस दस सारद सेषा। करहिं कलप कोटिक भरि लेखा।।
verses.translation: "You have honored me in every way, accepting me as your own servant. Even if a thousand Sharadas and Sheshas were to write for tens of millions of kalpas,"

verses.original:
मोर भाग्य राउर गुन गाथा। कहि न सिराहिं सुनहु रघुनाथा।।
मै कछु कहउँ एक बल मोरें। तुम्ह रीझहु सनेह सुठि थोरें।।
verses.translation: "my good fortune and the saga of your virtues, O Lord of the Raghus, could never be fully told. I dare to speak only because of one strength of mine: you are pleased with even the slightest love."

verses.original:
बार बार मागउँ कर जोरें। मनु परिहरै चरन जनि भोरें।।
सुनि बर बचन प्रेम जनु पोषे। पूरनकाम रामु परितोषे।।
verses.translation: "Again and again, with folded hands, I beg: may my mind never stray from your feet, even in forgetfulness." Hearing these excellent words, nurtured as if by love itself, Rama was gratified, his purpose fulfilled.

verses.original:
करि बर बिनय ससुर सनमाने। पितु कौसिक बसिष्ठ सम जाने।।
बिनती बहुरि भरत सन कीन्ही। मिलि सप्रेमु पुनि आसिष दीन्ही।।
verses.translation: With fine courtesy, Rama honored his father-in-law, regarding him as equal to his father, to Kaushika, and to Vasishtha. Then he made humble entreaty to Bharata; meeting with affection, he again gave blessings.

verses.original:
मिले लखन रिपुसूदनहि दीन्हि असीस महीस।
भए परस्पर प्रेमबस फिरि फिरि नावहिं सीस।।342।।
verses.translation: He met Lakshmana and Shatrughna; the king gave blessings. Overcome with mutual love, they bowed their heads to each other again and again.

verses.original:
बार बार करि बिनय बड़ाई। रघुपति चले संग सब भाई।।
जनक गहे कौसिक पद जाई। चरन रेनु सिर नयनन्ह लाई।।
verses.translation: Again and again making humble and praising entreaties, the Lord of the Raghus departed with all his brothers. Janaka then went and clasped the feet of Kaushika, placing the dust on his head and eyes.

verses.original:
सुनु मुनीस बर दरसन तोरें। अगमु न कछु प्रतीति मन मोरें।।
जो सुखु सुजसु लोकपति चहहीं। करत मनोरथ सकुचत अहहीं।।
verses.translation: "Listen, O great sage: having seen your blessed form, nothing seems unattainable to my mind. The happiness and renown that even the world-guardians desire, hesitating to even formulate the wish—"

verses.original:
सो सुखु सुजसु सुलभ मोहि स्वामी। सब सिधि तव दरसन अनुगामी।।
कीन्हि बिनय पुनि पुनि सिरु नाई। फिरे महीसु आसिषा पाई।।
verses.translation: "that happiness and renown have come easily to me, O master; all accomplishments follow your sight." Having made his entreaty and bowed again and again, the king received blessings and turned back.

verses.original:
चली बरात निसान बजाई। मुदित छोट बड़ सब समुदाई।।
रामहि निरखि ग्राम नर नारी। पाइ नयन फलु होहिं सुखारी।।
verses.translation: The wedding procession set forth with drums playing; the whole company, small and great, was joyful. Seeing Rama, the men and women of each village received the reward of their eyes and were made happy.

verses.original:
बीच बीच बर बास करि मग लोगन्ह सुख देत।
अवध समीप पुनीत दिन पहुँची आइ जनेत।।343।।
verses.translation: Making fine rest-stops at intervals and giving joy to the wayside people, the wedding party reached near Ayodhya on an auspicious day.

verses.original:
हने निसान पनव बर बाजे। भेरि संख धुनि हय गय गाजे।।
झाँझि बिरव डिंडमीं सुहाई। सरस राग बाजहिं सहनाई।।
verses.translation: Kettledrums, tabors, and fine instruments were struck; conch shells, horns, and the clamor of horses and elephants resounded. Cymbals, clashing percussion, and beautiful hand-drums played; melodious shehnais sounded in sweet ragas.

verses.original:
पुर जन आवत अकनि बराता। मुदित सकल पुलकावलि गाता।।
निज निज सुंदर सदन सँवारे। हाट बाट चौहट पुर द्वारे।।
verses.translation: The citizens, hearing the wedding party approaching, were all delighted, their bodies covered with goosebumps. Everyone decorated their own beautiful homes—the marketplaces, streets, crossroads, and city gates.

verses.original:
गलीं सकल अरगजाँ सिंचाई। जहँ तहँ चौकें चारु पुराई।।
बना बजारु न जाइ बखाना। तोरन केतु पताक बिताना।।
verses.translation: All the lanes were sprinkled with perfumed water; beautiful designs were drawn at various spots. The decorated marketplace defied description, with arched gateways, flagstaffs, banners, and canopies.

verses.original:
सफल पूगफल कदलि रसाला। रोपे बकुल कदंब तमाला।।
लगे सुभग तरु परसत धरनी। मनिमय आलबाल कल करनी।।
verses.translation: Betel-nut palms with fruit, banana plants, and mango trees were planted; bakula, kadamba, and tamala trees were set up. Beautiful trees were placed so they touched the ground, with gem-encrusted raised platforms artfully made.

verses.original:
बिबिध भाँति मंगल कलस गृह गृह रचे सँवारि।
सुर ब्रह्मादि सिहाहिं सब रघुबर पुरी निहारि।।344।।
verses.translation: Auspicious pots of many kinds were arranged and decorated in every home. Even Brahma and other gods gazed with envy at the city of Raghubira.

verses.original:
भूप भवन तेहि अवसर सोहा। रचना देखि मदन मनु मोहा।।
मंगल सगुन मनोहरताई। रिधि सिधि सुख संपदा सुहाई।।
verses.translation: The king's palace at that time was so splendid that even Kamadeva's mind was enchanted by its decoration. Auspicious omens, captivating beauty, prosperity, and every kind of happiness were present.

verses.original:
जनु उछाह सब सहज सुहाए। तनु धरि धरि दसरथ दसरथ गृहँ छाए।।
देखन हेतु राम बैदेही। कहहु लालसा होहि न केही।।
verses.translation: It was as if all forms of joy had assumed bodies and filled Dasharatha's home. Who would not yearn to see Rama and Vaidehi?

verses.original:
जुथ जूथ मिलि चलीं सुआसिनि। निज छबि निदरहिं मदन बिलासनि।।
सकल सुमंगल सजें आरती। गावहिं जनु बहु बेष भारती।।
verses.translation: Groups upon groups of married women set forth, their beauty surpassing that of Kamadeva's consorts. All adorned with auspicious articles and arati plates, they sang as if Saraswati herself had assumed many forms.

verses.original:
भूपति भवन कोलाहलु होई। जाइ न बरनि समउ सुखु सोई।।
कौसल्यादि राम महतारीं। प्रेम बिबस तन दसा बिसारीं।।
verses.translation: There was a tumult in the king's palace; the joy of that moment cannot be described. Kausalya and the other mothers of Rama were so overwhelmed with love that they forgot their bodily state.

verses.original:
दिए दान बिप्रन्ह बिपुल पूजि गनेस पुरारी।
प्रमुदित परम दरिद्र जनु पाइ पदारथ चारि।।345।।
verses.translation: Generous gifts were given to the Brahmins; Ganesha and Shiva were worshipped. They were supremely delighted, like one who had been utterly destitute finding all four treasures of life.

verses.original:
मोद प्रमोद बिबस सब माता। चलहिं न चरन सिथिल भए गाता।।
राम दरस हित अति अनुरागीं। परिछनि साजु सजन सब लागीं।।
verses.translation: All the mothers were so overwhelmed with joy and love that their feet could not move and their limbs grew weak. Filled with longing for a glimpse of Rama, all began preparing for the welcoming ceremony.

verses.original:
बिबिध बिधान बाजने बाजे। मंगल मुदित सुमित्राँ साजे।।
हरद दूब दधि पल्लव फूला। पान पूगफल मंगल मूला।।
verses.translation: Instruments of many kinds were played. Sumitra joyfully arranged the auspicious articles: turmeric, sacred grass, curds, leaves, flowers, betel, areca nut—all sources of auspiciousness.

verses.original:
अच्छत अंकुर लोचन लाजा। मंजुल मंजरि तुलसि बिराजा।।
छुहे पुरट घट सहज सुहाए। मदन सकुन जनु नीड़ बनाए।।
verses.translation: Unbroken rice, sprouted grain, collyrium, and rice-powder; lovely mango-blossoms and sacred basil. Golden vessels with delicate charm, as if Kamadeva had built his nests in them.

verses.original:
सगुन सुंगध न जाहिं बखानी। मंगल सकल सजहिं सब रानी।।
रचीं आरतीं बहुत बिधाना। मुदित करहिं कल मंगल गाना।।
verses.translation: The auspicious fragrances and items are beyond description; all the queens prepared every auspicious thing. Many forms of arati were prepared, and they sang sweet auspicious songs.

verses.original:
कनक थार भरि मंगलन्हि कमल करन्हि लिएँ मात।
चलीं मुदित परिछनि करन पुलक पल्लवित गात।।346।।
verses.translation: With golden plates filled with auspicious articles held in their lotus hands, the mothers set forth joyfully to perform the welcoming ceremony, their bodies trembling with ecstasy.

verses.original:
धूप धूम नभु मेचक भयऊ। सावन घन घमंडु जनु ठयऊ।।
सुरतरु सुमन माल सुर बरषहिं। मनहुँ बलाक अवलि मनु करषहिं।।
verses.translation: The smoke of incense darkened the sky, as if the monsoon clouds of Shravana had gathered. Gods showered garlands of wish-fulfilling flowers, like rows of cranes tugging at the heart.

verses.original:
मंजुल मनिमय बंदनिवारे। मनहुँ पाकरिपु चाप सँवारे।।
प्रगटहिं दुरहिं अटन्ह पर भामिनि। चारु चपल जनु दमकहिं दामिनि।।
verses.translation: The lovely gem-studded arched gateways looked like so many rainbows of Indra. Women appeared and disappeared on the rooftops like beautiful, restless lightning flashing in the clouds.

verses.original:
दुंदुभि धुनि घन गरजनि घोरा। जाचक चातक दादुर मोरा।।
सुर सुगन्ध सुचि बरषहिं बारी। सुखी सकल ससि पुर नर नारी।।
verses.translation: The sound of drums was like the deep rumbling of thunder; the supplicants were like chataka birds, frogs, and peacocks. The gods poured forth pure fragrant water; all the people of the city, men and women, were happy.

verses.original:
समउ जानी गुर आयसु दीन्हा। पुर प्रबेसु रघुकुलमनि कीन्हा।।
सुमिरि संभु गिरजा गनराजा। मुदित महीपति सहित समाजा।।
verses.translation: Knowing the right moment, the guru gave his command, and the jewel of the Raghu clan entered the city. Invoking Shambhu, Girija, and Ganesha, the king with his entire retinue was delighted.

verses.original:
होहिं सगुन बरषहिं सुमन सुर दुंदुभीं बजाइ।
बिबुध बधू नाचहिं मुदित मंजुल मंगल गाइ।।347।।
verses.translation: Auspicious omens appeared; gods showered flowers, sounding celestial drums. Divine maidens danced joyfully, singing sweet and auspicious songs.

verses.original:
मागध सूत बंदि नट नागर। गावहिं जसु तिहु लोक उजागर।।
जय धुनि बिमल बेद बर बानी। दस दिसि सुनिअ सुमंगल सानी।।
verses.translation: Bards, minstrels, and clever panegyrists sang his glory, resplendent in the three worlds. Cries of "Victory!" and the pure, beautiful words of the Vedas were heard in all ten directions, mingled with auspiciousness.

verses.original:
बिपुल बाजने बाजन लागे। नभ सुर नगर लोग अनुरागे।।
बने बराती बरनि न जाहीं। महा मुदित मन सुख न समाहीं।।
verses.translation: Many instruments began to play; the gods in the sky and the people of the city were enraptured. The adorned wedding guests were beyond description; their immense joy could not be contained in their hearts.

verses.original:
पुरबासिन्ह तब राय जोहारे। देखत रामहि भए सुखारे।।
करहिं निछावरि मनिगन चीरा। बारि बिलोचन पुलक सरीरा।।
verses.translation: The citizens then saluted the king; seeing Rama, they were made happy. They waved gems and fine garments as offerings, their eyes filled with tears and their bodies thrilling.

verses.original:
आरति करहिं मुदित पुर नारी। हरषहिं निरखि कुँअर बर चारी।।
सिबिका सुभग ओहार उघारी। देखि दुलहिनिन्ह होहिं सुखारी।।
verses.translation: The joyful women of the city performed arati and rejoiced beholding the four handsome princes. Opening the beautiful curtains of the palanquins, they gazed upon the brides and were delighted.

verses.original:
एहि बिधि सबही देत सुखु आए राजदुआर।
मुदित मातु परुछनि करहिं बधुन्ह समेत कुमार।।348।।
verses.translation: In this way, giving joy to all, they arrived at the royal gate. The delighted mothers performed the welcoming ceremony for the princes together with their brides.

verses.original:
करहिं आरती बारहिं बारा। प्रेमु प्रमोदु कहै को पारा।।
भूषन मनि पट नाना जाती।।करही निछावरि अगनित भाँती।।
verses.translation: They waved the arati again and again; who can describe the extent of their love and delight? They showered gifts of ornaments, gems, garments, and countless other things in many ways.

verses.original:
बधुन्ह समेत देखि सुत चारी। परमानंद मगन महतारी।।
पुनि पुनि सीय राम छबि देखी।।मुदित सफल जग जीवन लेखी।।
verses.translation: Seeing the four sons with their brides, the mothers were immersed in supreme bliss. Gazing again and again at Sita and Rama's beauty, they considered their lives in this world truly fulfilled.

verses.original:
सखीं सीय मुख पुनि पुनि चाही। गान करहिं निज सुकृत सराही।।
बरषहिं सुमन छनहिं छन देवा। नाचहिं गावहिं लावहिं सेवा।।
verses.translation: The friends gazed at Sita's face again and again, singing songs and praising their own good fortune. Every moment the gods showered flowers; they danced, sang, and rendered service.

verses.original:
देखि मनोहर चारिउ जोरीं। सारद उपमा सकल ढँढोरीं।।
देत न बनहिं निपट लघु लागी। एकटक रहीं रूप अनुरागीं।।
verses.translation: Seeing the four enchanting couples, Sharada searched through all her comparisons. None would do—they all seemed utterly inadequate. She remained gazing, single-eyed, captivated by their beauty.

verses.original:
निगम नीति कुल रीति करि अरघ पाँवड़े देत।
बधुन्ह सहित सुत परिछि सब चलीं लवाइ निकेत।।349।।
verses.translation: Following Vedic and family customs, offering arghya and spreading carpets, the mothers welcomed the brides with the sons and led them all inside the house.

verses.original:
चारि सिंघासन सहज सुहाए। जनु मनोज निज हाथ बनाए।।
तिन्ह पर कुअँरि कुअँर बैठारे। सादर पाय पुनित पखारे।।
verses.translation: Four naturally beautiful thrones, as if fashioned by Kamadeva's own hands, were there. The princes and princesses were seated upon them, and their sacred feet were reverently washed.

verses.original:
धूप दीप नैबेद बेद बिधि। पूजे बर दुलहिनि मंगलनिधि।।
बारहिं बार आरती करहीं। ब्यजन चारु चामर सिर ढरहीं।।
verses.translation: Incense, lamps, and offerings were presented as prescribed by the Vedas; the auspicious groom and bride, treasuries of blessings, were worshipped. Arati was performed again and again; beautiful fans and whisks were waved over their heads.

verses.original:
बस्तु अनेक निछावर होहीं। भरीं प्रमोद मातु सब सोहीं।।
पावा परम तत्व जनु जोगीं। अमृत लहेउ जनु संतत रोगीं।।
verses.translation: Many articles were waved as offerings; all the mothers shone, filled with delight. It was as if a yogi had found the supreme truth, or one perpetually ill had found the nectar of immortality.

verses.original:
जनम रंक जनु पारस पावा। अंधहि लोचन लाभु सुहावा।।
मूक बदन जनु सारद छाई। मानहुँ समर सूर जय पाई।।
verses.translation: As if one born a pauper had found the philosopher's stone, or a blind person had regained the gift of sight. As if Saraswati had graced a mute person's tongue, or a warrior had won victory in battle.

verses.original:
एहि सुख ते सत कोटि गुन पावहिं मातु अनंदु।।
भाइन्ह सहित बिआहि घर आए रघुकुलचंदु।।350(क)।।
लोक रीत जननी करहिं बर दुलहिनि सकुचाहिं।
मोदु बिनोदु बिलोकि बड़ रामु मनहिं मुसकाहिं।।350(ख)।।
verses.translation: A hundred million times greater than such joys was the mothers' bliss. With his brothers, having married, the Moon of the Raghu dynasty had come home. The mothers performed the customary rites; the brides shyly submitted. Seeing the great joy and merriment, Rama smiled within his heart.

verses.original:
देव पितर पूजे बिधि नीकी। पूजीं सकल बासना जी की।।
सबहिं बंदि मागहिं बरदाना। भाइन्ह सहित राम कल्याना।।
verses.translation: The gods and ancestors were worshipped properly; every desire of the heart was fulfilled. Bowing to all, they asked for boons: the welfare of Rama together with his brothers.

verses.original:
अंतरहित सुर आसिष देहीं। मुदित मातु अंचल भरि लेंहीं।।
भूपति बोलि बराती लीन्हे। जान बसन मनि भूषन दीन्हे।।
verses.translation: The gods gave blessings invisibly; the delighted mothers gathered them in their garment-ends. The king summoned all the wedding guests and gave them vehicles, garments, gems, and ornaments.

verses.original:
आयसु पाइ राखि उर रामहि। मुदित गए सब निज निज धामहि।।
पुर नर नारि सकल पहिराए। घर घर बाजन लगे बधाए।।
verses.translation: Receiving permission and treasuring Rama in their hearts, all happily departed to their own homes. The king presented gifts to all the men and women of the city; celebrations resounded in every home.

verses.original:
जाचक जन जाचहि जोइ जोई। प्रमुदित राउ देहिं सोइ सोई।।
सेवक सकल बजनिआ नाना। पूरन किए दान सनमाना।।
verses.translation: Whatever the supplicants asked for, the delighted king gave them that very thing. All the servants and attendants of every kind—all were fully honored with gifts.

verses.original:
देंहिं असीस जोहारि सब गावहिं गुन गन गाथ।
तब गुर भूसुर सहित गृहँ गवनु कीन्ह नरनाथ।।351।।
verses.translation: All bowed and gave blessings, singing the glorious saga of his virtues. Then the king, together with the Brahmins and guru, returned to his palace.

verses.original:
जो बसिष्ठ अनुसासन दीन्ही। लोक बेद बिधि सादर कीन्ही।।
भूसुर भीर देखि सब रानी। सादर उठीं भाग्य बड़ जानी।।
verses.translation: Whatever instructions Vasishtha had given, the king dutifully carried out according to both scriptural and worldly custom. Seeing the host of Brahmins, all the queens rose respectfully, knowing it was great good fortune.

verses.original:
पाय पखारि सकल अन्हवाए। पूजि भली बिधि भूप जेवाँए।।
आदर दान प्रेम परिपोषे। देत असीस चले मन तोषे।।
verses.translation: They washed their feet, bathed them, and after proper worship, the king feasted them well. Nourished with respect, gifts, and love, the Brahmins departed contented, bestowing blessings.

verses.original:
बहु बिधि कीन्हि गाधिसुत पूजा। नाथ मोहि सम धन्य न दूजा।।
कीन्हि प्रसंसा भूपति भूरी। रानिन्ह सहित लीन्हि पग धूरी।।
verses.translation: He honored Vishwamitra in many ways: "My Lord, none is as blessed as I." The king praised him profusely, and all the queens took the dust of his feet.

verses.original:
भीतर भवन दीन्ह बर बासु। मन जोगवत रह नृप रनिवासू।।
पूजे गुर पद कमल बहोरी। कीन्हि बिनय उर प्रीति न थोरी।।
verses.translation: A fine room within the palace was given to him. The king and queens attended to his every comfort. Then they worshipped the guru Vasishtha's lotus feet, their humble devotion arising from no small love.

verses.original:
बधुन्ह समेत कुमार सब रानिन्ह सहित महीसु।
पुनि पुनि बंदत गुर चरन देत असीस मुनीसु।।352।।
verses.translation: The princes together with their brides, and the king together with the queens, bowed again and again at the guru's feet, and the great sage bestowed his blessings.

verses.original:
बिनय कीन्हि उर अति अनुरागें। सुत संपदा राखि सब आगें।।
नेगु मागि मुनिनायक लीन्हा। आसिरबादु बहुत बिधि दीन्हा।।
verses.translation: With deep love in his heart, he made his humble entreaty and placed his sons and all his wealth before the sage. The great sage accepted the customary gift and gave abundant blessings.

verses.original:
उर धरि रामहि सीय समेता। हरषि कीन्ह गुर गवनु निकेता।।
बिप्रबधू सब भूप बोलाई। चैल चारु भूषन पहिराई।।
verses.translation: Holding Rama and Sita in his heart, the guru joyfully departed for his hermitage. The king summoned all the Brahmin ladies and honored them with fine garments and ornaments.

verses.original:
बहुरि बोलाइ सुआसिनि लीन्हीं। रुचि बिचारि पहिरावनि दीन्हीं।।
नेगी नेग जोग सब लेहीं। रुचि अनुरुप भूपमनि देहीं।।
verses.translation: He then summoned the married women and gave them gifts suited to their tastes. All the customary recipients received their proper dues; the jewel among kings gave according to each one's pleasure.

verses.original:
प्रिय पाहुने पूज्य जे जाने। भूपति भली भाँति सनमाने।।
देव देखि रघुबीर बिबाहू। बरषि प्रसून प्रसंसि उछाहू।।
verses.translation: Dear guests and all whom he knew to be worthy—the king honored them well in every way. The gods, having witnessed Rama's wedding, showered flowers and praised the celebration.

verses.original:
चले निसान बजाइ सुर निज निज पुर सुख पाइ।
कहत परसपर राम जसु प्रेम न हृदयँ समाइ।।353।।
verses.translation: Sounding drums, the gods departed for their own heavens, having found great joy. They spoke to each other of Rama's glory, their hearts unable to contain their love.

verses.original:
सब बिधि सबहि समदि नरनाहू। रहा हृदयँ भरि पूरि उछाहू।।
जहँ रनिवासु तहाँ पगु धारे। सहित बहूटिन्ह कुअँर निहारे।।
verses.translation: Having honored everyone in every way, the king's heart was brimming with joy. Going to the inner court, he beheld the princes together with their brides.

verses.original:
लिए गोद करि मोद समेता। को कहि सकइ भयउ सुखु जेता।।
बधू सप्रेम गोद बैठारीं। बार बार हियँ हरषि दुलारीं।।
verses.translation: He took them in his lap with great delight; who can describe the happiness he felt? With love, he seated the brides in his lap and fondled them again and again with a joyful heart.

verses.original:
देखि समाजु मुदित रनिवासू। सब कें उर अनंद कियो बासू।।
कहेउ भूप जिमि भयउ बिबाहू। सुनि हरषु होत सब काहू।।
verses.translation: Seeing the whole scene, the inner court was delighted; bliss took up residence in everyone's heart. The king narrated how the wedding had taken place, and everyone who heard it was filled with joy.

verses.original:
जनक राज गुन सीलु बड़ाई। प्रीति रीति संपदा सुहाई।।
बहुबिधि भूप भाट जिमि बरनी। रानीं सब प्रमुदित सुनि करनी।।
verses.translation: He described King Janaka's virtues, character, grandeur, affection, customs, and magnificent wealth in many ways, like a bard. Hearing the story, all the queens were supremely delighted.

verses.original:
सुतन्ह समेत नहाइ नृप बोलि बिप्र गुर ग्याति।
भोजन कीन्ह अनेक बिधि घरी पंच गइ राति।।354।।
verses.translation: After bathing with his sons, the king invited the Brahmins, guru, and kinsmen. A feast of many kinds was held, and five hours of the night passed.

verses.original:
मंगलगान करहिं बर भामिनि। भै सुखमूल मनोहर जामिनि।।
अँचइ पान सब काहूँ पाए। स्त्रग सुगंध भूषित छबि छाए।।
verses.translation: Beautiful women sang auspicious songs; the delightful night became the very source of happiness. Everyone received betel-leaf and fragrant garlands, and shone adorned with ornaments.

verses.original:
रामहि देखि रजायसु पाई। निज निज भवन चले सिर नाई।।
प्रेम प्रमोद बिनोदु बढ़ाई। समउ समाजु मनोहरताई।।
verses.translation: After gazing upon Rama and receiving his permission, all departed to their own homes, bowing their heads. The love, delight, joy, spectacle, occasion, company, and beauty of it all—

verses.original:
कहि न सकहि सत सारद सेसू। बेद बिरंचि महेस गनेसू।।
सो मै कहौं कवन बिधि बरनी। भूमिनागु सिर धरइ कि धरनी।।
verses.translation: —cannot be described even by a hundred Sharadas and Sheshas, nor by the Vedas, Brahma, Shiva, or Ganesha. How then can I attempt to describe it? Can the earth-serpent bear the earth upon his head?

verses.original:
नृप सब भाँति सबहि सनमानी। कहि मृदु बचन बोलाई रानी।।
बधू लरिकनीं पर घर आईं। राखेहु नयन पलक की नाई।।
verses.translation: The king honored everyone in every way, and calling the queens, spoke gentle words: "The brides are young girls who have come to a new home; guard them as you would the pupils of your eyes."

verses.original:
लरिका श्रमित उनीद बस सयन करावहु जाइ।
अस कहि गे बिश्रामगृहँ राम चरन चितु लाइ।।355।।
verses.translation: "The children are tired and drowsy; go and put them to sleep." Saying this, the king retired to his resting chamber with his mind fixed on Rama's feet.

verses.original:
भूप बचन सुनि सहज सुहाए। जरित कनक मनि पलँग डसाए।।
सुभग सुरभि पय फेन समाना। कोमल कलित सुपेतीं नाना।।
verses.translation: Hearing the king's naturally sweet words, beds of gold inlaid with gems were set up. Soft and beautiful white coverlets, delicate as the foam of cow's milk, were spread upon them.

verses.original:
उपबरहन बर बरनि न जाहीं। स्त्रग सुगंध मनिमंदिर माहीं।।
रतनदीप सुठि चारु चँदोवा। कहत न बनइ जान जेहिं जोवा।।
verses.translation: The pillows were beyond description. Garlands and perfumes filled the gem-studded chambers. The gem-lamps and the exquisite canopy above—only one who has seen them can appreciate them.

verses.original:
सेज रुचिर रचि रामु उठाए। प्रेम समेत पलँग पौढ़ाए।।
अग्या पुनि पुनि भाइन्ह दीन्ही। निज निज सेज सयन तिन्ह कीन्ही।।
verses.translation: Preparing lovely beds, the queens lifted Rama and lovingly laid him on the couch. They then directed the brothers, and each one retired to his own bed.

verses.original:
देखि स्याम मृदु मंजुल गाता। कहहिं सप्रेम बचन सब माता।।
मारग जात भयावनि भारी। केहि बिधि तात ताड़का मारी।।
verses.translation: Seeing his dark, tender, and beautiful limbs, all the mothers spoke with loving words: "The path was terrible and frightening; how, dear son, did you slay Tadaka?"

verses.original:
घोर निसाचर बिकट भट समर गनहिं नहिं काहु।।
मारे सहित सहाय किमि खल मारीच सुबाहु।।356।।
verses.translation: "Those fierce demons were terrible warriors who feared no one in battle. How did you slay the wicked Maricha and Subahu along with their allies?"

verses.original:
मुनि प्रसाद बलि तात तुम्हारी। ईस अनेक करवरें टारी।।
मख रखवारी करि दुहुँ भाई। गुरु प्रसाद सब बिद्या पाई।।
verses.translation: "By the sage's grace and your own valor, dear son, the Lord warded off many calamities. Guarding the sacrifice, both brothers received all knowledge by the guru's grace."

verses.original:
मुनितय तरी लगत पग धूरी। कीरति रही भुवन भरि पूरी।।
कमठ पीठि पबि कूट कठोरा। नृप समाज महुँ सिव धनु तोरा।।
verses.translation: "By the touch of your foot-dust, the sage's wife Ahalya was liberated; your fame has spread through all the worlds. On Shiva's bow, hard as the back of a tortoise or the edge of a thunderbolt, you exerted your might in the assembly of kings."

verses.original:
बिस्व बिजय जसु जानकि पाई। आए भवन ब्याहि सब भाई।।
सकल अमानुष करम तुम्हारे। केवल कौसिक कृपाँ सुधारे।।
verses.translation: "Having won world-conquering glory and won Janaki, all the brothers came home married. All your superhuman deeds were accomplished solely by the grace of Kaushika."

verses.original:
आजु सुफल जग जनमु हमारा। देखि तात बिधुबदन तुम्हारा।।
जे दिन गए तुम्हहि बिनु देखें। ते बिरंचि जनि पारहिं लेखें।।
verses.translation: "Today our birth in this world is truly rewarded, seeing your moon-like face, dear son. The days that passed without seeing you—may Brahma never count them."

verses.original:
राम प्रतोषीं मातु सब कहि बिनीत बर बैन।
सुमिरि संभु गुर बिप्र पद किए नीदबस नैन।।357।।
verses.translation: Rama pleased all his mothers with humble and gentle words. Then, meditating upon Shambhu, the guru, and the Brahmins, he closed his eyes in sleep.

verses.original:
नीदउँ बदन सोह सुठि लोना। मनहुँ साँझ सरसीरुह सोना।।
घर घर करहिं जागरन नारीं। देहिं परसपर मंगल गारीं।।
verses.translation: Even in sleep, his face looked supremely lovely, like a lotus at dusk drooping in gentle beauty. In every home, women kept night-vigils, exchanging auspicious humorous songs with one another.

verses.original:
पुरी बिराजति राजति रजनी। रानीं कहहिं बिलोकहु सजनी।।
सुंदर बधुन्ह सासु लै सोई। फनिकन्ह जनु सिरमनि उर गोई।।
verses.translation: The city and the night both shone gloriously. The queens said: "Look, dear friend! The mothers-in-law took the lovely brides and put them to sleep, as a cobra hides its crest-jewel in its hood."

verses.original:
प्रात पुनीत काल प्रभु जागे। अरुनचूड़ बर बोलन लागे।।
बंदि मागधन्हि गुनगन गाए। पुरजन द्वार जोहारन आए।।
verses.translation: At the pure hour of dawn, the Lord awoke; the fine roosters began to crow. Bards and panegyrists sang his glorious virtues; citizens came to the gate to pay their respects.

verses.original:
बंदि बिप्र सुर गुर पितु माता। पाइ असीस मुदित सब भ्राता।।
जननिन्ह सादर बदन निहारे। भूपति संग द्वार पगु धारे।।
verses.translation: After honoring the Brahmins, gods, guru, and parents, all the brothers joyfully received blessings. The mothers lovingly gazed upon their faces, and they went with the king to the palace gate.

verses.original:
कीन्ह सौच सब सहज सुचि सरित पुनीत नहाइ।
प्रातक्रिया करि तात पहिं आए चारिउ भाइ।।358।।
verses.translation: Performing their ablutions in the sacred river with natural purity, and completing their morning devotions, all four brothers came to their father.

verses.original:
भूप बिलोकि लिए उर लाई। बैठै हरषि रजायसु पाई।।
देखि रामु सब सभा जुड़ानी। लोचन लाभ अवधि अनुमानी।।
verses.translation: The king looked at them and drew them to his heart; receiving permission, they sat down joyfully. Seeing Rama, the entire court felt refreshed, as if they had reached the very limit of the eyes' reward.

verses.original:
पुनि बसिष्टु मुनि कौसिक आए। सुभग आसनन्हि मुनि बैठाए।।
सुतन्ह समेत पूजि पद लागे। निरखि रामु दोउ गुर अनुरागे।।
verses.translation: Then Vasishtha and sage Kaushika arrived; the king seated the sages on handsome seats. Together with his sons, he worshipped their feet; seeing Rama, both gurus were filled with love.

verses.original:
कहहिं बसिष्टु धरम इतिहासा। सुनहिं महीसु सहित रनिवासा।।
मुनि मन अगम गाधिसुत करनी। मुदित बसिष्ट बिपुल बिधि बरनी।।
verses.translation: Vasishtha narrated stories of dharma, which the king listened to along with the inner court. The deeds of Vishwamitra, unfathomable even to sages, Vasishtha described in many ways with great delight.

verses.original:
बोले बामदेउ सब साँची। कीरति कलित लोक तिहुँ माची।।
सुनि आनंदु भयउ सब काहू। राम लखन उर अधिक उछाहू।।
verses.translation: Vamadeva confirmed everything was true—a spotless fame had spread through the three worlds. Hearing this, everyone was overjoyed; Rama and Lakshmana's hearts swelled with special pride.

verses.original:
मंगल मोद उछाह नित जाहिं दिवस एहि भाँति।
उमगी अवध अनंद भरि अधिक अधिक अधिकाति।।359।।
verses.translation: Auspiciousness, delight, and festivity continued daily in this manner. Ayodhya overflowed with bliss, growing ever greater and greater.

verses.original:
सुदिन सोधि कल कंकन छौरे। मंगल मोद बिनोद न थोरे।।
नित नव सुखु सुर देखि सिहाहीं। अवध जन्म जाचहिं बिधि पाहीं।।
verses.translation: On an auspicious day, the wedding bracelets were ceremonially removed—no small festivity and joy. The ever-new happiness made even the gods envious; they prayed to Brahma for birth in Ayodhya.

verses.original:
बिस्वामित्रु चलन नित चहहीं। राम सप्रेम बिनय बस रहहीं।।
दिन दिन सयगुन भूपति भाऊ। देखि सराह महामुनिराऊ।।
verses.translation: Vishwamitra wished to leave every day, but Rama's loving entreaties kept him there. Day by day, the sage praised the king's growing devotion and excellent conduct.

verses.original:
मागत बिदा राउ अनुरागे। सुतन्ह समेत ठाढ़ भे आगे।।
नाथ सकल संपदा तुम्हारी। मैं सेवकु समेत सुत नारी।।
verses.translation: When at last Vishwamitra asked for leave, the loving king stood before him with his sons: "My Lord, all my wealth is yours; I am your servant, together with my sons and queens."

verses.original:
करब सदा लरिकनः पर छोहू। दरसन देत रहब मुनि मोहू।।
अस कहि राउ सहित सुत रानी। परेउ चरन मुख आव न बानी।।
verses.translation: "Always show your grace to these children; grant me your presence again, O sage." So saying, the king with his sons and queens fell at the sage's feet, unable to speak further.

verses.original:
दीन्ह असीस बिप्र बहु भाँती। चले न प्रीति रीति कहि जाती।।
रामु सप्रेम संग सब भाई। आयसु पाइ फिरे पहुँचाई।।
verses.translation: The sage gave many blessings and departed—the parting love cannot be described. Rama with all his brothers lovingly accompanied him and, receiving permission, returned after escorting him.

verses.original:
राम रूपु भूपति भगति ब्याहु उछाहु अनंदु।
जात सराहत मनहिं मन मुदित गाधिकुलचंदु।।360।।
verses.translation: Reflecting on Rama's beauty, the king's devotion, the wedding festivities, and the bliss, the delighted Moon of Gadhi's dynasty praised them in his heart as he went.

verses.original:
बामदेव रघुकुल गुर ग्यानी। बहुरि गाधिसुत कथा बखानी।।
सुनि मुनि सुजसु मनहिं मन राऊ। बरनत आपन पुन्य प्रभाऊ।।
verses.translation: Vamadeva, the wise guru of the Raghu clan, once again narrated the glory of Vishwamitra. The king heard the sage's fair renown and reflected in his heart upon his own good fortune.

verses.original:
बहुरे लोग रजायसु भयऊ। सुतन्ह समेत नृपति गृहँ गयऊ।।
जहँ तहँ राम ब्याहु सबु गावा। सुजसु पुनीत लोक तिहुँ छावा।।
verses.translation: The people were given leave and returned; the king went home with his sons. Everywhere, the marriage of Rama was being celebrated; its pure and holy fame spread through the three worlds.

verses.original:
आए ब्याहि रामु घर जब तें। बसइ अनंद अवध सब तब तें।।
प्रभु बिबाहँ जस भयउ उछाहू। सकहिं न बरनि गिरा अहिनाहू।।
verses.translation: Ever since Rama came home married, unending bliss has dwelt in Ayodhya. The joy that arose from the Lord's wedding—not even the tongue of the Serpent-King can describe it.

verses.original:
कबिकुल जीवनु पावन जानी।।राम सीय जसु मंगल खानी।।
तेहि ते मैं कछु कहा बखानी। करन पुनीत हेतु निज बानी।।
verses.translation: Knowing the glory of Rama and Sita to be the life-breath of poets and a mine of auspiciousness, I have narrated some portion of it here, to purify my own speech.

verses.original:
निज गिरा पावनि करन कारन राम जसु तुलसी कह्यो।
रघुबीर चरित अपार बारिधि पारु कबि कौनें लह्यो।।
उपबीत ब्याह उछाह मंगल सुनि जे सादर गावहीं।
बैदेहि राम प्रसाद ते जन सर्बदा सुखु पावहीं।।
verses.translation: To purify his own speech, Tulasidas has sung the glory of Rama. The boundless ocean of Raghubira's deeds—what poet has ever found its farther shore? Those who reverently sing or listen to the sacred thread ceremony, the wedding, and its joyful celebrations will, by the grace of Vaidehi and Rama, always find happiness.

verses.original:
सिय रघुबीर बिबाहु जे सप्रेम गावहिं सुनहिं।
तिन्ह कहुँ सदा उछाहु मंगलायतन राम जसु।।361।।
verses.translation: Those who lovingly sing or listen to the wedding of Sita and Raghubira—for them there is ever joy, for the glory of Rama is the abode of all auspiciousness.
